## 8319: perception, spiritual (id: 8319)

The ability to see beneath the outward form to the underlying, often hidden, reality. It is a necessary gift in dealing with people, understanding spiritual teaching and interpreting events. Perception is important for effective service, though is often lacking, even among God's people.

Perception in dealing with people
---------------------------------

### God's knowledge of human hearts

**[PSA 139:1–139:4](https://ref.ly/Ps139:1-Ps139:4)** *See also* [PSA 17:3](https://ref.ly/Ps17:3), [PSA 44:21](https://ref.ly/Ps44:21); [PRO 24:12](https://ref.ly/Prov24:12)

### Jesus Christ's perception of those around him

**[JHN 2:24–2:25](https://ref.ly/John2:24-John2:25)** *See also* [ISA 11:1–11:4](https://ref.ly/Isa11:1-Isa11:4); [MAT 9:3–9:4](https://ref.ly/Matt9:3-Matt9:4) parallels [MRK 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Mark2:7-Mark2:8), [LUK 5:21–5:22](https://ref.ly/Luke5:21-Luke5:22); [JHN 4:16–4:19](https://ref.ly/John4:16-John4:19); [REV 2:23](https://ref.ly/Rev2:23)

### True understanding by God's servants

[NEH 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Neh6:1-Neh6:2); ; [JOB 21:27](https://ref.ly/Job21:27); ; [ACT 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:3)

Perception of spiritual truth
-----------------------------

### Recognising God's ultimate authority

**[REV 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Rev4:1-Rev4:2)** *See also* [2KI 6:15–6:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:15-2Kgs6:17); [DAN 7:8–7:14](https://ref.ly/Dan7:8-Dan7:14)

### Understanding Jesus Christ's teaching

**[MAT 13:10–13:11](https://ref.ly/Matt13:10-Matt13:11)** parallels [MRK 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Mark4:10-Mark4:11), [LUK 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:9-Luke8:10) *See also* [MAT 13:16–13:23](https://ref.ly/Matt13:16-Matt13:23) parallels [MRK 4:13–4:20](https://ref.ly/Mark4:13-Mark4:20), [LUK 8:11–8:15](https://ref.ly/Luke8:11-Luke8:15) *The parable of the sower is about the way Jesus Christ's teaching in parables is received.*

### Understanding who Jesus Christ is

**[MAT 16:15–16:17](https://ref.ly/Matt16:15-Matt16:17)** parallels [MRK 8:29](https://ref.ly/Mark8:29), [LUK 9:20](https://ref.ly/Luke9:20) *See also* [ACT 8:30–8:35](https://ref.ly/Acts8:30-Acts8:35); [ISA 53:7–53:8](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7-Isa53:8)

### Understanding about Jesus Christ's death and resurrection

**[LUK 24:45–24:46](https://ref.ly/Luke24:45-Luke24:46)** *See also* [LUK 24:30–24:35](https://ref.ly/Luke24:30-Luke24:35); [JHN 12:16](https://ref.ly/John12:16)

Perception to interpret events
------------------------------

**[2SA 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:11-2Sam5:12)** *The building of a palace in the newly captured Jerusalem confirms to David his election by God*; **[ISA 43:9–43:12](https://ref.ly/Isa43:9-Isa43:12)** *Through his miraculous direction of his people's history, God is perceived as the one, true God.* *See also* [ISA 41:18–41:20](https://ref.ly/Isa41:18-Isa41:20), [ISA 64:4](https://ref.ly/Isa64:4); [JON 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:12) *Jonah recognises God's hand in the storm*; [ACT 10:34–10:35](https://ref.ly/Acts10:34-Acts10:35) *Through the circumstances of his guidance, Peter realises that God's salvation is for Gentiles too.*

Perception leading to effective service
---------------------------------------

**[1KI 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:9)** parallel [2CH 1:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:10) *See also* [1SA 16:7–16:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:7-1Sam16:13) *Samuel perceives that David is to be king*; [2KI 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:8-2Kgs4:10) *A Shunammite's perception of Elisha leads her to extend hospitality to him*; [HEB 11:27](https://ref.ly/Heb11:27) *Moses' perception of the invisible God*

Lack of perception
------------------

### By those in the OT

**[ISA 43:19](https://ref.ly/Isa43:19)** *See also* [EXO 5:2](https://ref.ly/Exod5:2) *Pharaoh*; [NUM 22:31–22:34](https://ref.ly/Num22:31-Num22:34) *Balaam*; [DEU 32:28–32:29](https://ref.ly/Deut32:28-Deut32:29) *Israel in the wilderness*; [ISA 29:10–29:12](https://ref.ly/Isa29:10-Isa29:12) *the people of Jerusalem*; [ISA 42:20](https://ref.ly/Isa42:20) *Israel*

### By some Jews in the NT

[MAT 13:13–13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:13-Matt13:15) parallels [MRK 4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:12), [LUK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:10); ; [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10); ; [LUK 12:56](https://ref.ly/Luke12:56); ; [JHN 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/John3:3-John3:4); ; [ACT 28:26](https://ref.ly/Acts28:26)

### By Jesus Christ's disciples

[MAT 15:16](https://ref.ly/Matt15:16) parallel [MRK 7:18](https://ref.ly/Mark7:18) *not understanding what makes a person unclean*; ; [MAT 16:9](https://ref.ly/Matt16:9) parallel [MRK 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Mark8:17-Mark8:18) *not understanding about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees*; ; [MAT 16:22](https://ref.ly/Matt16:22) parallel [MRK 8:32](https://ref.ly/Mark8:32) *Peter cannot accept that Jesus Christ must be crucified*; ; [JHN 14:9](https://ref.ly/John14:9) *Philip does not understand that Jesus Christ reveals the Father*; ; [JHN 20:9](https://ref.ly/John20:9) *not understanding about the resurrection*; [JHN 20:14](https://ref.ly/John20:14), [JHN 21:4](https://ref.ly/John21:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 139:1-4; Psalms 17:3; Psalms 44:21; Proverbs 24:12; John 2:24-25; Isaiah 11:1-4; Matthew 9:3-4; Mark 2:7-8; Luke 5:21-22; John 4:16-19; Revelation 2:23; Nehemiah 6:1-2; Job 21:27; Acts 5:1-3; Revelation 4:1-2; 2 Kings 6:15-17; Daniel 7:8-14; Matthew 13:10-11; Mark 4:10-11; Luke 8:9-10; Matthew 13:16-23; Mark 4:13-20; Luke 8:11-15; Matthew 16:15-17; Mark 8:29; Luke 9:20; Acts 8:30-35; Isaiah 53:7-8; Luke 24:45-46; Luke 24:30-35; John 12:16; 2 Samuel 5:11-12; Isaiah 43:9-12; Isaiah 41:18-20; Isaiah 64:4; Jonah 1:12; Acts 10:34-35; 1 Kings 3:9; 2 Chronicles 1:10; 1 Samuel 16:7-13; 2 Kings 4:8-10; Hebrews 11:27; Isaiah 43:19; Exodus 5:2; Numbers 22:31-34; Deuteronomy 32:28-29; Isaiah 29:10-12; Isaiah 42:20; Matthew 13:13-15; Mark 4:12; Luke 8:10; Isaiah 6:9-10; Luke 12:56; John 3:3-4; Acts 28:26; Matthew 15:16; Mark 7:18; Matthew 16:9; Mark 8:17-18; Matthew 16:22; Mark 8:32; John 14:9; John 20:9; John 20:14; John 21:4

## 8320: perfection (id: 8320)

The state of completion and total self\-sufficiency, characterised by an absence of any flaw or fault. This perfection is found in God himself and is the ultimate goal of the Christian life.

* **Associated Articles:** 8321: perfection, divine (ID: `8321@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8322: perfection, human (ID: `8322@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8321: perfection, divine (id: 8321)

The state of being whole, complete or without defect in any way.

Only God is absolutely perfect
------------------------------

### In his nature and character

**[DEU 32:4](https://ref.ly/Deut32:4)** *See also* [ECC 3:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:14); [MAT 5:48](https://ref.ly/Matt5:48); [ROM 11:33–11:36](https://ref.ly/Rom11:33-Rom11:36)

### In his word

**[PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6)** *See also* [PSA 19:7](https://ref.ly/Ps19:7); [PRO 8:8–8:9](https://ref.ly/Prov8:8-Prov8:9), [PRO 30:5](https://ref.ly/Prov30:5); [2TI 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:16-2Tim3:17); [JAS 1:25](https://ref.ly/Jas1:25)

### In his morality

**[HAB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hab1:13)** *See also* [2SA 22:26–22:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:26-2Sam22:27); [JOB 4:17](https://ref.ly/Job4:17); [1JN 3:3](https://ref.ly/1John3:3)

### In his will and providence

**[2SA 22:31](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:31)** parallel [PSA 18:30](https://ref.ly/Ps18:30) *See also* [ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2); [JAS 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jas1:17)

### In his knowledge

**[JOB 37:16](https://ref.ly/Job37:16)** *See also* [PSA 139:4](https://ref.ly/Ps139:4); [ISA 40:13–40:14](https://ref.ly/Isa40:13-Isa40:14); [HEB 4:13](https://ref.ly/Heb4:13)

### In his faithfulness

[ISA 25:1](https://ref.ly/Isa25:1)

God's perfect creation
----------------------

### In its original condition

**[GEN 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Gen2:1-Gen2:2)** *See also* [GEN 1:10](https://ref.ly/Gen1:10), [GEN 1:12](https://ref.ly/Gen1:12), [GEN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Gen1:18), [GEN 1:21](https://ref.ly/Gen1:21), [GEN 1:25](https://ref.ly/Gen1:25), [GEN 1:31](https://ref.ly/Gen1:31); [ISA 40:25–40:26](https://ref.ly/Isa40:25-Isa40:26); [HEB 4:3](https://ref.ly/Heb4:3)

### The coming new creation

**[1CO 13:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:10)** *See also* [2PE 3:13](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:13); [REV 21:1–21:5](https://ref.ly/Rev21:1-Rev21:5), [REV 22:1–22:5](https://ref.ly/Rev22:1-Rev22:5)

God's perfect self\-revelation in Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------------

**[JHN 14:9](https://ref.ly/John14:9)** *See also* [JHN 1:14](https://ref.ly/John1:14); [2CO 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:4); [COL 1:15](https://ref.ly/Col1:15); [HEB 1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:3)

The moral perfection of Jesus Christ as God's Son
-------------------------------------------------

**[1PE 2:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:22)** *See also* [ISA 53:9](https://ref.ly/Isa53:9); [2CO 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21); [HEB 2:10](https://ref.ly/Heb2:10), [HEB 4:15](https://ref.ly/Heb4:15), [HEB 5:9](https://ref.ly/Heb5:9), [HEB 7:26](https://ref.ly/Heb7:26); [1JN 3:5](https://ref.ly/1John3:5)

The perfect sacrifice of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------

**[HEB 9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:14)** [LEV 22:21](https://ref.ly/Lev22:21); ; [NUM 19:2](https://ref.ly/Num19:2); [1PE 1:19](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:19)

## 8322: perfection, human (id: 8322)

Human wholeness in the image of God, given at creation and lost in the fall, is to be fully restored in Jesus Christ.

Humanity was created perfect
----------------------------

**[GEN 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Gen1:26-Gen1:27)** *See also* [GEN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Gen5:1); [EZK 28:12–28:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:12-Ezek28:15) *The king of Tyre is described in terms of the creation and fall of humanity*; [JAS 3:9](https://ref.ly/Jas3:9)

Perfection was lost through sin
-------------------------------

**[ROM 3:23](https://ref.ly/Rom3:23)** *See also* [GEN 2:17](https://ref.ly/Gen2:17), [GEN 3:16–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:16-Gen3:19), [GEN 3:23](https://ref.ly/Gen3:23); [JOB 15:14–15:16](https://ref.ly/Job15:14-Job15:16); [EZK 28:15–28:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:15-Ezek28:17); [ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21)

The need for moral perfection
-----------------------------

**[DEU 18:13](https://ref.ly/Deut18:13)** *See also* [GEN 17:1](https://ref.ly/Gen17:1); [JOB 25:4](https://ref.ly/Job25:4); [PSA 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Ps24:3-Ps24:4)

Human inability to keep God's law perfectly
-------------------------------------------

**[HEB 7:19](https://ref.ly/Heb7:19)** *See also* [HEB 7:11](https://ref.ly/Heb7:11), [HEB 10:1](https://ref.ly/Heb10:1)

Perfection and redemption
-------------------------

### God's purpose for believers

**[COL 1:22](https://ref.ly/Col1:22)** *See also* [DAN 11:35](https://ref.ly/Dan11:35), [DAN 12:10](https://ref.ly/Dan12:10); [EPH 5:27](https://ref.ly/Eph5:27) *God's purpose for the church*; [HEB 10:14](https://ref.ly/Heb10:14), [HEB 11:40](https://ref.ly/Heb11:40), [HEB 12:23](https://ref.ly/Heb12:23); [JAS 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jas1:4); [JUD 1:24](https://ref.ly/Jude1:24)

### The aim of Christian ministry

**[COL 1:28](https://ref.ly/Col1:28)** *See also* [2CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:2)

### The aim of the Christian

**[PHP 3:12](https://ref.ly/Phil3:12)** *See also* [2CO 13:9–13:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:9-2Cor13:11); [EPH 4:13](https://ref.ly/Eph4:13); [HEB 6:1](https://ref.ly/Heb6:1); [JAS 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jas1:4)

Characteristics of perfection
-----------------------------

### Perfect love

**[MAT 5:48](https://ref.ly/Matt5:48)** *Jesus Christ patterning his disciples' love on the love God has for all people.* *See also* [1JN 4:12](https://ref.ly/1John4:12), [1JN 4:18](https://ref.ly/1John4:18)

### Perfect holiness

**[2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1)** *See also* [PHP 1:10](https://ref.ly/Phil1:10), [PHP 2:15](https://ref.ly/Phil2:15); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27); [2PE 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:14)

### Perfect obedience

[1TI 6:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:14); ; [1JN 2:5](https://ref.ly/1John2:5)

### Perfect speech

[JAS 3:2](https://ref.ly/Jas3:2)

### Perfect unity with other believers

**[JHN 17:23](https://ref.ly/John17:23)** *See also* [1CO 1:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:10); [COL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Col3:14)

### Perfect joy

**[JHN 15:11](https://ref.ly/John15:11)** *See also* [JHN 3:29](https://ref.ly/John3:29), [JHN 16:24](https://ref.ly/John16:24); [1JN 1:4](https://ref.ly/1John1:4); [2JN 1:12](https://ref.ly/2John1:12)

### Perfect peace

**[ISA 26:3](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3)** *See also* [PSA 119:165](https://ref.ly/Ps119:165); [PHP 4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:7)

### Perfect knowledge

**[1CO 13:9–13:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:9-1Cor13:12)** *See also* [ROM 15:14](https://ref.ly/Rom15:14) *Although complete knowledge is inaccessible here and now, believers have all they need to live the Christian life*; [COL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Col2:2)

Perfection in describing human physical beauty
----------------------------------------------

[2SA 14:25–14:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam14:25-2Sam14:27); ; [SNG 4:7](https://ref.ly/Song4:7), [SNG 5:2](https://ref.ly/Song5:2), [SNG 6:9](https://ref.ly/Song6:9)

## 8324: purity (id: 8324)

An absence of blemish or stain, especially sin. The state of being morally and spiritually pure, which is seen by Scripture as the result of being the people of God and also as an expected distinguishing mark of the church.

* **Associated Articles:** 8325: purity, nature of (ID: `8325@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8326: purity, moral and spiritual (ID: `8326@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8325: purity, nature of (id: 8325)

The OT recognises a close link between outer cleanliness and inner purity. In the NT, special emphasis is placed on inner purity resulting from spiritual renewal.

Physical cleansing from impurity by washing or refining
-------------------------------------------------------

[GEN 18:4](https://ref.ly/Gen18:4); ; [JOB 9:30](https://ref.ly/Job9:30); ; [PSA 12:6](https://ref.ly/Ps12:6)

Ritual impurity must be avoided or cleansed
-------------------------------------------

**[LEV 10:10–10:11](https://ref.ly/Lev10:10-Lev10:11)** *See also* [ISA 35:8](https://ref.ly/Isa35:8), [ISA 52:1](https://ref.ly/Isa52:1), [ISA 52:11](https://ref.ly/Isa52:11); [MRK 7:2](https://ref.ly/Mark7:2), [MRK 7:4](https://ref.ly/Mark7:4)

Sexual sources of ritual impurity
---------------------------------

### Childbirth

**[LEV 12:2](https://ref.ly/Lev12:2)** *See also* [LEV 12:5](https://ref.ly/Lev12:5); [LUK 2:22](https://ref.ly/Luke2:22)

### Sexual intercourse

[EXO 19:15](https://ref.ly/Exod19:15); ; [LEV 15:18](https://ref.ly/Lev15:18); ; [1SA 21:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:4)

### Seminal emission

[LEV 15:16–15:17](https://ref.ly/Lev15:16-Lev15:17); ; [DEU 23:10–23:11](https://ref.ly/Deut23:10-Deut23:11); ; [1SA 20:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:26)

### Menstruation

[LEV 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Lev15:19-Lev15:20); ; [2SA 11:2–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:4); ; [EZK 18:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:6)

### Abnormal discharge

[LEV 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Lev15:1-Lev15:2), [LEV 15:25–15:27](https://ref.ly/Lev15:25-Lev15:27); ; [MAT 9:20](https://ref.ly/Matt9:20) parallels [MRK 5:25](https://ref.ly/Mark5:25), [LUK 8:43](https://ref.ly/Luke8:43)

Other sources of ritual impurity
--------------------------------

### Dead bodies

**[NUM 19:11–19:16](https://ref.ly/Num19:11-Num19:16)**

### Unclean creatures

**[GEN 7:2](https://ref.ly/Gen7:2)** *See also* [LEV 11:24](https://ref.ly/Lev11:24), [LEV 11:46–11:47](https://ref.ly/Lev11:46-Lev11:47); [DEU 14:3](https://ref.ly/Deut14:3); [HOS 9:3](https://ref.ly/Hos9:3)

### Skin diseases

**[LEV 13:45](https://ref.ly/Lev13:45)** *See also* [MAT 8:2–8:4](https://ref.ly/Matt8:2-Matt8:4) parallels [MRK 1:40–1:44](https://ref.ly/Mark1:40-Mark1:44), [LUK 5:12–5:14](https://ref.ly/Luke5:12-Luke5:14)

### Mildew

[LEV 14:36](https://ref.ly/Lev14:36)

### Contact with a sin offering

**[LEV 16:26](https://ref.ly/Lev16:26)**

Cleansing from ritual impurity
------------------------------

**[LEV 14:8](https://ref.ly/Lev14:8)** *See also* [LEV 16:26](https://ref.ly/Lev16:26); [NUM 19:17–19:21](https://ref.ly/Num19:17-Num19:21), [NUM 19:9](https://ref.ly/Num19:9), [NUM 19:17–19:21](https://ref.ly/Num19:17-Num19:21), [NUM 31:22–31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:22-Num31:23) *Fire is also a purifying agent.*

Contact with God demanded purity
--------------------------------

**[EXO 19:10](https://ref.ly/Exod19:10)** *See also* [EXO 19:14–19:15](https://ref.ly/Exod19:14-Exod19:15), [EXO 30:18–30:21](https://ref.ly/Exod30:18-Exod30:21); [HEB 10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:22)

Ritual purity is not required under the new covenant
----------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 15:11](https://ref.ly/Matt15:11)** parallel [MRK 7:15](https://ref.ly/Mark7:15) *What concerns God is moral impurity, associated with actions, not ritual impurity, associated with food, etc.* *See also* [ACT 10:12–10:15](https://ref.ly/Acts10:12-Acts10:15), [ACT 21:26](https://ref.ly/Acts21:26) *Paul took part in Jewish purification rites in accordance with his Jewish heritage*; [ROM 14:14](https://ref.ly/Rom14:14); [GAL 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11-Gal2:12) *Peter temporarily relapsed into the habit of treating Gentiles as unclean*; [GAL 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Gal2:14-Gal2:15); [1TI 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:4)

## 8326: purity, moral and spiritual (id: 8326)

Moral and spiritual purity is demanded by God of his people. God calls his people to be holy, just as he is holy.

Divine purity
-------------

### The purity of God

**[HAB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hab1:13)** *See also* [JOB 4:17](https://ref.ly/Job4:17); [PSA 18:26](https://ref.ly/Ps18:26); [1JN 1:5](https://ref.ly/1John1:5)

### The purity of Jesus Christ

**[1JN 3:3](https://ref.ly/1John3:3)** *See also* [MAT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:2); [HEB 7:26](https://ref.ly/Heb7:26)

Fellowship with God demands purity
----------------------------------

**[PSA 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Ps24:3-Ps24:4)**; **[MAT 5:8](https://ref.ly/Matt5:8)** *See also* [PSA 51:6](https://ref.ly/Ps51:6), [PSA 73:1](https://ref.ly/Ps73:1)

God's people are to live pure lives
-----------------------------------

**[COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5)** *See also* [LEV 18:30](https://ref.ly/Lev18:30); [JOB 31:1](https://ref.ly/Job31:1); [PSA 51:10](https://ref.ly/Ps51:10); [1CO 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:7-1Cor5:8) *Yeast is a symbol of sin*; [1CO 6:15–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:15-1Cor6:20); [1TH 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:3); [TIT 1:15](https://ref.ly/Titus1:15), [TIT 2:5](https://ref.ly/Titus2:5); [HEB 13:4](https://ref.ly/Heb13:4); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27), [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:8)

Purity is unobtainable by human effort
--------------------------------------

### Because of the sinful human nature

**[JOB 14:4](https://ref.ly/Job14:4)** *See also* [JOB 15:14](https://ref.ly/Job15:14), [JOB 25:4](https://ref.ly/Job25:4); [ECC 7:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:20)

### Because of the tendency to idolatry

**[HOS 8:5](https://ref.ly/Hos8:5)**

### Because of self\-deception

**[PRO 30:12](https://ref.ly/Prov30:12)** *See also* [PRO 20:9](https://ref.ly/Prov20:9)

### Because of an unwillingness to be cleansed

**[EZK 24:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:13)**

Symbols of purification from sin
--------------------------------

**[PSA 51:7](https://ref.ly/Ps51:7)** *See also* [ISA 6:6](https://ref.ly/Isa6:6) *fire*; [MRK 1:4](https://ref.ly/Mark1:4) *water baptism*; [2CO 11:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:2) *virginity*; [2PE 3:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:14) *spotlessness*

God promises to purify his people
---------------------------------

**[EZK 36:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:25)** *See also* [ZEP 3:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:9); [ZEC 13:1](https://ref.ly/Zech13:1)

Purification through Jesus Christ
---------------------------------

### Through pardon offered in his death

**[HEB 9:14](https://ref.ly/Heb9:14)** *See also* [HEB 12:24](https://ref.ly/Heb12:24); [1JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/1John1:7)

### Through his word

**[JHN 15:3](https://ref.ly/John15:3)** *See also* [JHN 13:10](https://ref.ly/John13:10)

### Through his presence in resisting temptation

**[JAS 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:7-Jas4:8)** *See also* [1PE 5:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:9)

### Through hope built on his promises

**[TIT 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Titus2:12-Titus2:13)** *See also* [1JN 3:3](https://ref.ly/1John3:3)

Purification through judgment
-----------------------------

### In the OT

**[LEV 18:28](https://ref.ly/Lev18:28)** *See also* [DEU 13:5](https://ref.ly/Deut13:5), [DEU 17:7](https://ref.ly/Deut17:7), [DEU 17:12](https://ref.ly/Deut17:12); [JDG 20:13](https://ref.ly/Judg20:13); [MAL 3:2–3:4](https://ref.ly/Mal3:2-Mal3:4)

### In the NT

**[1CO 5:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:13)**

Symbols of purity
-----------------

### Whiteness

**[DAN 7:9](https://ref.ly/Dan7:9)** *See also* [PSA 51:7](https://ref.ly/Ps51:7); [ISA 1:18](https://ref.ly/Isa1:18); [MAT 17:2](https://ref.ly/Matt17:2) parallel [MRK 9:3](https://ref.ly/Mark9:3); [MAT 28:3](https://ref.ly/Matt28:3); [MRK 16:5](https://ref.ly/Mark16:5); [JHN 20:12](https://ref.ly/John20:12); [REV 1:14](https://ref.ly/Rev1:14)

### Washing

**[PSA 26:6](https://ref.ly/Ps26:6)** *See also* [PSA 51:2](https://ref.ly/Ps51:2)

### Fire

## 8328: quietness (id: 8328)

A calm, peaceful and restrained attitude to life and way of approaching God frequently commended in Scripture even in adverse circumstances. It is also a condition experienced by God's friends and enemies when confronted by his majesty.

Quietness of life
-----------------

### A quiet manner of life commended

**[1TI 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1-1Tim2:2)** *See also* [PSA 35:20](https://ref.ly/Ps35:20), [PSA 131:1–131:2](https://ref.ly/Ps131:1-Ps131:2); [MAT 12:19](https://ref.ly/Matt12:19); [ISA 42:2](https://ref.ly/Isa42:2); [1TH 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:11); [2TH 3:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:12); [1TI 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:11); [1PE 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1-1Pet3:4)

### A quiet temperament

**[GEN 25:27](https://ref.ly/Gen25:27)**

### Quiet activity

**[MAT 1:19](https://ref.ly/Matt1:19)** *See also* [JDG 4:21](https://ref.ly/Judg4:21); [RUT 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:7); [EZK 24:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:17); [ACT 16:37](https://ref.ly/Acts16:37)

### The wisdom of being quiet

**[AMO 5:13](https://ref.ly/Amos5:13)** *See also* [GEN 34:5](https://ref.ly/Gen34:5); [JDG 18:19–18:20](https://ref.ly/Judg18:19-Judg18:20); [EST 7:4](https://ref.ly/Esth7:4); [PRO 15:18](https://ref.ly/Prov15:18); [ECC 9:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:17); [ACT 19:35–19:36](https://ref.ly/Acts19:35-Acts19:36)

Quietness in different circumstances
------------------------------------

### In peace and rest

**[PSA 23:2](https://ref.ly/Ps23:2)** *See also* [1CH 4:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:40); [PRO 17:1](https://ref.ly/Prov17:1); [ISA 32:17](https://ref.ly/Isa32:17); [MRK 6:31](https://ref.ly/Mark6:31)

### After conflict

**[1CH 22:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:9)** *See also* [JOS 11:23](https://ref.ly/Josh11:23), [JOS 14:15](https://ref.ly/Josh14:15); [JDG 3:11](https://ref.ly/Judg3:11), [JDG 5:31](https://ref.ly/Judg5:31), [JDG 8:28](https://ref.ly/Judg8:28); [2CH 14:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr14:1), [2CH 20:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:30); [ISA 14:7](https://ref.ly/Isa14:7); [JER 30:10](https://ref.ly/Jer30:10) parallel [JER 46:27](https://ref.ly/Jer46:27); [ZEC 1:11](https://ref.ly/Zech1:11)

### When in distress

[2SA 13:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:20); ; [NEH 8:9–8:11](https://ref.ly/Neh8:9-Neh8:11); ; [ISA 7:4](https://ref.ly/Isa7:4)

### Because of a sense of shame

[GEN 3:8](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8); ; [NEH 5:8](https://ref.ly/Neh5:8); ; [MRK 9:34](https://ref.ly/Mark9:34)

### When before a higher authority

[JDG 3:19](https://ref.ly/Judg3:19); ; [MRK 1:25](https://ref.ly/Mark1:25) parallel [LUK 4:35](https://ref.ly/Luke4:35)

### God's quiet voice

[1KI 19:12–19:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:12-1Kgs19:13)

Absence of quietness
--------------------

### Because of inner conflict

**[JOB 3:26](https://ref.ly/Job3:26)** *See also* [RUT 3:18](https://ref.ly/Ruth3:18); [JOB 6:24](https://ref.ly/Job6:24); [ROM 7:21–7:24](https://ref.ly/Rom7:21-Rom7:24)

### Because of sin

**[ISA 57:20–57:21](https://ref.ly/Isa57:20-Isa57:21)**

The quietness of God
--------------------

### God chooses quietness

**[ISA 18:4](https://ref.ly/Isa18:4)** *See also* [JOB 34:29](https://ref.ly/Job34:29); [ISA 42:14](https://ref.ly/Isa42:14); [EZK 16:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:42)

### God rejects quietness

**[ISA 62:1](https://ref.ly/Isa62:1)**

### God brings quietness

**[PSA 76:8](https://ref.ly/Ps76:8)** *See also* [PSA 46:10](https://ref.ly/Ps46:10), [PSA 65:7](https://ref.ly/Ps65:7), [PSA 89:9](https://ref.ly/Ps89:9), [PSA 107:29](https://ref.ly/Ps107:29); [ISA 24:8](https://ref.ly/Isa24:8), [ISA 25:5](https://ref.ly/Isa25:5); [ZEP 3:17](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:17); [MAT 8:26](https://ref.ly/Matt8:26) parallels [MRK 4:39](https://ref.ly/Mark4:39), [LUK 8:24](https://ref.ly/Luke8:24)

Quietness before God
--------------------

### Being quiet before God

**[PSA 37:7](https://ref.ly/Ps37:7)**; **[ISA 30:15](https://ref.ly/Isa30:15)** *See also* [EXO 14:14](https://ref.ly/Exod14:14); [LAM 3:26](https://ref.ly/Lam3:26); [1CO 14:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:28), [1CO 14:34–14:35](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:34-1Cor14:35); [REV 8:1](https://ref.ly/Rev8:1)

### Quietness in prayer

**[MAT 6:6](https://ref.ly/Matt6:6)** *See also* [NEH 2:4](https://ref.ly/Neh2:4); [MRK 6:46](https://ref.ly/Mark6:46) parallel [MAT 14:23](https://ref.ly/Matt14:23); [LUK 5:16](https://ref.ly/Luke5:16), [LUK 6:12](https://ref.ly/Luke6:12), [LUK 9:18](https://ref.ly/Luke9:18)

Quietness as calmness
---------------------

### In contrast with the storm

**[MAT 8:26](https://ref.ly/Matt8:26)** parallels [MRK 4:39](https://ref.ly/Mark4:39), [LUK 8:24](https://ref.ly/Luke8:24) *See also* [PSA 107:30](https://ref.ly/Ps107:30); [JON 1:11–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:11-Jonah1:15)

### In contrast with rashness

**[ECC 10:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:4)** *See also* [PRO 15:18](https://ref.ly/Prov15:18)

### In contrast with unrest

**[ISA 7:4](https://ref.ly/Isa7:4)** *See also* [NEH 8:11](https://ref.ly/Neh8:11)

### In contrast with anger

**[EZK 16:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:42)**

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Timothy 2:1-2; Psalms 35:20; Psalms 131:1-2; Matthew 12:19; Isaiah 42:2; 1 Thessalonians 4:11; 2 Thessalonians 3:12; 1 Timothy 2:11; 1 Peter 3:1-4; Genesis 25:27; Matthew 1:19; Judges 4:21; Ruth 3:7; Ezekiel 24:17; Acts 16:37; Amos 5:13; Genesis 34:5; Judges 18:19-20; Esther 7:4; Proverbs 15:18; Ecclesiastes 9:17; Acts 19:35-36; Psalms 23:2; 1 Chronicles 4:40; Proverbs 17:1; Isaiah 32:17; Mark 6:31; 1 Chronicles 22:9; Joshua 11:23; Joshua 14:15; Judges 3:11; Judges 5:31; Judges 8:28; 2 Chronicles 14:1; 2 Chronicles 20:30; Isaiah 14:7; Jeremiah 30:10; Jeremiah 46:27; Zechariah 1:11; 2 Samuel 13:20; Nehemiah 8:9-11; Isaiah 7:4; Genesis 3:8; Nehemiah 5:8; Mark 9:34; Judges 3:19; Mark 1:25; Luke 4:35; 1 Kings 19:12-13; Job 3:26; Ruth 3:18; Job 6:24; Romans 7:21-24; Isaiah 57:20-21; Isaiah 18:4; Job 34:29; Isaiah 42:14; Ezekiel 16:42; Isaiah 62:1; Psalms 76:8; Psalms 46:10; Psalms 65:7; Psalms 89:9; Psalms 107:29; Isaiah 24:8; Isaiah 25:5; Zephaniah 3:17; Matthew 8:26; Mark 4:39; Luke 8:24; Psalms 37:7; Isaiah 30:15; Exodus 14:14; Lamentations 3:26; 1 Corinthians 14:28; 1 Corinthians 14:34-35; Revelation 8:1; Matthew 6:6; Nehemiah 2:4; Mark 6:46; Matthew 14:23; Luke 5:16; Luke 6:12; Luke 9:18; Psalms 107:30; Jonah 1:11-15; Ecclesiastes 10:4; Nehemiah 8:11

## 8329: readiness (id: 8329)

A state of preparedness for future needs or happenings, whether predictable or uncertain. God through Jesus Christ prepared all that was necessary for the salvation of sinners. God's people are exhorted to be ready for warfare (physical and spiritual), death and the second coming of Jesus Christ. All humanity should be ready for future judgment.

God has prepared everything for the salvation of sinners
--------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 22:4](https://ref.ly/Matt22:4)** *A wedding banquet is often used to portray the blessings of salvation and the gathering of Jesus Christ with the redeemed in heaven.* *See also* [LUK 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/Luke14:16-Luke14:17); [1PE 1:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:5)

Exhortations to and descriptions of readiness for war
-----------------------------------------------------

### Against earthly enemies

**[JOL 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Joel3:9-Joel3:10)** *See also* [1KI 20:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:12); [1CH 12:23–12:35](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:23-1Chr12:35), [1CH 12:33–12:37](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:33-1Chr12:37); [2CH 25:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:5); [JER 51:11–51:12](https://ref.ly/Jer51:11-Jer51:12); [DAN 11:10](https://ref.ly/Dan11:10)

### Against spiritual forces of evil

**[EPH 6:11–6:18](https://ref.ly/Eph6:11-Eph6:18)**

God's people should always be ready
-----------------------------------

### To face temptation

**[1PE 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8)** *See also* [PSA 39:1](https://ref.ly/Ps39:1); [MAT 26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:41) parallels [MRK 14:38](https://ref.ly/Mark14:38), [LUK 22:46](https://ref.ly/Luke22:46); [1CO 10:12](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:12)

### To face death

**[2KI 20:1](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:1)** parallel [ISA 38:1](https://ref.ly/Isa38:1) *See also* [GEN 46:30](https://ref.ly/Gen46:30); [ACT 21:13](https://ref.ly/Acts21:13)

### To serve

**[EPH 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Eph4:11-Eph4:12)**; **[1PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13)** *See also* [NUM 8:11](https://ref.ly/Num8:11); [1SA 25:41](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:41); [JHN 9:4](https://ref.ly/John9:4); [2CO 8:19](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:19), [2CO 9:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:2); [2TI 2:21](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:21); [TIT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Titus3:1)

### To speak the truth

**[1PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15)** *See also* [PRO 22:17–22:18](https://ref.ly/Prov22:17-Prov22:18); [JER 1:17](https://ref.ly/Jer1:17); [2TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:2)

### For the second coming of Chirst

**[1JN 2:28](https://ref.ly/1John2:28)** *See also* [MAT 24:42–24:44](https://ref.ly/Matt24:42-Matt24:44), [MAT 24:45–24:51](https://ref.ly/Matt24:45-Matt24:51) parallel [LUK 12:42–12:46](https://ref.ly/Luke12:42-Luke12:46); [MAT 25:1–25:12](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:12), [MAT 25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:13); [MRK 13:33–13:37](https://ref.ly/Mark13:33-Mark13:37); [LUK 12:35–12:40](https://ref.ly/Luke12:35-Luke12:40); [1TH 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:8); [REV 3:11](https://ref.ly/Rev3:11), [REV 16:15](https://ref.ly/Rev16:15), [REV 19:7](https://ref.ly/Rev19:7)

All humanity should be ready for future judgment
------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 12:47–12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:47-Luke12:48)** *This is one of many parables that Jesus Christ taught about his second coming and the need to be ready when he returns.* *See also* [AMO 4:12](https://ref.ly/Amos4:12); [1PE 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 22:4; Luke 14:16-17; 1 Peter 1:5; Joel 3:9-10; 1 Kings 20:12; 1 Chronicles 12:23-35; 1 Chronicles 12:33-37; 2 Chronicles 25:5; Jeremiah 51:11-12; Daniel 11:10; Ephesians 6:11-18; 1 Peter 5:8; Psalms 39:1; Matthew 26:41; Mark 14:38; Luke 22:46; 1 Corinthians 10:12; 2 Kings 20:1; Isaiah 38:1; Genesis 46:30; Acts 21:13; Ephesians 4:11-12; 1 Peter 1:13; Numbers 8:11; 1 Samuel 25:41; John 9:4; 2 Corinthians 8:19; 2 Corinthians 9:2; 2 Timothy 2:21; Titus 3:1; 1 Peter 3:15; Proverbs 22:17-18; Jeremiah 1:17; 2 Timothy 4:2; 1 John 2:28; Matthew 24:42-44; Matthew 24:45-51; Luke 12:42-46; Matthew 25:1-12; Matthew 25:13; Mark 13:33-37; Luke 12:35-40; 1 Thessalonians 5:8; Revelation 3:11; Revelation 16:15; Revelation 19:7; Luke 12:47-48; Amos 4:12; 1 Peter 4:5

## 8330: receptiveness (id: 8330)

An openness to people and ideas which needs to be tempered by discernment. It is an attitude which God desires and which he shows to all who call to him.

Receptiveness as a human quality
--------------------------------

### It is characterised by an open attitude

**[MAT 13:16–13:17](https://ref.ly/Matt13:16-Matt13:17)** *See also* [PRO 18:15](https://ref.ly/Prov18:15), [PRO 20:12](https://ref.ly/Prov20:12)

### It is an aspect of love

**[JHN 19:26–19:27](https://ref.ly/John19:26-John19:27)** *See also* [RUT 4:3–4:5](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:3-Ruth4:5), [RUT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:13); [MAT 18:5](https://ref.ly/Matt18:5) parallels [MRK 9:37](https://ref.ly/Mark9:37), [LUK 9:48](https://ref.ly/Luke9:48); [MAT 25:35](https://ref.ly/Matt25:35); [ACT 2:44–2:45](https://ref.ly/Acts2:44-Acts2:45), [ACT 4:32](https://ref.ly/Acts4:32); [1CO 13:6–13:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:6-1Cor13:7); [GAL 4:14](https://ref.ly/Gal4:14)

### Thoughtful receptiveness requires discernment

**[1JN 4:1](https://ref.ly/1John4:1)** *See also* [1CO 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:14); [PHP 1:10](https://ref.ly/Phil1:10); [1TH 5:21](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:21); [HEB 5:14](https://ref.ly/Heb5:14)

### Thoughtless receptiveness is dangerous

**[2CO 11:3–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3-2Cor11:4)** *See also* [DEU 11:16](https://ref.ly/Deut11:16); [1KI 11:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4); [PRO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Prov7:21); [COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8); [HEB 13:9](https://ref.ly/Heb13:9); [2JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/2John1:10)

### Spiritual receptiveness is fruitful

**[JHN 15:5](https://ref.ly/John15:5)** *See also* [PSA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:3); [PRO 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Prov3:5-Prov3:6), [PRO 6:20–6:22](https://ref.ly/Prov6:20-Prov6:22); [JER 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/Jer17:7-Jer17:8); [JHN 20:29](https://ref.ly/John20:29)

God is receptive to human beings
--------------------------------

### To their needs

**[MAT 9:36](https://ref.ly/Matt9:36)** *See also* [2KI 14:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs14:26); [PSA 103:13–103:14](https://ref.ly/Ps103:13-Ps103:14); [ISA 63:9](https://ref.ly/Isa63:9); [MAT 6:8](https://ref.ly/Matt6:8), [MAT 15:32](https://ref.ly/Matt15:32) parallel [MRK 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/Mark8:2-Mark8:3); [LUK 7:13](https://ref.ly/Luke7:13); [1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:7)

### To their cries

**[PSA 38:9](https://ref.ly/Ps38:9)** *See also* [EXO 3:7](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7); [1KI 9:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:3) parallel [2CH 7:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:12); [PSA 6:8–6:9](https://ref.ly/Ps6:8-Ps6:9), [PSA 40:1](https://ref.ly/Ps40:1); [ISA 65:24](https://ref.ly/Isa65:24); [1PE 3:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:12)

### To their repentance

**[HOS 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Hos14:1-Hos14:4)** *See also* [2SA 12:13](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:13); [EZK 18:27–18:28](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:27-Ezek18:28); [JOL 2:13](https://ref.ly/Joel2:13); [JON 3:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:10), [JON 4:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:2); [LUK 15:7](https://ref.ly/Luke15:7), [LUK 15:10](https://ref.ly/Luke15:10), [LUK 18:13–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:13-Luke18:14)

### God's receptiveness may be withdrawn

**[ZEC 7:13](https://ref.ly/Zech7:13)** *See also* [PSA 66:18](https://ref.ly/Ps66:18); [PRO 21:13](https://ref.ly/Prov21:13); [ISA 59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:2); [JER 11:11](https://ref.ly/Jer11:11); [MAT 7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:23), [MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46) parallel [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34); [PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1); [JAS 1:7](https://ref.ly/Jas1:7)

People should be receptive to God
---------------------------------

### Spiritual receptiveness begins by receiving Jesus Christ

**[JHN 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/John1:12-John1:13)** *See also* [MAT 10:40](https://ref.ly/Matt10:40); [ACT 2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:41), [ACT 16:14](https://ref.ly/Acts16:14); [COL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Col2:6)

### Spiritual receptiveness requires openness to God's Spirit

**[1CO 2:12–2:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:12-1Cor2:16)** *See also* [ROM 8:5](https://ref.ly/Rom8:5), [ROM 8:16](https://ref.ly/Rom8:16); [1CO 7:40](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:40); [GAL 5:16](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16), [GAL 5:25](https://ref.ly/Gal5:25); [REV 2:7](https://ref.ly/Rev2:7)

### Receptiveness involves seeking and listening

**[ISA 50:4–50:5](https://ref.ly/Isa50:4-Isa50:5)** *See also* [DEU 4:29](https://ref.ly/Deut4:29); [1CH 28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:9); [PSA 119:11](https://ref.ly/Ps119:11); [PRO 8:34](https://ref.ly/Prov8:34); [MAT 7:8](https://ref.ly/Matt7:8) parallel [LUK 11:10](https://ref.ly/Luke11:10); [LUK 10:39](https://ref.ly/Luke10:39); [EPH 1:18](https://ref.ly/Eph1:18); [JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19)

### Receptiveness involves accepting and learning

**[MRK 10:15](https://ref.ly/Mark10:15)** parallel [LUK 18:17](https://ref.ly/Luke18:17) *See also* [PSA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:2); [LUK 11:1](https://ref.ly/Luke11:1); [JHN 3:27](https://ref.ly/John3:27), [JHN 6:45](https://ref.ly/John6:45); [ACT 8:30–8:31](https://ref.ly/Acts8:30-Acts8:31), [ACT 17:11](https://ref.ly/Acts17:11); [1TH 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:13); [1JN 2:27](https://ref.ly/1John2:27)

### Receptiveness requires obedience and action

**[JHN 14:23](https://ref.ly/John14:23)** *See also* [1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22); [ISA 58:6–58:7](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6-Isa58:7); [MAT 7:21](https://ref.ly/Matt7:21); [JHN 5:17](https://ref.ly/John5:17), [JHN 7:17](https://ref.ly/John7:17); [JAS 1:25](https://ref.ly/Jas1:25), [JAS 2:14](https://ref.ly/Jas2:14); [1PE 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:10)

Refusing to be receptive to God
-------------------------------

**[ZEC 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Zech7:11-Zech7:12)** *See also* [2CH 24:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:19); [JER 32:33](https://ref.ly/Jer32:33); [MAT 10:14](https://ref.ly/Matt10:14) parallels [MRK 6:11](https://ref.ly/Mark6:11), [LUK 9:5](https://ref.ly/Luke9:5); [JHN 1:11](https://ref.ly/John1:11); [ACT 7:51–7:53](https://ref.ly/Acts7:51-Acts7:53); [JAS 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Jas1:7-Jas1:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 13:16-17; Proverbs 18:15; Proverbs 20:12; John 19:26-27; Ruth 4:3-5; Ruth 4:13; Matthew 18:5; Mark 9:37; Luke 9:48; Matthew 25:35; Acts 2:44-45; Acts 4:32; 1 Corinthians 13:6-7; Galatians 4:14; 1 John 4:1; 1 Corinthians 2:14; Philippians 1:10; 1 Thessalonians 5:21; Hebrews 5:14; 2 Corinthians 11:3-4; Deuteronomy 11:16; 1 Kings 11:4; Proverbs 7:21; Colossians 2:8; Hebrews 13:9; 2 John 1:10; John 15:5; Psalms 1:3; Proverbs 3:5-6; Proverbs 6:20-22; Jeremiah 17:7-8; John 20:29; Matthew 9:36; 2 Kings 14:26; Psalms 103:13-14; Isaiah 63:9; Matthew 6:8; Matthew 15:32; Mark 8:2-3; Luke 7:13; 1 Peter 5:7; Psalms 38:9; Exodus 3:7; 1 Kings 9:3; 2 Chronicles 7:12; Psalms 6:8-9; Psalms 40:1; Isaiah 65:24; 1 Peter 3:12; Hosea 14:1-4; 2 Samuel 12:13; Ezekiel 18:27-28; Joel 2:13; Jonah 3:10; Jonah 4:2; Luke 15:7; Luke 15:10; Luke 18:13-14; Zechariah 7:13; Psalms 66:18; Proverbs 21:13; Isaiah 59:2; Jeremiah 11:11; Matthew 7:23; Matthew 27:46; Mark 15:34; Psalms 22:1; James 1:7; John 1:12-13; Matthew 10:40; Acts 2:41; Acts 16:14; Colossians 2:6; 1 Corinthians 2:12-16; Romans 8:5; Romans 8:16; 1 Corinthians 7:40; Galatians 5:16; Galatians 5:25; Revelation 2:7; Isaiah 50:4-5; Deuteronomy 4:29; 1 Chronicles 28:9; Psalms 119:11; Proverbs 8:34; Matthew 7:8; Luke 11:10; Luke 10:39; Ephesians 1:18; James 1:19; Mark 10:15; Luke 18:17; Psalms 1:2; Luke 11:1; John 3:27; John 6:45; Acts 8:30-31; Acts 17:11; 1 Thessalonians 2:13; 1 John 2:27; John 14:23; 1 Samuel 15:22; Isaiah 58:6-7; Matthew 7:21; John 5:17; John 7:17; James 1:25; James 2:14; 1 Peter 4:10; Zechariah 7:11-12; 2 Chronicles 24:19; Jeremiah 32:33; Matthew 10:14; Mark 6:11; Luke 9:5; John 1:11; Acts 7:51-53; James 1:7-8

## 8331: reliability (id: 8331)

The quality of consistency and dependability which is the basis for confidence and reliance. It is exemplified by God in his changelessness and is required of God's people in their service and witness. Scripture gives warning, too, of those things which may prove unreliable.

The reliability of God
----------------------

### God's character and nature are reliable

**[DEU 32:4](https://ref.ly/Deut32:4)** *See also* [1SA 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:2); [PSA 18:2](https://ref.ly/Ps18:2), [PSA 62:7](https://ref.ly/Ps62:7), [PSA 78:35](https://ref.ly/Ps78:35); [ISA 26:4](https://ref.ly/Isa26:4)

### God is faithful to his covenants

**[DEU 7:9](https://ref.ly/Deut7:9)** *See also* [GEN 9:15–9:16](https://ref.ly/Gen9:15-Gen9:16); [PSA 105:8–105:11](https://ref.ly/Ps105:8-Ps105:11), [PSA 111:5](https://ref.ly/Ps111:5); [ISA 54:10](https://ref.ly/Isa54:10); [EZK 16:60](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:60)

### God's love is reliable

**[PSA 89:1–89:2](https://ref.ly/Ps89:1-Ps89:2)** *See also* [EXO 20:6](https://ref.ly/Exod20:6) parallel [DEU 5:10](https://ref.ly/Deut5:10); [EXO 34:6–34:7](https://ref.ly/Exod34:6-Exod34:7); [NUM 14:18](https://ref.ly/Num14:18); [PSA 103:17–103:18](https://ref.ly/Ps103:17-Ps103:18); [LAM 3:22–3:24](https://ref.ly/Lam3:22-Lam3:24); [1JN 4:16](https://ref.ly/1John4:16)

### God's promises are reliable

**[1KI 8:56](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:56)** *See also* [JOS 21:44–21:45](https://ref.ly/Josh21:44-Josh21:45), [JOS 23:15](https://ref.ly/Josh23:15); [JER 29:10](https://ref.ly/Jer29:10); [HEB 6:13–6:18](https://ref.ly/Heb6:13-Heb6:18)

### God's presence is reliable

**[DEU 31:6](https://ref.ly/Deut31:6)** *See also* [GEN 28:15](https://ref.ly/Gen28:15); [ISA 41:10](https://ref.ly/Isa41:10); [MAT 28:20](https://ref.ly/Matt28:20); [ACT 18:10](https://ref.ly/Acts18:10)

### God's strength and protection are reliable

**[PSA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Ps46:1)**; **[1CO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:8-1Cor1:9)** *See also* [JOS 14:9](https://ref.ly/Josh14:9); [2CH 13:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:18), [2CH 16:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:8); [PSA 71:5–71:7](https://ref.ly/Ps71:5-Ps71:7); [PHP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Phil1:6); [2TH 3:3](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:3); [JUD 1:24](https://ref.ly/Jude1:24)

### God's words are reliable

**[JHN 8:26](https://ref.ly/John8:26)** *See also* [PSA 33:4](https://ref.ly/Ps33:4); [EZK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:25); [MAT 5:18](https://ref.ly/Matt5:18); [LUK 21:33](https://ref.ly/Luke21:33); [ROM 3:4](https://ref.ly/Rom3:4)

The reliability of times and seasons
------------------------------------

**[GEN 8:22](https://ref.ly/Gen8:22)** *See also* [GEN 1:14](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14); [PSA 104:19](https://ref.ly/Ps104:19); [ECC 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:4-Eccl1:5)

The reliability of God's people
-------------------------------

### Reliability in witness

**[ISA 8:2](https://ref.ly/Isa8:2)** *See also* [PRO 12:17](https://ref.ly/Prov12:17), [PRO 14:5](https://ref.ly/Prov14:5), [PRO 14:25](https://ref.ly/Prov14:25); [JHN 19:35](https://ref.ly/John19:35); [ACT 4:20](https://ref.ly/Acts4:20)

### Reliability in teaching

**[PRO 22:20–22:21](https://ref.ly/Prov22:20-Prov22:21)** *See also* [ECC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:10); [2TI 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:2)

### Reliability of character

[1TI 3:2–3:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2-1Tim3:10); ; [TIT 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Titus1:6-Titus1:9)

### Examples of reliable people

[GEN 21:23–21:24](https://ref.ly/Gen21:23-Gen21:24) *Abraham*; [NUM 12:7](https://ref.ly/Num12:7); ; [HEB 3:5](https://ref.ly/Heb3:5); [NUM 14:24](https://ref.ly/Num14:24); ; [JOS 14:9–14:12](https://ref.ly/Josh14:9-Josh14:12); [2CH 34:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:12) *men working on the temple*; ; [NEH 7:2](https://ref.ly/Neh7:2) *Hanani*; ; [EZK 44:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek44:15) *Priests*; ; [MAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6) *Levi*; ; [1CO 4:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:17) *Timothy*; ; [COL 1:7](https://ref.ly/Col1:7) *Epaphras*; ; [COL 4:7–4:9](https://ref.ly/Col4:7-Col4:9) *Tychicus and Onesimus*; ; [2TI 4:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:11) *Luke*

Warnings against unreliability
------------------------------

### Examples of unreliable people

**[PSA 78:57](https://ref.ly/Ps78:57)** *See also* [1SA 29:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam29:6) *David was trusted by Achish, but was secretly deceiving him*; [JER 2:21](https://ref.ly/Jer2:21) *the people of Israel*[EZK 13:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:9); [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2); [MAT 7:15](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15)[GAL 2:4](https://ref.ly/Gal2:4) *Judaisers*; [HOS 10:4](https://ref.ly/Hos10:4) *Israel's kings*

### Examples of things proving unreliable

**[JER 17:5](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5)** *See also* [2KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:21) parallel [ISA 36:6](https://ref.ly/Isa36:6) *false dependence upon Egypt*[PSA 33:16](https://ref.ly/Ps33:16); [HOS 10:13](https://ref.ly/Hos10:13)[ISA 30:12–30:13](https://ref.ly/Isa30:12-Isa30:13) *false dependence upon human scheming*[GAL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Gal3:18); [COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 32:4; 1 Samuel 2:2; Psalms 18:2; Psalms 62:7; Psalms 78:35; Isaiah 26:4; Deuteronomy 7:9; Genesis 9:15-16; Psalms 105:8-11; Psalms 111:5; Isaiah 54:10; Ezekiel 16:60; Psalms 89:1-2; Exodus 20:6; Deuteronomy 5:10; Exodus 34:6-7; Numbers 14:18; Psalms 103:17-18; Lamentations 3:22-24; 1 John 4:16; 1 Kings 8:56; Joshua 21:44-45; Joshua 23:15; Jeremiah 29:10; Hebrews 6:13-18; Deuteronomy 31:6; Genesis 28:15; Isaiah 41:10; Matthew 28:20; Acts 18:10; Psalms 46:1; 1 Corinthians 1:8-9; Joshua 14:9; 2 Chronicles 13:18; 2 Chronicles 16:8; Psalms 71:5-7; Philippians 1:6; 2 Thessalonians 3:3; Jude 1:24; John 8:26; Psalms 33:4; Ezekiel 12:25; Matthew 5:18; Luke 21:33; Romans 3:4; Genesis 8:22; Genesis 1:14; Psalms 104:19; Ecclesiastes 1:4-5; Isaiah 8:2; Proverbs 12:17; Proverbs 14:5; Proverbs 14:25; John 19:35; Acts 4:20; Proverbs 22:20-21; Ecclesiastes 12:10; 2 Timothy 2:2; 1 Timothy 3:2-10; Titus 1:6-9; Genesis 21:23-24; Numbers 12:7; Hebrews 3:5; Numbers 14:24; Joshua 14:9-12; 2 Chronicles 34:12; Nehemiah 7:2; Ezekiel 44:15; Malachi 2:6; 1 Corinthians 4:17; Colossians 1:7; Colossians 4:7-9; 2 Timothy 4:11; Psalms 78:57; 1 Samuel 29:6; Jeremiah 2:21; Ezekiel 13:9; Zechariah 10:2; Matthew 7:15; Galatians 2:4; Hosea 10:4; Jeremiah 17:5; 2 Kings 18:21; Isaiah 36:6; Psalms 33:16; Hosea 10:13; Isaiah 30:12-13; Galatians 3:18; Colossians 2:8

## 8332: reputation (id: 8332)

The estimation of a person in the sight of God or other people. Scripture stresses the importance of believers having a good reputation with those outside the faith.

God's defence of his own reputation
-----------------------------------

### God's actions in history to uphold his reputation

**[EXO 9:13–9:16](https://ref.ly/Exod9:13-Exod9:16)** *See also* [EZK 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:9-Ezek20:10)

### God's rebukes his people for failing to uphold his reputation

**[MAL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mal2:2)** *See also* [MAL 1:6–1:11](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6-Mal1:11)

### God's promises to uphold his reputation

**[EZK 36:22–36:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:22-Ezek36:23)** *See also* [ISA 42:8](https://ref.ly/Isa42:8)

The reputation of believers
---------------------------

### Believers must seek a good reputation with God

**[ACT 24:16](https://ref.ly/Acts24:16)** *See also* [2CO 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:9-2Cor5:10); [1TH 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:1)

### Believers must have a good reputation with outsiders

**[ACT 5:13–5:16](https://ref.ly/Acts5:13-Acts5:16)** *See also* [ACT 2:46–2:47](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46-Acts2:47), [ACT 24:16](https://ref.ly/Acts24:16); [1TI 3:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:7)

### Believers must not boast to improve their own reputation

**[2CO 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:17-2Cor10:18)** *See also* [1CO 3:5–3:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:5-1Cor3:7); [PHP 3:4–3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:9)

### Believers must not seek a good reputation through outward show

**[MAT 6:16–6:18](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16-Matt6:18)** *See also* [MAT 6:1–6:8](https://ref.ly/Matt6:1-Matt6:8), [MAT 23:2–23:12](https://ref.ly/Matt23:2-Matt23:12)

### Believers may find that serving God leads to a bad reputation with unbelievers

**[ACT 17:5–17:8](https://ref.ly/Acts17:5-Acts17:8)** *See also* [1KI 19:1–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1-1Kgs19:5); [LUK 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/Luke23:1-Luke23:2)

Good reputations
----------------

### Examples of God\-fearers with a good reputation

[LUK 7:3–7:5](https://ref.ly/Luke7:3-Luke7:5) *the centurion*; ; [ACT 10:1–10:4](https://ref.ly/Acts10:1-Acts10:4) *Cornelius*

### Examples of believers with a good reputation

[DAN 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/Dan6:3-Dan6:5) *Daniel*; ; [ACT 9:36–9:39](https://ref.ly/Acts9:36-Acts9:39) *Dorcas*; ; [ACT 16:1–16:2](https://ref.ly/Acts16:1-Acts16:2) *Timothy*; ; [ACT 22:12–22:13](https://ref.ly/Acts22:12-Acts22:13) *Ananias*; ; [ROM 1:8](https://ref.ly/Rom1:8) *the church at Rome*

### The blessing of a good reputation

**[PRO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Prov22:1)** *See also* [ECC 7:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:1)

Bad repuations
--------------

### The reputation of sinners is known to God

**[GEN 18:20–18:21](https://ref.ly/Gen18:20-Gen18:21)** *See also* [REV 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/Rev3:1-Rev3:2), [REV 18:4–18:5](https://ref.ly/Rev18:4-Rev18:5)

### Examples of reputed sinners

[1KI 16:29–16:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:29-1Kgs16:33), [1KI 21:25–21:26](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:25-1Kgs21:26); ; [2KI 21:16–21:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:16-2Kgs21:17)

Outward reputation cannot compare with a righteous heart
--------------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 16:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:7)** *See also* [1CH 29:14–29:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:14-1Chr29:19); [REV 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Rev3:1-Rev3:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 9:13-16; Ezekiel 20:9-10; Malachi 2:2; Malachi 1:6-11; Ezekiel 36:22-23; Isaiah 42:8; Acts 24:16; 2 Corinthians 5:9-10; 1 Thessalonians 4:1; Acts 5:13-16; Acts 2:46-47; 1 Timothy 3:7; 2 Corinthians 10:17-18; 1 Corinthians 3:5-7; Philippians 3:4-9; Matthew 6:16-18; Matthew 6:1-8; Matthew 23:2-12; Acts 17:5-8; 1 Kings 19:1-5; Luke 23:1-2; Luke 7:3-5; Acts 10:1-4; Daniel 6:3-5; Acts 9:36-39; Acts 16:1-2; Acts 22:12-13; Romans 1:8; Proverbs 22:1; Ecclesiastes 7:1; Genesis 18:20-21; Revelation 3:1-2; Revelation 18:4-5; 1 Kings 16:29-33; 1 Kings 21:25-26; 2 Kings 21:16-17; 1 Samuel 16:7; 1 Chronicles 29:14-19; Revelation 3:1-3
* **Associated Articles:** 5017: heart, results of renewal in (ID: `5017@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5041: name (ID: `5041@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5173: outward appearance (ID: `5173@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5857: fame (ID: `5857@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5859: favour (ID: `5859@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6121: boasting (ID: `6121@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8203: character (ID: `8203@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8276: humility (ID: `8276@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8444: honouring God (ID: `8444@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8460: pleasing God (ID: `8460@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8471: respect, for human beings (ID: `8471@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8333: reverence (id: 8333)

The proper sense of awe, respect and wonder that is inspired and demanded by an encounter with, or meditation upon, the character and activity of the living God.

* **Associated Articles:** 8334: reverence, and God's nature (ID: `8334@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8335: reverence, and blessing (ID: `8335@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8336: reverence, and obedience (ID: `8336@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8337: reverence, and social behaviour (ID: `8337@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8334: reverence, and God's nature (id: 8334)

Reverence for God results from considering his nature and all that he has done.

Believers should revere God and meditate upon his deeds
-------------------------------------------------------

**[1SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:24)** *See also* [JOS 24:14](https://ref.ly/Josh24:14); [JOB 25:2](https://ref.ly/Job25:2), [JOB 37:23–37:24](https://ref.ly/Job37:23-Job37:24); [JER 32:40–32:41](https://ref.ly/Jer32:40-Jer32:41); [MAL 2:5](https://ref.ly/Mal2:5)

Reverence is to be reserved exclusively for God
-----------------------------------------------

**[DEU 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Deut6:13-Deut6:14)**

Reverence for God's creative activity
-------------------------------------

**[DEU 10:20–10:21](https://ref.ly/Deut10:20-Deut10:21)** *See also* [1SA 12:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:18); [1CH 16:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:25); [PSA 33:8–33:9](https://ref.ly/Ps33:8-Ps33:9); [ECC 3:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:14); [JER 5:22–5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:22-Jer5:24); [HAB 3:2](https://ref.ly/Hab3:2)

Reverence for God's eternal existence
-------------------------------------

**[1TI 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:17)** *See also* [1TI 6:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:16)

Reverence for God's name
------------------------

**[MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2)** *See also* [ISA 59:19](https://ref.ly/Isa59:19); [MAL 1:14](https://ref.ly/Mal1:14); [REV 11:18](https://ref.ly/Rev11:18)

Reverence for God's holiness
----------------------------

**[ISA 8:13](https://ref.ly/Isa8:13)** *See also* [PSA 96:4](https://ref.ly/Ps96:4); [ISA 29:23](https://ref.ly/Isa29:23); [JER 10:7](https://ref.ly/Jer10:7); [REV 15:4](https://ref.ly/Rev15:4)

Reverence for God's word
------------------------

**[ISA 66:2](https://ref.ly/Isa66:2)** *See also* [DEU 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Deut4:10-Deut4:11); [EZR 9:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:4), [EZR 10:3](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:3)

Reverence for God's works
-------------------------

**[ECC 3:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:14)** *See also* [EXO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Exod14:31); [PSA 64:9](https://ref.ly/Ps64:9)

Reverence for God's mercy and compassion
----------------------------------------

[PSA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Ps5:7), [PSA 22:23](https://ref.ly/Ps22:23), [PSA 130:4](https://ref.ly/Ps130:4)

Reverence for God's intervention in healing
-------------------------------------------

[MAT 9:8](https://ref.ly/Matt9:8) parallels [MRK 2:12](https://ref.ly/Mark2:12), [LUK 5:26](https://ref.ly/Luke5:26)

Reverence for God's judgment
----------------------------

[EXO 14:31](https://ref.ly/Exod14:31); ; [AMO 3:8](https://ref.ly/Amos3:8); ; [JON 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:16); ; [REV 14:7](https://ref.ly/Rev14:7)

Reverence for God's anger
-------------------------

[DEU 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:14-Deut6:15); ; [PSA 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Ps2:11-Ps2:12), [PSA 76:7](https://ref.ly/Ps76:7)

Gentile nations will also revere and fear God
---------------------------------------------

**[PSA 67:7](https://ref.ly/Ps67:7)** *See also* [JOS 4:24](https://ref.ly/Josh4:24); [1KI 8:43](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:43) parallel [2CH 6:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:33); [2CH 20:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:29); [PSA 76:11–76:12](https://ref.ly/Ps76:11-Ps76:12), [PSA 102:15](https://ref.ly/Ps102:15); [ISA 59:19](https://ref.ly/Isa59:19); [JER 33:9](https://ref.ly/Jer33:9)

Reverent fear of God is not mere terror but includes trust
----------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:20](https://ref.ly/Exod20:20)** *See also* [GEN 28:16–28:17](https://ref.ly/Gen28:16-Gen28:17) *Jacob feels reverent awe in the presence of the God who has just promised to bless him*; [PSA 2:11](https://ref.ly/Ps2:11); [ACT 9:31](https://ref.ly/Acts9:31)

Reverent fear of God is a remedy against other fears
----------------------------------------------------

[PSA 56:4](https://ref.ly/Ps56:4); ; [LUK 12:4–12:5](https://ref.ly/Luke12:4-Luke12:5); ; [1PE 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:14-1Pet3:15)

## 8335: reverence, and blessing (id: 8335)

Reverence for God brings favour to individual believers, their families and the community to which they belong.

God's blessing is conditional upon reverence for him
----------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 128:1](https://ref.ly/Ps128:1)** *See also* [PSA 115:1](https://ref.ly/Ps115:1); [ECC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:12); [JER 32:39](https://ref.ly/Jer32:39)

The characteristics of reverence
--------------------------------

### Wisdom is based on reverence for God

**[PSA 111:10](https://ref.ly/Ps111:10)** *See also* [PRO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Prov1:7), [PRO 9:10](https://ref.ly/Prov9:10)

### Reverence is pure and eternal

[PSA 19:9](https://ref.ly/Ps19:9)

### Reverence will characterise the Messiah

[ISA 11:2–11:3](https://ref.ly/Isa11:2-Isa11:3)

God responds to those who revere him
------------------------------------

**[PSA 147:11](https://ref.ly/Ps147:11)** *See also* [PSA 25:14](https://ref.ly/Ps25:14); [MAL 3:16–3:18](https://ref.ly/Mal3:16-Mal3:18); [ACT 10:35](https://ref.ly/Acts10:35)

### Reverence brings God's special attention and compassion

[NEH 1:11](https://ref.ly/Neh1:11); ; [PSA 33:18](https://ref.ly/Ps33:18), [PSA 103:13](https://ref.ly/Ps103:13), [PSA 103:17](https://ref.ly/Ps103:17), [PSA 145:19](https://ref.ly/Ps145:19); ; [LUK 1:50](https://ref.ly/Luke1:50)

### God is good to those who revere him

[PSA 31:19](https://ref.ly/Ps31:19); ; [ISA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Isa33:6)

### Reverence brings God's protection and deliverance

[PSA 34:7](https://ref.ly/Ps34:7), [PSA 60:4](https://ref.ly/Ps60:4), [PSA 115:11](https://ref.ly/Ps115:11); ; [PRO 14:26](https://ref.ly/Prov14:26)

### God meets the needs of those who revere him

[EXO 1:21](https://ref.ly/Exod1:21); ; [PSA 34:9](https://ref.ly/Ps34:9), [PSA 111:5](https://ref.ly/Ps111:5); ; [JER 5:24–5:25](https://ref.ly/Jer5:24-Jer5:25)

Reverence for God is rewarded
-----------------------------

### God rewards the reverent with prosperity

**[DEU 6:24](https://ref.ly/Deut6:24)** *See also* [JOB 1:9](https://ref.ly/Job1:9); [PSA 25:12–25:13](https://ref.ly/Ps25:12-Ps25:13), [PSA 85:9](https://ref.ly/Ps85:9), [PSA 112:1](https://ref.ly/Ps112:1), [PSA 128:4](https://ref.ly/Ps128:4); [PRO 22:4](https://ref.ly/Prov22:4); [MAL 4:2](https://ref.ly/Mal4:2)

### Those who revere God avoid his judgment

[DEU 28:58–28:67](https://ref.ly/Deut28:58-Deut28:67)

### God rewards reverence with long life and future hope

[PRO 10:27](https://ref.ly/Prov10:27), [PRO 14:27](https://ref.ly/Prov14:27), [PRO 19:23](https://ref.ly/Prov19:23), [PRO 23:17–23:18](https://ref.ly/Prov23:17-Prov23:18); ; [ECC 8:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:13)

God's blessings on those who revere him are a witness to the nations
--------------------------------------------------------------------

[JER 33:9](https://ref.ly/Jer33:9)

Reverence ensures success in leadership
---------------------------------------

[2SA 23:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:3) *David*; ; [2CH 26:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:5) *Uzziah*; ; [ACT 9:31](https://ref.ly/Acts9:31)

## 8336: reverence, and obedience (id: 8336)

Reverence towards God expresses itself in the actions, as well as the words and worship of believers. True reverence for God leads to obedience towards him.

Reverent fear is expressed in worship
-------------------------------------

**[JON 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:16)**

### Reverence in worship is expressed in bodily attitudes

Bowing: [EXO 12:27](https://ref.ly/Exod12:27); ; [PSA 95:6](https://ref.ly/Ps95:6); [GEN 17:3](https://ref.ly/Gen17:3); ; [NUM 14:5](https://ref.ly/Num14:5); ; [DAN 10:15](https://ref.ly/Dan10:15); ; [REV 5:14](https://ref.ly/Rev5:14); [EXO 3:5](https://ref.ly/Exod3:5) *removing shoes*; ; [EXO 3:6](https://ref.ly/Exod3:6) *covering the face*; [EZR 9:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:5); ; [DAN 6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10); ; [EPH 3:14](https://ref.ly/Eph3:14)

Reverence can be learnt through religious observances
-----------------------------------------------------

### A shared meal in God's presence

[DEU 14:23](https://ref.ly/Deut14:23)

### Hearing God's word

[DEU 4:9–4:14](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9-Deut4:14), [DEU 31:12–31:13](https://ref.ly/Deut31:12-Deut31:13)

### Reading God's word

[DEU 17:18–17:19](https://ref.ly/Deut17:18-Deut17:19)

Reverence motivates people to avoid doing wrong
-----------------------------------------------

**[PRO 16:6](https://ref.ly/Prov16:6)** *See also* [EXO 20:20](https://ref.ly/Exod20:20); [2CH 19:9–19:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:9-2Chr19:10); [JOB 1:1](https://ref.ly/Job1:1), [JOB 28:28](https://ref.ly/Job28:28); [PRO 3:7](https://ref.ly/Prov3:7), [PRO 8:13](https://ref.ly/Prov8:13), [PRO 14:16](https://ref.ly/Prov14:16); [2CO 7:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:1)

Reverence leads people to obey God's law
----------------------------------------

**[ECC 12:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:13)** *See also* [DEU 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Deut10:12-Deut10:13), [DEU 13:4](https://ref.ly/Deut13:4); [NEH 5:14–5:18](https://ref.ly/Neh5:14-Neh5:18) *generous treatment of dependants*; [2CO 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:11) *evangelism*; [EPH 5:21](https://ref.ly/Eph5:21) *mutual submission*; [COL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Col3:22) *obedience to masters*

Reverence is characterised by fearing God
-----------------------------------------

[ECC 8:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:12); ; [ACT 2:5](https://ref.ly/Acts2:5)

### Gentile believers were called \`\`God\-fearing''

[ACT 10:2](https://ref.ly/Acts10:2), [ACT 13:26](https://ref.ly/Acts13:26), [ACT 13:50](https://ref.ly/Acts13:50), [ACT 17:4](https://ref.ly/Acts17:4), [ACT 17:17](https://ref.ly/Acts17:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Jonah 1:16; Exodus 12:27; Psalms 95:6; Genesis 17:3; Numbers 14:5; Daniel 10:15; Revelation 5:14; Exodus 3:5; Exodus 3:6; Ezra 9:5; Daniel 6:10; Ephesians 3:14; Deuteronomy 14:23; Deuteronomy 4:9-14; Deuteronomy 31:12-13; Deuteronomy 17:18-19; Proverbs 16:6; Exodus 20:20; 2 Chronicles 19:9-10; Job 1:1; Job 28:28; Proverbs 3:7; Proverbs 8:13; Proverbs 14:16; 2 Corinthians 7:1; Ecclesiastes 12:13; Deuteronomy 10:12-13; Deuteronomy 13:4; Nehemiah 5:14-18; 2 Corinthians 5:11; Ephesians 5:21; Colossians 3:22; Ecclesiastes 8:12; Acts 2:5; Acts 10:2; Acts 13:26; Acts 13:50; Acts 17:4; Acts 17:17

## 8337: reverence, and social behaviour (id: 8337)

Reverence for God leads to proper respect for others and right relationships among the people of God.

Reverence for God affects believers' attitudes
----------------------------------------------

### Reverence produces respect for others

**[EPH 5:21](https://ref.ly/Eph5:21)** *See also* [1PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:17)

### Reverence affects social attitudes

[LEV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Lev19:14), [LEV 25:17](https://ref.ly/Lev25:17); ; [EPH 6:5](https://ref.ly/Eph6:5); ; [LEV 25:36](https://ref.ly/Lev25:36), [LEV 25:43](https://ref.ly/Lev25:43)

### Attitudes to work are affected by reverence to God

[EXO 1:17](https://ref.ly/Exod1:17) *a conflict of duty*; ; [EXO 18:21](https://ref.ly/Exod18:21) *appointments in administration*; ; [2CH 19:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:7) *justice*; ; [2CH 19:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:9)

Reverence for God affects believers' behaviour
----------------------------------------------

[LEV 19:14](https://ref.ly/Lev19:14), [LEV 19:32](https://ref.ly/Lev19:32), [LEV 25:14–25:17](https://ref.ly/Lev25:14-Lev25:17), [LEV 25:35–25:36](https://ref.ly/Lev25:35-Lev25:36); ; [PSA 15:4](https://ref.ly/Ps15:4); ; [PRO 31:30](https://ref.ly/Prov31:30) *Reverence for God should be respected*; ; [COL 3:22](https://ref.ly/Col3:22) *Reverence for God inspires a slave's respect for his master.*; [TIT 2:2–2:3](https://ref.ly/Titus2:2-Titus2:3); ; [1PE 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1-1Pet3:2)

Reverence for God may reduce hostility
--------------------------------------

[2CH 17:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:10)

Lack of reverence has an adverse effect on behaviour
----------------------------------------------------

[DEU 25:18](https://ref.ly/Deut25:18); ; [NEH 5:7–5:9](https://ref.ly/Neh5:7-Neh5:9); ; [PSA 36:1](https://ref.ly/Ps36:1)

Reverence can be shown to fellow human beings as God's representatives
----------------------------------------------------------------------

[JOS 4:14](https://ref.ly/Josh4:14); ; [ACT 10:25](https://ref.ly/Acts10:25)

## 8339: self-control (id: 8339)

Physical and emotional self\-mastery, particularly in situations of intense provocation or temptation.

Self\-control is the mark of a wise person
------------------------------------------

**[PRO 29:11](https://ref.ly/Prov29:11)** *See also* [PRO 1:1–1:5](https://ref.ly/Prov1:1-Prov1:5)

Self\-control is an aspect of Christian character
-------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Gal5:22-Gal5:23)**; **[TIT 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11-Titus2:12)** *See also* [1TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:2); [TIT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Titus1:8), [TIT 2:2](https://ref.ly/Titus2:2), [TIT 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Titus2:5-Titus2:6); [2PE 1:5–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:5-2Pet1:9)

Self\-control affects the whole person
--------------------------------------

### Physical self\-control

**[1CO 9:26–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:26-1Cor9:27)** *See also* [1CO 7:36–7:38](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:36-1Cor7:38); [1TH 4:3–4:7](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:3-1Thess4:7)

### A mental discipline

**[1PE 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:13)** *See also* [1TH 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:6-1Thess5:8); [1PE 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:7), [1PE 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8)

### Controlled speech

**[PSA 141:3](https://ref.ly/Ps141:3)**; **[JAS 1:19](https://ref.ly/Jas1:19)** *See also* [PSA 17:3](https://ref.ly/Ps17:3); [PRO 16:23](https://ref.ly/Prov16:23), [PRO 21:23](https://ref.ly/Prov21:23); [ECC 5:2](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:2); [JAS 3:1–3:12](https://ref.ly/Jas3:1-Jas3:12)

Self\-control in response to persecution
----------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:39–5:40](https://ref.ly/Matt5:39-Matt5:40)** parallel [LUK 6:27–6:29](https://ref.ly/Luke6:27-Luke6:29) *See also* [1PE 2:18–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:18-1Pet2:23)

Examples of self\-control
-------------------------

[GEN 39:7–39:12](https://ref.ly/Gen39:7-Gen39:12) *Joseph*; [1SA 24:1–24:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:1-1Sam24:7), [1SA 26:7–26:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:7-1Sam26:12); ; [2SA 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:9-2Sam16:10); [JOB 31:1](https://ref.ly/Job31:1), [JOB 31:30](https://ref.ly/Job31:30); [ISA 53:7](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7) *the servant, understood in the NT as a prophecy concerning Jesus Christ*; ; [MRK 14:61](https://ref.ly/Mark14:61); ; [MAT 27:27–27:30](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27-Matt27:30); [1CO 4:12–4:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:12-1Cor4:13), [1CO 9:24–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:24-1Cor9:27)

The dangers of a loss of self\-control
--------------------------------------

**[PRO 18:7](https://ref.ly/Prov18:7)**; **[JER 14:10](https://ref.ly/Jer14:10)** *See also* [1SA 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:10-1Sam18:11) *Saul*; [2SA 13:7–13:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:7-2Sam13:14) *Amnon*; [PSA 106:32–106:33](https://ref.ly/Ps106:32-Ps106:33) *Moses*; [PRO 6:1–6:3](https://ref.ly/Prov6:1-Prov6:3), [PRO 29:18](https://ref.ly/Prov29:18); [ACT 24:25](https://ref.ly/Acts24:25); [ROM 1:24–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:24-Rom1:31); [1CO 7:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:5) *aimed at those who thought that abstaining from sexual relations in marriage was spiritual*; [1CO 7:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:9); [COL 2:232](https://ref.ly/Col2:232)PE [2:12–2:14](https://ref.ly/Col2:12-Col2:14), [2PE 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:18-2Pet2:19); [JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)

## 8340: self-respect (id: 8340)

A sense of personal worth and dignity. Scripture points to the need for believers to ground their self\-respect in God himself, rather than trusting in the opinion and valuation of other individuals or society as a whole.

Self\-respect is often assumed
------------------------------

**[MAT 22:39](https://ref.ly/Matt22:39)** parallel [MRK 12:31](https://ref.ly/Mark12:31) *See also* [MAT 7:12](https://ref.ly/Matt7:12), [MAT 19:19](https://ref.ly/Matt19:19); [ROM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Rom12:3); [EPH 5:29](https://ref.ly/Eph5:29)

Examples of those who had self\-respect
---------------------------------------

**[JOB 12:3](https://ref.ly/Job12:3)** *See also* [JOB 10:16](https://ref.ly/Job10:16); [PRO 31:25](https://ref.ly/Prov31:25); [PHP 3:4](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4)

The basis of self\-respect
--------------------------

### A relationship with God

**[PSA 139:13–139:14](https://ref.ly/Ps139:13-Ps139:14)** *See also* [PSA 30:6](https://ref.ly/Ps30:6); [1CO 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:16-1Cor3:17), [1CO 6:12–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:12-1Cor6:20); [2CO 6:16](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:16); [EPH 2:22](https://ref.ly/Eph2:22); [1TI 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:4)

### Equality in God's community

**[JAS 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Jas1:9-Jas1:10)** *See also* [1CO 12:22–12:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:22-1Cor12:25)

### A clear conscience and faithfulness to God

**[2CO 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:12)** *See also* [JHN 7:18](https://ref.ly/John7:18); [GAL 6:4](https://ref.ly/Gal6:4); [HEB 13:18](https://ref.ly/Heb13:18)

God's strength is a source of self\-respect
-------------------------------------------

**[DAN 10:19](https://ref.ly/Dan10:19)** *See also* [EXO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Exod15:2); [2SA 22:33](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:33); [PSA 71:5](https://ref.ly/Ps71:5), [PSA 89:17](https://ref.ly/Ps89:17), [PSA 118:14](https://ref.ly/Ps118:14); [2TI 4:17](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:17)

Self\-respect results from disciplined living
---------------------------------------------

**[JOB 4:6](https://ref.ly/Job4:6)** *See also* [PRO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Prov20:3); [1TH 4:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:4); [TIT 2:2](https://ref.ly/Titus2:2)

Self\-respect can be lost through humiliation
---------------------------------------------

**[JER 31:19](https://ref.ly/Jer31:19)** *See also* [2SA 10:1–10:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:1-2Sam10:5) parallel [1CH 19:1–19:5](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:1-1Chr19:5); [2SA 13:1–13:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:1-2Sam13:22); [DAN 4:33](https://ref.ly/Dan4:33)

Self\-respect can rest on wrong foundations
-------------------------------------------

**[GAL 6:3](https://ref.ly/Gal6:3)** *See also* [DEU 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:17-Deut8:18); [JOB 20:6–20:7](https://ref.ly/Job20:6-Job20:7); [PHP 3:4–3:7](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:7)

Ways in which self\-respect can be undermined
---------------------------------------------

### By others

[JOB 30:1–30:15](https://ref.ly/Job30:1-Job30:15)

### By the effects of sin and the work of Satan

[MRK 5:2–5:5](https://ref.ly/Mark5:2-Mark5:5) parallel [LUK 8:27–8:29](https://ref.ly/Luke8:27-Luke8:29); ; [MRK 5:25–5:28](https://ref.ly/Mark5:25-Mark5:28) parallels [MAT 9:20–9:22](https://ref.ly/Matt9:20-Matt9:22), [LUK 8:43–8:48](https://ref.ly/Luke8:43-Luke8:48); ; [LUK 15:13–15:16](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13-Luke15:16), [LUK 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/Luke17:12-Luke17:13)

### By unwise spiritual zeal
