## 8627: worship, elements of (id: 8627)

Praise and thankfulness are important elements of worship, which also includes confession of sin, the reading of Scripture and music.

Worship with awe
----------------

**[DEU 10:12](https://ref.ly/Deut10:12)** *See also* [LEV 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Lev10:1-Lev10:3); [2CH 7:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:3); [PSA 2:11](https://ref.ly/Ps2:11), [PSA 68:35](https://ref.ly/Ps68:35), [PSA 96:9](https://ref.ly/Ps96:9); [ECC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1)

Worship includes trust
----------------------

**[PSA 4:5](https://ref.ly/Ps4:5)** *See also* [PSA 37:7](https://ref.ly/Ps37:7); [HEB 11:6](https://ref.ly/Heb11:6)

Worship includes praise
-----------------------

**[PSA 22:22](https://ref.ly/Ps22:22), [PSA 107:32](https://ref.ly/Ps107:32)**; **[HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)** *See also* [2CH 31:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr31:2); [NEH 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Neh9:5-Neh9:6); [PSA 150:1–150:6](https://ref.ly/Ps150:1-Ps150:6); [HEB 2:12](https://ref.ly/Heb2:12); [REV 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Rev7:11-Rev7:12)

Worship includes thanksgiving
-----------------------------

**[PSA 100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:4)**; **[REV 11:16–11:17](https://ref.ly/Rev11:16-Rev11:17)** *See also* [2CH 7:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:3); [PSA 50:14](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14), [PSA 50:23](https://ref.ly/Ps50:23); [EPH 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19-Eph5:20); [PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6); [REV 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Rev7:11-Rev7:12)

Worship with joy
----------------

**[PSA 95:1](https://ref.ly/Ps95:1)** *See also* [PSA 27:6](https://ref.ly/Ps27:6), [PSA 43:4](https://ref.ly/Ps43:4), [PSA 100:2](https://ref.ly/Ps100:2); [LUK 24:52–24:53](https://ref.ly/Luke24:52-Luke24:53); [ACT 2:46–2:47](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46-Acts2:47)

Worship includes the confession of Jesus Christ as Lord
-------------------------------------------------------

**[HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)**

Worship includes confession of sin
----------------------------------

**[HOS 14:2](https://ref.ly/Hos14:2)** *See also* [LEV 16:21](https://ref.ly/Lev16:21); [NEH 9:2](https://ref.ly/Neh9:2); [PSA 66:18](https://ref.ly/Ps66:18)

Worship includes the reading of God's word
------------------------------------------

**[COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)**; **[1TI 4:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:13)** *See also* [NEH 8:5–8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:5-Neh8:6), [NEH 9:3](https://ref.ly/Neh9:3)

Worship includes music and song
-------------------------------

**[PSA 95:2–95:3](https://ref.ly/Ps95:2-Ps95:3)** *See also* [2SA 6:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:5); [PSA 100:2](https://ref.ly/Ps100:2); [EPH 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19-Eph5:20)

Worship includes dance
----------------------

**[PSA 149:3](https://ref.ly/Ps149:3)** *See also* [EXO 15:20](https://ref.ly/Exod15:20); [PSA 30:11](https://ref.ly/Ps30:11)

## 8628: worship, hindrances to (id: 8628)

True worship goes beyond mere form and can therefore be hindered by a wrong relationship to God or to others.

Worship that is merely formal is unacceptable
---------------------------------------------

**[1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22)** *See also* [ISA 1:13](https://ref.ly/Isa1:13); [EZK 33:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:31); [HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6); [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5); [2TI 3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:5)

Worship is hindered by wrong relationships
------------------------------------------

### To God

**[PSA 66:18](https://ref.ly/Ps66:18)**; **[MAT 15:7–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:7-Matt15:9)** parallel [MRK 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6-Mark7:7) *See also* [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13); [PSA 32:5–32:6](https://ref.ly/Ps32:5-Ps32:6); [ISA 59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:2), [ISA 64:7](https://ref.ly/Isa64:7); [JAS 4:3](https://ref.ly/Jas4:3)

### To others

**[ISA 1:11–1:17](https://ref.ly/Isa1:11-Isa1:17)**; **[MAT 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23-Matt5:24)** *See also* [AMO 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:24)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Samuel 15:22; Isaiah 1:13; Ezekiel 33:31; Hosea 6:6; Matthew 6:5; 2 Timothy 3:5; Psalms 66:18; Matthew 15:7-9; Mark 7:6-7; Isaiah 29:13; Psalms 32:5-6; Isaiah 59:2; Isaiah 64:7; James 4:3; Isaiah 1:11-17; Matthew 5:23-24; Amos 5:21-24
* **Associated Articles:** 5908: motives (ID: `5908@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8142: religion (ID: `8142@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8834: unbelief (ID: `8834@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8629: worship, times for (id: 8629)

Scripture stresses the importance of regular worship, while at the same time recognising that believers may worship God spontaneously.

Examples of regular worship
---------------------------

### On a daily basis

**[ACT 2:46–2:47](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46-Acts2:47)** *See also* [EXO 29:38–29:43](https://ref.ly/Exod29:38-Exod29:43); [PSA 141:2](https://ref.ly/Ps141:2); [EZK 46:13–46:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:13-Ezek46:15)

### Several times a day

**[DAN 6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10)** *See also* [1CH 16:37](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:37); [PSA 119:164](https://ref.ly/Ps119:164); [ACT 3:1](https://ref.ly/Acts3:1); [HEB 10:25](https://ref.ly/Heb10:25)

### On holy days

**[2CH 8:12–8:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr8:12-2Chr8:13)** *See also* [EZK 46:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:3)

### At the three annual pilgrim festivals

**[DEU 16:16](https://ref.ly/Deut16:16)** [EXO 12:1–12:20](https://ref.ly/Exod12:1-Exod12:20); ; [LUK 2:41](https://ref.ly/Luke2:41); [EXO 34:22](https://ref.ly/Exod34:22); ; [ACT 2:1](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1); [EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16); ; [LEV 23:33–23:36](https://ref.ly/Lev23:33-Lev23:36); ; [NUM 29:12–29:39](https://ref.ly/Num29:12-Num29:39); ; [DEU 16:13–16:15](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13-Deut16:15)

### On the Day of Atonement

**[LEV 16:34](https://ref.ly/Lev16:34)** *See also* [EXO 30:10](https://ref.ly/Exod30:10); [LEV 16:3–16:33](https://ref.ly/Lev16:3-Lev16:33), [LEV 23:26–23:32](https://ref.ly/Lev23:26-Lev23:32)

### On Sabbath days

**[LUK 4:16](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16)** *See also* [LEV 24:5–24:8](https://ref.ly/Lev24:5-Lev24:8); [NUM 28:9–28:10](https://ref.ly/Num28:9-Num28:10)

Examples of spontaneous worship
-------------------------------

### In response to an awareness of the closeness of God

[EXO 34:8](https://ref.ly/Exod34:8); ; [JDG 7:15](https://ref.ly/Judg7:15)

### In response to the experience of God's mercy

**[EXO 4:29–4:31](https://ref.ly/Exod4:29-Exod4:31)** *See also* [1SA 1:19–1:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:19-1Sam1:28)

### In response to the presence and power of Jesus Christ

**[MAT 28:8–28:9](https://ref.ly/Matt28:8-Matt28:9)** *See also* [MAT 14:33](https://ref.ly/Matt14:33); [LUK 24:52](https://ref.ly/Luke24:52)

Worship should not be dependent on circumstances
------------------------------------------------

**[PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6)** *See also* [JOB 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:20-Job1:21); [DAN 6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10); [ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25); [1TH 5:16–5:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:16-1Thess5:18)

The continuous worship of God in heaven
---------------------------------------

[REV 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:10-Rev4:11), [REV 5:14](https://ref.ly/Rev5:14), [REV 7:11](https://ref.ly/Rev7:11), [REV 11:1](https://ref.ly/Rev11:1), [REV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Rev19:4)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 2:46-47; Exodus 29:38-43; Psalms 141:2; Ezekiel 46:13-15; Daniel 6:10; 1 Chronicles 16:37; Psalms 119:164; Acts 3:1; Hebrews 10:25; 2 Chronicles 8:12-13; Ezekiel 46:3; Deuteronomy 16:16; Exodus 12:1-20; Luke 2:41; Exodus 34:22; Acts 2:1; Exodus 23:16; Leviticus 23:33-36; Numbers 29:12-39; Deuteronomy 16:13-15; Leviticus 16:34; Exodus 30:10; Leviticus 16:3-33; Leviticus 23:26-32; Luke 4:16; Leviticus 24:5-8; Numbers 28:9-10; Exodus 34:8; Judges 7:15; Exodus 4:29-31; 1 Samuel 1:19-28; Matthew 28:8-9; Matthew 14:33; Luke 24:52; Philippians 4:6; Job 1:20-21; Acts 16:25; 1 Thessalonians 5:16-18; Revelation 4:10-11; Revelation 5:14; Revelation 7:11; Revelation 11:1; Revelation 19:4
* **Associated Articles:** 2066: Jesus Christ, power of (ID: `2066@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6686: mercy (ID: `6686@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7308: Atonement, Day of (ID: `7308@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7354: feasts and festivals (ID: `7354@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7428: Sabbath (ID: `7428@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8642: celebration (ID: `8642@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8630: worship, results of (id: 8630)

Worship not only gives God what is due to him but also results in many benefits for his people.

True worship brings benefits for God's people
---------------------------------------------

### Blessing

**[EXO 23:25–23:26](https://ref.ly/Exod23:25-Exod23:26)** *See also* [DEU 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/Deut11:13-Deut11:15)

### Guidance

**[ACT 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/Acts13:2-Acts13:3)** *See also* [ISA 58:6–58:11](https://ref.ly/Isa58:6-Isa58:11); [NUM 7:89](https://ref.ly/Num7:89)

### Deliverance

**[ACT 16:25–16:26](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25-Acts16:26)** *See also* [PSA 50:14–50:15](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14-Ps50:15)

### Joy

**[1CH 29:21–29:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:21-1Chr29:22)** *See also* [2CH 29:30](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:30); [PSA 43:4](https://ref.ly/Ps43:4); [ISA 56:7](https://ref.ly/Isa56:7); [LUK 24:52](https://ref.ly/Luke24:52)

### A sense of God's presence

**[2CH 5:13–5:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:13-2Chr5:14)** parallel [1KI 8:10–8:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:10-1Kgs8:11) *See also* [EXO 40:35](https://ref.ly/Exod40:35)

### A deeper sense of Jesus Christ's lordship

**[PHP 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:9-Phil2:11)** *See also* [REV 1:10–1:18](https://ref.ly/Rev1:10-Rev1:18)

Boldness to witness
-------------------

**[ACT 4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:31)** *See also* [PSA 57:9](https://ref.ly/Ps57:9); [ACT 18:9–18:10](https://ref.ly/Acts18:9-Acts18:10)

True worship convicts sinners
-----------------------------

**[1CO 14:24–14:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:24-1Cor14:25)**

## 8632: adoration (id: 8632)

An attitude of worship characterised by love and reverence towards God. It can also describe the love between man and woman.

Adoration of God by his people
------------------------------

**[DEU 10:12](https://ref.ly/Deut10:12)**

### Adoration and love for God

**[PSA 31:23](https://ref.ly/Ps31:23), [PSA 116:1](https://ref.ly/Ps116:1)** *See also* [DEU 6:5](https://ref.ly/Deut6:5), [DEU 11:1](https://ref.ly/Deut11:1); [JOS 22:5](https://ref.ly/Josh22:5); [PSA 18:1](https://ref.ly/Ps18:1), [PSA 26:8](https://ref.ly/Ps26:8)

### Adoration and reverence for God

**[PSA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Ps5:7)** *See also* [EXO 3:5](https://ref.ly/Exod3:5); [DEU 13:4](https://ref.ly/Deut13:4); [JOS 5:15](https://ref.ly/Josh5:15); [1CH 16:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:29); [PSA 29:2](https://ref.ly/Ps29:2), [PSA 33:8](https://ref.ly/Ps33:8), [PSA 95:6](https://ref.ly/Ps95:6), [PSA 99:5](https://ref.ly/Ps99:5); [DAN 6:26](https://ref.ly/Dan6:26); [JON 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:16); [HAB 2:20](https://ref.ly/Hab2:20)

Examples of adoration in worship
--------------------------------

Moses and the Israelites: [EXO 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1-Exod15:2), [EXO 15:6–15:7](https://ref.ly/Exod15:6-Exod15:7), [EXO 15:11–15:12](https://ref.ly/Exod15:11-Exod15:12); [1SA 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:1-1Sam2:2) *Hannah*; ; [2SA 22:47–22:51](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:47-2Sam22:51) *David*; ; [1KI 8:23–8:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:23-1Kgs8:24) *Solomon*; [PSA 27:4](https://ref.ly/Ps27:4), [PSA 63:2–63:5](https://ref.ly/Ps63:2-Ps63:5), [PSA 73:25–73:26](https://ref.ly/Ps73:25-Ps73:26), [PSA 84:1–84:2](https://ref.ly/Ps84:1-Ps84:2), [PSA 103:1–103:5](https://ref.ly/Ps103:1-Ps103:5), [PSA 103:20–103:22](https://ref.ly/Ps103:20-Ps103:22); [LUK 1:46–1:49](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46-Luke1:49) *Mary*; [REV 4:8–4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:8-Rev4:11), [REV 5:8–5:14](https://ref.ly/Rev5:8-Rev5:14), [REV 7:9–7:12](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9-Rev7:12), [REV 15:3–15:4](https://ref.ly/Rev15:3-Rev15:4), [REV 19:6–19:8](https://ref.ly/Rev19:6-Rev19:8)

Adoration also describes the love between man and woman
-------------------------------------------------------

[PRO 5:18–5:19](https://ref.ly/Prov5:18-Prov5:19); ; [SNG 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Song1:2-Song1:4)

## 8634: amen (id: 8634)

A Hebrew term expressing the ideas of truth and reliability, often used in prayer and adoration.

Amen as an expression of worship
--------------------------------

**[1CH 16:36](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:36)**; **[EPH 3:21](https://ref.ly/Eph3:21)**; **[REV 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Rev7:11-Rev7:12)** *See also* [NEH 5:13](https://ref.ly/Neh5:13), [NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6) *The repetition of “Amen” here conveys Ezra's intensity of feeling as he read the Book of the Law. The response of the people represents a solemn, formal acceptance of God's commandments*; [PSA 72:19](https://ref.ly/Ps72:19), [PSA 106:48](https://ref.ly/Ps106:48); [ROM 9:5](https://ref.ly/Rom9:5) *“Amen” can mean either “Yes indeed, it is so” or “So be it”*; [ROM 11:36](https://ref.ly/Rom11:36), [ROM 16:25–16:27](https://ref.ly/Rom16:25-Rom16:27); [1CO 14:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:16); [GAL 1:3–1:5](https://ref.ly/Gal1:3-Gal1:5); [PHP 4:20](https://ref.ly/Phil4:20); [1TI 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:17), [1TI 6:15–6:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15-1Tim6:16); [2TI 4:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:18); [HEB 13:20–13:21](https://ref.ly/Heb13:20-Heb13:21); [1PE 4:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:11), [1PE 5:10–5:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:10-1Pet5:11); [2PE 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17-2Pet3:18); [JUD 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/Jude1:24-Jude1:25); [REV 1:4–1:7](https://ref.ly/Rev1:4-Rev1:7), [REV 19:4](https://ref.ly/Rev19:4) *the worship of heaven*

Amen confirms a grace or blessing
---------------------------------

**[GAL 6:18](https://ref.ly/Gal6:18)** *See also* [ROM 15:33](https://ref.ly/Rom15:33); [1CO 16:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:24); [PHP 4:23](https://ref.ly/Phil4:23); [REV 22:20–22:21](https://ref.ly/Rev22:20-Rev22:21)

Amen confirms a vow or action
-----------------------------

**[JER 28:5–28:6](https://ref.ly/Jer28:5-Jer28:6)** *See also* [NUM 5:20–5:22](https://ref.ly/Num5:20-Num5:22); [DEU 27:15](https://ref.ly/Deut27:15); [1KI 1:32–1:36](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:32-1Kgs1:36); [JER 11:1–11:5](https://ref.ly/Jer11:1-Jer11:5)

Amen as a title of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------

**[REV 3:14](https://ref.ly/Rev3:14)** *See also* [2CO 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:18-2Cor1:20) *The implication is that all God's promises find their fulfilment in Jesus Christ.*

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 16:36; Ephesians 3:21; Revelation 7:11-12; Nehemiah 5:13; Nehemiah 8:6; Psalms 72:19; Psalms 106:48; Romans 9:5; Romans 11:36; Romans 16:25-27; 1 Corinthians 14:16; Galatians 1:3-5; Philippians 4:20; 1 Timothy 1:17; 1 Timothy 6:15-16; 2 Timothy 4:18; Hebrews 13:20-21; 1 Peter 4:11; 1 Peter 5:10-11; 2 Peter 3:17-18; Jude 1:24-25; Revelation 1:4-7; Revelation 19:4; Galatians 6:18; Romans 15:33; 1 Corinthians 16:24; Philippians 4:23; Revelation 22:20-21; Jeremiah 28:5-6; Numbers 5:20-22; Deuteronomy 27:15; 1 Kings 1:32-36; Jeremiah 11:1-5; Revelation 3:14; 2 Corinthians 1:18-20

## 8636: asking (id: 8636)

The act of making requests of another, as an expression of dependence. Scripture encourages believers to ask things of God in prayer, and gives guidance concerning the motivation in doing so.

Asking as an aspect of human relationships
------------------------------------------

### Examples of everyday requests

**[JOS 4:20–4:22](https://ref.ly/Josh4:20-Josh4:22)**; **[ECC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:10)**; **[1PE 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:15)** *See also* [EXO 3:22](https://ref.ly/Exod3:22); [DEU 32:7](https://ref.ly/Deut32:7); [1SA 12:19](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:19) *The people asked for a king*; [2KI 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:3); [NEH 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Neh2:1-Neh2:2); [DAN 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Dan2:10-Dan2:11); [MAT 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Matt2:1-Matt2:2) *the Magi seek the king of the Jews*; [LUK 2:46](https://ref.ly/Luke2:46) *Jesus Christ asks questions in the temple*; [JHN 4:9](https://ref.ly/John4:9); [ROM 16:1–16:2](https://ref.ly/Rom16:1-Rom16:2); [GAL 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:9-Gal2:10); [PHM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Phlm1:21)

### Examples of stronger appeals

**[PHP 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/Phil4:2-Phil4:3)** *See also* [GEN 50:17](https://ref.ly/Gen50:17); [EXO 5:15](https://ref.ly/Exod5:15); [LUK 23:20](https://ref.ly/Luke23:20); [ACT 25:3](https://ref.ly/Acts25:3); [1TH 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:1)

Asking as an aspect of prayer
-----------------------------

### General petitions

**[MAT 7:7–7:11](https://ref.ly/Matt7:7-Matt7:11)** parallel [LUK 11:9–11:13](https://ref.ly/Luke11:9-Luke11:13)**[PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6)** *See also* [DEU 18:16](https://ref.ly/Deut18:16); [JDG 6:39](https://ref.ly/Judg6:39); [1SA 1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:20); [1CH 16:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:4); [2CH 6:32–6:33](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:32-2Chr6:33); [JOB 6:8–6:9](https://ref.ly/Job6:8-Job6:9); [PSA 5:3](https://ref.ly/Ps5:3); [PRO 30:7–30:8](https://ref.ly/Prov30:7-Prov30:8); [MAT 9:37–9:38](https://ref.ly/Matt9:37-Matt9:38); [1TI 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1)

### Frequent requests for guidance or insight

**[2SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:1)**; **[EPH 1:17](https://ref.ly/Eph1:17)** *See also* [GEN 25:21–25:22](https://ref.ly/Gen25:21-Gen25:22); [EXO 33:7](https://ref.ly/Exod33:7); [JOS 9:14](https://ref.ly/Josh9:14); [1SA 23:1–23:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:1-1Sam23:2); [2KI 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:7-2Kgs8:8); [1CH 21:29–21:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:29-1Chr21:30); [2CH 20:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:3); [ISA 58:2](https://ref.ly/Isa58:2); [JER 10:21](https://ref.ly/Jer10:21); [COL 1:9](https://ref.ly/Col1:9); [JAS 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jas1:5)

----------------------

### According to God's will

**[1JN 5:14–5:15](https://ref.ly/1John5:14-1John5:15)**

### Trusting in God's great mercy

## 8638: benedictions (id: 8638)

Prayers invoking God's blessing upon others. They are often spoken at a time of impending separation, although they may sometimes constitute greetings.

Benedictions pronounced by priests
----------------------------------

**[NUM 6:23–6:27](https://ref.ly/Num6:23-Num6:27)** *See also* [DEU 21:5](https://ref.ly/Deut21:5); [1SA 2:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:20); [2CH 30:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:27)

Benedictions pronounced by kings
--------------------------------

**[1KI 8:54–8:61](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:54-1Kgs8:61)** *See also* [GEN 14:18–14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18-Gen14:20); [2SA 6:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:20); [2CH 6:3–6:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:3-2Chr6:11)

----------------------------------------------------------------

**[JOS 22:6–22:8](https://ref.ly/Josh22:6-Josh22:8)** *See also* [DEU 33:1–33:29](https://ref.ly/Deut33:1-Deut33:29)

-----------------------------------------

**[RUT 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Ruth1:8-Ruth1:9)** *See also* [GEN 24:60](https://ref.ly/Gen24:60), [GEN 27:27–27:29](https://ref.ly/Gen27:27-Gen27:29), [GEN 48:9–48:22](https://ref.ly/Gen48:9-Gen48:22), [GEN 49:1–49:28](https://ref.ly/Gen49:1-Gen49:28)

**[ROM 1:7](https://ref.ly/Rom1:7)** *See also* [ROM 16:20](https://ref.ly/Rom16:20); [1CO 1:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:3), [1CO 16:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:23); [GAL 1:2–1:5](https://ref.ly/Gal1:2-Gal1:5), [GAL 6:16](https://ref.ly/Gal6:16); [EPH 1:2](https://ref.ly/Eph1:2), [EPH 6:23](https://ref.ly/Eph6:23); [1TI 1:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:2); [REV 22:21](https://ref.ly/Rev22:21)

## 8640: calling upon God (id: 8640)

Asking God to act, in dependence upon him and out of a sense of sometimes desperate need.

Calling upon God as an act of worship
-------------------------------------

### By those who depend upon him and proclaim his name

**[GEN 4:26](https://ref.ly/Gen4:26)** *See also* [GEN 12:8](https://ref.ly/Gen12:8), [GEN 26:25](https://ref.ly/Gen26:25); [1CH 16:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:8) parallel [PSA 105:1](https://ref.ly/Ps105:1); [PSA 86:5](https://ref.ly/Ps86:5), [PSA 116:2](https://ref.ly/Ps116:2), [PSA 116:17](https://ref.ly/Ps116:17); [JOB 27:10](https://ref.ly/Job27:10); [2TI 2:22](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:22)

### Those who do not acknowledge God or call upon his name

[PSA 14:4](https://ref.ly/Ps14:4) parallel [PSA 53:4](https://ref.ly/Ps53:4); ; [PSA 79:6](https://ref.ly/Ps79:6); ; [ISA 65:1](https://ref.ly/Isa65:1); ; [JER 10:25](https://ref.ly/Jer10:25); ; [ZEP 3:9](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:9)

Calling upon God in time of need
--------------------------------

**[PSA 50:15](https://ref.ly/Ps50:15)**; **[LAM 3:55](https://ref.ly/Lam3:55)** *See also* [1SA 12:17–12:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:17-1Sam12:18); [2SA 22:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:4) parallel [PSA 18:3](https://ref.ly/Ps18:3); [1KI 18:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:24); [2KI 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:11); [JOB 19:7](https://ref.ly/Job19:7); [PSA 4:1](https://ref.ly/Ps4:1), [PSA 17:6–17:9](https://ref.ly/Ps17:6-Ps17:9), [PSA 18:6](https://ref.ly/Ps18:6), [PSA 27:7–27:12](https://ref.ly/Ps27:7-Ps27:12), [PSA 28:1–28:2](https://ref.ly/Ps28:1-Ps28:2), [PSA 55:16](https://ref.ly/Ps55:16), [PSA 86:3](https://ref.ly/Ps86:3), [PSA 86:7](https://ref.ly/Ps86:7), [PSA 91:15](https://ref.ly/Ps91:15), [PSA 120:1](https://ref.ly/Ps120:1); [PRO 1:28](https://ref.ly/Prov1:28); [ISA 58:9](https://ref.ly/Isa58:9), [ISA 65:24](https://ref.ly/Isa65:24); [JER 11:14](https://ref.ly/Jer11:14), [JER 33:3](https://ref.ly/Jer33:3); [JOL 1:19](https://ref.ly/Joel1:19); [HAB 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hab1:2); [ZEC 13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:9)

Calling upon God for his salvation
----------------------------------

**[ACT 2:21](https://ref.ly/Acts2:21)** *See also* [JOL 2:32](https://ref.ly/Joel2:32); [ROM 10:12–10:14](https://ref.ly/Rom10:12-Rom10:14)

------------------------------------------------------------------

## 8642: celebration (id: 8642)

Although there are examples of private celebrations in Scripture, most celebration is associated with the acts of God. The enemies of God's people also celebrate, but in the end it is the triumph of God's purpose that is celebrated.

Celebrations in everyday life
-----------------------------

Wedding: [JDG 14:10](https://ref.ly/Judg14:10); ; [MAT 22:2–22:3](https://ref.ly/Matt22:2-Matt22:3); [MAT 14:6](https://ref.ly/Matt14:6) parallel [MRK 6:21–6:22](https://ref.ly/Mark6:21-Mark6:22) *birthday*; ; [LUK 5:29](https://ref.ly/Luke5:29) *for an honoured guest*; ; [LUK 15:22–15:24](https://ref.ly/Luke15:22-Luke15:24) *family reunion*

The Israelites celebrated God's acts
------------------------------------

### Celebrating God's gifts of harvest: Pentecost and Tabernacles

[EXO 23:16](https://ref.ly/Exod23:16), [EXO 34:22](https://ref.ly/Exod34:22); ; [LEV 23:15–23:21](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:21), [LEV 23:33–23:36](https://ref.ly/Lev23:33-Lev23:36), [LEV 23:39–23:43](https://ref.ly/Lev23:39-Lev23:43); ; [NUM 28:26](https://ref.ly/Num28:26), [NUM 29:12](https://ref.ly/Num29:12); ; [DEU 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Deut16:9-Deut16:10)

### Celebrating their deliverance from slavery in Egypt: Passover

**[EXO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Exod13:3)** *See also* [EXO 12:14](https://ref.ly/Exod12:14), [EXO 23:15](https://ref.ly/Exod23:15), [EXO 34:18](https://ref.ly/Exod34:18); [NUM 9:2](https://ref.ly/Num9:2); [DEU 16:1](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1)

### Celebrating the deliverance of the nation through Esther: Purim

[EST 9:18–9:19](https://ref.ly/Esth9:18-Esth9:19)

Religious celebrations
----------------------

### The bringing of the ark of the covenant to Jerusalem

**[2SA 6:12–6:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:12-2Sam6:15)** parallel [1CH 15:27–15:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr15:27-1Chr15:28)

### The bringing of the ark of the covenant to the temple

*See also* [1KI 8:3–8:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:3-1Kgs8:6) parallel [2CH 5:4–5:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:4-2Chr5:6); [2CH 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:12-2Chr5:13)

### The completion and dedication of the first temple

[1KI 8:63](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:63), [1KI 8:65–8:66](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:65-1Kgs8:66) parallel [2CH 7:8–7:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:8-2Chr7:10)

### The dedication of the second temple

[EZR 6:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:16)

### The reading of the law after the building of the walls of Jersualem

[NEH 8:9–8:12](https://ref.ly/Neh8:9-Neh8:12)

### Christians celebrate the death of Christ and his return

[1CO 11:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:26)

The enemies of God's people celebrate
-------------------------------------

[JDG 16:23](https://ref.ly/Judg16:23) *capture of Samson*; ; [DAN 5:1](https://ref.ly/Dan5:1) *Belshazzar's feast*; [REV 11:3](https://ref.ly/Rev11:3), [REV 11:7–11:10](https://ref.ly/Rev11:7-Rev11:10)

Celebration in heaven
---------------------

### Creation

[JOB 38:4–38:7](https://ref.ly/Job38:4-Job38:7)

### The repentance of sinners

[LUK 15:7](https://ref.ly/Luke15:7), [LUK 15:10](https://ref.ly/Luke15:10)

### Divine deliverance through Jesus Christ

[REV 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9-Rev5:10)

### The downfall of evil

[REV 19:1–19:5](https://ref.ly/Rev19:1-Rev19:5)

### The final triumph of Jesus Christ

**[REV 19:9](https://ref.ly/Rev19:9)** *See also* [REV 11:16–11:18](https://ref.ly/Rev11:16-Rev11:18), [REV 15:2–15:4](https://ref.ly/Rev15:2-Rev15:4)

## 8644: commemoration (id: 8644)

The act of remembering particular people, places or events in order to reflect on and be thankful for God's faithfulness.

Commemoration of people
-----------------------

**[2CH 35:25–35:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:25-2Chr35:27)** *See also* [JDG 11:28–11:40](https://ref.ly/Judg11:28-Judg11:40); [1KI 11:41](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:41) parallel [2CH 9:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:29)

### Commemoration of Jesus Christ

**[LUK 22:19](https://ref.ly/Luke22:19)**; **[1CO 11:23–11:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23-1Cor11:26)**

Commemoration of places
-----------------------

**[GEN 35:1](https://ref.ly/Gen35:1)** *See also* [JOS 4:19–4:24](https://ref.ly/Josh4:19-Josh4:24); [JDG 6:24](https://ref.ly/Judg6:24); [2SA 24:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:18)

Commemoration of events
-----------------------

### Commemoration of God's creation of the world

**[EXO 20:8–20:11](https://ref.ly/Exod20:8-Exod20:11)** parallel [DEU 5:12–5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:12-Deut5:15) *See also* [EXO 31:12–31:17](https://ref.ly/Exod31:12-Exod31:17)

### Commemoration of God's acts in history

**[EXO 12:14–12:17](https://ref.ly/Exod12:14-Exod12:17)** *See also* [EXO 13:1–13:10](https://ref.ly/Exod13:1-Exod13:10); [PSA 9:1–9:6](https://ref.ly/Ps9:1-Ps9:6), [PSA 126:1–126:3](https://ref.ly/Ps126:1-Ps126:3)

### Commemoration of God's provision of food

**[LEV 23:9–23:14](https://ref.ly/Lev23:9-Lev23:14)** *See also* [LEV 23:15–23:22](https://ref.ly/Lev23:15-Lev23:22) parallels [NUM 28:26–28:31](https://ref.ly/Num28:26-Num28:31), [DEU 16:9–16:12](https://ref.ly/Deut16:9-Deut16:12); [LEV 23:33–23:43](https://ref.ly/Lev23:33-Lev23:43) parallels [NUM 29:12–29:39](https://ref.ly/Num29:12-Num29:39), [DEU 16:13–16:17](https://ref.ly/Deut16:13-Deut16:17)

## 8646: doxology (id: 8646)

A form of words that offers praise to God, especially for his work of creation and redemption. Scripture records a number of these forms of praise which were used in both public and private worship.

Doxologies used in the formal worship of Israel
-----------------------------------------------

**[1CH 29:10–29:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:10-1Chr29:13)** *See also* [PSA 41:13](https://ref.ly/Ps41:13), [PSA 72:18–72:19](https://ref.ly/Ps72:18-Ps72:19), [PSA 106:48](https://ref.ly/Ps106:48), [PSA 150:1–150:6](https://ref.ly/Ps150:1-Ps150:6)

Doxologies used in the spontaneous praise of God's people
---------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 11:33–11:36](https://ref.ly/Rom11:33-Rom11:36)** *See also* [PSA 57:5](https://ref.ly/Ps57:5), [PSA 57:11](https://ref.ly/Ps57:11); [MAT 21:9](https://ref.ly/Matt21:9) parallels [MRK 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/Mark11:9-Mark11:10), [LUK 19:38](https://ref.ly/Luke19:38); [GAL 1:5](https://ref.ly/Gal1:5); [EPH 3:21](https://ref.ly/Eph3:21); [PHP 4:20](https://ref.ly/Phil4:20); [1TI 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:17)

Doxologies used as a benediction
--------------------------------

**[JUD 1:24–1:25](https://ref.ly/Jude1:24-Jude1:25)** *See also* [EPH 3:20–3:21](https://ref.ly/Eph3:20-Eph3:21); [ROM 16:25–16:27](https://ref.ly/Rom16:25-Rom16:27); [HEB 13:20–13:21](https://ref.ly/Heb13:20-Heb13:21); [2PE 3:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:18)

Doxologies used in heavenly worship
-----------------------------------

**[REV 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/Rev5:12-Rev5:13)** *See also* [ISA 6:3](https://ref.ly/Isa6:3); [LUK 2:13–2:14](https://ref.ly/Luke2:13-Luke2:14); [REV 4:8](https://ref.ly/Rev4:8), [REV 4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:11), [REV 5:9–5:14](https://ref.ly/Rev5:9-Rev5:14), [REV 7:12](https://ref.ly/Rev7:12), [REV 19:1](https://ref.ly/Rev19:1)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 29:10-13; Psalms 41:13; Psalms 72:18-19; Psalms 106:48; Psalms 150:1-6; Romans 11:33-36; Psalms 57:5; Psalms 57:11; Matthew 21:9; Mark 11:9-10; Luke 19:38; Galatians 1:5; Ephesians 3:21; Philippians 4:20; 1 Timothy 1:17; Jude 1:24-25; Ephesians 3:20-21; Romans 16:25-27; Hebrews 13:20-21; 2 Peter 3:18; Revelation 5:12-13; Isaiah 6:3; Luke 2:13-14; Revelation 4:8; Revelation 4:11; Revelation 5:9-14; Revelation 7:12; Revelation 19:1
* **Associated Articles:** 1045: God, glory of (ID: `1045@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1060: God, greatness of (ID: `1060@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2321: Jesus Christ, as redeemer (ID: `2321@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4114: angels, and praise of God (ID: `4114@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7927: hymn (ID: `7927@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8444: honouring God (ID: `8444@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8624: worship, reasons for (ID: `8624@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8632: adoration (ID: `8632@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8638: benedictions (ID: `8638@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8664: praise (ID: `8664@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8676: thanksgiving (ID: `8676@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8648: enquiring of God (id: 8648)

Requesting from God a definite direct response to a particular question. In the OT, people are frequently said to enquire of God for knowledge or guidance, often through the intermediacy of a priest or prophet.

Enquiring of God for knowledge
------------------------------

**[GEN 25:22–25:23](https://ref.ly/Gen25:22-Gen25:23)** *See also* [JDG 13:17](https://ref.ly/Judg13:17), [JDG 18:5–18:6](https://ref.ly/Judg18:5-Judg18:6); [1SA 10:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:22)

Enquiring of God for guidance
-----------------------------

**[2SA 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam2:1)** *See also* [JDG 20:18](https://ref.ly/Judg20:18), [JDG 20:23](https://ref.ly/Judg20:23), [JDG 20:27–20:28](https://ref.ly/Judg20:27-Judg20:28); [1SA 23:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:2), [1SA 23:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:4), [1SA 30:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam30:8); [2SA 5:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:19) parallel [1CH 14:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:10); [2SA 5:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:23) parallel [1CH 14:14](https://ref.ly/1Chr14:14)

Enquiring of God through intermediaries
---------------------------------------

### Priests

**[JDG 18:5–18:6](https://ref.ly/Judg18:5-Judg18:6)** *See also* [NUM 27:18–27:21](https://ref.ly/Num27:18-Num27:21); [1SA 22:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:10), [1SA 22:13–22:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:13-1Sam22:15)

### Prophets

**[1KI 22:6–22:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:6-1Kgs22:9)** parallel [2CH 18:5–18:8](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:5-2Chr18:8) *See also* [1SA 9:6–9:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam9:6-1Sam9:10), [1SA 28:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:6); [2KI 3:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:11), [2KI 22:11–22:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs22:11-2Kgs22:20) parallel [2CH 34:19–34:28](https://ref.ly/2Chr34:19-2Chr34:28); [JER 21:1–21:7](https://ref.ly/Jer21:1-Jer21:7), [JER 37:6–37:8](https://ref.ly/Jer37:6-Jer37:8); [EZK 14:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:7)

Enquiring of God by casting lots
--------------------------------

**[ACT 1:24–1:26](https://ref.ly/Acts1:24-Acts1:26)** *See also* [NUM 27:21](https://ref.ly/Num27:21) *The Urim and Thummim were sacred lots carried in the breastpiece of the High Priest's ephod*; [1SA 10:20–10:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam10:20-1Sam10:22), [1SA 14:36–14:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:36-1Sam14:42)

Enquiring of God in prayer
--------------------------

**[2CH 20:1–20:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:1-2Chr20:17)**

Enquiring of God at the tabernacle
----------------------------------

**[EXO 33:7](https://ref.ly/Exod33:7)** *See also* [JDG 20:26–20:28](https://ref.ly/Judg20:26-Judg20:28); [1CH 13:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr13:3); [2CH 1:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr1:5)

Devotion to God as a necessary prerequisite for enquiring of him
----------------------------------------------------------------

**[EZK 20:30–20:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:30-Ezek20:31)** *See also* [EZK 14:1–14:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:1-Ezek14:11), [EZK 20:1–20:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:1-Ezek20:3)

## 8650: hands, lifting up of (id: 8650)

A human gesture which can be a sign of worship and adoration or indicate the swearing of a solemn oath. God is portrayed as raising his hands as a sign of authority over his creation.

God's uplifted hand
-------------------

### God's uplifted hand signifies his power

**[PSA 118:16](https://ref.ly/Ps118:16)** *See also* [EXO 24:11](https://ref.ly/Exod24:11); [PSA 89:13](https://ref.ly/Ps89:13); [ISA 9:12](https://ref.ly/Isa9:12), [ISA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:17), [ISA 9:21](https://ref.ly/Isa9:21), [ISA 26:11](https://ref.ly/Isa26:11)

### God's uplifted hand signifies his judgment

**[ISA 5:25](https://ref.ly/Isa5:25)** *See also* [1SA 6:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:3), [1SA 6:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:5); [ISA 19:16](https://ref.ly/Isa19:16); [MIC 5:9](https://ref.ly/Mic5:9); [ZEC 2:9](https://ref.ly/Zech2:9)

Hands uplifted for swearing oaths
---------------------------------

### Oaths taken by God

**[EZK 20:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:5)** *See also* [EXO 6:8](https://ref.ly/Exod6:8); [DEU 32:40–32:41](https://ref.ly/Deut32:40-Deut32:41); [NEH 9:15](https://ref.ly/Neh9:15); [EZK 20:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:15), [EZK 36:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:7), [EZK 47:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:14)

### Oaths taken by people and angels

[GEN 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Gen14:22-Gen14:23); ; [DAN 12:7](https://ref.ly/Dan12:7); ; [REV 10:5](https://ref.ly/Rev10:5)

Hands uplifted in worship
-------------------------

### Hands lifted up in prayer

**[PSA 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ps28:2)** *See also* [EXO 17:11–17:13](https://ref.ly/Exod17:11-Exod17:13); [PSA 141:2](https://ref.ly/Ps141:2); [LAM 2:19](https://ref.ly/Lam2:19), [LAM 3:41](https://ref.ly/Lam3:41); [1TI 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:8)

### Hands lifted up in praise

**[PSA 63:4](https://ref.ly/Ps63:4)** *See also* [NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6); [PSA 119:48](https://ref.ly/Ps119:48), [PSA 134:2](https://ref.ly/Ps134:2)

----------------------------

[LEV 9:22](https://ref.ly/Lev9:22); ; [LUK 24:50](https://ref.ly/Luke24:50)

Hands lifted up as a threat
---------------------------

**[1SA 24:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:6)** *See also* [1SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17), [1SA 22:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam22:17), [1SA 24:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:10); [2SA 18:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:12), [2SA 18:28](https://ref.ly/2Sam18:28), [2SA 20:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:21); [JOB 31:21](https://ref.ly/Job31:21)

Uplifted hands as a sign of minimal effort
------------------------------------------

[GEN 41:44](https://ref.ly/Gen41:44); ; [DEU 28:32](https://ref.ly/Deut28:32); ; [EZR 6:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:12); ; [PSA 76:5](https://ref.ly/Ps76:5)

Uplifted hands as a sign of support
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 91:12](https://ref.ly/Ps91:12)** *See also* [MAT 4:6](https://ref.ly/Matt4:6) parallel [LUK 4:11](https://ref.ly/Luke4:11)

## 8652: importunity (id: 8652)

Persistence and boldness in making requests to God in prayer and to other people, and persevering until a favourable response is received.

* **Associated Articles:** 8653: importunity, towards God (ID: `8653@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8654: importunity, towards people (ID: `8654@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8653: importunity, towards God (id: 8653)

Earnestness and perseverance in presenting requests to God on behalf of oneself and others; seen in persistent pleading, faithful intercession and bold appeal.

Perseverance in prayer
----------------------

### Persistence in prayer

**[PSA 55:17](https://ref.ly/Ps55:17)** *See also* [PSA 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1-Ps22:2), [PSA 86:3](https://ref.ly/Ps86:3), [PSA 88:1](https://ref.ly/Ps88:1), [PSA 88:9](https://ref.ly/Ps88:9); [1TH 5:17](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:17); [1TI 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:5)**[MAT 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Matt7:7-Matt7:8)** parallel [LUK 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/Luke11:9-Luke11:10)**[LUK 18:1](https://ref.ly/Luke18:1)** *See also* [LUK 18:2–18:8](https://ref.ly/Luke18:2-Luke18:8)

### Faithfulness in prayer

**[EPH 6:18](https://ref.ly/Eph6:18)** *See also* [ROM 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Rom1:9-Rom1:10); [COL 4:12](https://ref.ly/Col4:12); [1TH 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/1Thess1:2-1Thess1:3); [2TI 1:3](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:3)

Earnestness in prayer
---------------------

**[PSA 119:58](https://ref.ly/Ps119:58), [PSA 130:1–130:2](https://ref.ly/Ps130:1-Ps130:2)** *See also* [PSA 55:1–55:2](https://ref.ly/Ps55:1-Ps55:2), [PSA 61:1–61:2](https://ref.ly/Ps61:1-Ps61:2); [JON 3:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:8)**[LUK 22:44](https://ref.ly/Luke22:44)** *See also* [HEB 5:7](https://ref.ly/Heb5:7)

Boldness in prayer
------------------

**[GEN 18:27](https://ref.ly/Gen18:27)**; **[LUK 11:8](https://ref.ly/Luke11:8)** *See also* [EXO 33:12–33:16](https://ref.ly/Exod33:12-Exod33:16)EPH [3:12](https://ref.ly/Exod3:12); [HEB 4:16](https://ref.ly/Heb4:16)

Further examples of importunity in prayer
-----------------------------------------

### Repeated requests in prayer

[GEN 18:22–18:32](https://ref.ly/Gen18:22-Gen18:32) *Abraham prays for Sodom*; ; [JDG 6:36–6:40](https://ref.ly/Judg6:36-Judg6:40) *Gideon asks for assurance*; ; [MAT 15:22–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:22-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:25–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:25-Mark7:30) *a Canaanite woman begs for Jesus Christ's help*; ; [MAT 20:30–20:31](https://ref.ly/Matt20:30-Matt20:31) parallels [MRK 10:47–10:48](https://ref.ly/Mark10:47-Mark10:48), [LUK 18:38–18:39](https://ref.ly/Luke18:38-Luke18:39) *Blind men call out to Jesus Christ*; ; [MAT 26:44](https://ref.ly/Matt26:44) *Jesus Christ prays in Gethsemane*; ; [MRK 5:10](https://ref.ly/Mark5:10) parallel [LUK 8:31](https://ref.ly/Luke8:31) *Legion begs for mercy*; ; [2CO 12:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:8) *Paul pleads with God to take away his “thorn”.*

### Further examples of perseverance in prayer

[GEN 32:26](https://ref.ly/Gen32:26) *Jacob wrestles with God*; ; [DEU 9:18](https://ref.ly/Deut9:18) *Moses fasts and prays because of Israel's sin*; ; [2SA 12:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:16) *David pleads for his sick child*; ; [1KI 18:28–18:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:28-1Kgs18:29) *The prophets of Baal appeal unsuccessfully to their god*; ; [NEH 1:4–1:6](https://ref.ly/Neh1:4-Neh1:6) *Nehemiah prays for God's people*; ; [DAN 10:2–10:3](https://ref.ly/Dan10:2-Dan10:3) *Daniel waits for the interpretation of his vision*; ; [LUK 2:37](https://ref.ly/Luke2:37) *Anna looks for God to redeem his people.*

### Further examples of earnestness in prayer

[1SA 1:12–1:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:12-1Sam1:16) *Hannah prays for a child*; ; [1KI 8:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:22) parallel [2CH 6:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:12) *Solomon prays at the dedication of the Temple*; ; [JAS 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Jas5:17-Jas5:18) *Elijah prays for rain to cease*; ; [EZR 9:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:5) *Ezra prays about the people's sin*; ; [ISA 38:2–38:3](https://ref.ly/Isa38:2-Isa38:3) *Hezekiah prays for healing*; ; [DAN 9:3](https://ref.ly/Dan9:3) *Daniel prays for Jerusalem*; ; [MRK 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Mark5:22-Mark5:23) parallel [LUK 8:41–8:42](https://ref.ly/Luke8:41-Luke8:42) *Jairus asks Jesus Christ to heal his daughter*; ; [JHN 4:47](https://ref.ly/John4:47) *A nobleman begs Jesus Christ to heal his son*; ; [ACT 12:5](https://ref.ly/Acts12:5) *The early church prays for Peter's release from prison.*

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 55:17; Psalms 22:1-2; Psalms 86:3; Psalms 88:1; Psalms 88:9; 1 Thessalonians 5:17; 1 Timothy 5:5; Matthew 7:7-8; Luke 11:9-10; Luke 18:1; Luke 18:2-8; Ephesians 6:18; Romans 1:9-10; Colossians 4:12; 1 Thessalonians 1:2-3; 2 Timothy 1:3; Psalms 119:58; Psalms 130:1-2; Psalms 55:1-2; Psalms 61:1-2; Jonah 3:8; Luke 22:44; Hebrews 5:7; Genesis 18:27; Luke 11:8; Exodus 33:12-16; Exodus 3:12; Hebrews 4:16; Genesis 18:22-32; Judges 6:36-40; Matthew 15:22-28; Mark 7:25-30; Matthew 20:30-31; Mark 10:47-48; Luke 18:38-39; Matthew 26:44; Mark 5:10; Luke 8:31; 2 Corinthians 12:8; Genesis 32:26; Deuteronomy 9:18; 2 Samuel 12:16; 1 Kings 18:28-29; Nehemiah 1:4-6; Daniel 10:2-3; Luke 2:37; 1 Samuel 1:12-16; 1 Kings 8:22; 2 Chronicles 6:12; James 5:17-18; Ezra 9:5; Isaiah 38:2-3; Daniel 9:3; Mark 5:22-23; Luke 8:41-42; John 4:47; Acts 12:5
* **Associated Articles:** 5767: attitudes, in prayer (ID: `5767@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8239: earnestness (ID: `8239@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8613: prayer, persistence in (ID: `8613@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8618: prayerfulness (ID: `8618@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8654: importunity, towards people (id: 8654)

Boldness, urgency and persistence in appealing to others; seen in making requests of all kinds, presenting the gospel and giving Christian instruction; seen too in God's patient and persistent pleading with people.

Making requests of others
-------------------------

[GEN 19:3](https://ref.ly/Gen19:3) *Lot prevails on his angelic visitors to stay with him*; ; [GEN 39:10](https://ref.ly/Gen39:10) *Potiphar's wife propositions Joseph*; ; [NUM 22:37](https://ref.ly/Num22:37) *Balak summons Balaam*; ; [JDG 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/Judg14:16-Judg14:17) *Samson's wife presses for the answer to his riddle*; ; [JDG 16:6–16:16](https://ref.ly/Judg16:6-Judg16:16) *Delilah nags Samson to know the secret of his strength*; ; [2KI 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:16-2Kgs2:17) *Elisha is requested to authorise a search for Elijah*; ; [EST 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/Esth3:3-Esth3:4) *Royal officials plead with Mordecai*; ; [EST 8:3](https://ref.ly/Esth8:3) *Esther pleads with the king*; ; [PRO 19:7](https://ref.ly/Prov19:7) *A poor man pleads in vain with fair\-weather friends*; ; [JER 38:26](https://ref.ly/Jer38:26) *Jeremiah pleads with King Zedekiah*; ; [LUK 23:23](https://ref.ly/Luke23:23) *The crowds call for Jesus Christ to be crucified*; ; [ACT 12:16](https://ref.ly/Acts12:16) *Peter knocks on the door after his release from prison*; ; [ACT 25:3](https://ref.ly/Acts25:3) *The chief priests urge that Paul be taken to Jerusalem*; ; [2CO 8:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor8:4) *The Macedonian churches ask to share in the giving to God's people*; ; [PHP 4:2](https://ref.ly/Phil4:2) *Paul pleads with Euodia and Syntyche to agree.*

Importunity in preaching the gospel
-----------------------------------

**[2CO 5:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:20)**

### Persistence

**[ACT 5:42](https://ref.ly/Acts5:42)**

### Urgent appeal

**[ACT 2:40](https://ref.ly/Acts2:40)**

### Boldness

**[ACT 19:8](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8)** *See also* [ACT 4:29](https://ref.ly/Acts4:29), [ACT 4:31](https://ref.ly/Acts4:31), [ACT 9:27](https://ref.ly/Acts9:27), [ACT 14:3](https://ref.ly/Acts14:3), [ACT 18:26](https://ref.ly/Acts18:26), [ACT 28:31](https://ref.ly/Acts28:31); [PHP 1:14](https://ref.ly/Phil1:14)

### Persuasion

**[2CO 5:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:11)** *See also* [ACT 18:4](https://ref.ly/Acts18:4), [ACT 19:8](https://ref.ly/Acts19:8), [ACT 26:28](https://ref.ly/Acts26:28)

Importunity in giving instruction
---------------------------------

**[2CO 10:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:1)**; **[1TH 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:1)** *See also* [ROM 15:15](https://ref.ly/Rom15:15); [ACT 13:43](https://ref.ly/Acts13:43); [2CO 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:1); [GAL 4:12](https://ref.ly/Gal4:12); [EPH 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eph4:1), [EPH 4:17](https://ref.ly/Eph4:17); [1TH 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:10)

God's persistent appeal
-----------------------

### God's repeated call to individuals

**[1SA 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:8)**; **[JHN 21:17](https://ref.ly/John21:17)**

### God's appeal to his wayward people

**[2CH 36:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15)**; **[JER 7:13](https://ref.ly/Jer7:13)**; **[MAT 21:35–21:37](https://ref.ly/Matt21:35-Matt21:37)** *Finally, God sends his Son.* *See also* [JER 7:25](https://ref.ly/Jer7:25), [JER 11:7](https://ref.ly/Jer11:7), [JER 25:3–25:4](https://ref.ly/Jer25:3-Jer25:4), [JER 26:5](https://ref.ly/Jer26:5), [JER 29:19](https://ref.ly/Jer29:19), [JER 32:33](https://ref.ly/Jer32:33), [JER 35:14–35:15](https://ref.ly/Jer35:14-Jer35:15), [JER 44:4](https://ref.ly/Jer44:4)

## 8656: longing for God (id: 8656)

A deep desire for God, which Scripture often compares to hunger or thirst. Only Jesus Christ can truly satisfy this longing for God.

The individual's longing for God
--------------------------------

**[PSA 119:20](https://ref.ly/Ps119:20)**; **[ISA 26:9](https://ref.ly/Isa26:9)** *See also* [PSA 73:25](https://ref.ly/Ps73:25), [PSA 119:81](https://ref.ly/Ps119:81), [PSA 119:131](https://ref.ly/Ps119:131)

The longing for God compared to hunger and thirst
-------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:2), [PSA 63:1](https://ref.ly/Ps63:1)** *See also* [PSA 143:6](https://ref.ly/Ps143:6); [AMO 8:11](https://ref.ly/Amos8:11)

The longing for God is satisfied through Jesus Christ
-----------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/John4:13-John4:14), [JHN 6:35](https://ref.ly/John6:35)** *See also* [PSA 42:2](https://ref.ly/Ps42:2); [ISA 55:1](https://ref.ly/Isa55:1); [JHN 6:27](https://ref.ly/John6:27), [JHN 6:50–6:51](https://ref.ly/John6:50-John6:51), [JHN 7:37](https://ref.ly/John7:37); [REV 21:6](https://ref.ly/Rev21:6), [REV 22:17](https://ref.ly/Rev22:17)

## 8658: Lord's Prayer, the (id: 8658)

The name given to the prayer Jesus Christ taught his disciples, probably as a model prayer for regular use.

Aspects of the Lord's Prayer
----------------------------

### A recognition of God as Father and Lord

**[MAT 6:9](https://ref.ly/Matt6:9)** parallel [LUK 11:2](https://ref.ly/Luke11:2) *See also* [MAT 7:21](https://ref.ly/Matt7:21), [MAT 16:17](https://ref.ly/Matt16:17); [LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Luke10:21); [EPH 3:14–3:15](https://ref.ly/Eph3:14-Eph3:15)

### A desire for God's perfect will to be done

**[MAT 6:10](https://ref.ly/Matt6:10)** parallel [LUK 11:2](https://ref.ly/Luke11:2) *See also* [MAT 12:50](https://ref.ly/Matt12:50), [MAT 26:39](https://ref.ly/Matt26:39), [MAT 26:42](https://ref.ly/Matt26:42); [ACT 21:14](https://ref.ly/Acts21:14); [COL 1:9](https://ref.ly/Col1:9); [HEB 10:9](https://ref.ly/Heb10:9)

### Asking for physical provision

**[MAT 6:11](https://ref.ly/Matt6:11)** parallel [LUK 11:3](https://ref.ly/Luke11:3) *See also* [MAT 6:31–6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:31-Matt6:32), [MAT 7:9–7:11](https://ref.ly/Matt7:9-Matt7:11)

### Asking for forgiveness

**[MAT 6:12](https://ref.ly/Matt6:12)** parallel [LUK 11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:4) *See also* [MAT 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Matt6:14-Matt6:15), [MAT 18:23–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:23-Matt18:35); [MRK 11:25](https://ref.ly/Mark11:25); [LUK 23:34](https://ref.ly/Luke23:34); [COL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Col3:13); [1JN 1:9](https://ref.ly/1John1:9)

### Asking for spiritual protection

**[MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13)** parallel [LUK 11:4](https://ref.ly/Luke11:4) *See also* [2CO 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:10-2Cor1:11); [JUD 1:24](https://ref.ly/Jude1:24); [REV 3:10](https://ref.ly/Rev3:10)

### The doxology

**[MAT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Matt6:13)** *NIV footnote.*

## 8660: magnifying God (id: 8660)

Giving glory to God through worship and through upright living. God's people are exhorted to praise him and to bring honour to his name.

Ways of magnifying God
----------------------

### Through praise

**[PSA 103:19–103:22](https://ref.ly/Ps103:19-Ps103:22)** *See also* [PSA 33:1–33:5](https://ref.ly/Ps33:1-Ps33:5), [PSA 68:26](https://ref.ly/Ps68:26), [PSA 104:1](https://ref.ly/Ps104:1), [PSA 106:1–106:2](https://ref.ly/Ps106:1-Ps106:2), [PSA 111:1–111:10](https://ref.ly/Ps111:1-Ps111:10), [PSA 117:1–117:2](https://ref.ly/Ps117:1-Ps117:2), [PSA 135:1–135:3](https://ref.ly/Ps135:1-Ps135:3), [PSA 135:19–135:21](https://ref.ly/Ps135:19-Ps135:21), [PSA 150:1–150:2](https://ref.ly/Ps150:1-Ps150:2); [LUK 1:46](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46), [LUK 19:37–19:38](https://ref.ly/Luke19:37-Luke19:38)

### In worship

**[1CH 16:28–16:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:28-1Chr16:29)** parallel [PSA 96:7–96:9](https://ref.ly/Ps96:7-Ps96:9) *See also* [PSA 29:1–29:2](https://ref.ly/Ps29:1-Ps29:2), [PSA 100:1–100:2](https://ref.ly/Ps100:1-Ps100:2)

### Through reverence

**[PSA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Ps5:7)** *See also* [DAN 6:25–6:27](https://ref.ly/Dan6:25-Dan6:27); [MAL 2:4–2:7](https://ref.ly/Mal2:4-Mal2:7); [HEB 12:28–12:29](https://ref.ly/Heb12:28-Heb12:29)

### By glorifying him

**[PSA 34:1–34:3](https://ref.ly/Ps34:1-Ps34:3)** *See also* [PSA 63:3–63:4](https://ref.ly/Ps63:3-Ps63:4), [PSA 69:30–69:31](https://ref.ly/Ps69:30-Ps69:31), [PSA 86:12–86:13](https://ref.ly/Ps86:12-Ps86:13), [PSA 96:3–96:9](https://ref.ly/Ps96:3-Ps96:9); [2TH 1:11–1:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:11-2Thess1:12); [1PE 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:11-1Pet2:12); [REV 4:6–4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:6-Rev4:11), [REV 15:2–15:4](https://ref.ly/Rev15:2-Rev15:4)

### By honouring him

**[1TI 6:15–6:16](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:15-1Tim6:16)** *See also* [ISA 26:13](https://ref.ly/Isa26:13); [MAL 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Mal2:1-Mal2:2); [JHN 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/John5:22-John5:23); [1CO 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:19-1Cor6:20); [1TI 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:17); [REV 7:9–7:12](https://ref.ly/Rev7:9-Rev7:12)

## 8662: meditation (id: 8662)

Spending time in quietness and usually alone, drawing close to God and listening to him, pondering on his word, his creation, his mighty works or other aspects of his self\-revelation.

The importance of meditation
----------------------------

**[PSA 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1-Ps1:3)** *See also* [1SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:24); [JOB 37:14](https://ref.ly/Job37:14); [PSA 19:14](https://ref.ly/Ps19:14), [PSA 48:9](https://ref.ly/Ps48:9), [PSA 77:11–77:12](https://ref.ly/Ps77:11-Ps77:12), [PSA 104:34](https://ref.ly/Ps104:34), [PSA 107:43](https://ref.ly/Ps107:43); [PHP 4:8](https://ref.ly/Phil4:8); [2TI 2:7](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:7)

Meditation on the person of God
-------------------------------

**[PSA 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ps16:8), [PSA 63:6](https://ref.ly/Ps63:6)** *See also* [PSA 104:34](https://ref.ly/Ps104:34)

Meditation on God's word
------------------------

**[PSA 119:15–119:16](https://ref.ly/Ps119:15-Ps119:16)** *See also* [JOS 1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:8); [PSA 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1-Ps1:3), [PSA 119:23](https://ref.ly/Ps119:23), [PSA 119:48](https://ref.ly/Ps119:48), [PSA 119:78](https://ref.ly/Ps119:78), [PSA 119:95](https://ref.ly/Ps119:95), [PSA 119:97](https://ref.ly/Ps119:97), [PSA 119:99](https://ref.ly/Ps119:99)

Meditation on the works of God
------------------------------

**[PSA 77:12](https://ref.ly/Ps77:12)** *See also* [PSA 111:2](https://ref.ly/Ps111:2), [PSA 143:5](https://ref.ly/Ps143:5), [PSA 145:5](https://ref.ly/Ps145:5)

Meditation on the creation
--------------------------

**[PSA 8:1–8:9](https://ref.ly/Ps8:1-Ps8:9)** *See also* [PSA 104:1–104:34](https://ref.ly/Ps104:1-Ps104:34); [PRO 6:6](https://ref.ly/Prov6:6); [MAT 6:26–6:30](https://ref.ly/Matt6:26-Matt6:30) parallel [LUK 12:24–12:27](https://ref.ly/Luke12:24-Luke12:27)

Meditation at night
-------------------

**[PSA 119:148](https://ref.ly/Ps119:148)** *See also* [PSA 16:7](https://ref.ly/Ps16:7), [PSA 42:8](https://ref.ly/Ps42:8), [PSA 63:6](https://ref.ly/Ps63:6)

Examples of people meditating
-----------------------------

**[JOS 1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:8)**; **[PSA 19:14](https://ref.ly/Ps19:14)** *See also* [JOB 22:22](https://ref.ly/Job22:22) *Eliphaz' advice to Job*; [PSA 39:3](https://ref.ly/Ps39:3), [PSA 119:78](https://ref.ly/Ps119:78), [PSA 119:148](https://ref.ly/Ps119:148); [LUK 2:19](https://ref.ly/Luke2:19) *Mary*; [ACT 8:27–8:35](https://ref.ly/Acts8:27-Acts8:35) *the Ethiopian eunuch*

The results of meditation
-------------------------

### Obedience

**[PSA 119:11](https://ref.ly/Ps119:11)** *See also* [JOS 1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:8); [PSA 119:55](https://ref.ly/Ps119:55)

### Understanding and wisdom

**[PSA 119:97–119:98](https://ref.ly/Ps119:97-Ps119:98)** *See also* [PSA 119:27](https://ref.ly/Ps119:27)

### Praise and worship

[PSA 48:9–48:10](https://ref.ly/Ps48:9-Ps48:10), [PSA 63:5–63:6](https://ref.ly/Ps63:5-Ps63:6), [PSA 104:33–104:35](https://ref.ly/Ps104:33-Ps104:35), [PSA 119:97](https://ref.ly/Ps119:97)

### Prosperity and success

**[JOS 1:8](https://ref.ly/Josh1:8)**

### Delight in the LORD

**[PSA 1:2](https://ref.ly/Ps1:2)** *See also* [PSA 119:15–119:16](https://ref.ly/Ps119:15-Ps119:16), [PSA 119:23–119:24](https://ref.ly/Ps119:23-Ps119:24), [PSA 119:77–119:78](https://ref.ly/Ps119:77-Ps119:78)

### Confidence and faith

**[PSA 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ps16:8)**

## 8664: praise (id: 8664)

The celebration, honouring and adoration of God, in the power of the Holy Spirit, whether by individual believers or communities of believers.

* **Associated Articles:** 8665: praise, reasons for (ID: `8665@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8666: praise, manner and methods of (ID: `8666@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8667: praise, examples of (ID: `8667@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8668: praise, and Holy Spirit (ID: `8668@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8665: praise, reasons for (id: 8665)

Scripture treats praise as the natural response of believers to God's person and actions.

Praise is commanded of God's people
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 68:32](https://ref.ly/Ps68:32)**; **[1PE 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:9)**; **[REV 19:5](https://ref.ly/Rev19:5)** *See also* [PSA 30:4](https://ref.ly/Ps30:4), [PSA 150:6](https://ref.ly/Ps150:6); [ISA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Isa42:10); [PHP 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:9-Phil2:11)

Praise is due to God alone
--------------------------

**[DEU 10:21](https://ref.ly/Deut10:21)**; **[1CH 16:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:25)**; **[ISA 42:8](https://ref.ly/Isa42:8)** *See also* [PSA 66:4](https://ref.ly/Ps66:4), [PSA 118:15–118:21](https://ref.ly/Ps118:15-Ps118:21), [PSA 148:13](https://ref.ly/Ps148:13); [REV 4:11](https://ref.ly/Rev4:11)

Praise is pleasing to God
-------------------------

**[PSA 69:30–69:31](https://ref.ly/Ps69:30-Ps69:31)** *See also* [PSA 135:3](https://ref.ly/Ps135:3), [PSA 147:1](https://ref.ly/Ps147:1); [ISA 43:20–43:21](https://ref.ly/Isa43:20-Isa43:21), [ISA 61:10–61:11](https://ref.ly/Isa61:10-Isa61:11); [JER 13:11](https://ref.ly/Jer13:11)

Praise as an act of witness
---------------------------

**[PSA 9:11](https://ref.ly/Ps9:11)** *See also* [2SA 22:50](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:50); [PSA 34:1–34:3](https://ref.ly/Ps34:1-Ps34:3); [ISA 42:12](https://ref.ly/Isa42:12); [2CO 9:13](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:13)

Praise in response to God's nature
----------------------------------

### For his greatness

**[DEU 32:3](https://ref.ly/Deut32:3)**; **[PSA 150:2](https://ref.ly/Ps150:2)** *See also* [1CH 16:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:25); [NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6); [PSA 104:1](https://ref.ly/Ps104:1); [MAT 9:8](https://ref.ly/Matt9:8)

### For his righteousness

**[PSA 98:8–98:9](https://ref.ly/Ps98:8-Ps98:9)** *See also* [DAN 4:37](https://ref.ly/Dan4:37)

### For his faithfulness

**[PSA 57:9–57:10](https://ref.ly/Ps57:9-Ps57:10), [PSA 138:2](https://ref.ly/Ps138:2)** *See also* [1KI 8:15–8:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:15-1Kgs8:20), [1KI 8:56](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:56); [PSA 89:1](https://ref.ly/Ps89:1)

### For his strength

**[PSA 59:16](https://ref.ly/Ps59:16)** *See also* [PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7), [PSA 81:1](https://ref.ly/Ps81:1)

Praise in response to God's deeds
---------------------------------

### For deliverance from enemies

**[EXO 18:10](https://ref.ly/Exod18:10)**; **[PSA 18:46–18:48](https://ref.ly/Ps18:46-Ps18:48)** *See also* [GEN 14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:20); [JDG 16:24](https://ref.ly/Judg16:24); [PSA 43:1–43:4](https://ref.ly/Ps43:1-Ps43:4), [PSA 124:1–124:7](https://ref.ly/Ps124:1-Ps124:7)

### For answered prayer

**[PSA 28:6](https://ref.ly/Ps28:6)** *See also* [PSA 66:19–66:20](https://ref.ly/Ps66:19-Ps66:20)

### For sending his Son, Jesus Christ

**[LUK 1:68–1:69](https://ref.ly/Luke1:68-Luke1:69)**; **[EPH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Eph1:3)** *See also* [LUK 2:10–2:14](https://ref.ly/Luke2:10-Luke2:14), [LUK 2:25–2:28](https://ref.ly/Luke2:25-Luke2:28), [LUK 24:53](https://ref.ly/Luke24:53); [1PE 1:3–1:6](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:3-1Pet1:6)

## 8666: praise, manner and methods of (id: 8666)

Praise is the natural response of believers to God at all times and in all places, involving adoration in music and song. God himself assists believers to praise him through his Spirit.

The manner of praise
--------------------

### God is praised in faith

**[PSA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Ps28:7), [PSA 106:12](https://ref.ly/Ps106:12)**

### God is praised through Jesus Christ

**[HEB 13:15](https://ref.ly/Heb13:15)** *See also* [PHP 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/Phil2:9-Phil2:11)

### God can be praised at any time and in any place

**[PSA 104:33](https://ref.ly/Ps104:33), [PSA 145:2](https://ref.ly/Ps145:2)** *See also* [PSA 146:2](https://ref.ly/Ps146:2)

### God helps believers to praise him

**[PSA 51:15](https://ref.ly/Ps51:15)** *See also* [PSA 40:3](https://ref.ly/Ps40:3); [ISA 61:3](https://ref.ly/Isa61:3)

### The Holy Spirit moves believers to praise

**[LUK 1:67](https://ref.ly/Luke1:67)**; **[EPH 5:18–5:20](https://ref.ly/Eph5:18-Eph5:20)** *See also* [LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Luke10:21); [ACT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Acts2:11), [ACT 10:44–10:46](https://ref.ly/Acts10:44-Acts10:46); [COL 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/Col3:16-Col3:17)

The methods of praise
---------------------

### In singing

**[EPH 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19)** *See also* [NEH 12:46](https://ref.ly/Neh12:46); [PSA 149:1](https://ref.ly/Ps149:1); [ISA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Isa42:10); [LUK 1:46–1:47](https://ref.ly/Luke1:46-Luke1:47), [LUK 1:68](https://ref.ly/Luke1:68); [ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25); [COL 3:16](https://ref.ly/Col3:16)

### With musical instruments

**[PSA 150:3–150:5](https://ref.ly/Ps150:3-Ps150:5)** *See also* [1CH 25:3](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:3); [2CH 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:6); [PSA 33:2](https://ref.ly/Ps33:2), [PSA 92:1–92:3](https://ref.ly/Ps92:1-Ps92:3); [ISA 38:20](https://ref.ly/Isa38:20)

### In dancing

**[PSA 149:3](https://ref.ly/Ps149:3)** *See also* [EXO 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Exod15:19-Exod15:20); [2SA 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:14); [PSA 150:4](https://ref.ly/Ps150:4)

### With thanksgiving

**[PSA 42:4](https://ref.ly/Ps42:4), [PSA 100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:4)** *See also* [2CH 5:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr5:13)

Hallelujah as a frequent expression of praise to God
----------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 106:48](https://ref.ly/Ps106:48)** *“Hallelujah” translates “praise the LORD”.* *See also* [PSA 111:1](https://ref.ly/Ps111:1), [PSA 113:1](https://ref.ly/Ps113:1), [PSA 135:1](https://ref.ly/Ps135:1), [PSA 149:1](https://ref.ly/Ps149:1), [PSA 150:1](https://ref.ly/Ps150:1); [REV 19:3–19:4](https://ref.ly/Rev19:3-Rev19:4)

### Praise for his patient love

**[PSA 106:1](https://ref.ly/Ps106:1)** *See also* [PSA 117:2](https://ref.ly/Ps117:2)

### Praise for his election of Israel

**[PSA 135:3–135:4](https://ref.ly/Ps135:3-Ps135:4)** *See also* [1CH 16:36](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:36); [PSA 148:14](https://ref.ly/Ps148:14)

### Praise for his sovereign rule

**[PSA 146:10](https://ref.ly/Ps146:10)**; **[REV 19:6](https://ref.ly/Rev19:6)** *See also* [REV 19:1](https://ref.ly/Rev19:1)

Hosanna as an acclamation of praise
-----------------------------------

### At Jerusalem

**[MAT 21:9](https://ref.ly/Matt21:9)** parallels [MRK 11:9–11:10](https://ref.ly/Mark11:9-Mark11:10), [JHN 12:13](https://ref.ly/John12:13) *“Hosanna” is the Greek form of the Hebrew term seeking God's salvation.* *See also* [MAT 21:5](https://ref.ly/Matt21:5)

### The OT background

**[PSA 118:25–118:26](https://ref.ly/Ps118:25-Ps118:26)** *See also* [1CH 16:35–16:36](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:35-1Chr16:36); [PSA 79:9](https://ref.ly/Ps79:9), [PSA 106:47](https://ref.ly/Ps106:47)

\`\`Selah'' in the context of praise
------------------------------------

### Following the assurance of answered prayer

**[PSA 3:4](https://ref.ly/Ps3:4)** *There are a number of possible ways of understanding this obscure term: pausing or lifting up, perhaps indicating that the singers or musicians should sing or play “forte”.* *See also* [PSA 21:2](https://ref.ly/Ps21:2), [PSA 24:6](https://ref.ly/Ps24:6), [PSA 32:5](https://ref.ly/Ps32:5), [PSA 81:7](https://ref.ly/Ps81:7), [PSA 84:8](https://ref.ly/Ps84:8)

### Following an expression of deliverance

**[PSA 3:8](https://ref.ly/Ps3:8)** *See also* [PSA 32:7](https://ref.ly/Ps32:7), [PSA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Ps49:15), [PSA 57:3](https://ref.ly/Ps57:3), [PSA 68:19](https://ref.ly/Ps68:19), [PSA 76:9](https://ref.ly/Ps76:9); [HAB 3:13](https://ref.ly/Hab3:13)

### After a statement comparing God's greatness with human insignificance

**[PSA 9:20](https://ref.ly/Ps9:20)** *See also* [PSA 39:5](https://ref.ly/Ps39:5), [PSA 39:11](https://ref.ly/Ps39:11), [PSA 47:4](https://ref.ly/Ps47:4), [PSA 52:5](https://ref.ly/Ps52:5), [PSA 55:19](https://ref.ly/Ps55:19), [PSA 59:5](https://ref.ly/Ps59:5), [PSA 67:4](https://ref.ly/Ps67:4), [PSA 75:3](https://ref.ly/Ps75:3), [PSA 89:37](https://ref.ly/Ps89:37); [HAB 3:3](https://ref.ly/Hab3:3)

### After an affirmation of security in God

**[PSA 46:7](https://ref.ly/Ps46:7)** *See also* [PSA 46:11](https://ref.ly/Ps46:11), [PSA 48:8](https://ref.ly/Ps48:8), [PSA 61:4](https://ref.ly/Ps61:4)

### On reflection of evil opposition

**[PSA 54:3](https://ref.ly/Ps54:3)** *See also* [PSA 62:4](https://ref.ly/Ps62:4), [PSA 140:3](https://ref.ly/Ps140:3), [PSA 140:5](https://ref.ly/Ps140:5)
