## 8667: praise, examples of (id: 8667)

Scripture provides many examples of individuals who praised God. These examples illustrate the variety of ways and methods in which God can be praised, as well as the motivation for that praise.

OT examples of those who praised God
------------------------------------

### Melchizedek

**[GEN 14:18–14:20](https://ref.ly/Gen14:18-Gen14:20)**

### Moses

**[EXO 15:1–15:2](https://ref.ly/Exod15:1-Exod15:2)**

### Jethro

**[EXO 18:9–18:10](https://ref.ly/Exod18:9-Exod18:10)**

### David

**[1CH 29:10–29:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:10-1Chr29:13)**

### Ezra

**[NEH 8:6](https://ref.ly/Neh8:6)**

NT examples of those who praised God
------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ

**[LUK 10:21](https://ref.ly/Luke10:21)**

### Zechariah

**[LUK 1:67–1:68](https://ref.ly/Luke1:67-Luke1:68)**

### The shepherds

**[LUK 2:20](https://ref.ly/Luke2:20)**

### Simeon

**[LUK 2:28](https://ref.ly/Luke2:28)**

### The disciples

**[LUK 19:37](https://ref.ly/Luke19:37)**; **[ACT 2:46–2:47](https://ref.ly/Acts2:46-Acts2:47)** *See also* [LUK 24:50–24:53](https://ref.ly/Luke24:50-Luke24:53); [ACT 16:25](https://ref.ly/Acts16:25)

### Those who were healed by Jesus Christ

[LUK 18:35–18:43](https://ref.ly/Luke18:35-Luke18:43); ; [ACT 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/Acts3:1-Acts3:10)

### The citizens of heaven

**[REV 15:2–15:3](https://ref.ly/Rev15:2-Rev15:3)**

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 14:18-20; Exodus 15:1-2; Exodus 18:9-10; 1 Chronicles 29:10-13; Nehemiah 8:6; Luke 10:21; Luke 1:67-68; Luke 2:20; Luke 2:28; Luke 19:37; Acts 2:46-47; Luke 24:50-53; Acts 16:25; Luke 18:35-43; Acts 3:1-10; Revelation 15:2-3
* **Associated Articles:** 2039: Jesus Christ, joy of (ID: `2039@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5088: David, character of (ID: `5088@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8609: prayer, as praise and thanksgiving (ID: `8609@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9412: heaven, worship and service in (ID: `9412@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8668: praise, and Holy Spirit (id: 8668)

See [3218: Holy Spirit, and praise](3218.md)



## 8670: remembering (id: 8670)

The process of recalling the past, especially the presence and activity of God in the history of his people. Remembering God's work in the past can lead to praise and rejoicing, and to hope for the future. God himself remembers.

Divine remembering
------------------

### God always remembers his covenant

**[1CH 16:15](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:15)** parallel [PSA 105:8](https://ref.ly/Ps105:8) *See also* [GEN 9:14–9:16](https://ref.ly/Gen9:14-Gen9:16); [EXO 2:23–2:25](https://ref.ly/Exod2:23-Exod2:25); [PSA 111:5](https://ref.ly/Ps111:5); [LUK 1:69–1:73](https://ref.ly/Luke1:69-Luke1:73)

### God always remembers his promises

**[PSA 105:42](https://ref.ly/Ps105:42)** *See also* [DEU 6:20–6:23](https://ref.ly/Deut6:20-Deut6:23); [JOS 1:3](https://ref.ly/Josh1:3); [2PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:9)

### God always remembers his people

**[ISA 49:15–49:16](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15-Isa49:16)** *See also* [GEN 7:24–8:1](https://ref.ly/Gen7:24-Gen8:1), [GEN 19:29](https://ref.ly/Gen19:29), [GEN 30:22](https://ref.ly/Gen30:22); [1SA 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:19-1Sam1:20); [LUK 1:54–1:55](https://ref.ly/Luke1:54-Luke1:55)

### Situations in which God will not remember

**[HEB 8:10–8:12](https://ref.ly/Heb8:10-Heb8:12)** *See also* [JER 31:33–31:34](https://ref.ly/Jer31:33-Jer31:34); [ISA 65:16–65:17](https://ref.ly/Isa65:16-Isa65:17); [EZK 18:21–18:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:21-Ezek18:24)

The Holy Spirit helps believers to remember Jesus Christ and his words
----------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26)** *See also* [JHN 2:22](https://ref.ly/John2:22), [JHN 12:16](https://ref.ly/John12:16), [JHN 16:4](https://ref.ly/John16:4)

Human remembering
-----------------

### Believers are to remember God's person

**[DEU 8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:18)**; **[PSA 119:55](https://ref.ly/Ps119:55)** *See also* [PSA 42:5–42:6](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5-Ps42:6); [JER 51:50](https://ref.ly/Jer51:50); [JON 2:7](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:7)

### Believers are to remember God's acts in the past

**[ISA 46:9](https://ref.ly/Isa46:9)** *See also* [DEU 5:15](https://ref.ly/Deut5:15), [DEU 7:17–7:18](https://ref.ly/Deut7:17-Deut7:18), [DEU 8:10–8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:10-Deut8:18); [1CH 16:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:12); [MAT 16:9–16:10](https://ref.ly/Matt16:9-Matt16:10)

### Believers are to remember God's wrath

**[DEU 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Deut9:7-Deut9:8)** *See also* [2PE 3:1–3:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:1-2Pet3:10)

### Believers are to remember God's commands

**[NUM 15:38–15:40](https://ref.ly/Num15:38-Num15:40)** *See also* [DEU 8:1–8:2](https://ref.ly/Deut8:1-Deut8:2); [PSA 103:17–103:18](https://ref.ly/Ps103:17-Ps103:18)

### Believers are to remember their responsibilities towards one another

**[ACT 20:35](https://ref.ly/Acts20:35)** *See also* [GAL 2:10](https://ref.ly/Gal2:10); [HEB 13:2–13:3](https://ref.ly/Heb13:2-Heb13:3)

### Believers are to remember the death of Jesus Christ

**[1CO 11:24–11:28](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:24-1Cor11:28)**; **[LUK 22:17–22:20](https://ref.ly/Luke22:17-Luke22:20)**

The importance of remembering
-----------------------------

### Remembering should lead to rejoicing

**[PSA 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Ps13:5-Ps13:6)** *See also* [DEU 26:4–26:11](https://ref.ly/Deut26:4-Deut26:11); [PSA 66:5–66:6](https://ref.ly/Ps66:5-Ps66:6)

### Remembering should lead to repentance

**[REV 3:3](https://ref.ly/Rev3:3)** *See also* [EZK 16:59–16:63](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:59-Ezek16:63); [MAT 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23-Matt5:24), [MAT 26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:75) parallels [MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:72), [LUK 22:61–22:62](https://ref.ly/Luke22:61-Luke22:62); [REV 2:5](https://ref.ly/Rev2:5)

### Remembering should lead to understanding

**[LUK 24:6–24:8](https://ref.ly/Luke24:6-Luke24:8)** *See also* [JHN 15:20](https://ref.ly/John15:20), [JHN 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/John16:1-John16:4); [ACT 11:15–11:18](https://ref.ly/Acts11:15-Acts11:18)

### Remembering should produce hope for the future

**[LAM 3:21–3:24](https://ref.ly/Lam3:21-Lam3:24)** *See also* [PSA 42:4–42:11](https://ref.ly/Ps42:4-Ps42:11), [PSA 103:1–103:2](https://ref.ly/Ps103:1-Ps103:2); [HEB 10:32–10:36](https://ref.ly/Heb10:32-Heb10:36)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 16:15; Psalms 105:8; Genesis 9:14-16; Exodus 2:23-25; Psalms 111:5; Luke 1:69-73; Psalms 105:42; Deuteronomy 6:20-23; Joshua 1:3; 2 Peter 3:9; Isaiah 49:15-16; Genesis 7:24-8:1; Genesis 19:29; Genesis 30:22; 1 Samuel 1:19-20; Luke 1:54-55; Hebrews 8:10-12; Jeremiah 31:33-34; Isaiah 65:16-17; Ezekiel 18:21-24; John 14:26; John 2:22; John 12:16; John 16:4; Deuteronomy 8:18; Psalms 119:55; Psalms 42:5-6; Jeremiah 51:50; Jonah 2:7; Isaiah 46:9; Deuteronomy 5:15; Deuteronomy 7:17-18; Deuteronomy 8:10-18; 1 Chronicles 16:12; Matthew 16:9-10; Deuteronomy 9:7-8; 2 Peter 3:1-10; Numbers 15:38-40; Deuteronomy 8:1-2; Psalms 103:17-18; Acts 20:35; Galatians 2:10; Hebrews 13:2-3; 1 Corinthians 11:24-28; Luke 22:17-20; Psalms 13:5-6; Deuteronomy 26:4-11; Psalms 66:5-6; Revelation 3:3; Ezekiel 16:59-63; Matthew 5:23-24; Matthew 26:75; Mark 14:72; Luke 22:61-62; Revelation 2:5; Luke 24:6-8; John 15:20; John 16:1-4; Acts 11:15-18; Lamentations 3:21-24; Psalms 42:4-11; Psalms 103:1-2; Hebrews 10:32-36
* **Associated Articles:** 1035: God, faithfulness of (ID: `1035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1345: covenant (ID: `1345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3140: Holy Spirit, as teacher (ID: `3140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4945: history (ID: `4945@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4963: past, the (ID: `4963@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6732: repentance (ID: `6732@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7355: feasts and festivals, nature of (ID: `7355@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7394: memorial (ID: `7394@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8355: understanding (ID: `8355@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8467: reminders (ID: `8467@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8644: commemoration (ID: `8644@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8763: forgetting (ID: `8763@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9610: hope (ID: `9610@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8672: striving with God (id: 8672)

Striving with God can refer both to the fruitless opposition of the ungodly to God's will and purposes, and to the struggle of the righteous with God, as they seek to find his will and obey it.

The striving of the wicked with God
-----------------------------------

### It is the result of a guilty conscience

**[ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/Acts26:14)**

### Striving against God cannot succeed

**[2CH 13:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:12)** *See also* [PRO 21:30](https://ref.ly/Prov21:30); [ISA 8:10](https://ref.ly/Isa8:10); [ACT 11:17](https://ref.ly/Acts11:17)

### It leads to the destruction of those who attempt it

**[PSA 2:2–2:5](https://ref.ly/Ps2:2-Ps2:5)** *See also* [JOB 15:20–15:26](https://ref.ly/Job15:20-Job15:26), [JOB 15:30](https://ref.ly/Job15:30)

Ways in which the wicked strive with God
----------------------------------------

### By resisting the Holy Spirit

**[ACT 7:51](https://ref.ly/Acts7:51)**

### By arguing with God

**[ROM 9:20](https://ref.ly/Rom9:20)** *See also* [JOB 40:2](https://ref.ly/Job40:2); [ISA 45:9–45:11](https://ref.ly/Isa45:9-Isa45:11)

### Through unbelief

[NUM 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Num14:1-Num14:4), [NUM 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Num14:10-Num14:11), [NUM 20:13](https://ref.ly/Num20:13); ; [PSA 95:8–95:9](https://ref.ly/Ps95:8-Ps95:9)

### By attacking God's people

**[ACT 5:39](https://ref.ly/Acts5:39)** *To attack God's people is to attempt to attack God himself.* *See also* [1SA 17:45](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:45); [ISA 37:22–37:25](https://ref.ly/Isa37:22-Isa37:25); [ACT 9:4–9:5](https://ref.ly/Acts9:4-Acts9:5)

Jacob strives with God
----------------------

**[GEN 32:24–32:28](https://ref.ly/Gen32:24-Gen32:28)** *Jacob's struggle with God represents the conflict within himself between self\-will and dependence on God. As “Jacob”, the supplanter, he had overcome Esau and Laban, but God demonstrates his superior power in the wrestling match so that as “Israel” he will be submissive to the divine will.* *See also* [HOS 12:2–12:5](https://ref.ly/Hos12:2-Hos12:5)

The striving of the righteous with God
--------------------------------------

### The struggle for righteousness

**[ACT 24:16](https://ref.ly/Acts24:16)** *See also* [LUK 13:24](https://ref.ly/Luke13:24); [ROM 7:14–7:25](https://ref.ly/Rom7:14-Rom7:25); [1CO 9:25–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:25-1Cor9:27); [PHP 3:10–3:14](https://ref.ly/Phil3:10-Phil3:14); [1TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:10); [HEB 12:4](https://ref.ly/Heb12:4)

### The discipline of prayer

**[COL 4:12](https://ref.ly/Col4:12)** *See also* [ISA 64:7](https://ref.ly/Isa64:7); [LUK 22:44](https://ref.ly/Luke22:44)

### The struggle to understand God's purposes

**[EXO 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Exod5:22-Exod5:23)** *See also* [JOB 10:18](https://ref.ly/Job10:18); [PSA 73:16](https://ref.ly/Ps73:16), [PSA 88:14–88:18](https://ref.ly/Ps88:14-Ps88:18); [HAB 1:2–1:4](https://ref.ly/Hab1:2-Hab1:4), [HAB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hab1:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 26:14; 2 Chronicles 13:12; Proverbs 21:30; Isaiah 8:10; Acts 11:17; Psalms 2:2-5; Job 15:20-26; Job 15:30; Acts 7:51; Romans 9:20; Job 40:2; Isaiah 45:9-11; Numbers 14:1-4; Numbers 14:10-11; Numbers 20:13; Psalms 95:8-9; Acts 5:39; 1 Samuel 17:45; Isaiah 37:22-25; Acts 9:4-5; Genesis 32:24-28; Hosea 12:2-5; Acts 24:16; Luke 13:24; Romans 7:14-25; 1 Corinthians 9:25-27; Philippians 3:10-14; 1 Timothy 4:10; Hebrews 12:4; Colossians 4:12; Isaiah 64:7; Luke 22:44; Exodus 5:22-23; Job 10:18; Psalms 73:16; Psalms 88:14-18; Habakkuk 1:2-4; Habakkuk 1:13
* **Associated Articles:** 1130: God, sovereignty of (ID: `1130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5094: Jacob (ID: `5094@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5829: defiance (ID: `5829@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5931: resistance (ID: `5931@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6166: flesh, sinful nature (ID: `6166@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8355: understanding (ID: `8355@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8476: self-discipline (ID: `8476@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8482: spiritual warfare (ID: `8482@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8613: prayer, persistence in (ID: `8613@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8653: importunity, towards God (ID: `8653@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8785: opposition (ID: `8785@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8823: self-righteousness (ID: `8823@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8674: study (id: 8674)

The application of the mind with a view to acquiring knowledge, particularly of the Scriptures. God promises his help to those who give themselves to this work.

The study of the works of the LORD
----------------------------------

**[PSA 111:2](https://ref.ly/Ps111:2)** *The works mentioned are his acts of providence and grace.*

God's people are to study in order to gain wisdom
-------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 2:1–2:5](https://ref.ly/Prov2:1-Prov2:5), [PRO 4:5](https://ref.ly/Prov4:5)** *See also* [JOB 28:12](https://ref.ly/Job28:12), [JOB 28:20](https://ref.ly/Job28:20); [PSA 90:12](https://ref.ly/Ps90:12); [PRO 3:13](https://ref.ly/Prov3:13), [PRO 15:14](https://ref.ly/Prov15:14), [PRO 23:23](https://ref.ly/Prov23:23)

The study of human activity, achievement and wisdom
---------------------------------------------------

**[ECC 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:13-Eccl1:14)** *See also* [ECC 1:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:17), [ECC 2:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:12), [ECC 7:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:25), [ECC 8:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:9), [ECC 8:16](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:16), [ECC 9:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:1), [ECC 12:9–12:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:9-Eccl12:11) *The Teacher turned from human wisdom to searching out and setting forth proverbs*; [ECC 12:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:12) *He concluded that all human study was wearisome.*

The study of the word of God
----------------------------

### The study of the word of God is commended

**[2TI 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:15)** *See also* [ISA 8:20](https://ref.ly/Isa8:20); [JER 15:16](https://ref.ly/Jer15:16); [MAT 22:29](https://ref.ly/Matt22:29); [ROM 15:4](https://ref.ly/Rom15:4); [1PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:2)

### Ezra studied the law of the LORD

[EZR 7:10](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:10)

### The Bereans studied the Scriptures

[ACT 17:11](https://ref.ly/Acts17:11)

### Some Jews studied the Scriptures without finding Jesus Christ

[JHN 5:39](https://ref.ly/John5:39)

God promises his help in the study of the Scriptures
----------------------------------------------------

[JHN 14:26](https://ref.ly/John14:26), [JHN 16:13](https://ref.ly/John16:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 111:2; Proverbs 2:1-5; Proverbs 4:5; Job 28:12; Job 28:20; Psalms 90:12; Proverbs 3:13; Proverbs 15:14; Proverbs 23:23; Ecclesiastes 1:13-14; Ecclesiastes 1:17; Ecclesiastes 2:12; Ecclesiastes 7:25; Ecclesiastes 8:9; Ecclesiastes 8:16; Ecclesiastes 9:1; Ecclesiastes 12:9-11; Ecclesiastes 12:12; 2 Timothy 2:15; Isaiah 8:20; Jeremiah 15:16; Matthew 22:29; Romans 15:4; 1 Peter 2:2; Ezra 7:10; Acts 17:11; John 5:39; John 14:26; John 16:13
* **Associated Articles:** 1610: Scripture (ID: `1610@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3140: Holy Spirit, as teacher (ID: `3140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5026: knowledge (ID: `5026@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5035: mind (ID: `5035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5302: education (ID: `5302@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5514: scribes (ID: `5514@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5894: intelligence (ID: `5894@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7793: teachers (ID: `7793@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7797: teaching (ID: `7797@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8476: self-discipline (ID: `8476@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8662: meditation (ID: `8662@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8676: thanksgiving (id: 8676)

The offering of thanks, especially for gifts received. Scripture emphasises the importance of giving thanks to God for all his gifts and works, both as an expression of our dependence upon him and gratitude to him.

Thanksgiving is sometimes linked with praise to God
---------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 69:30](https://ref.ly/Ps69:30)** *See also* [1CH 16:4](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:4); [PSA 42:4](https://ref.ly/Ps42:4), [PSA 100:4](https://ref.ly/Ps100:4)

The duty of thanksgiving to God
-------------------------------

**[1TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:3-1Tim4:4)** *See also* [PSA 107:8–107:9](https://ref.ly/Ps107:8-Ps107:9), [PSA 147:7–147:9](https://ref.ly/Ps147:7-Ps147:9); [LUK 17:17–17:18](https://ref.ly/Luke17:17-Luke17:18); [ROM 1:20–1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:20-Rom1:21), [ROM 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rom14:6)

The expression of thanksgiving to God
-------------------------------------

### Through OT offerings

**[PSA 50:14](https://ref.ly/Ps50:14)** *See also* [LEV 7:12–7:15](https://ref.ly/Lev7:12-Lev7:15); [JON 2:9](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:9)

### In Christian gatherings

**[1CO 10:16](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:16)** *See also* [1CO 10:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:30), [1CO 14:16–14:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:16-1Cor14:17)

### Through song

**[PSA 95:2](https://ref.ly/Ps95:2)** *See also* [1CH 16:7–16:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:7-1Chr16:9); [EZR 3:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra3:11); [NEH 12:8](https://ref.ly/Neh12:8), [NEH 12:24](https://ref.ly/Neh12:24), [NEH 12:27](https://ref.ly/Neh12:27), [NEH 12:46](https://ref.ly/Neh12:46); [PSA 147:7](https://ref.ly/Ps147:7); [ISA 51:3](https://ref.ly/Isa51:3); [JER 30:19](https://ref.ly/Jer30:19); [EPH 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Eph5:19-Eph5:20)

### Through prayer

**[1TI 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:1)** *See also* [NEH 11:17](https://ref.ly/Neh11:17); [DAN 6:10](https://ref.ly/Dan6:10)

### Through giving

**[2CO 9:11–9:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:11-2Cor9:12)**

### In conversation

*See also* [EPH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:4)

Believers are to give thanks in all circumstances
-------------------------------------------------

**[1TH 5:18](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:18)** *See also* [PHP 4:6](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6); [COL 3:17](https://ref.ly/Col3:17)

Thanksgiving for the blessings of salvation
-------------------------------------------

**[1CO 15:57](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:57)** *See also* [ROM 7:24–7:25](https://ref.ly/Rom7:24-Rom7:25); [2CO 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:14) *for victory through Christ*; [2CO 4:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:15) *for God's grace*; [2CO 9:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:15) *for Jesus Christ himself*; [COL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Col1:12), [COL 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Col2:6-Col2:7) *for union with Christ*

Examples of those who gave thanks to God
----------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ

[MAT 14:19](https://ref.ly/Matt14:19) parallel [LUK 9:16](https://ref.ly/Luke9:16); ; [MRK 8:6](https://ref.ly/Mark8:6); ; [LUK 24:30](https://ref.ly/Luke24:30); ; [1CO 11:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:24)

### Paul

[ACT 28:15](https://ref.ly/Acts28:15); ; [ROM 6:17](https://ref.ly/Rom6:17); ; [EPH 1:16](https://ref.ly/Eph1:16); ; [1TI 1:12](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:12)

### Heavenly beings

[REV 4:9](https://ref.ly/Rev4:9), [REV 7:12](https://ref.ly/Rev7:12), [REV 11:17](https://ref.ly/Rev11:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 69:30; 1 Chronicles 16:4; Psalms 42:4; Psalms 100:4; 1 Timothy 4:3-4; Psalms 107:8-9; Psalms 147:7-9; Luke 17:17-18; Romans 1:20-21; Romans 14:6; Psalms 50:14; Leviticus 7:12-15; Jonah 2:9; 1 Corinthians 10:16; 1 Corinthians 10:30; 1 Corinthians 14:16-17; Psalms 95:2; 1 Chronicles 16:7-9; Ezra 3:11; Nehemiah 12:8; Nehemiah 12:24; Nehemiah 12:27; Nehemiah 12:46; Psalms 147:7; Isaiah 51:3; Jeremiah 30:19; Ephesians 5:19-20; 1 Timothy 2:1; Nehemiah 11:17; Daniel 6:10; 2 Corinthians 9:11-12; Ephesians 5:4; 1 Thessalonians 5:18; Philippians 4:6; Colossians 3:17; 1 Corinthians 15:57; Romans 7:24-25; 2 Corinthians 2:14; 2 Corinthians 4:15; 2 Corinthians 9:15; Colossians 1:12; Colossians 2:6-7; Matthew 14:19; Luke 9:16; Mark 8:6; Luke 24:30; 1 Corinthians 11:24; Acts 28:15; Romans 6:17; Ephesians 1:16; 1 Timothy 1:12; Revelation 4:9; Revelation 7:12; Revelation 11:17

## 8678: waiting on God (id: 8678)

Being prepared to patiently look towards God for his guidance and accepting the timing he proposes. Waiting on God can lead to an atmosphere of expectation and confidence in God and a realisation of the unreliability of one's own judgment.

Waiting on God is something that is commanded
---------------------------------------------

**[HOS 12:6](https://ref.ly/Hos12:6)** *See also* [PSA 27:14](https://ref.ly/Ps27:14), [PSA 37:7](https://ref.ly/Ps37:7), [PSA 37:34](https://ref.ly/Ps37:34), [PSA 123:2](https://ref.ly/Ps123:2); [ISA 30:18](https://ref.ly/Isa30:18)

Reasons for waiting on God
--------------------------

[JER 14:22](https://ref.ly/Jer14:22) *for his providence*; ; [PSA 25:5](https://ref.ly/Ps25:5) *for his salvation*; ; [PSA 145:15–145:16](https://ref.ly/Ps145:15-Ps145:16) *for his blessings*

Waiting on Christ
-----------------

**[JAS 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/Jas5:7-Jas5:8)** *“Waiting on Christ” is often seen in terms of waiting patiently for God to bring about the end of all things through the promised second coming of Jesus Christ.* *See also* [LUK 12:35–12:40](https://ref.ly/Luke12:35-Luke12:40); [1CO 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:7), [1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5); [TIT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Titus2:13); [HEB 9:28](https://ref.ly/Heb9:28); [JUD 1:21](https://ref.ly/Jude1:21)

The benefits of waiting on God
------------------------------

### It leads to expectancy

**[PSA 5:3](https://ref.ly/Ps5:3)** *See also* [ROM 8:23](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23); [1CO 4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:5); [GAL 5:5](https://ref.ly/Gal5:5); [TIT 2:13](https://ref.ly/Titus2:13)

### It leads to hope

**[PSA 33:20](https://ref.ly/Ps33:20)** *See also* [PSA 130:5](https://ref.ly/Ps130:5); [ISA 51:5](https://ref.ly/Isa51:5); [MIC 7:7](https://ref.ly/Mic7:7)

### It leads to trust

**[ISA 8:17](https://ref.ly/Isa8:17)**

### It leads to patience

**[ROM 8:23–8:25](https://ref.ly/Rom8:23-Rom8:25)** *See also* [LAM 3:24–3:26](https://ref.ly/Lam3:24-Lam3:26); [REV 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/Rev6:9-Rev6:11)

Examples of people who waited on God
------------------------------------

[GEN 49:18](https://ref.ly/Gen49:18) *Jacob*; ; [PSA 39:7](https://ref.ly/Ps39:7) *David*; ; [ISA 8:17](https://ref.ly/Isa8:17) *Isaiah*; ; [MIC 7:7](https://ref.ly/Mic7:7) *Micah*; ; [MRK 15:43](https://ref.ly/Mark15:43) *Joseph of Arimathea*; ; [LUK 2:25](https://ref.ly/Luke2:25) *Simeon*

* **Associated Passages:** Hosea 12:6; Psalms 27:14; Psalms 37:7; Psalms 37:34; Psalms 123:2; Isaiah 30:18; Jeremiah 14:22; Psalms 25:5; Psalms 145:15-16; James 5:7-8; Luke 12:35-40; 1 Corinthians 1:7; 1 Corinthians 4:5; Titus 2:13; Hebrews 9:28; Jude 1:21; Psalms 5:3; Romans 8:23; Galatians 5:5; Psalms 33:20; Psalms 130:5; Isaiah 51:5; Micah 7:7; Isaiah 8:17; Romans 8:23-25; Lamentations 3:24-26; Revelation 6:9-11; Genesis 49:18; Psalms 39:7; Mark 15:43; Luke 2:25
* **Associated Articles:** 4925: delay, divine (ID: `4925@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5767: attitudes, in prayer (ID: `5767@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5977: waiting (ID: `5977@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8030: trust (ID: `8030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8213: confidence (ID: `8213@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8318: patience (ID: `8318@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8329: readiness (ID: `8329@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8331: reliability (ID: `8331@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8613: prayer, persistence in (ID: `8613@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8618: prayerfulness (ID: `8618@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8640: calling upon God (ID: `8640@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9610: hope (ID: `9610@DictionaryBibleThemes`)

## 8701: affluence (id: 8701)

Literally, “flowing” with prosperity. Wealth is not condemned in Scripture but is seen as a good gift of God. However, affluence has its dangers.

Godly affluence
---------------

**[EXO 3:8](https://ref.ly/Exod3:8)** *See also* [DEU 28:1–28:6](https://ref.ly/Deut28:1-Deut28:6), [DEU 28:11](https://ref.ly/Deut28:11); [PSA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Ps1:3), [PSA 112:3](https://ref.ly/Ps112:3); [PRO 8:18–8:21](https://ref.ly/Prov8:18-Prov8:21), [PRO 22:4](https://ref.ly/Prov22:4); [ISA 60:5](https://ref.ly/Isa60:5), [ISA 61:6](https://ref.ly/Isa61:6), [ISA 66:12](https://ref.ly/Isa66:12); [MIC 4:4](https://ref.ly/Mic4:4)

### Examples of affluence

[GEN 2:8–2:12](https://ref.ly/Gen2:8-Gen2:12) *Eden*; ; [GEN 13:2](https://ref.ly/Gen13:2) *Abraham*; ; [GEN 26:12–26:13](https://ref.ly/Gen26:12-Gen26:13) *Isaac*; ; [GEN 33:11](https://ref.ly/Gen33:11) *Jacob*; ; [JOB 42:10–42:12](https://ref.ly/Job42:10-Job42:12) *Job*; [1KI 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs3:13), [1KI 10:23–10:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:23-1Kgs10:29); [2CH 32:27–32:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27-2Chr32:29) *Hezekiah*; ; [MAT 27:57](https://ref.ly/Matt27:57) *Joseph of Arimathea*

The dangers of affluence
------------------------

### Pride

**[DEU 8:17–8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:17-Deut8:18)**; **[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)** *See also* [PSA 10:4–10:6](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4-Ps10:6), [PSA 73:3–73:6](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3-Ps73:6)

### Reliance upon riches

**[PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28)**; **[1TI 6:17](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17)** *See also* [PSA 62:10](https://ref.ly/Ps62:10); [PRO 23:5](https://ref.ly/Prov23:5); [ECC 10:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:19); [MRK 10:24](https://ref.ly/Mark10:24); [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21)

### Over\-attachment to riches

**[MAT 19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:22)** parallels [MRK 10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:22), [LUK 18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:23)

### Greed for more riches

**[ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10)**

### Temptation

**[1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10)**

### Cares and anxieties

**[MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22)** parallels [MRK 4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14)

### Spiritual complacency

**[REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)**

### Contempt for God

*See also* [PRO 30:8–30:9](https://ref.ly/Prov30:8-Prov30:9)

### Contempt for the poor or unfortunate

[JOB 12:5](https://ref.ly/Job12:5); ; [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16); ; [AMO 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:8), [AMO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Amos4:1), [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11); ; [LUK 16:24–16:31](https://ref.ly/Luke16:24-Luke16:31); ; [JAS 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:6)

Affluence is temporary
----------------------

### It is for this life only

**[1TI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7)** *See also* [JOB 1:21](https://ref.ly/Job1:21); [PSA 37:35–37:36](https://ref.ly/Ps37:35-Ps37:36), [PSA 49:10–49:12](https://ref.ly/Ps49:10-Ps49:12), [PSA 49:16–49:20](https://ref.ly/Ps49:16-Ps49:20); [ECC 5:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:15)

### It is often lost

[JOB 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Job1:13-Job1:15); ; [ECC 5:13–5:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:13-Eccl5:14)

Spiritual affluence
-------------------

### The riches of God

**[PHP 4:19](https://ref.ly/Phil4:19)** *See also* [PSA 36:7–36:9](https://ref.ly/Ps36:7-Ps36:9); [2CO 9:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor9:9); [EPH 3:8](https://ref.ly/Eph3:8)

### Spiritual wealth

[EPH 1:3](https://ref.ly/Eph1:3); ; [1PE 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:4); ; [REV 2:9](https://ref.ly/Rev2:9)

### The shared wealth of the church

[MRK 10:29–10:30](https://ref.ly/Mark10:29-Mark10:30); ; [ACT 4:34–4:35](https://ref.ly/Acts4:34-Acts4:35); ; [PHP 4:18](https://ref.ly/Phil4:18)

### The wealth of heaven

[MAT 6:20](https://ref.ly/Matt6:20); ; [LUK 12:33](https://ref.ly/Luke12:33); ; [REV 7:16–7:17](https://ref.ly/Rev7:16-Rev7:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 3:8; Deuteronomy 28:1-6; Deuteronomy 28:11; Psalms 1:3; Psalms 112:3; Proverbs 8:18-21; Proverbs 22:4; Isaiah 60:5; Isaiah 61:6; Isaiah 66:12; Micah 4:4; Genesis 2:8-12; Genesis 13:2; Genesis 26:12-13; Genesis 33:11; Job 42:10-12; 1 Kings 3:13; 1 Kings 10:23-29; 2 Chronicles 32:27-29; Matthew 27:57; Deuteronomy 8:17-18; 1 Timothy 6:17; Psalms 10:4-6; Psalms 73:3-6; Proverbs 11:28; Psalms 62:10; Proverbs 23:5; Ecclesiastes 10:19; Mark 10:24; Luke 12:16-21; Matthew 19:22; Mark 10:22; Luke 18:23; Ecclesiastes 5:10; 1 Timothy 6:9-10; Matthew 13:22; Mark 4:19; Luke 8:14; Revelation 3:17; Proverbs 30:8-9; Job 12:5; Proverbs 22:16; Amos 2:6-8; Amos 4:1; Amos 5:11; Luke 16:24-31; James 5:1-6; 1 Timothy 6:7; Job 1:21; Psalms 37:35-36; Psalms 49:10-12; Psalms 49:16-20; Ecclesiastes 5:15; Job 1:13-15; Ecclesiastes 5:13-14; Philippians 4:19; Psalms 36:7-9; 2 Corinthians 9:9; Ephesians 3:8; Ephesians 1:3; 1 Peter 1:4; Revelation 2:9; Mark 10:29-30; Acts 4:34-35; Philippians 4:18; Matthew 6:20; Luke 12:33; Revelation 7:16-17

## 8702: agnosticism (id: 8702)

The inability to decide whether or not God exists. It is characterised by indecision and compromise when faced with the challenge to wholehearted commitment, and is condemned as sinful, inexcusable and unnecessary in the light of God's clear self\-revelation.

Agnosticism is inexcusable in the light of God's self\-revelation
-----------------------------------------------------------------

### In the creation

**[ROM 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:19-Rom1:20)** *See also* [PSA 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/Ps19:1-Ps19:2); [ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17)

### In the person of Jesus Christ

**[JHN 1:18](https://ref.ly/John1:18)** *See also* [JHN 14:9](https://ref.ly/John14:9); [HEB 1:1–1:3](https://ref.ly/Heb1:1-Heb1:3)

Agnosticism is sinful
---------------------

### Because God calls for wholehearted commitment

**[JOS 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Josh24:14-Josh24:15)** *See also* [EXO 32:26](https://ref.ly/Exod32:26); [DEU 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Deut6:13-Deut6:14), [DEU 30:15–30:19](https://ref.ly/Deut30:15-Deut30:19); [ACT 14:15](https://ref.ly/Acts14:15)

### Because God's call demands total obedience

**[MAT 9:9](https://ref.ly/Matt9:9)** parallels [MRK 2:14](https://ref.ly/Mark2:14), [LUK 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Luke5:27-Luke5:28) *See also* [MAT 4:18–4:22](https://ref.ly/Matt4:18-Matt4:22) parallel [MRK 1:16–1:20](https://ref.ly/Mark1:16-Mark1:20)

Agnosticism results in compromise
---------------------------------

### In worship

**[1KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:21)**; **[2KI 17:41](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:41)** *See also* [2KI 17:29–17:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:29-2Kgs17:33); [1CO 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:21)

### In commitment

[MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24) parallel [LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13) *in the attitude to money*; ; [MAT 8:19–8:22](https://ref.ly/Matt8:19-Matt8:22) parallel [LUK 9:57–9:62](https://ref.ly/Luke9:57-Luke9:62) *in service*; ; [2CO 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:14) *in relationships with unbelievers*; ; [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14) *in accommodating false teaching*

Agnosticism as a result of ignorance
------------------------------------

### Ignorance through being uninformed

**[ACT 17:23–17:30](https://ref.ly/Acts17:23-Acts17:30)** *See also* [ACT 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Acts3:17-Acts3:18)

### Foolish ignorance of God's existence

**[PSA 14:1](https://ref.ly/Ps14:1)** parallel [PSA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Ps53:1) *See also* [PSA 92:5–92:7](https://ref.ly/Ps92:5-Ps92:7); [JER 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jer5:4)

### Ignorance of God's presence

**[PSA 115:2](https://ref.ly/Ps115:2)** *See also* [PSA 42:3](https://ref.ly/Ps42:3); [JOL 2:17](https://ref.ly/Joel2:17); [MIC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Mic7:10)

### Ignorance as a result of rejecting God's truth

**[PSA 10:4](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4)** *See also* [PSA 36:1](https://ref.ly/Ps36:1); [JER 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:21-Jer5:24); [ZEC 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Zech7:11-Zech7:12); [ACT 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/Acts2:22-Acts2:23)

### Ignorance as a result of sin

**[EPH 4:17–4:19](https://ref.ly/Eph4:17-Eph4:19)** *See also* [ISA 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Isa1:3-Isa1:4); [JER 4:22](https://ref.ly/Jer4:22)

### Ignorance is no defence against God's judgment

**[ROM 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rom1:18)** *See also* [JDG 2:10–2:14](https://ref.ly/Judg2:10-Judg2:14); [ISA 44:9–44:11](https://ref.ly/Isa44:9-Isa44:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 1:19-20; Psalms 19:1-2; Acts 14:17; John 1:18; John 14:9; Hebrews 1:1-3; Joshua 24:14-15; Exodus 32:26; Deuteronomy 6:13-14; Deuteronomy 30:15-19; Acts 14:15; Matthew 9:9; Mark 2:14; Luke 5:27-28; Matthew 4:18-22; Mark 1:16-20; 1 Kings 18:21; 2 Kings 17:41; 2 Kings 17:29-33; 1 Corinthians 10:21; Matthew 6:24; Luke 16:13; Matthew 8:19-22; Luke 9:57-62; 2 Corinthians 6:14; Ephesians 4:14; Acts 17:23-30; Acts 3:17-18; Psalms 14:1; Psalms 53:1; Psalms 92:5-7; Jeremiah 5:4; Psalms 115:2; Psalms 42:3; Joel 2:17; Micah 7:10; Psalms 10:4; Psalms 36:1; Jeremiah 5:21-24; Zechariah 7:11-12; Acts 2:22-23; Ephesians 4:17-19; Isaiah 1:3-4; Jeremiah 4:22; Romans 1:18; Judges 2:10-14; Isaiah 44:9-11
* **Associated Articles:** 1439: revelation (ID: `1439@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2426: gospel, responses to (ID: `2426@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3284: Holy Spirit, resisting (ID: `3284@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5811: compromise (ID: `5811@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5884: indecision (ID: `5884@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6181: ignorance (ID: `6181@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6257: unbelievers (ID: `6257@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6628: conversion, God's demands for (ID: `6628@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8023: faith, necessity of (ID: `8023@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8710: atheism (ID: `8710@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8721: doubt (ID: `8721@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8834: unbelief (ID: `8834@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8703: antinomianism (id: 8703)

A form of spiritual anarchy, which rejects the law as having any place in the Christian life, whether as instructor or as assessor. Paul's teaching that Christians are free from the law is sometimes misunderstood as antinomian. But Paul reveres God's law, and teaches believers, who are free from the law as a system of salvation, to keep it out of gratitude for salvation freely given, and because holiness as defined by the law is the Christian calling.

Antinomianism as a rejection of God's laws
------------------------------------------

**[JER 2:20](https://ref.ly/Jer2:20)** *See also* [PRO 29:18](https://ref.ly/Prov29:18); [JER 5:5](https://ref.ly/Jer5:5); [HOS 8:12](https://ref.ly/Hos8:12); [2PE 2:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:10)

Various people described as antinomian
--------------------------------------

### Israel described as antinomian

**[EZK 20:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:13)** *See also* [EXO 32:25](https://ref.ly/Exod32:25); [JER 2:23–2:25](https://ref.ly/Jer2:23-Jer2:25); [HOS 8:1](https://ref.ly/Hos8:1); [ZEC 7:11–7:12](https://ref.ly/Zech7:11-Zech7:12)

### The ungodly described as antinomian

**[JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)** *See also* [2TI 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:5); [2PE 2:13–2:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:13-2Pet2:15); [JUD 1:8](https://ref.ly/Jude1:8)

### The antichrist described as antinomian

**[2TH 2:3–2:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:3-2Thess2:10)** *See also* [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25), [DAN 8:9–8:12](https://ref.ly/Dan8:9-Dan8:12), [DAN 8:23–8:25](https://ref.ly/Dan8:23-Dan8:25) *The object of these prophecies, Antiochus Epiphanes, foreshadowed the antichrist in his behaviour.*

Jesus Christ and his followers accused of antinomianism
-------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:19)** *See also* [MAT 12:2](https://ref.ly/Matt12:2); [JHN 9:16](https://ref.ly/John9:16); [ACT 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11-Acts6:14)

### Jesus Christ came to fulfil the law, not abolish it

**[MAT 5:17–5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17-Matt5:19)**

Paul rejects antinomianism
--------------------------

**[ROM 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Rom6:1-Rom6:2)** *See also* [ROM 3:31](https://ref.ly/Rom3:31), [ROM 7:7](https://ref.ly/Rom7:7)

Misunderstandings, by opponents and converts alike, about Paul's teaching on freedom from the law
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Paul accused of antinomianism

**[ACT 21:28](https://ref.ly/Acts21:28)** *See also* [ROM 3:8](https://ref.ly/Rom3:8), [ROM 6:15](https://ref.ly/Rom6:15)

### Paul corrects the Corinthians' interpretation of his teaching as a licence for antinomianism

**[1CO 10:23](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:23)** *The phrase “Everything is permissible” is probably the Corinthians' misinterpretation of Paul's teaching.* *See also* [1CO 5:1–5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:8), [1CO 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:12-1Cor6:13)

Punishment of antinomians
-------------------------

**[JER 6:19](https://ref.ly/Jer6:19)** *See also* [EZR 7:26](https://ref.ly/Ezra7:26); [AMO 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Amos2:4-Amos2:5); [REV 22:15](https://ref.ly/Rev22:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 2:20; Proverbs 29:18; Jeremiah 5:5; Hosea 8:12; 2 Peter 2:10; Ezekiel 20:13; Exodus 32:25; Jeremiah 2:23-25; Hosea 8:1; Zechariah 7:11-12; Jude 1:4; 2 Timothy 3:1-5; 2 Peter 2:13-15; Jude 1:8; 2 Thessalonians 2:3-10; Daniel 7:25; Daniel 8:9-12; Daniel 8:23-25; Matthew 11:19; Matthew 12:2; John 9:16; Acts 6:11-14; Matthew 5:17-19; Romans 6:1-2; Romans 3:31; Romans 7:7; Acts 21:28; Romans 3:8; Romans 6:15; 1 Corinthians 10:23; 1 Corinthians 5:1-8; 1 Corinthians 6:12-13; Jeremiah 6:19; Ezra 7:26; Amos 2:4-5; Revelation 22:15

## 8704: apostasy (id: 8704)

A general falling away from religion or a denial of the faith by those who once held it or professed to hold it. It is frequently described in terms of spiritual adultery and thus provokes the jealous anger of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 8705: apostasy, in OT (ID: `8705@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8706: apostasy, warnings against personal (ID: `8706@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8707: apostasy, dealing with personal (ID: `8707@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8705: apostasy, in OT (id: 8705)

Israel was in danger of abandoning God from her earliest days. The OT frequently warns against apostasy and identifies its likely consequences.

Apostasy in Egypt and in the desert
-----------------------------------

**[PSA 78:9–78:41](https://ref.ly/Ps78:9-Ps78:41)** *See also* [EXO 32:25–32:29](https://ref.ly/Exod32:25-Exod32:29) *the death of 3,000 after Aaron made the golden calf*; [LEV 17:7](https://ref.ly/Lev17:7), [LEV 20:1–20:6](https://ref.ly/Lev20:1-Lev20:6); [NUM 14:9–14:12](https://ref.ly/Num14:9-Num14:12) *The people rebel against Moses and Aaron after the return of the spies*; [NUM 14:31–14:35](https://ref.ly/Num14:31-Num14:35), [NUM 25:1–25:4](https://ref.ly/Num25:1-Num25:4), [NUM 32:10–32:11](https://ref.ly/Num32:10-Num32:11); [DEU 31:16–31:18](https://ref.ly/Deut31:16-Deut31:18), [DEU 32:15–32:21](https://ref.ly/Deut32:15-Deut32:21); [PSA 95:7–95:8](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:8), [PSA 106:13–106:33](https://ref.ly/Ps106:13-Ps106:33); [HEB 3:15–3:19](https://ref.ly/Heb3:15-Heb3:19)

Apostasy in the time of the judges
----------------------------------

**[PSA 78:55–78:64](https://ref.ly/Ps78:55-Ps78:64)** *See also* [JDG 2:6–2:23](https://ref.ly/Judg2:6-Judg2:23), [JDG 8:27](https://ref.ly/Judg8:27), [JDG 8:33–8:34](https://ref.ly/Judg8:33-Judg8:34); [NEH 9:26–9:31](https://ref.ly/Neh9:26-Neh9:31); [PSA 106:34–106:39](https://ref.ly/Ps106:34-Ps106:39)

Apostasy in the kingdom of Israel
---------------------------------

**[HOS 5:3–5:4](https://ref.ly/Hos5:3-Hos5:4)** *See also* [2KI 17:7–17:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:7-2Kgs17:20); [HOS 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2), [HOS 4:10–4:19](https://ref.ly/Hos4:10-Hos4:19), [HOS 7:4](https://ref.ly/Hos7:4)

Apostasy in the kingdom of Judah
--------------------------------

**[JER 3:1–3:25](https://ref.ly/Jer3:1-Jer3:25)** *See also* [2KI 23:26–23:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:26-2Kgs23:27); [2CH 29:6–29:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:6-2Chr29:9) *Hezekiah's reforms to purify the temple*; [2CH 30:7–30:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:7-2Chr30:9); [ISA 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/Isa1:1-Isa1:6), [ISA 30:1–30:5](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1-Isa30:5) *Judah's alliance with Egypt*; [JER 2:7–2:13](https://ref.ly/Jer2:7-Jer2:13), [JER 2:19–2:25](https://ref.ly/Jer2:19-Jer2:25), [JER 5:5–5:7](https://ref.ly/Jer5:5-Jer5:7), [JER 7:9–7:15](https://ref.ly/Jer7:9-Jer7:15), [JER 9:2](https://ref.ly/Jer9:2), [JER 13:23–13:27](https://ref.ly/Jer13:23-Jer13:27), [JER 17:5–17:6](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5-Jer17:6), [JER 17:13](https://ref.ly/Jer17:13), [JER 23:10–23:15](https://ref.ly/Jer23:10-Jer23:15) *lying prophets denounced*; [JER 31:22](https://ref.ly/Jer31:22); [EZK 6:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:9), [EZK 16:15–16:42](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:15-Ezek16:42), [EZK 23:1–23:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:1-Ezek23:35); [DAN 9:5–9:14](https://ref.ly/Dan9:5-Dan9:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 78:9-41; Exodus 32:25-29; Leviticus 17:7; Leviticus 20:1-6; Numbers 14:9-12; Numbers 14:31-35; Numbers 25:1-4; Numbers 32:10-11; Deuteronomy 31:16-18; Deuteronomy 32:15-21; Psalms 95:7-8; Psalms 106:13-33; Hebrews 3:15-19; Psalms 78:55-64; Judges 2:6-23; Judges 8:27; Judges 8:33-34; Nehemiah 9:26-31; Psalms 106:34-39; Hosea 5:3-4; 2 Kings 17:7-20; Hosea 1:2; Hosea 4:10-19; Hosea 7:4; Jeremiah 3:1-25; 2 Kings 23:26-27; 2 Chronicles 29:6-9; 2 Chronicles 30:7-9; Isaiah 1:1-6; Isaiah 30:1-5; Jeremiah 2:7-13; Jeremiah 2:19-25; Jeremiah 5:5-7; Jeremiah 7:9-15; Jeremiah 9:2; Jeremiah 13:23-27; Jeremiah 17:5-6; Jeremiah 17:13; Jeremiah 23:10-15; Jeremiah 31:22; Ezekiel 6:9; Ezekiel 16:15-42; Ezekiel 23:1-35; Daniel 9:5-14

## 8706: apostasy, warnings against personal (id: 8706)

It is possible for individuals to fall away from faith or turn against Jesus Christ. Scripture identifies this danger and warns against its negative consequences.

Personal apostasy described
---------------------------

**[HEB 6:4–6:8](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4-Heb6:8)** *See also* [MAT 13:20–13:21](https://ref.ly/Matt13:20-Matt13:21) parallels [MRK 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/Mark4:16-Mark4:17), [LUK 8:13](https://ref.ly/Luke8:13); [MAT 24:10–24:12](https://ref.ly/Matt24:10-Matt24:12); [GAL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6), [GAL 5:4](https://ref.ly/Gal5:4); [2TH 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:3); [2TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:3-2Tim4:4); [1TI 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1); [1JN 2:19](https://ref.ly/1John2:19), [1JN 5:16](https://ref.ly/1John5:16)

Warnings against personal apostasy
----------------------------------

**[HEB 10:26–10:31](https://ref.ly/Heb10:26-Heb10:31)** *See also* [MAT 10:33](https://ref.ly/Matt10:33) parallels [MRK 8:38](https://ref.ly/Mark8:38), [LUK 12:9](https://ref.ly/Luke12:9); [JHN 15:6](https://ref.ly/John15:6); [2TI 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:12); [HEB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Heb3:12); [2PE 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17)

OT examples of personal apostasy
--------------------------------

[DEU 13:1–13:11](https://ref.ly/Deut13:1-Deut13:11) *an unfruitful prophet or relative*; [1SA 15:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:11), [1SA 15:28](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:28), [1SA 16:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam16:14); ; [2SA 7:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:15); [2CH 26:16–26:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16-2Chr26:21) *King Uzziah*; ; [2CH 28:19–28:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:19-2Chr28:27) *King Ahaz*

NT examples of personal apostasy
--------------------------------

[MAT 26:14–26:16](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:16) parallels [MRK 14:10–14:11](https://ref.ly/Mark14:10-Mark14:11), [LUK 22:3–22:6](https://ref.ly/Luke22:3-Luke22:6) *Judas*; ; [LUK 13:26–13:27](https://ref.ly/Luke13:26-Luke13:27); ; [JHN 6:66](https://ref.ly/John6:66); ; [ACT 5:1–5:11](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:11) *Ananias and Sapphira*; ; [1TI 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:19-1Tim1:20) *Hymenaeus and Alexander*; ; [TIT 1:16](https://ref.ly/Titus1:16); ; [2TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:10) *Demas*; ; [2PE 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1-2Pet2:3), [2PE 2:10–2:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:10-2Pet2:15), [2PE 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:20-2Pet2:22); ; [JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4), [JUD 1:8–1:16](https://ref.ly/Jude1:8-Jude1:16); ; [REV 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20-Rev2:22) *Jezebel*

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 6:4-8; Matthew 13:20-21; Mark 4:16-17; Luke 8:13; Matthew 24:10-12; Galatians 1:6; Galatians 5:4; 2 Thessalonians 2:3; 2 Timothy 4:3-4; 1 Timothy 4:1; 1 John 2:19; 1 John 5:16; Hebrews 10:26-31; Matthew 10:33; Mark 8:38; Luke 12:9; John 15:6; 2 Timothy 2:12; Hebrews 3:12; 2 Peter 3:17; Deuteronomy 13:1-11; 1 Samuel 15:11; 1 Samuel 15:28; 1 Samuel 16:14; 2 Samuel 7:15; 2 Chronicles 26:16-21; 2 Chronicles 28:19-27; Matthew 26:14-16; Mark 14:10-11; Luke 22:3-6; Luke 13:26-27; John 6:66; Acts 5:1-11; 1 Timothy 1:19-20; Titus 1:16; 2 Timothy 4:10; 2 Peter 2:1-3; 2 Peter 2:10-15; 2 Peter 2:20-22; Jude 1:4; Jude 1:8-16; Revelation 2:20-22
* **Associated Articles:** 6218: provoking God (ID: `6218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6232: rejection of God, results of (ID: `6232@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8712: denial of Jesus Christ (ID: `8712@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8749: false teachers (ID: `8749@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8766: heresies (ID: `8766@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8839: unfaithfulness (ID: `8839@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8707: apostasy, dealing with personal (id: 8707)

Individual believers can fall away from faith and be restored. Scripture gives examples of such falling away, and offers advice on how such people should be dealt with pastorally.

Warnings of the dangers of personal apostasy
--------------------------------------------

**[2CO 11:2–11:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:2-2Cor11:3)** *See also* [PSA 125:4–125:5](https://ref.ly/Ps125:4-Ps125:5); [LUK 9:59–9:62](https://ref.ly/Luke9:59-Luke9:62); [GAL 3:1–3:3](https://ref.ly/Gal3:1-Gal3:3), [GAL 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/Gal4:8-Gal4:10) *Those who return to strict observance of Jewish days and festivals have not understood the gospel.*

Warnings against leading others into apostasy
---------------------------------------------

**[MAT 18:6](https://ref.ly/Matt18:6)** *See also* [PRO 28:10](https://ref.ly/Prov28:10); [GAL 5:7–5:10](https://ref.ly/Gal5:7-Gal5:10)

Encouragements to guard against personal apostasy
-------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:12-1Cor10:13)**; **[HEB 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Heb3:12-Heb3:14)** *See also* [PSA 85:8](https://ref.ly/Ps85:8); [MAT 24:10–24:13](https://ref.ly/Matt24:10-Matt24:13), [MAT 24:24–24:25](https://ref.ly/Matt24:24-Matt24:25) parallel [MRK 13:22–13:23](https://ref.ly/Mark13:22-Mark13:23); [COL 1:21–1:23](https://ref.ly/Col1:21-Col1:23); [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21); [HEB 10:35–10:39](https://ref.ly/Heb10:35-Heb10:39), [HEB 12:1–12:8](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1-Heb12:8); [2PE 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17-2Pet3:18); [REV 13:5–13:10](https://ref.ly/Rev13:5-Rev13:10)

Dealing with apostate believers
-------------------------------

**[GAL 6:1](https://ref.ly/Gal6:1)** *See also* [1CO 5:4–5:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:4-1Cor5:5); [JAS 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Jas5:19-Jas5:20); [1JN 5:16–5:17](https://ref.ly/1John5:16-1John5:17)

Examples of those who were restored from apostasy
-------------------------------------------------

Samson: [JDG 16:20](https://ref.ly/Judg16:20), [JDG 16:28–16:30](https://ref.ly/Judg16:28-Judg16:30); [PSA 51:1–51:17](https://ref.ly/Ps51:1-Ps51:17) *David*; [MAT 26:74](https://ref.ly/Matt26:74) parallels [MRK 14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:72), [LUK 22:61–22:62](https://ref.ly/Luke22:61-Luke22:62), [JHN 18:27](https://ref.ly/John18:27) *for the account of Peter's reinstatement see [Jn 21:15–17](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:17)*; [ACT 15:37–15:38](https://ref.ly/Acts15:37-Acts15:38) *for evidence of John Mark's recovery see [2Ti 4:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:11)*

The punishment of apostates
---------------------------

[ISA 65:12–65:15](https://ref.ly/Isa65:12-Isa65:15); ; [EZK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:20), [EZK 18:24–18:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:24-Ezek18:26); ; [2TH 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:11-2Thess2:12); ; [2PE 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17)

### Punishment through misfortune

[DEU 28:63](https://ref.ly/Deut28:63); ; [AMO 2:4–2:6](https://ref.ly/Amos2:4-Amos2:6)

### Punishment through defeat

[NUM 14:43](https://ref.ly/Num14:43); ; [JDG 2:12–2:15](https://ref.ly/Judg2:12-Judg2:15)

### Punishment through rejection

[2CH 24:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:20); ; [HOS 4:6](https://ref.ly/Hos4:6)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Corinthians 11:2-3; Psalms 125:4-5; Luke 9:59-62; Galatians 3:1-3; Galatians 4:8-10; Matthew 18:6; Proverbs 28:10; Galatians 5:7-10; 1 Corinthians 10:12-13; Hebrews 3:12-14; Psalms 85:8; Matthew 24:10-13; Matthew 24:24-25; Mark 13:22-23; Colossians 1:21-23; 1 Timothy 6:20-21; Hebrews 10:35-39; Hebrews 12:1-8; 2 Peter 3:17-18; Revelation 13:5-10; Galatians 6:1; 1 Corinthians 5:4-5; James 5:19-20; 1 John 5:16-17; Judges 16:20; Judges 16:28-30; Psalms 51:1-17; Matthew 26:74; Mark 14:72; Luke 22:61-62; John 18:27; John 21:15-17; Acts 15:37-38; 2 Timothy 4:11; Isaiah 65:12-15; Ezekiel 3:20; Ezekiel 18:24-26; 2 Thessalonians 2:11-12; 2 Peter 2:17; Deuteronomy 28:63; Amos 2:4-6; Numbers 14:43; Judges 2:12-15; 2 Chronicles 24:20; Hosea 4:6

## 8709: astrology (id: 8709)

The study of the stars and planets, based on the pagan assumption that they exercise a decisive influence over human destinies. Scripture rejects such beliefs, insisting upon the total sovereignty of God over his entire creation.

Astrology is to be rejected
---------------------------

### The worship of heavenly bodies is forbidden

**[DEU 4:19](https://ref.ly/Deut4:19)**; **[2KI 17:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:16)** *See also* [2KI 21:3–21:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:3-2Kgs21:5) parallel [2CH 33:3–33:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:3-2Chr33:5); [2KI 23:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:4), [2KI 23:11](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:11); [JOB 31:26–31:28](https://ref.ly/Job31:26-Job31:28); [EZK 8:16–8:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:16-Ezek8:18); [ZEP 1:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:5)

### The consultation of stars is condemned

**[ISA 47:13](https://ref.ly/Isa47:13)** *See also* [JER 8:2](https://ref.ly/Jer8:2)

Astrology at the time of Daniel
-------------------------------

[DAN 2:1–2:13](https://ref.ly/Dan2:1-Dan2:13), [DAN 4:1–4:7](https://ref.ly/Dan4:1-Dan4:7), [DAN 5:1–5:8](https://ref.ly/Dan5:1-Dan5:8)

Astrologers came to worship the newborn Jesus
---------------------------------------------

[MAT 2:1–2:10](https://ref.ly/Matt2:1-Matt2:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 4:19; 2 Kings 17:16; 2 Kings 21:3-5; 2 Chronicles 33:3-5; 2 Kings 23:4; 2 Kings 23:11; Job 31:26-28; Ezekiel 8:16-18; Zephaniah 1:5; Isaiah 47:13; Jeremiah 8:2; Daniel 2:1-13; Daniel 4:1-7; Daniel 5:1-8; Matthew 2:1-10
* **Associated Articles:** 1130: God, sovereignty of (ID: `1130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4055: heaven and earth (ID: `4055@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4170: host of heaven (ID: `4170@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4212: astronomy (ID: `4212@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4251: moon (ID: `4251@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4281: stars (ID: `4281@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4284: sun (ID: `4284@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4937: fate, and fatalism (ID: `4937@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8710: atheism (id: 8710)

The denial of God's existence and thus also the denial of his word, truth and authority. It is seen in the worship of created things, in human boasting and in the decision to live a life without God which results in godlessness and brings God's judgment.

Characteristics of atheism
--------------------------

### Denying God's existence

**[PSA 14:1](https://ref.ly/Ps14:1)** parallel [PSA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Ps53:1)

### Worshipping created things

**[ROM 1:25](https://ref.ly/Rom1:25)** *See also* [DAN 5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:23); [REV 9:20](https://ref.ly/Rev9:20)

### Denying God's truth

**[ISA 32:6](https://ref.ly/Isa32:6)**; **[ROM 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rom1:18)** *See also* [JER 5:12](https://ref.ly/Jer5:12), [JER 5:31](https://ref.ly/Jer5:31), [JER 11:21](https://ref.ly/Jer11:21), [JER 14:14](https://ref.ly/Jer14:14), [JER 27:15](https://ref.ly/Jer27:15)

### Denying God's ability to fulfil his word

**[JER 17:15](https://ref.ly/Jer17:15)** *See also* [ISA 5:19](https://ref.ly/Isa5:19); [ACT 17:18](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18); [1TH 4:8](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:8); [2PE 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:3-2Pet3:4); [REV 11:7–11:10](https://ref.ly/Rev11:7-Rev11:10)

### Showing contempt for God's name

[LEV 19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:12); ; [JER 5:2](https://ref.ly/Jer5:2); ; [EZK 36:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:20), [EZK 43:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek43:8)

### Boasting against God

**[PSA 10:3–10:5](https://ref.ly/Ps10:3-Ps10:5)** *See also* [JOB 15:25](https://ref.ly/Job15:25); [PSA 12:3–12:4](https://ref.ly/Ps12:3-Ps12:4), [PSA 36:1–36:2](https://ref.ly/Ps36:1-Ps36:2), [PSA 73:11](https://ref.ly/Ps73:11); [PRO 30:9](https://ref.ly/Prov30:9); [EZK 35:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek35:13); [REV 13:6](https://ref.ly/Rev13:6)

### Not turning to God in trouble

**[JOB 35:10](https://ref.ly/Job35:10)** *See also* [JOB 36:13](https://ref.ly/Job36:13); [JER 2:8](https://ref.ly/Jer2:8), [JER 17:5](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5)

### Living in ignorance of God

**[JER 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jer5:4)** *See also* [EPH 2:12](https://ref.ly/Eph2:12)

### Denying Jesus Christ

**[JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)** *See also* [1JN 2:22](https://ref.ly/1John2:22), [1JN 4:3](https://ref.ly/1John4:3); [2JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/2John1:7)

Atheism results in godless living
---------------------------------

**[ROM 1:28–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:28-Rom1:31)** *See also* [GEN 6:5](https://ref.ly/Gen6:5); [1SA 2:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:12); [PRO 11:9](https://ref.ly/Prov11:9); [ISA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:17), [ISA 26:10](https://ref.ly/Isa26:10); [JER 5:5](https://ref.ly/Jer5:5), [JER 23:11](https://ref.ly/Jer23:11); [MAL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mal3:14), [MAL 3:18](https://ref.ly/Mal3:18); [1TI 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9-1Tim1:10); [JUD 1:18](https://ref.ly/Jude1:18)

Atheism brings condemnation
---------------------------

### It cannot be excused

[JOB 12:7–12:8](https://ref.ly/Job12:7-Job12:8); ; [ACT 14:17](https://ref.ly/Acts14:17); ; [ROM 1:19–1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:19-Rom1:20)

### It results in lack of hope

[JOB 8:13](https://ref.ly/Job8:13), [JOB 15:34](https://ref.ly/Job15:34), [JOB 20:5](https://ref.ly/Job20:5), [JOB 27:8](https://ref.ly/Job27:8); ; [PSA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Ps9:17)

### It results in God's judgment

[ISA 10:6](https://ref.ly/Isa10:6), [ISA 33:14](https://ref.ly/Isa33:14); ; [JER 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jer5:5-Jer5:6), [JER 5:9–5:11](https://ref.ly/Jer5:9-Jer5:11), [JER 5:29](https://ref.ly/Jer5:29); ; [ROM 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rom1:18); ; [1PE 4:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:17); ; [2PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:7); ; [JUD 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jude1:14-Jude1:15)

God desires to save the atheist
-------------------------------

**[ACT 17:27](https://ref.ly/Acts17:27)** *See also* [ISA 55:7](https://ref.ly/Isa55:7); [JER 3:12](https://ref.ly/Jer3:12); [EZK 18:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek18:23), [EZK 33:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:11); [ROM 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/Rom5:6-Rom5:8)

## 8711: covenant breakers (id: 8711)

Covenants were central to OT life, with the result that those who broke covenants, and especially those who violated the covenant between God and Israel, were regarded as a serious threat to social stability.

The importance of covenants
---------------------------

### The covenant between God and his people

**[GEN 9:16](https://ref.ly/Gen9:16)** *See also* [2SA 23:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:5); [ISA 24:5](https://ref.ly/Isa24:5), [ISA 55:3](https://ref.ly/Isa55:3)

### God will remain faithful to his covenant

**[JDG 2:1](https://ref.ly/Judg2:1)** *See also* [PSA 105:8](https://ref.ly/Ps105:8); [ISA 54:10](https://ref.ly/Isa54:10)

Covenants between people
------------------------

### Covenants made in the sight of God were permanently binding

**[NUM 30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:2)**; **[1SA 23:16–23:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:16-1Sam23:18)** *See also* [DEU 23:21](https://ref.ly/Deut23:21); [ECC 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:4)

### Condemnation of those who break covenants

**[HOS 6:7](https://ref.ly/Hos6:7)** *See also* [PRO 2:17](https://ref.ly/Prov2:17)

### Examples of covenants between people

[GEN 21:27](https://ref.ly/Gen21:27); ; [JOS 24:24](https://ref.ly/Josh24:24); ; [2SA 5:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam5:3); ; [1KI 5:12](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:12), [1KI 20:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:34); ; [JER 34:8](https://ref.ly/Jer34:8)

Examples of breaking the covenant with God
------------------------------------------

### Coveting the possessions of others

**[ECC 4:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:4)** *See also* [EXO 20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:17)

### Stealing

**[EXO 20:15](https://ref.ly/Exod20:15)** parallel [DEU 5:19](https://ref.ly/Deut5:19) *See also* [DEU 23:24](https://ref.ly/Deut23:24)

### Using dishonest weights or scales

**[LEV 19:35–19:36](https://ref.ly/Lev19:35-Lev19:36)**; **[PRO 20:23](https://ref.ly/Prov20:23)** *See also* [DEU 25:13–25:16](https://ref.ly/Deut25:13-Deut25:16); [HOS 12:7–12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:7-Hos12:8); [AMO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Amos8:5)

### Moving boundary stones

**[PRO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10)** *See also* [DEU 19:14](https://ref.ly/Deut19:14), [DEU 27:17](https://ref.ly/Deut27:17)

### Extortion

**[HAB 2:6](https://ref.ly/Hab2:6)** *See also* [ISA 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:2); [EZK 22:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:12); [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11)

### Breach of trust

[LEV 6:2](https://ref.ly/Lev6:2); ; [EZK 16:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:17)

### Not repaying loans

**[PSA 37:21](https://ref.ly/Ps37:21)**

### Not paying fair wages

*See also* [GEN 31:6–31:9](https://ref.ly/Gen31:6-Gen31:9)

### Failing to care for aliens

Aliens living in Israel were regarded as having privileged status amongst Gentiles, and were treated as covenant people in some respects: [EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9); ; [DEU 29:9–29:15](https://ref.ly/Deut29:9-Deut29:15), [DEU 31:12](https://ref.ly/Deut31:12); ; [JOS 8:33–8:35](https://ref.ly/Josh8:33-Josh8:35)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 9:16; 2 Samuel 23:5; Isaiah 24:5; Isaiah 55:3; Judges 2:1; Psalms 105:8; Isaiah 54:10; Numbers 30:2; 1 Samuel 23:16-18; Deuteronomy 23:21; Ecclesiastes 5:4; Hosea 6:7; Proverbs 2:17; Genesis 21:27; Joshua 24:24; 2 Samuel 5:3; 1 Kings 5:12; 1 Kings 20:34; Jeremiah 34:8; Ecclesiastes 4:4; Exodus 20:17; Exodus 20:15; Deuteronomy 5:19; Deuteronomy 23:24; Leviticus 19:35-36; Proverbs 20:23; Deuteronomy 25:13-16; Hosea 12:7-8; Amos 8:5; Proverbs 23:10; Deuteronomy 19:14; Deuteronomy 27:17; Habakkuk 2:6; Isaiah 10:1-2; Ezekiel 22:12; Amos 5:11; Leviticus 6:2; Ezekiel 16:17; Psalms 37:21; Genesis 31:6-9; Exodus 23:9; Deuteronomy 29:9-15; Deuteronomy 31:12; Joshua 8:33-35
* **Associated Articles:** 1345: covenant (ID: `1345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5311: extortion (ID: `5311@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5466: promises (ID: `5466@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8719: distrust (ID: `8719@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8712: denial of Jesus Christ (id: 8712)

The making of a conscious, informed decision not to accept Jesus Christ or his claims and teaching.

Jesus Christ was denied by the Jewish people
--------------------------------------------

**[JHN 1:11](https://ref.ly/John1:11)** *See also* [MAT 11:16–11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:16-Matt11:19) parallel [LUK 7:31–7:35](https://ref.ly/Luke7:31-Luke7:35); [MAT 13:54–13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54-Matt13:58) parallel [MRK 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:1-Mark6:6); [MAT 26:63–26:68](https://ref.ly/Matt26:63-Matt26:68) parallels [MRK 14:61–14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:61-Mark14:65), [LUK 22:66–22:71](https://ref.ly/Luke22:66-Luke22:71); [LUK 4:16–4:30](https://ref.ly/Luke4:16-Luke4:30); [JHN 5:37–5:43](https://ref.ly/John5:37-John5:43); [ACT 4:8–4:12](https://ref.ly/Acts4:8-Acts4:12); [PSA 118:22](https://ref.ly/Ps118:22)

The consequences of denying Jesus Christ
----------------------------------------

### Denying Jesus Christ results in judgment

**[JHN 12:48](https://ref.ly/John12:48)** *See also* [MAT 12:38–12:42](https://ref.ly/Matt12:38-Matt12:42) parallel [LUK 11:29–11:32](https://ref.ly/Luke11:29-Luke11:32)

### Denying Jesus Christ means replacement by others

**[ACT 13:46](https://ref.ly/Acts13:46)** *See also* [MAT 21:40–21:41](https://ref.ly/Matt21:40-Matt21:41) parallels [MRK 12:9](https://ref.ly/Mark12:9), [LUK 20:15–20:16](https://ref.ly/Luke20:15-Luke20:16); [ACT 18:5–18:6](https://ref.ly/Acts18:5-Acts18:6)

### To deny Christ is to be denied by Christ

**[2TI 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:12)** *See also* [MAT 10:32](https://ref.ly/Matt10:32); [MRK 8:38](https://ref.ly/Mark8:38)

Warnings against those who deny Jesus Christ
--------------------------------------------

**[2PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1)** *See also* [1JN 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/1John2:22-1John2:23); [2JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/2John1:7); [JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)

Denial, repentance and restoration: the example of Peter
--------------------------------------------------------

### Peter's denial predicted by Jesus Christ

**[MRK 14:29–14:30](https://ref.ly/Mark14:29-Mark14:30)** parallels [MAT 26:31–26:34](https://ref.ly/Matt26:31-Matt26:34), [LUK 22:31–22:34](https://ref.ly/Luke22:31-Luke22:34)

### Peter declares he will never deny Jesus Christ

**[MRK 14:31](https://ref.ly/Mark14:31)** parallel [MAT 26:35](https://ref.ly/Matt26:35)

### Peter denied that he knew Jesus Christ

**[MAT 26:69–26:75](https://ref.ly/Matt26:69-Matt26:75)** parallels [MRK 14:66–14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:66-Mark14:72), [LUK 22:55–22:62](https://ref.ly/Luke22:55-Luke22:62), [JHN 18:15–18:18](https://ref.ly/John18:15-John18:18), [JHN 18:25–18:27](https://ref.ly/John18:25-John18:27)

### Peter's restoration to Christ

*See also* [JHN 21:15–21:19](https://ref.ly/John21:15-John21:19); [ACT 1:15–1:22](https://ref.ly/Acts1:15-Acts1:22), [ACT 2:14–2:41](https://ref.ly/Acts2:14-Acts2:41)

* **Associated Passages:** John 1:11; Matthew 11:16-19; Luke 7:31-35; Matthew 13:54-58; Mark 6:1-6; Matthew 26:63-68; Mark 14:61-65; Luke 22:66-71; Luke 4:16-30; John 5:37-43; Acts 4:8-12; Psalms 118:22; John 12:48; Matthew 12:38-42; Luke 11:29-32; Acts 13:46; Matthew 21:40-41; Mark 12:9; Luke 20:15-16; Acts 18:5-6; 2 Timothy 2:12; Matthew 10:32; Mark 8:38; 2 Peter 2:1; 1 John 2:22-23; 2 John 1:7; Jude 1:4; Mark 14:29-30; Matthew 26:31-34; Luke 22:31-34; Mark 14:31; Matthew 26:35; Matthew 26:69-75; Mark 14:66-72; Luke 22:55-62; John 18:15-18; John 18:25-27; John 21:15-19; Acts 1:15-22; Acts 2:14-41

## 8713: discouragement (id: 8713)

A sense of unhappiness arising from a loss of confidence in one's own abilities, in the reliability of God or in the power of the gospel. Discouragement can occur in the Christian life, especially when there is resistance to the gospel or in instances of personal failure. Scripture provides reassurance for those who experience discouragement.

The experience of discouragement
--------------------------------

**[JOB 3:20–3:26](https://ref.ly/Job3:20-Job3:26)** *See also* [NUM 21:4–21:5](https://ref.ly/Num21:4-Num21:5) *the Israelites in the desert*; [JOS 2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/Josh2:8-Josh2:11) *the Canaanites and Rahab*; [1SA 17:10–17:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:10-1Sam17:11) *the Israelites and Goliath*; [2KI 19:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:26) *God's judgment on Sennacherib*; [JOB 5:6–5:7](https://ref.ly/Job5:6-Job5:7); [PSA 38:1–38:14](https://ref.ly/Ps38:1-Ps38:14); [PRO 1:26–1:27](https://ref.ly/Prov1:26-Prov1:27) *Wisdom speaks to the sinner*; [ECC 2:17–2:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:17-Eccl2:20) *the Teacher's disillusion*; [ISA 21:2–21:4](https://ref.ly/Isa21:2-Isa21:4); [JER 45:3](https://ref.ly/Jer45:3) *to Baruch*; [EZK 21:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:7) *God's judgment on Israel*; [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2); [2CO 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:8-2Cor1:9) *Paul*

Prayer in discouragement
------------------------

**[PSA 42:5–42:11](https://ref.ly/Ps42:5-Ps42:11)** *See also* [DEU 26:6–26:7](https://ref.ly/Deut26:6-Deut26:7); [2SA 22:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:7) *David*; [2CH 20:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:9) *Jehoshaphat*; [NEH 9:32](https://ref.ly/Neh9:32); [PSA 31:9–31:12](https://ref.ly/Ps31:9-Ps31:12); [ISA 38:14](https://ref.ly/Isa38:14) *Hezekiah*; [JER 8:18–8:19](https://ref.ly/Jer8:18-Jer8:19); [JON 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:1-Jonah2:2)

Endurance in discouragement
---------------------------

**[ISA 42:3–42:4](https://ref.ly/Isa42:3-Isa42:4)** *See also* [2CO 6:4–6:10](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:4-2Cor6:10) *Paul*; [2TH 1:4](https://ref.ly/2Thess1:4); [HEB 10:32](https://ref.ly/Heb10:32), [HEB 12:3](https://ref.ly/Heb12:3); [REV 2:3](https://ref.ly/Rev2:3) *concerning the church at Ephesus*

Discouragement through disobedience
-----------------------------------

**[JER 8:9](https://ref.ly/Jer8:9)** *See also* [1SA 28:15–28:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:15-1Sam28:18); [JOB 15:24–15:25](https://ref.ly/Job15:24-Job15:25) *Eliphaz to Job*; [ZEP 1:17](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:17)

Discouragement and discipline
-----------------------------

**[HEB 12:5–12:7](https://ref.ly/Heb12:5-Heb12:7)** *See also* [JDG 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Judg2:14-Judg2:15); [ISA 26:16](https://ref.ly/Isa26:16)

Effects of discouragement
-------------------------

**[PRO 17:22](https://ref.ly/Prov17:22)** *See also* [EXO 6:9](https://ref.ly/Exod6:9) *the Israelites in Egypt*; [2SA 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:1); [EZR 4:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:4) *the rebuilding of the temple*; [JOB 4:5](https://ref.ly/Job4:5); [PRO 15:13](https://ref.ly/Prov15:13), [PRO 18:14](https://ref.ly/Prov18:14)

Resisting discouragement
------------------------

**[DEU 1:21](https://ref.ly/Deut1:21)** *See also* [JOS 1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:9), [JOS 8:1](https://ref.ly/Josh8:1); [1SA 17:32](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:32) *David reassuring Saul about Goliath*; [1CH 22:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:13) *David to Solomon*; [2CH 32:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:7) *Hezekiah*; [JER 51:46](https://ref.ly/Jer51:46); [LUK 18:1](https://ref.ly/Luke18:1); [JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1); [GAL 6:9](https://ref.ly/Gal6:9); [EPH 3:13](https://ref.ly/Eph3:13); [2TI 4:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:5)

God's help in discouragement
----------------------------

**[ROM 8:35–8:39](https://ref.ly/Rom8:35-Rom8:39)** *See also* [DEU 31:6](https://ref.ly/Deut31:6), [DEU 31:8](https://ref.ly/Deut31:8), [DEU 31:23](https://ref.ly/Deut31:23); [JOS 1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:9); [1CH 28:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:20); [2CH 20:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:15), [2CH 20:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr20:17); [PSA 34:18](https://ref.ly/Ps34:18); [ISA 40:29–40:31](https://ref.ly/Isa40:29-Isa40:31), [ISA 61:1](https://ref.ly/Isa61:1); [LUK 4:18](https://ref.ly/Luke4:18); [JER 30:10](https://ref.ly/Jer30:10); [JHN 16:33](https://ref.ly/John16:33); [2CO 7:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:6) *Paul*

Warnings not to discourage others
---------------------------------

### The Israelites discouraged when on the point of entering the promised land

**[OBA 1:12](https://ref.ly/Obad1:12)** *See also* [DEU 1:28](https://ref.ly/Deut1:28) *by the spies*; [NUM 32:7](https://ref.ly/Num32:7) *by the Gadites and Reubenites*

### General warnings not to discourage others

[PSA 109:16](https://ref.ly/Ps109:16); ; [ZEC 10:2](https://ref.ly/Zech10:2); ; [ACT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Acts15:19) *James advises against discouraging the Gentiles*; ; [COL 3:21](https://ref.ly/Col3:21)

The need to stand with the discouraged
--------------------------------------

**[HEB 13:3](https://ref.ly/Heb13:3)** *See also* [JOB 6:14](https://ref.ly/Job6:14); [JER 8:21](https://ref.ly/Jer8:21); [ROM 12:15](https://ref.ly/Rom12:15); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27)
