## 8741: failure (id: 8741)

Scripture indicates various ways in which believers may fail God, while emphasising that God will never fail his people.

God will never fail his people
------------------------------

**[1CH 28:20](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:20)** *See also* [JOS 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Josh1:8-Josh1:9); [PSA 89:28](https://ref.ly/Ps89:28)

### God's righteousness will not fail

[ISA 51:6](https://ref.ly/Isa51:6)

### God's compassion will not fail

**[LAM 3:22–3:23](https://ref.ly/Lam3:22-Lam3:23)**

God's word will not fail
------------------------

**[ISA 55:10–55:11](https://ref.ly/Isa55:10-Isa55:11)** *See also* [ROM 9:6](https://ref.ly/Rom9:6)

Examples of failing God
-----------------------

### Failing to abide in him

**[PSA 14:3](https://ref.ly/Ps14:3)** *See also* [PSA 58:3](https://ref.ly/Ps58:3), [PSA 78:57](https://ref.ly/Ps78:57); [JER 2:19](https://ref.ly/Jer2:19), [JER 3:22](https://ref.ly/Jer3:22), [JER 15:6](https://ref.ly/Jer15:6); [JHN 15:6](https://ref.ly/John15:6); [2PE 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17)

### Failing to believe in him

**[JHN 20:27](https://ref.ly/John20:27)** *See also* [NUM 14:11–14:12](https://ref.ly/Num14:11-Num14:12); [PSA 78:21–78:33](https://ref.ly/Ps78:21-Ps78:33); [HEB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Heb3:12)

### Failing to fear him

**[GEN 20:11](https://ref.ly/Gen20:11)** *See also* [EXO 9:30](https://ref.ly/Exod9:30); [DEU 25:18](https://ref.ly/Deut25:18); [JOB 6:14](https://ref.ly/Job6:14); [LUK 23:40](https://ref.ly/Luke23:40)

### Failing to honour him

**[MAL 2:2](https://ref.ly/Mal2:2)** *See also* [NUM 20:12](https://ref.ly/Num20:12); [1SA 2:29–2:30](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:29-1Sam2:30); [DAN 5:22–5:23](https://ref.ly/Dan5:22-Dan5:23); [ROM 2:23](https://ref.ly/Rom2:23)

### Failing to love him

**[1CO 16:22](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:22)** *See also* [JOS 23:10–23:13](https://ref.ly/Josh23:10-Josh23:13); [MAT 10:37](https://ref.ly/Matt10:37); [REV 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rev2:4)

### Failing to obey him

[2KI 17:14–17:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:14-2Kgs17:20); ; [MAT 7:26](https://ref.ly/Matt7:26)

### Failing to remember him

**[DEU 8:11](https://ref.ly/Deut8:11), [DEU 32:18](https://ref.ly/Deut32:18)** *See also* [JDG 3:7](https://ref.ly/Judg3:7), [JDG 8:33–8:34](https://ref.ly/Judg8:33-Judg8:34); [1SA 12:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:9); [PSA 78:11](https://ref.ly/Ps78:11), [PSA 106:21](https://ref.ly/Ps106:21); [ISA 17:10](https://ref.ly/Isa17:10)

### Failing to seek him

**[2CH 12:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:14)** *See also* [PSA 14:2–14:3](https://ref.ly/Ps14:2-Ps14:3), [PSA 119:155](https://ref.ly/Ps119:155); [ISA 9:13](https://ref.ly/Isa9:13); [ZEP 1:6](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:6)

### Failing to serve him

**[DEU 28:47–28:48](https://ref.ly/Deut28:47-Deut28:48)** *See also* [JOB 21:14–21:15](https://ref.ly/Job21:14-Job21:15); [JER 2:20](https://ref.ly/Jer2:20); [MAL 3:14](https://ref.ly/Mal3:14); [ROM 1:25](https://ref.ly/Rom1:25)

### Failing to trust him

**[MAT 6:30](https://ref.ly/Matt6:30)** parallel [LUK 12:28](https://ref.ly/Luke12:28) *See also* [MAT 14:31](https://ref.ly/Matt14:31), [MAT 16:8](https://ref.ly/Matt16:8), [MAT 17:17–17:20](https://ref.ly/Matt17:17-Matt17:20)

### Peter's failure of Jesus Christ

[MAT 26:33–26:35](https://ref.ly/Matt26:33-Matt26:35) parallel [MRK 14:29–14:31](https://ref.ly/Mark14:29-Mark14:31); ; [LUK 22:31–22:34](https://ref.ly/Luke22:31-Luke22:34); ; [JHN 18:25–18:27](https://ref.ly/John18:25-John18:27)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Chronicles 28:20; Joshua 1:8-9; Psalms 89:28; Isaiah 51:6; Lamentations 3:22-23; Isaiah 55:10-11; Romans 9:6; Psalms 14:3; Psalms 58:3; Psalms 78:57; Jeremiah 2:19; Jeremiah 3:22; Jeremiah 15:6; John 15:6; 2 Peter 3:17; John 20:27; Numbers 14:11-12; Psalms 78:21-33; Hebrews 3:12; Genesis 20:11; Exodus 9:30; Deuteronomy 25:18; Job 6:14; Luke 23:40; Malachi 2:2; Numbers 20:12; 1 Samuel 2:29-30; Daniel 5:22-23; Romans 2:23; 1 Corinthians 16:22; Joshua 23:10-13; Matthew 10:37; Revelation 2:4; 2 Kings 17:14-20; Matthew 7:26; Deuteronomy 8:11; Deuteronomy 32:18; Judges 3:7; Judges 8:33-34; 1 Samuel 12:9; Psalms 78:11; Psalms 106:21; Isaiah 17:10; 2 Chronicles 12:14; Psalms 14:2-3; Psalms 119:155; Isaiah 9:13; Zephaniah 1:6; Deuteronomy 28:47-48; Job 21:14-15; Jeremiah 2:20; Malachi 3:14; Romans 1:25; Matthew 6:30; Luke 12:28; Matthew 14:31; Matthew 16:8; Matthew 17:17-20; Matthew 26:33-35; Mark 14:29-31; Luke 22:31-34; John 18:25-27

## 8742: faithlessness (id: 8742)

The lack or absence of faith, or the turning away from a professed commitment to God.

* **Associated Articles:** 8743: faithlessness, nature and causes of (ID: `8743@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8744: faithlessness, as disobedience (ID: `8744@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8743: faithlessness, nature and causes of (id: 8743)

The abandoning of a commitment to the Lord, seen in disowning him, wandering from the truth and becoming entangled with the world, and resulting in judgment.

Warnings against faithlessness
------------------------------

**[2TI 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:12-2Tim2:13)** *See also* [MAT 10:33](https://ref.ly/Matt10:33) parallel [LUK 12:9](https://ref.ly/Luke12:9); [LUK 9:62](https://ref.ly/Luke9:62); [HEB 10:35–10:39](https://ref.ly/Heb10:35-Heb10:39); [HAB 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/Hab2:3-Hab2:4)

Ways in which faithlessness is seen
-----------------------------------

### Turning away from the Lord

**[HEB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Heb3:12)**

### Abandoning the gospel of grace

**[GAL 1:6](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6)**

### Becoming entangled in the world

**[2PE 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:20-2Pet2:22)** *See also* [PRO 26:11](https://ref.ly/Prov26:11); [2TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:10)

Causes of faithlessness
-----------------------

### False teaching

**[2TI 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:17-2Tim2:18)** *See also* [GAL 1:7](https://ref.ly/Gal1:7), [GAL 4:8–4:11](https://ref.ly/Gal4:8-Gal4:11); [1TI 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:3); [2TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:3-2Tim4:4); [2PE 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17)

### Persecution and trials

**[MAT 13:20–13:21](https://ref.ly/Matt13:20-Matt13:21)** parallels [MRK 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/Mark4:16-Mark4:17), [LUK 8:13](https://ref.ly/Luke8:13) *See also* [MAT 24:9–24:13](https://ref.ly/Matt24:9-Matt24:13)

### Concern for money and possessions

**[1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)** *See also* [PRO 30:8–30:9](https://ref.ly/Prov30:8-Prov30:9); [MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22) parallels [MRK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:18-Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14)

### Love growing cold

**[MAT 24:12](https://ref.ly/Matt24:12)** *See also* [REV 2:4](https://ref.ly/Rev2:4)

### Sexual desire

[1TI 5:11–5:15](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:11-1Tim5:15)

Faithlessness brings punishment
-------------------------------

### Church discipline

[1TI 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:18-1Tim1:20) *These men had been excluded from the church, a sanctuary from Satan's power.*

### God's judgment

**[MAT 10:33](https://ref.ly/Matt10:33)** parallel [LUK 12:9](https://ref.ly/Luke12:9) *See also* [HEB 6:4–6:6](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4-Heb6:6), [HEB 10:26–10:27](https://ref.ly/Heb10:26-Heb10:27)

The faithlessness of those who fall away
----------------------------------------

### The absence of true faith

**[1JN 2:19](https://ref.ly/1John2:19)**

### True faith is proved by perseverance

*See also* [JAS 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Jas1:2-Jas1:3); [1PE 1:5–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:5-1Pet1:7)

Israel's faithlessness in the OT
--------------------------------

### Israel's unfaithfulness led to God's rejection

[PSA 78:56–78:59](https://ref.ly/Ps78:56-Ps78:59)

### Warnings not to repeat Israel's unfaithfulness

[PSA 95:7–95:11](https://ref.ly/Ps95:7-Ps95:11); ; [HEB 3:7–3:12](https://ref.ly/Heb3:7-Heb3:12), [HEB 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:1-Heb4:2)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Timothy 2:12-13; Matthew 10:33; Luke 12:9; Luke 9:62; Hebrews 10:35-39; Habakkuk 2:3-4; Hebrews 3:12; Galatians 1:6; 2 Peter 2:20-22; Proverbs 26:11; 2 Timothy 4:10; 2 Timothy 2:17-18; Galatians 1:7; Galatians 4:8-11; 1 Timothy 4:1-3; 2 Timothy 4:3-4; 2 Peter 3:17; Matthew 13:20-21; Mark 4:16-17; Luke 8:13; Matthew 24:9-13; 1 Timothy 6:10; Proverbs 30:8-9; Matthew 13:22; Mark 4:18-19; Luke 8:14; Matthew 24:12; Revelation 2:4; 1 Timothy 5:11-15; 1 Timothy 1:18-20; Hebrews 6:4-6; Hebrews 10:26-27; 1 John 2:19; James 1:2-3; 1 Peter 1:5-7; Psalms 78:56-59; Psalms 95:7-11; Hebrews 3:7-12; Hebrews 4:1-2
* **Associated Articles:** 1035: God, faithfulness of (ID: `1035@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 1310: God, as judge (ID: `1310@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8021: faith, nature of (ID: `8021@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8230: discipline (ID: `8230@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8234: doctrine (ID: `8234@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8316: orthodoxy, in NT (ID: `8316@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8459: perseverance (ID: `8459@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8704: apostasy (ID: `8704@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8712: denial of Jesus Christ (ID: `8712@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8810: riches, dangers of (ID: `8810@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8840: unfaithfulness, to God (ID: `8840@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8744: faithlessness, as disobedience (id: 8744)

A lack of faith and obedience to God, which is seen especially in the lives of unbelievers. However, Scripture makes clear that believers can occasionally lapse into faithlessness towards God.

Faithlessness among the ungodly
-------------------------------

### In their character and lifestyle

**[PSA 101:3–101:5](https://ref.ly/Ps101:3-Ps101:5), [PSA 119:158](https://ref.ly/Ps119:158)** *See also* [PSA 10:4](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4), [PSA 14:1](https://ref.ly/Ps14:1); [JHN 16:8–16:9](https://ref.ly/John16:8-John16:9); [2TH 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:2); [TIT 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Titus1:15-Titus1:16)

### Examples of faithless men

[1SA 25:2–25:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:2-1Sam25:3) *Nabal*; ; [EST 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Esth3:5-Esth3:6) *Haman*; ; [MAT 2:16](https://ref.ly/Matt2:16) *Herod the Great*; ; [ACT 19:23–19:27](https://ref.ly/Acts19:23-Acts19:27) *Demetrius*

### The faithless appear to prosper

**[JER 12:1](https://ref.ly/Jer12:1)** *See also* [JOB 21:7](https://ref.ly/Job21:7); [PSA 73:3–73:5](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3-Ps73:5), [PSA 73:12](https://ref.ly/Ps73:12); [MAL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Mal3:15)

### Faithlessness results in judgment

**[PRO 14:14](https://ref.ly/Prov14:14)**; **[JHN 3:36](https://ref.ly/John3:36)** *See also* [PSA 73:17–73:18](https://ref.ly/Ps73:17-Ps73:18), [PSA 73:27](https://ref.ly/Ps73:27); [MAL 4:1](https://ref.ly/Mal4:1); [MRK 16:16](https://ref.ly/Mark16:16); [JHN 8:24](https://ref.ly/John8:24); [2TH 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:12); [REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8)

Faithlessness among the Jews
----------------------------

### Their failure to trust God for deliverance

**[ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1)** *See also* [ISA 22:8–22:11](https://ref.ly/Isa22:8-Isa22:11), [ISA 30:1–30:2](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1-Isa30:2), [ISA 30:12–30:13](https://ref.ly/Isa30:12-Isa30:13)

### Their rejection of the gospel

**[JHN 12:37–12:38](https://ref.ly/John12:37-John12:38)** *See also* [ISA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Isa53:1); [JHN 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/John3:11-John3:12), [JHN 5:37–5:40](https://ref.ly/John5:37-John5:40), [JHN 8:45](https://ref.ly/John8:45), [JHN 10:25–10:26](https://ref.ly/John10:25-John10:26)

### Specific examples

[MAT 13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:58) parallel [MRK 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:5-Mark6:6) *the people of Nazareth*; ; [MAT 19:21–19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21-Matt19:22) parallels [MRK 10:21–10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:21-Mark10:22), [LUK 18:22–18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22-Luke18:23) *the rich young ruler*; ; [ACT 28:24](https://ref.ly/Acts28:24) *Many Jews reject Paul's ministry in Rome*; ; [1TI 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:13) *Paul's own persecution of the church arose from unbelief in Jesus Christ.*

### Israel's estrangement from God

**[MAT 21:43](https://ref.ly/Matt21:43)** *See also* [PSA 118:22](https://ref.ly/Ps118:22); [MAT 21:41](https://ref.ly/Matt21:41) parallel [MRK 12:9](https://ref.ly/Mark12:9); [ROM 9:32–9:33](https://ref.ly/Rom9:32-Rom9:33)

### The acceptance of the Gentiles

[ROM 11:17–11:21](https://ref.ly/Rom11:17-Rom11:21) *The Jews' rejection of the gospel opens the way for Gentiles to be incorporated into the people of God, represented here as branches grafted onto a cultivated olive tree.*

### Israel's faithlessness is partial and temporary

[ROM 11:5](https://ref.ly/Rom11:5) *Though the people as a whole have rejected Jesus Christ, a faithful remnant remains*; ; [ROM 11:11–11:12](https://ref.ly/Rom11:11-Rom11:12), [ROM 11:23–11:27](https://ref.ly/Rom11:23-Rom11:27) *God's purpose for the faithful of Israel is their ultimate salvation.*

Faithlessness among believers
-----------------------------

### Lack of faith is rebuked

**[MAT 14:30–14:31](https://ref.ly/Matt14:30-Matt14:31)** *See also* [MAT 6:30–6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:30-Matt6:32) parallel [LUK 12:28–12:30](https://ref.ly/Luke12:28-Luke12:30) *worrying about food and clothing*; [MAT 8:24–8:26](https://ref.ly/Matt8:24-Matt8:26) parallels [MRK 4:38–4:40](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38-Mark4:40), [LUK 8:23–8:25](https://ref.ly/Luke8:23-Luke8:25) *Fear during a storm at sea*; [MAT 17:17–17:20](https://ref.ly/Matt17:17-Matt17:20) parallels [MRK 9:17–9:19](https://ref.ly/Mark9:17-Mark9:19), [LUK 9:40–9:42](https://ref.ly/Luke9:40-Luke9:42) *inability to cast out a demon*

### Causes of faithlessness

[MAT 16:8–16:10](https://ref.ly/Matt16:8-Matt16:10) *failure to learn from the lessons of the past*; ; [LUK 24:25](https://ref.ly/Luke24:25) *failure to accept teaching that has been given*

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 101:3-5; Psalms 119:158; Psalms 10:4; Psalms 14:1; John 16:8-9; 2 Thessalonians 3:2; Titus 1:15-16; 1 Samuel 25:2-3; Esther 3:5-6; Matthew 2:16; Acts 19:23-27; Jeremiah 12:1; Job 21:7; Psalms 73:3-5; Psalms 73:12; Malachi 3:15; Proverbs 14:14; John 3:36; Psalms 73:17-18; Psalms 73:27; Malachi 4:1; Mark 16:16; John 8:24; 2 Thessalonians 2:12; Revelation 21:8; Isaiah 31:1; Isaiah 22:8-11; Isaiah 30:1-2; Isaiah 30:12-13; John 12:37-38; Isaiah 53:1; John 3:11-12; John 5:37-40; John 8:45; John 10:25-26; Matthew 13:58; Mark 6:5-6; Matthew 19:21-22; Mark 10:21-22; Luke 18:22-23; Acts 28:24; 1 Timothy 1:13; Matthew 21:43; Psalms 118:22; Matthew 21:41; Mark 12:9; Romans 9:32-33; Romans 11:17-21; Romans 11:5; Romans 11:11-12; Romans 11:23-27; Matthew 14:30-31; Matthew 6:30-32; Luke 12:28-30; Matthew 8:24-26; Mark 4:38-40; Luke 8:23-25; Matthew 17:17-20; Mark 9:17-19; Luke 9:40-42; Matthew 16:8-10; Luke 24:25

## 8746: false Christs (id: 8746)

Mistaken and deceptive people claiming to be Jesus Christ. Jesus Christ warns his followers to be on their guard against the appearance of such people.

Jesus Christ warns that false Christs will appear
-------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 24:5](https://ref.ly/Matt24:5)** parallels [MRK 13:6](https://ref.ly/Mark13:6), [LUK 21:8](https://ref.ly/Luke21:8) *See also* [MAT 24:23](https://ref.ly/Matt24:23) parallel [MRK 13:21](https://ref.ly/Mark13:21)

False Christs will perform signs and miracles in order to deceive
-----------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 24:24](https://ref.ly/Matt24:24)** parallel [MRK 13:22](https://ref.ly/Mark13:22)

False Christs can be recognised because the return of Jesus Christ will be obvious to everyone
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 24:27–24:31](https://ref.ly/Matt24:27-Matt24:31)** parallels [MRK 13:24–13:27](https://ref.ly/Mark13:24-Mark13:27), [LUK 21:25–21:28](https://ref.ly/Luke21:25-Luke21:28) *The personal return of the Lord Jesus Christ is not described in terms of an appearance to a select group, but to the whole world. The attempt of anyone to convince others that he is Christ therefore is a sure sign of deception.*

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 24:5; Mark 13:6; Luke 21:8; Matthew 24:23; Mark 13:21; Matthew 24:24; Mark 13:22; Matthew 24:27-31; Mark 13:24-27; Luke 21:25-28
* **Associated Articles:** 2565: Jesus Christ, second coming of (ID: `2565@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8747: false gods (ID: `8747@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8749: false teachers (ID: `8749@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8767: hypocrisy (ID: `8767@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8776: lies (ID: `8776@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9115: antichrist, the (ID: `9115@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8747: false gods (id: 8747)

Any gods other than the LORD himself. Some of these divinities took the form of images, others were mythical. Some Israelites became involved in idolatrous worship of such gods. The book of Acts records attempts to deify human beings.

False gods associated with foreign nations in the OT
----------------------------------------------------

### Amon, the chief god of Egypt

*See also* [JER 46:25](https://ref.ly/Jer46:25)

### Asherah, a Canaanite goddess

**[EXO 34:13–34:14](https://ref.ly/Exod34:13-Exod34:14)** *Asherah was the consort of El, the chief Canaanite god. Wooden poles, perhaps carved in her image, were often set up in her honour and placed near other pagan objects of worship.* *See also* [DEU 7:5](https://ref.ly/Deut7:5); [JDG 6:25–6:30](https://ref.ly/Judg6:25-Judg6:30) *Gideon destroys an Asherah pole*; [1KI 14:15](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:15), [1KI 14:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:23), [1KI 15:13](https://ref.ly/1Kgs15:13), [1KI 16:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:33), [1KI 18:19](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:19) *Elijah summons 400 prophets of Asherah to Mount Carmel.*[2KI 23:4–23:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:4-2Kgs23:7), [2KI 23:13–23:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:13-2Kgs23:16)[ISA 27:9](https://ref.ly/Isa27:9); [JER 17:2](https://ref.ly/Jer17:2); [MIC 5:14](https://ref.ly/Mic5:14)

### Ashtoreth, a goddess of war and fertility

**[JDG 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Judg2:12-Judg2:13)** *Ashtoreth, the consort of Baal, was associated with the evening star and was worshipped as Ishtar in Babylon and as Athtart in Aram. To the Greeks she was Astarte or Aphrodite and to the Romans, Venus.* *See also* [JDG 10:6](https://ref.ly/Judg10:6); [1SA 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:3-1Sam7:4), [1SA 12:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:10), [1SA 31:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam31:10); [1KI 11:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:5), [1KI 11:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:33)

### Baal, a Canaanite and Phoenician god of fertility and rain

**[JDG 2:10–2:13](https://ref.ly/Judg2:10-Judg2:13)** *Baal, meaning “lord”, was pictured standing on a bull, a popular symbol of fertility and strength. Baal was associated with Asherah and Ashtoreth, goddesses of fertility.*

### Baal\-Zebub, a popular deity of the Philistines

**[MAT 12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:24)** parallels [MRK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22), [LUK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Luke11:15) *Beelzebub is the Greek form of the Hebrew name “Baal\-Zebub”, meaning “lord of the flies”.* *See also* [2KI 1:1–1:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:1-2Kgs1:6), [2KI 1:16–1:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:16-2Kgs1:17)

### Bel, the chief deity of Babylon

**[ISA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Isa46:1)** *Bel was another name for the sun god, Marduk. Nebo, the god of learning and writing was the son of Marduk.* *See also* [JER 50:2](https://ref.ly/Jer50:2), [JER 51:44](https://ref.ly/Jer51:44)

### Chemosh, the chief god of Moab

**[1KI 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:7)** *See also* [NUM 21:29](https://ref.ly/Num21:29); [1KI 11:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:33); [2KI 23:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:13); [JER 48:7](https://ref.ly/Jer48:7), [JER 48:13](https://ref.ly/Jer48:13), [JER 48:46](https://ref.ly/Jer48:46)

### Dagon, worshipped in Babylonia and Philistia

**[JDG 16:23](https://ref.ly/Judg16:23)** *See also* [1SA 5:2–5:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam5:2-1Sam5:7); [1CH 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:10)

### Molech, the chief deity of Ammon

**[1KI 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4-1Kgs11:5)** *See also* [LEV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Lev18:21) *The practice of sacrificing children to Molech was common in Phoenicia and the region*; [LEV 20:2–20:5](https://ref.ly/Lev20:2-Lev20:5); [1KI 11:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:7), [1KI 11:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:33); [2KI 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:10) *Josiah destroyed the area where the altars for child sacrifice were located*; [2KI 23:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:13); [ISA 57:9](https://ref.ly/Isa57:9); [JER 32:35](https://ref.ly/Jer32:35), [JER 49:1](https://ref.ly/Jer49:1), [JER 49:3](https://ref.ly/Jer49:3); [ZEP 1:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:5); [ACT 7:43](https://ref.ly/Acts7:43)

### Tammuz, a Babylonian fertility god

[EZK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:14)

The worship of false gods was a snare to God's people
-----------------------------------------------------

### Their worship included disgusting rites

[1KI 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:23-1Kgs14:24), [1KI 19:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:18); ; [JER 7:31](https://ref.ly/Jer7:31); ; [HOS 13:2](https://ref.ly/Hos13:2)

### Numerous attempts were made to stop the worship of Baal and other false gods

**[JDG 6:28–6:32](https://ref.ly/Judg6:28-Judg6:32)**; **[1KI 18:17–18:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:17-1Kgs18:40)** *See also* [2KI 10:18–10:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:18-2Kgs10:28), [2KI 11:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs11:18), [2KI 23:4–23:5](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:4-2Kgs23:5), [2KI 23:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:13)

### Attempts to stop false worship proved unsuccessful

**[2KI 21:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:3)** *The word “Baal” was not orginally a proper name but came to be used as such.* *See also* [2CH 28:1–28:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:1-2Chr28:4); [HOS 13:1–13:2](https://ref.ly/Hos13:1-Hos13:2)

Warnings against and condemnation of, the worship of false gods
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 40:4](https://ref.ly/Ps40:4)**; **[DAN 3:29](https://ref.ly/Dan3:29)** *Nebuchadnezzar came to realise the foolishness of worshipping false gods* *See also* [PSA 4:2](https://ref.ly/Ps4:2); [JER 13:25](https://ref.ly/Jer13:25), [JER 16:19](https://ref.ly/Jer16:19); [AMO 2:4](https://ref.ly/Amos2:4); [ZEP 1:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:4)

The first Christians were confronted with the worship of Greek and Roman deities
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Zeus and Hermes

**[ACT 14:12](https://ref.ly/Acts14:12)** *Zeus was the patron god of the city of Lystra and his temple was there. Paul was identified as the god Hermes (the Roman Mercury), Zeus' attendant and spokesman.*

### Artemis

**[ACT 19:24–19:28](https://ref.ly/Acts19:24-Acts19:28)** *Artemis was the Greek name for the Roman goddess, Diana.*

### Castor and Pollux

*See also* [ACT 28:11](https://ref.ly/Acts28:11) *the two “sons of Zeus”, regarded as the guardian deities of sailors*

Attempts to deify human beings
------------------------------

[ACT 12:21–12:22](https://ref.ly/Acts12:21-Acts12:22), [ACT 14:12–14:15](https://ref.ly/Acts14:12-Acts14:15), [ACT 28:6](https://ref.ly/Acts28:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 46:25; Exodus 34:13-14; Deuteronomy 7:5; Judges 6:25-30; 1 Kings 14:15; 1 Kings 14:23; 1 Kings 15:13; 1 Kings 16:33; 1 Kings 18:19; 2 Kings 23:4-7; 2 Kings 23:13-16; Isaiah 27:9; Jeremiah 17:2; Micah 5:14; Judges 2:12-13; Judges 10:6; 1 Samuel 7:3-4; 1 Samuel 12:10; 1 Samuel 31:10; 1 Kings 11:5; 1 Kings 11:33; Judges 2:10-13; Matthew 12:24; Mark 3:22; Luke 11:15; 2 Kings 1:1-6; 2 Kings 1:16-17; Isaiah 46:1; Jeremiah 50:2; Jeremiah 51:44; 1 Kings 11:7; Numbers 21:29; 2 Kings 23:13; Jeremiah 48:7; Jeremiah 48:13; Jeremiah 48:46; Judges 16:23; 1 Samuel 5:2-7; 1 Chronicles 10:10; 1 Kings 11:4-5; Leviticus 18:21; Leviticus 20:2-5; 2 Kings 23:10; Isaiah 57:9; Jeremiah 32:35; Jeremiah 49:1; Jeremiah 49:3; Zephaniah 1:5; Acts 7:43; Ezekiel 8:14; 1 Kings 14:23-24; 1 Kings 19:18; Jeremiah 7:31; Hosea 13:2; Judges 6:28-32; 1 Kings 18:17-40; 2 Kings 10:18-28; 2 Kings 11:18; 2 Kings 23:4-5; 2 Kings 21:3; 2 Chronicles 28:1-4; Hosea 13:1-2; Psalms 40:4; Daniel 3:29; Psalms 4:2; Jeremiah 13:25; Jeremiah 16:19; Amos 2:4; Zephaniah 1:4; Acts 14:12; Acts 19:24-28; Acts 28:11; Acts 12:21-22; Acts 14:12-15; Acts 28:6
* **Associated Articles:** 1080: God, living and self-sustaining (ID: `1080@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4251: moon (ID: `4251@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6103: abomination (ID: `6103@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6243: adultery, spiritual (ID: `6243@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7312: Baal (ID: `7312@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7324: calf worship (ID: `7324@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7384: household gods (ID: `7384@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7471: temples, heathen (ID: `7471@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8799: polytheism (ID: `8799@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8840: unfaithfulness, to God (ID: `8840@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8748: false religion (id: 8748)

The worship of anything that is not God, including any form of religion that fails to do justice to the glory and majesty of God. Scripture criticises both the practices and the beliefs of false religions.

The nature of false religion
----------------------------

### Man\-made images are worshipped

**[ACT 17:29](https://ref.ly/Acts17:29)** *See also* [DEU 4:28](https://ref.ly/Deut4:28); [PSA 115:4–115:7](https://ref.ly/Ps115:4-Ps115:7), [PSA 135:15–135:17](https://ref.ly/Ps135:15-Ps135:17); [ISA 44:10–44:20](https://ref.ly/Isa44:10-Isa44:20); [JER 10:3–10:5](https://ref.ly/Jer10:3-Jer10:5), [JER 16:20](https://ref.ly/Jer16:20); [DAN 3:4–3:7](https://ref.ly/Dan3:4-Dan3:7); [ACT 19:26](https://ref.ly/Acts19:26)

### Created things are worshipped

**[ROM 1:25](https://ref.ly/Rom1:25)** *See also* [2KI 21:3](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:3) parallel [2CH 33:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:5); [JER 8:2](https://ref.ly/Jer8:2), [JER 19:13](https://ref.ly/Jer19:13); [EZK 8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:16)

### Secular authorities are worshipped

**[REV 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rev13:4)** *The dragon in Revelation may be understood as the devil and the beast as the antichrist: the forces at work behind the human authorities that defy the rule of God.* *See also* [ACT 12:21–12:23](https://ref.ly/Acts12:21-Acts12:23); [REV 13:8](https://ref.ly/Rev13:8), [REV 13:12](https://ref.ly/Rev13:12)

### Demons are worshipped

**[DEU 32:17](https://ref.ly/Deut32:17)** *See also* [LEV 17:7](https://ref.ly/Lev17:7); [PSA 106:37–106:38](https://ref.ly/Ps106:37-Ps106:38); [1CO 10:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:20); [REV 9:20](https://ref.ly/Rev9:20)

False religion is based on man\-made traditions, myths and philosophies
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

[COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8), [COL 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:23); ; [1TI 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:4), [1TI 6:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20)

False religious practices
-------------------------

### Immorality

**[1KI 14:23–14:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:23-1Kgs14:24)** *See also* [EZK 16:16–16:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:16-Ezek16:17); [HOS 4:13–4:14](https://ref.ly/Hos4:13-Hos4:14)

### Human sacrifice

**[2KI 17:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:17)** *See also* [LEV 18:21](https://ref.ly/Lev18:21); [PSA 106:37–106:38](https://ref.ly/Ps106:37-Ps106:38)

False religion ensnares its adherents
-------------------------------------

### It offers no help in time of need

**[1SA 12:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:21)** *See also* [JDG 10:14](https://ref.ly/Judg10:14); [1KI 18:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:29); [2KI 19:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:12) parallel [ISA 37:12](https://ref.ly/Isa37:12); [ISA 16:12](https://ref.ly/Isa16:12), [ISA 44:17–44:20](https://ref.ly/Isa44:17-Isa44:20), [ISA 45:20](https://ref.ly/Isa45:20), [ISA 46:7](https://ref.ly/Isa46:7), [ISA 57:13](https://ref.ly/Isa57:13); [JER 11:12](https://ref.ly/Jer11:12)

### It prevents people from finding the truth

**[JON 2:8](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:8)** *See also* [MAT 23:13](https://ref.ly/Matt23:13); [LUK 11:52](https://ref.ly/Luke11:52); [2CO 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:4)

### It devalues, degrades and defiles

**[2KI 17:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:15)** *See also* [PSA 106:39](https://ref.ly/Ps106:39), [PSA 115:8](https://ref.ly/Ps115:8), [PSA 135:18](https://ref.ly/Ps135:18); [ISA 44:9](https://ref.ly/Isa44:9); [JER 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jer2:5); [EZK 20:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:18)

### It leads to immorality

**[NUM 25:1–25:2](https://ref.ly/Num25:1-Num25:2)** *See also* [NUM 25:3](https://ref.ly/Num25:3), [NUM 25:6–25:8](https://ref.ly/Num25:6-Num25:8); [ROM 1:23–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:23-Rom1:31); [REV 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/Rev9:20-Rev9:21)

### It enslaves

**[GAL 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gal4:8)** *See also* [DEU 12:30](https://ref.ly/Deut12:30); [GAL 4:3](https://ref.ly/Gal4:3), [GAL 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Gal4:9-Gal4:10); [COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)

False religion receives the full force of God's anger
-----------------------------------------------------

**[JER 16:18](https://ref.ly/Jer16:18)** *See also* [1KI 13:1–13:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:1-1Kgs13:3), [1KI 13:33–13:34](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:33-1Kgs13:34); [2KI 17:18–17:23](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:18-2Kgs17:23); [JER 22:8–22:9](https://ref.ly/Jer22:8-Jer22:9); [MIC 5:13–5:15](https://ref.ly/Mic5:13-Mic5:15); [NAM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Nah1:14); [ZEP 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:4-Zeph1:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 17:29; Deuteronomy 4:28; Psalms 115:4-7; Psalms 135:15-17; Isaiah 44:10-20; Jeremiah 10:3-5; Jeremiah 16:20; Daniel 3:4-7; Acts 19:26; Romans 1:25; 2 Kings 21:3; 2 Chronicles 33:5; Jeremiah 8:2; Jeremiah 19:13; Ezekiel 8:16; Revelation 13:4; Acts 12:21-23; Revelation 13:8; Revelation 13:12; Deuteronomy 32:17; Leviticus 17:7; Psalms 106:37-38; 1 Corinthians 10:20; Revelation 9:20; Colossians 2:8; Colossians 2:20-23; 1 Timothy 1:4; 1 Timothy 6:20; 1 Kings 14:23-24; Ezekiel 16:16-17; Hosea 4:13-14; 2 Kings 17:17; Leviticus 18:21; 1 Samuel 12:21; Judges 10:14; 1 Kings 18:29; 2 Kings 19:12; Isaiah 37:12; Isaiah 16:12; Isaiah 44:17-20; Isaiah 45:20; Isaiah 46:7; Isaiah 57:13; Jeremiah 11:12; Jonah 2:8; Matthew 23:13; Luke 11:52; 2 Corinthians 4:4; 2 Kings 17:15; Psalms 106:39; Psalms 115:8; Psalms 135:18; Isaiah 44:9; Jeremiah 2:5; Ezekiel 20:18; Numbers 25:1-2; Numbers 25:3; Numbers 25:6-8; Romans 1:23-31; Revelation 9:20-21; Galatians 4:8; Deuteronomy 12:30; Galatians 4:3; Galatians 4:9-10; Jeremiah 16:18; 1 Kings 13:1-3; 1 Kings 13:33-34; 2 Kings 17:18-23; Jeremiah 22:8-9; Micah 5:13-15; Nahum 1:14; Zephaniah 1:4-5
* **Associated Articles:** 4130: demons (ID: `4130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6187: immorality (ID: `6187@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7348: defilement (ID: `7348@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7449: slavery, spiritual (ID: `7449@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8142: religion (ID: `8142@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8747: false gods (ID: `8747@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8750: false teachings (ID: `8750@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8784: nominal religion (ID: `8784@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8807: profanity (ID: `8807@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8829: superstition (ID: `8829@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8749: false teachers (id: 8749)

Those who teach error and in so doing lead others astray. They are to be distinguished from false prophets who are equally condemned in Scripture.

The OT prophets warned God's people against being led astray by false teachers
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 9:16](https://ref.ly/Isa9:16)**; **[EZK 34:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:2)** *See also* [ISA 3:12](https://ref.ly/Isa3:12), [ISA 8:20](https://ref.ly/Isa8:20); [JER 2:8](https://ref.ly/Jer2:8), [JER 10:21](https://ref.ly/Jer10:21), [JER 23:1](https://ref.ly/Jer23:1); [EZK 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:26); [MAL 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Mal2:7-Mal2:8)

Jesus Christ condemned false teachers
-------------------------------------

### He opposed legalism and hypocrisy

**[MAT 5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:19)**

### He identified the teachers of the law and Pharisees as false teachers

**[MAT 23:1–23:33](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:33)** parallel [LUK 11:42–11:52](https://ref.ly/Luke11:42-Luke11:52) *See also* [MAT 15:14](https://ref.ly/Matt15:14)

### He warned his followers against them

**[MAT 16:6](https://ref.ly/Matt16:6), [MAT 16:12](https://ref.ly/Matt16:12)** parallels [MRK 8:15](https://ref.ly/Mark8:15), [LUK 12:1](https://ref.ly/Luke12:1)

The condemnation of false teachers in the early church
------------------------------------------------------

### Paul urges his readers to avoid false teachers

**[1TI 6:3–6:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:3-1Tim6:6)** *See also* [ACT 20:29–20:31](https://ref.ly/Acts20:29-Acts20:31); [ROM 16:17–16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:17-Rom16:18); [2CO 11:3–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3-2Cor11:4) *The Corinthian church had been undiscerningly tolerant of Jewish deceivers in their midst*; [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14)COL [2:4](https://ref.ly/Eph2:4), [COL 2:81](https://ref.ly/Col2:81)TI [1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Col1:3-Col1:4), [1TI 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:7)[1TI 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:3), [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21); [TIT 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:11); [HEB 13:9](https://ref.ly/Heb13:9); [2JN 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/2John1:9-2John1:10)REV [2:6](https://ref.ly/2John2:6), [REV 2:15](https://ref.ly/Rev2:15)[REV 2:14](https://ref.ly/Rev2:14) *the followers of Balaam*; [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20)

### Hymenaeus and Philetus are cited as examples of false teachers

[2TI 2:17–2:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:17-2Tim2:18)

### Those who teach will be judged particularly strictly

**[JAS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Jas3:1)** *See also* [2CO 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:13-2Cor11:15); [GAL 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:9), [GAL 5:10](https://ref.ly/Gal5:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 9:16; Ezekiel 34:2; Isaiah 3:12; Isaiah 8:20; Jeremiah 2:8; Jeremiah 10:21; Jeremiah 23:1; Ezekiel 22:26; Malachi 2:7-8; Matthew 5:19; Matthew 23:1-33; Luke 11:42-52; Matthew 15:14; Matthew 16:6; Matthew 16:12; Mark 8:15; Luke 12:1; 1 Timothy 6:3-6; Acts 20:29-31; Romans 16:17-18; 2 Corinthians 11:3-4; Ephesians 4:14; Ephesians 2:4; Colossians 2:81; Colossians 1:3-4; 1 Timothy 1:7; 1 Timothy 4:1-3; 1 Timothy 6:20-21; Titus 1:10-11; Hebrews 13:9; 2 John 1:9-10; 2 John 2:6; Revelation 2:15; Revelation 2:14; Revelation 2:20; 2 Timothy 2:17-18; James 3:1; 2 Corinthians 11:13-15; Galatians 1:6-9; Galatians 5:10
* **Associated Articles:** 5509: rulers (ID: `5509@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5804: charm (ID: `5804@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5863: flattery (ID: `5863@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7464: teachers of the law (ID: `7464@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7550: Pharisees (ID: `7550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7766: priests (ID: `7766@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7784: shepherd (ID: `7784@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7797: teaching (ID: `7797@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8704: apostasy (ID: `8704@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8750: false teachings (ID: `8750@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8751: false witness (ID: `8751@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8766: heresies (ID: `8766@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8750: false teachings (id: 8750)

Scripture repeatedly warns against false teachings, which deny or distort some aspect of the gospel. The origin of such teachings is attributed either to human error or to demonic inspiration.

Examples of false teachings
---------------------------

**[REV 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Rev2:14-Rev2:15)** *See also* [MAT 5:19](https://ref.ly/Matt5:19); [2TH 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:1-2Thess2:2); [1TI 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:3); [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20)

Qualities of false teachings
----------------------------

### They are valueless

**[MAT 15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:9)** parallel [MRK 7:7](https://ref.ly/Mark7:7) *See also* [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13); [EPH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eph5:6); [COL 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:23); [1TI 1:3–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:7)

### They are destructive in their nature

**[2PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:1)** *See also* [MAT 7:15](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15); [ACT 20:29–20:30](https://ref.ly/Acts20:29-Acts20:30); [TIT 1:10–1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:11)

### They have the capacity to lead people astray

**[ACT 20:30](https://ref.ly/Acts20:30)** *See also* [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14); [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21); [HEB 13:9](https://ref.ly/Heb13:9); [2PE 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:14-2Pet2:15); [1JN 2:26](https://ref.ly/1John2:26)

### They may be popular

**[2PE 2:2](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:2)** *See also* [MAT 24:4–24:5](https://ref.ly/Matt24:4-Matt24:5) parallel [MRK 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Mark13:5-Mark13:6); [2TI 4:3](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:3); [2PE 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:18-2Pet2:19); [1JN 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/1John4:1-1John4:5)

### They may be accompanied by miraculous signs

**[MAT 24:24](https://ref.ly/Matt24:24)** parallel [MRK 13:22](https://ref.ly/Mark13:22) *See also* [2TH 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:9-2Thess2:10); [REV 13:11–13:15](https://ref.ly/Rev13:11-Rev13:15)

The origin of false teachings
-----------------------------

### Demonic inspiration

**[1TI 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:2)** *See also* [2CO 11:3–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:3-2Cor11:4); [1JN 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/1John4:1-1John4:3); [REV 16:13–16:14](https://ref.ly/Rev16:13-Rev16:14)

### Human error

**[2PE 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:3)** *See also* [COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8); [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21)

Discerning false teachings
--------------------------

### By their content

**[1JN 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/1John4:2-1John4:3)** *See also* [1CO 12:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:3); [1JN 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/1John2:20-1John2:23); [2JN 1:7–1:11](https://ref.ly/2John1:7-2John1:11)

### By the teacher's lifestyle

**[MAT 7:15–7:20](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15-Matt7:20)** *See also* [2CO 11:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:20); [1JN 3:7–3:10](https://ref.ly/1John3:7-1John3:10); [JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)

### By their effects

**[1TI 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:3-1Tim6:5)** *See also* [1TI 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:4); [2TI 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:16-2Tim2:18); [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20)

The antidote to false teachings
-------------------------------

### Holding fast to the true gospel

**[1JN 2:24](https://ref.ly/1John2:24)** *See also* [GAL 1:6–1:9](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:9); [1TI 1:18–1:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:18-1Tim1:20); [2TI 1:13–1:14](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:13-2Tim1:14)

### Shunning false teachers

**[ROM 16:17–16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:17-Rom16:18)** *See also* [2TI 3:1–3:9](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:9); [TIT 3:9–3:11](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9-Titus3:11); [2JN 1:9–1:11](https://ref.ly/2John1:9-2John1:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Revelation 2:14-15; Matthew 5:19; 2 Thessalonians 2:1-2; 1 Timothy 4:1-3; Revelation 2:20; Matthew 15:9; Mark 7:7; Isaiah 29:13; Ephesians 5:6; Colossians 2:20-23; 1 Timothy 1:3-7; 2 Peter 2:1; Matthew 7:15; Acts 20:29-30; Titus 1:10-11; Acts 20:30; Ephesians 4:14; 1 Timothy 6:20-21; Hebrews 13:9; 2 Peter 2:14-15; 1 John 2:26; 2 Peter 2:2; Matthew 24:4-5; Mark 13:5-6; 2 Timothy 4:3; 2 Peter 2:18-19; 1 John 4:1-5; Matthew 24:24; Mark 13:22; 2 Thessalonians 2:9-10; Revelation 13:11-15; 1 Timothy 4:1-2; 2 Corinthians 11:3-4; 1 John 4:1-3; Revelation 16:13-14; 2 Peter 2:3; Colossians 2:8; 1 John 4:2-3; 1 Corinthians 12:3; 1 John 2:20-23; 2 John 1:7-11; Matthew 7:15-20; 2 Corinthians 11:20; 1 John 3:7-10; Jude 1:4; 1 Timothy 6:3-5; 1 Timothy 1:3-4; 2 Timothy 2:16-18; 1 John 2:24; Galatians 1:6-9; 1 Timothy 1:18-20; 2 Timothy 1:13-14; Romans 16:17-18; 2 Timothy 3:1-9; Titus 3:9-11; 2 John 1:9-11

## 8751: false witness (id: 8751)

The telling of lies about another so as to make them appear guilty when they are innocent. Condemned in Scripture, false witness was experienced by Jesus Christ and is to be expected by those who follow him.

False witness is condemned in Scripture
---------------------------------------

**[EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16)** parallel [DEU 5:20](https://ref.ly/Deut5:20) *See also* [EXO 23:1](https://ref.ly/Exod23:1); [DEU 19:15–19:19](https://ref.ly/Deut19:15-Deut19:19); [PSA 101:5–101:7](https://ref.ly/Ps101:5-Ps101:7); [MAT 19:18](https://ref.ly/Matt19:18) parallels [MRK 10:19](https://ref.ly/Mark10:19), [LUK 18:20](https://ref.ly/Luke18:20)

False witness arises from an evil heart
---------------------------------------

**[MAT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19-Matt15:20)** parallel [MRK 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:23)

False witness will be subject to judgment
-----------------------------------------

**[PSA 101:5–101:8](https://ref.ly/Ps101:5-Ps101:8)**; **[PRO 21:28](https://ref.ly/Prov21:28)** *See also* [PRO 19:9](https://ref.ly/Prov19:9); [DAN 6:24](https://ref.ly/Dan6:24); [MAT 12:35–12:37](https://ref.ly/Matt12:35-Matt12:37)

Examples of those who were the victims of false witness
-------------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ

**[MAT 26:59–26:61](https://ref.ly/Matt26:59-Matt26:61)** parallel [MRK 14:55–14:59](https://ref.ly/Mark14:55-Mark14:59) *See also* [MAT 11:18–11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:18-Matt11:19) parallel [LUK 7:33–7:34](https://ref.ly/Luke7:33-Luke7:34)

### Other examples

[1KI 21:8–21:14](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8-1Kgs21:14) *Naboth*; ; [PSA 27:12](https://ref.ly/Ps27:12) *David*; ; [DAN 6:24](https://ref.ly/Dan6:24) *Daniel*; ; [ACT 6:11–6:13](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11-Acts6:13) *Stephen*; [ACT 17:5–17:7](https://ref.ly/Acts17:5-Acts17:7), [ACT 24:5–24:9](https://ref.ly/Acts24:5-Acts24:9), [ACT 25:7](https://ref.ly/Acts25:7); ; [ROM 3:8](https://ref.ly/Rom3:8)

False witness against believers is to be expected by them
---------------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Matt5:11-Matt5:12)** parallel [LUK 6:22–6:23](https://ref.ly/Luke6:22-Luke6:23) *See also* [MAT 24:9–24:10](https://ref.ly/Matt24:9-Matt24:10); [ACT 19:9](https://ref.ly/Acts19:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:16; Deuteronomy 5:20; Exodus 23:1; Deuteronomy 19:15-19; Psalms 101:5-7; Matthew 19:18; Mark 10:19; Luke 18:20; Matthew 15:19-20; Mark 7:21-23; Psalms 101:5-8; Proverbs 21:28; Proverbs 19:9; Daniel 6:24; Matthew 12:35-37; Matthew 26:59-61; Mark 14:55-59; Matthew 11:18-19; Luke 7:33-34; 1 Kings 21:8-14; Psalms 27:12; Acts 6:11-13; Acts 17:5-7; Acts 24:5-9; Acts 25:7; Romans 3:8; Matthew 5:11-12; Luke 6:22-23; Matthew 24:9-10; Acts 19:9
* **Associated Articles:** 2585: Jesus Christ, trial of (ID: `2585@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5202: accusation, false (ID: `5202@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5625: witnesses, false (ID: `5625@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5951: slander (ID: `5951@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8776: lies (ID: `8776@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8752: false worship (id: 8752)

Forms of worship that are not worthy of God, especially worship offered without sincerity and obedience or that is contrary to the commands of God.

Examples of false worship
-------------------------

### Worship conducted in disobedience to God

**[LEV 10:1–10:2](https://ref.ly/Lev10:1-Lev10:2)** *See also* [NUM 3:2–3:4](https://ref.ly/Num3:2-Num3:4); [1SA 15:1–15:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:1-1Sam15:23); [2KI 17:33](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:33)

### Worship and sacrifices offered by those who were not priests

**[1SA 13:11–13:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:11-1Sam13:14)** *See also* [2CH 26:19–26:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:19-2Chr26:20)

### Insincere worship

**[ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)** *See also* [MAT 15:8–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:8-Matt15:9); [MRK 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6-Mark7:7); [COL 2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:23)

### Worship lacking in due humility and reverence

**[MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5)** *See also* [LUK 18:10–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:10-Luke18:14)

### Worship conducted in ignorance of God

**[JHN 4:22](https://ref.ly/John4:22)** *See also* [ACT 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:22-Acts17:23)

Scripture vividly contrasts false worship with true worship
-----------------------------------------------------------

### True worship involves the worshipper's heart, soul and mind

[DEU 6:5](https://ref.ly/Deut6:5) parallels [MAT 22:37](https://ref.ly/Matt22:37), [MRK 12:30](https://ref.ly/Mark12:30), [LUK 10:27](https://ref.ly/Luke10:27)

### True worship is costly

[2SA 24:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:24); ; [MRK 12:41–12:44](https://ref.ly/Mark12:41-Mark12:44); ; [ROM 12:1](https://ref.ly/Rom12:1) *True worship involves the whole of a person's life and not just formal acts of worship.*

### True worship is in the spirit

**[JHN 4:23–4:24](https://ref.ly/John4:23-John4:24)** *See also* [ISA 29:23](https://ref.ly/Isa29:23); [PHP 3:3](https://ref.ly/Phil3:3); [1TI 2:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim2:9); [HEB 12:28](https://ref.ly/Heb12:28)

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 10:1-2; Numbers 3:2-4; 1 Samuel 15:1-23; 2 Kings 17:33; 1 Samuel 13:11-14; 2 Chronicles 26:19-20; Isaiah 29:13; Matthew 15:8-9; Mark 7:6-7; Colossians 2:23; Matthew 6:5; Luke 18:10-14; John 4:22; Acts 17:22-23; Deuteronomy 6:5; Matthew 22:37; Mark 12:30; Luke 10:27; 2 Samuel 24:24; Mark 12:41-44; Romans 12:1; John 4:23-24; Isaiah 29:23; Philippians 3:3; 1 Timothy 2:9; Hebrews 12:28
* **Associated Articles:** 5135: blindness, spiritual (ID: `5135@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6183: ignorance, of God (ID: `6183@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7413: priesthood, in OT (ID: `7413@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8218: consecration (ID: `8218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8333: reverence (ID: `8333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8622: worship (ID: `8622@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8718: disobedience (ID: `8718@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8748: false religion (ID: `8748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8784: nominal religion (ID: `8784@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8753: favouritism (id: 8753)

The unfair treatment of a person or group in preference to another. Favouritism is shown in Scripture as causing division both in the family and in the church. It is warned against in the administration of justice. The human tendency to partiality is contrasted with God's impartial dealings.

Favouritism in family life
--------------------------

### Isaac's preference for Esau

[GEN 25:23](https://ref.ly/Gen25:23), [GEN 25:28](https://ref.ly/Gen25:28)

### Rebekah's preference for Jacob

[GEN 27:6–27:10](https://ref.ly/Gen27:6-Gen27:10)

### Jacob's preference for Joseph

[GEN 37:3–37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:3-Gen37:4)

### Elkanah's preference for Hannah

[1SA 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:5-1Sam1:6)

### The child of a favourite wife must not receive preferential treatment

[DEU 21:15–21:16](https://ref.ly/Deut21:15-Deut21:16)

Favouritism in church relationships
-----------------------------------

### In the treatment of widows

[ACT 6:1](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1)

### James warned against favouring rich Christians

[JAS 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Jas2:1-Jas2:4), [JAS 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Jas2:8-Jas2:9)

### Paul warned Timothy against showing favouritism

**[1TI 5:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:21)**

Justice must be impartial
-------------------------

**[LEV 19:15](https://ref.ly/Lev19:15)** *See also* [EXO 23:3](https://ref.ly/Exod23:3); [DEU 16:19](https://ref.ly/Deut16:19); [JOB 13:10](https://ref.ly/Job13:10); [PSA 82:2](https://ref.ly/Ps82:2); [PRO 24:23](https://ref.ly/Prov24:23), [PRO 28:21](https://ref.ly/Prov28:21); [MAL 2:9](https://ref.ly/Mal2:9)

God has no favourites
---------------------

**[ROM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:11)** *See also* [JOB 34:19](https://ref.ly/Job34:19); [MAT 5:45](https://ref.ly/Matt5:45)

### God has no favourite nation

**[ACT 10:34–10:35](https://ref.ly/Acts10:34-Acts10:35)** *See also* [ROM 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rom2:10); [EPH 6:8–6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:8-Eph6:9)

### God does not differentiate between Jewish and Gentile Christians

[ACT 15:7–15:9](https://ref.ly/Acts15:7-Acts15:9); ; [ROM 10:12–10:13](https://ref.ly/Rom10:12-Rom10:13)

### God judges all without favouritism

[DEU 10:17](https://ref.ly/Deut10:17); ; [2CH 19:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:7); ; [ROM 2:9](https://ref.ly/Rom2:9); ; [COL 3:25](https://ref.ly/Col3:25); ; [1PE 1:17](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 25:23; Genesis 25:28; Genesis 27:6-10; Genesis 37:3-4; 1 Samuel 1:5-6; Deuteronomy 21:15-16; Acts 6:1; James 2:1-4; James 2:8-9; 1 Timothy 5:21; Leviticus 19:15; Exodus 23:3; Deuteronomy 16:19; Job 13:10; Psalms 82:2; Proverbs 24:23; Proverbs 28:21; Malachi 2:9; Romans 2:11; Job 34:19; Matthew 5:45; Acts 10:34-35; Romans 2:10; Ephesians 6:8-9; Acts 15:7-9; Romans 10:12-13; Deuteronomy 10:17; 2 Chronicles 19:7; Romans 2:9; Colossians 3:25; 1 Peter 1:17
* **Associated Articles:** 2042: Jesus Christ, justice of (ID: `2042@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5095: Jacob, life and character of (ID: `5095@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5347: injustice (ID: `5347@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5359: justice (ID: `5359@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5765: attitudes, to other people (ID: `5765@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5859: favour (ID: `5859@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5863: flattery (ID: `5863@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5882: impartiality (ID: `5882@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8773: jealousy (ID: `8773@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8800: prejudice (ID: `8800@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8754: fear (id: 8754)

An attitude of anxiety or distress, caused by concern over a threat to one's future. Scripture provides numerous examples of situations in which fear is experienced. It declares, however, that God alone is to be feared, and moments of human fear can be opportunities for deepening faith in him.

Reverent fear of God commended
------------------------------

**[PRO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Prov1:7)** *See also* [DEU 6:13](https://ref.ly/Deut6:13), [DEU 31:12–31:13](https://ref.ly/Deut31:12-Deut31:13); [JOS 4:24](https://ref.ly/Josh4:24), [JOS 24:14](https://ref.ly/Josh24:14); [1SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:24); [PSA 2:11](https://ref.ly/Ps2:11), [PSA 34:7–34:9](https://ref.ly/Ps34:7-Ps34:9); [PRO 15:33](https://ref.ly/Prov15:33); [ECC 12:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:13); [ISA 33:6](https://ref.ly/Isa33:6); [REV 14:7](https://ref.ly/Rev14:7)

Fear of natural phenomena
-------------------------

**[PSA 91:5](https://ref.ly/Ps91:5)**; **[JON 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:4-Jonah1:5)** *See also* [GEN 19:30](https://ref.ly/Gen19:30); [JOB 5:22](https://ref.ly/Job5:22); [ECC 12:5](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:5); [ISA 7:25](https://ref.ly/Isa7:25); [MAT 8:26](https://ref.ly/Matt8:26); [REV 11:13](https://ref.ly/Rev11:13)

Fear of the unnatural
---------------------

### Fear of thoughts and visions

**[DAN 4:5](https://ref.ly/Dan4:5)**; **[ACT 10:4](https://ref.ly/Acts10:4)** *See also* [1SA 3:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:15); [JOB 4:13–4:15](https://ref.ly/Job4:13-Job4:15); [ISA 21:4](https://ref.ly/Isa21:4); [DAN 8:17](https://ref.ly/Dan8:17), [DAN 10:11–10:12](https://ref.ly/Dan10:11-Dan10:12); [MAT 1:20](https://ref.ly/Matt1:20), [MAT 28:4](https://ref.ly/Matt28:4); [LUK 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Luke1:12-Luke1:13), [LUK 1:29–1:30](https://ref.ly/Luke1:29-Luke1:30), [LUK 2:9–2:10](https://ref.ly/Luke2:9-Luke2:10)

### Fear of the unknown or the abnormal

**[MAT 14:26–14:27](https://ref.ly/Matt14:26-Matt14:27)** parallels [MRK 6:50](https://ref.ly/Mark6:50), [JHN 6:19–6:20](https://ref.ly/John6:19-John6:20)**[ACT 5:5](https://ref.ly/Acts5:5)** *See also* [EXO 20:18](https://ref.ly/Exod20:18), [EXO 34:30](https://ref.ly/Exod34:30) *The radiance of Moses' face reflected God's glory and made the Israelites afraid*; [DEU 5:5](https://ref.ly/Deut5:5); [1SA 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam4:7); [NEH 6:16](https://ref.ly/Neh6:16); [JER 10:2](https://ref.ly/Jer10:2); [DAN 5:9](https://ref.ly/Dan5:9) *Belshazzar's fear at the writing on the wall*; [JON 1:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:10); [MAT 17:6](https://ref.ly/Matt17:6) *the fear of the disciples at the transfiguration*; [MAT 27:54](https://ref.ly/Matt27:54); [MRK 5:33](https://ref.ly/Mark5:33), [MRK 9:32](https://ref.ly/Mark9:32), [MRK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Mark16:8); [LUK 5:10](https://ref.ly/Luke5:10), [LUK 8:37](https://ref.ly/Luke8:37) *Fear seized the people when the Gerasene demoniac was healed*; [LUK 9:34](https://ref.ly/Luke9:34); [JHN 19:8](https://ref.ly/John19:8); [ACT 19:17](https://ref.ly/Acts19:17)

Fear of embarrassment or shame
------------------------------

**[GEN 3:10](https://ref.ly/Gen3:10)** *See also* [ISA 54:4](https://ref.ly/Isa54:4); [JHN 3:20](https://ref.ly/John3:20); [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20)

Fear of the future
------------------

**[JER 51:46](https://ref.ly/Jer51:46)** *See also* [ISA 41:23](https://ref.ly/Isa41:23); [MRK 10:32](https://ref.ly/Mark10:32); [LUK 12:32](https://ref.ly/Luke12:32); [ACT 27:24](https://ref.ly/Acts27:24)

Fear of persecution
-------------------

**[EZR 4:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:4)**; **[JHN 12:42–12:43](https://ref.ly/John12:42-John12:43)** *See also* [1KI 19:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3); [JHN 7:13](https://ref.ly/John7:13), [JHN 9:22](https://ref.ly/John9:22), [JHN 20:19](https://ref.ly/John20:19); [1PE 3:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:14); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10)

Fear of death
-------------

**[HEB 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Heb2:14-Heb2:15)** *See also* [PSA 55:4–55:5](https://ref.ly/Ps55:4-Ps55:5); [MRK 5:36](https://ref.ly/Mark5:36) parallel [LUK 8:50](https://ref.ly/Luke8:50); [ACT 23:10](https://ref.ly/Acts23:10)

Fear of people
--------------

### Of enemies

**[EXO 14:10](https://ref.ly/Exod14:10)**; **[1SA 7:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:7)** *See also* [NUM 22:3](https://ref.ly/Num22:3); [JDG 7:3](https://ref.ly/Judg7:3); [1SA 17:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:24), [1SA 17:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:11); [2SA 10:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:19); [2CH 12:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:5); [PSA 27:1–27:3](https://ref.ly/Ps27:1-Ps27:3), [PSA 56:1–56:4](https://ref.ly/Ps56:1-Ps56:4); [ISA 20:5](https://ref.ly/Isa20:5), [ISA 41:5](https://ref.ly/Isa41:5); [JER 22:25](https://ref.ly/Jer22:25), [JER 41:18](https://ref.ly/Jer41:18); [OBA 1:9](https://ref.ly/Obad1:9)

### Of one's own family

**[GEN 32:11](https://ref.ly/Gen32:11), [GEN 45:3](https://ref.ly/Gen45:3)**; **[JDG 9:21](https://ref.ly/Judg9:21)** *See also* [GEN 31:31](https://ref.ly/Gen31:31), [GEN 32:7](https://ref.ly/Gen32:7), [GEN 42:4](https://ref.ly/Gen42:4), [GEN 50:19](https://ref.ly/Gen50:19); [JDG 6:27](https://ref.ly/Judg6:27)

### Of particular people

**[1SA 15:24](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:24)**; **[1CO 2:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:3)** *See also* [GEN 26:7](https://ref.ly/Gen26:7); [EXO 2:14](https://ref.ly/Exod2:14); [DEU 2:4](https://ref.ly/Deut2:4); [1SA 18:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:15) *Saul's fear of David*; [2SA 3:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:11); [NEH 2:2](https://ref.ly/Neh2:2) *Nehemiah's fear before the king*; [EST 7:6](https://ref.ly/Esth7:6); [JER 26:21](https://ref.ly/Jer26:21), [JER 38:19](https://ref.ly/Jer38:19); [MAT 2:22](https://ref.ly/Matt2:22); [MRK 12:12](https://ref.ly/Mark12:12); [LUK 19:21](https://ref.ly/Luke19:21); [ACT 9:26](https://ref.ly/Acts9:26) *the disciples fear of Paul, not believing his conversion*; [1CO 16:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:10); [2CO 7:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor7:15); [GAL 2:12](https://ref.ly/Gal2:12)

### Of human beings in general

**[PRO 29:25](https://ref.ly/Prov29:25)** *See also* [DEU 1:17](https://ref.ly/Deut1:17); [ISA 51:7](https://ref.ly/Isa51:7)

Fear of judgment
----------------

**[EZK 11:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek11:8)**; **[ACT 24:25](https://ref.ly/Acts24:25)** *See also* [1SA 28:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:20); [1CH 21:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:30); [ISA 33:14](https://ref.ly/Isa33:14); [JER 17:18](https://ref.ly/Jer17:18), [JER 30:5](https://ref.ly/Jer30:5), [JER 42:15–42:16](https://ref.ly/Jer42:15-Jer42:16); [EZK 12:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:18), [EZK 27:35](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:35); [ZEC 9:5](https://ref.ly/Zech9:5); [1JN 4:18](https://ref.ly/1John4:18) *Christians, assured of God's love, no longer fear judgment*; [REV 18:10](https://ref.ly/Rev18:10), [REV 18:15](https://ref.ly/Rev18:15)

Fear as a deterrent
-------------------

**[ROM 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rom13:4)** *See also* [DEU 13:11](https://ref.ly/Deut13:11), [DEU 17:13](https://ref.ly/Deut17:13), [DEU 19:20](https://ref.ly/Deut19:20), [DEU 21:21](https://ref.ly/Deut21:21); [JOB 19:28–19:29](https://ref.ly/Job19:28-Job19:29)

--------------------------------

## 8755: folly (id: 8755)

A human attitude of lack of wisdom or contempt for wisdom, which shows itself in a lack of understanding and obedience. Scripture contrasts human foolishness with divine wisdom, and indicates the serious consequences of a rejection of the wisdom of God.

* **Associated Articles:** 8756: folly, examples of (ID: `8756@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8757: folly, effects of (ID: `8757@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8756: folly, examples of (id: 8756)

Human folly is seen in rebellion against God, ignoring his way and will, a failure to understand him: and moral or spiritual irresponsibility.

Examples of human folly
-----------------------

### The folly of God's people

**[JER 4:22](https://ref.ly/Jer4:22)** *See also* [DEU 32:6](https://ref.ly/Deut32:6) *rejecting God*; [JER 10:8](https://ref.ly/Jer10:8) *worshipping idols*; [HOS 7:11](https://ref.ly/Hos7:11) *seeking foreign help rather than trusting God*

### The folly of Israel's leaders

**[JER 10:21](https://ref.ly/Jer10:21)** *The shepherds are those entrusted with the task of guiding and caring for God's people*; **[EZK 13:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:3)** *See also* [MAT 23:16–23:17](https://ref.ly/Matt23:16-Matt23:17)

### Particular demonstrations of human folly

[NUM 12:1–12:11](https://ref.ly/Num12:1-Num12:11) *Aaron and Miriam*; [1SA 13:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:13), [1SA 26:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:21); [1SA 25:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:25) *Nabal. In Hebrew this name means “fool”.*; [2SA 24:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:10) parallel [1CH 21:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:8); [2CH 16:7–16:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:7-2Chr16:9) *Asa, king of Judah*; ; [ISA 19:11–19:13](https://ref.ly/Isa19:11-Isa19:13) *Pharaoh's advisors*; ; [MAT 7:26](https://ref.ly/Matt7:26) *the foolish builder*; ; [MAT 25:1–25:3](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:3) *the foolish virgins*; ; [LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21) *the rich fool*; ; [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13) *the Galatian Christians*

Paul's \`\`foolishness''
------------------------

**[2CO 11:16–11:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:16-2Cor11:21)** *Paul engages in what he admits is the foolish boasting of his accomplishments in order to challenge the claims of false “apostles”.* *See also* [2CO 11:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:1), [2CO 12:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:6), [2CO 12:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:11)

God's wisdom regarded as \`\`foolishness''
------------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:18)** *See also* [HOS 9:7](https://ref.ly/Hos9:7); [ZEC 11:15](https://ref.ly/Zech11:15); [1CO 1:20–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:20-1Cor1:25), [1CO 1:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:27), [1CO 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:10)

Human wisdom is foolishness to God
----------------------------------

**[1CO 3:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:19)** *See also* [JOB 12:17](https://ref.ly/Job12:17); [ISA 29:14](https://ref.ly/Isa29:14), [ISA 44:25](https://ref.ly/Isa44:25), [ISA 55:8](https://ref.ly/Isa55:8); [JER 50:36](https://ref.ly/Jer50:36); [ROM 1:22](https://ref.ly/Rom1:22)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 4:22; Deuteronomy 32:6; Jeremiah 10:8; Hosea 7:11; Jeremiah 10:21; Ezekiel 13:3; Matthew 23:16-17; Numbers 12:1-11; 1 Samuel 13:13; 1 Samuel 26:21; 1 Samuel 25:25; 2 Samuel 24:10; 1 Chronicles 21:8; 2 Chronicles 16:7-9; Isaiah 19:11-13; Matthew 7:26; Matthew 25:1-3; Luke 12:16-21; Galatians 3:13; 2 Corinthians 11:16-21; 2 Corinthians 11:1; 2 Corinthians 12:6; 2 Corinthians 12:11; 1 Corinthians 1:18; Hosea 9:7; Zechariah 11:15; 1 Corinthians 1:20-25; 1 Corinthians 1:27; 1 Corinthians 4:10; 1 Corinthians 3:19; Job 12:17; Isaiah 29:14; Isaiah 44:25; Isaiah 55:8; Jeremiah 50:36; Romans 1:22
* **Associated Articles:** 1180: God, wisdom of (ID: `1180@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5164: lips (ID: `5164@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5173: outward appearance (ID: `5173@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5401: madness (ID: `5401@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5575: talk, idle (ID: `5575@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5813: conceit (ID: `5813@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5907: miserliness (ID: `5907@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6121: boasting (ID: `6121@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6188: immorality, nature of sexual (ID: `6188@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6222: rebellion, against God (ID: `6222@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8361: wisdom (ID: `8361@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8759: fools (ID: `8759@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8757: folly, effects of (id: 8757)

The attitude that leads to a closing of the mind towards God and his truth, and a false self\-confidence, which results in being easily led astray, failure to see danger, unpreparedness and ultimate ruin.

Folly gives a false basis for confidence
----------------------------------------

### Contempt for God's wisdom

**[PRO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Prov1:7)** *See also* [JOB 28:28](https://ref.ly/Job28:28); [PSA 14:1](https://ref.ly/Ps14:1), [PSA 85:8](https://ref.ly/Ps85:8), [PSA 111:10](https://ref.ly/Ps111:10); [PRO 1:20–1:27](https://ref.ly/Prov1:20-Prov1:27), [PRO 12:1](https://ref.ly/Prov12:1), [PRO 18:2](https://ref.ly/Prov18:2)

### Blindness to God's truth

**[EPH 5:17](https://ref.ly/Eph5:17)** *See also* [PSA 92:6](https://ref.ly/Ps92:6); [ECC 5:1–5:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1-Eccl5:4); [JER 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jer5:4); [LUK 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Luke11:39-Luke11:40) *hypocrisy in worship*; [ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21); [1CO 15:36](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:36) *failure to understand the nature of death and resurrection*; [2TI 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:23); [TIT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9) *futile controversies*

### Rebellion against God's authority

[PSA 107:17](https://ref.ly/Ps107:17); ; [JER 5:21–5:25](https://ref.ly/Jer5:21-Jer5:25)

### Self\-reliance

**[PRO 28:26](https://ref.ly/Prov28:26)** *See also* [PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28), [PRO 12:15](https://ref.ly/Prov12:15), [PRO 14:12](https://ref.ly/Prov14:12); [LUK 12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:20)

Folly cannot be hidden
----------------------

**[ECC 10:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:3)** *See also* [PSA 69:5](https://ref.ly/Ps69:5); [PRO 12:23](https://ref.ly/Prov12:23), [PRO 13:16](https://ref.ly/Prov13:16), [PRO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Prov15:2); [2TI 3:9](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:9)

Folly opens the way to deception
--------------------------------

**[PRO 14:8](https://ref.ly/Prov14:8)**

### Being led astray

**[PRO 14:15](https://ref.ly/Prov14:15)** *See also* [PRO 7:7–7:23](https://ref.ly/Prov7:7-Prov7:23), [PRO 9:13–9:18](https://ref.ly/Prov9:13-Prov9:18); [ECC 7:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:26); [HOS 7:11](https://ref.ly/Hos7:11); [GAL 3:1](https://ref.ly/Gal3:1)

### Failing to see danger

**[PRO 22:3](https://ref.ly/Prov22:3)** *See also* [ECC 4:13](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:13)

### Being unprepared

**[MAT 25:10](https://ref.ly/Matt25:10)**

The consequences of folly
-------------------------

### Destruction and ruin

**[PRO 1:32](https://ref.ly/Prov1:32)** *See also* [PRO 5:23](https://ref.ly/Prov5:23), [PRO 10:8](https://ref.ly/Prov10:8); [ECC 7:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:17); [1TI 6:9](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9)

### Shame

**[PRO 3:35](https://ref.ly/Prov3:35)** *See also* [PRO 18:13](https://ref.ly/Prov18:13); [ISA 44:9](https://ref.ly/Isa44:9); [JER 8:9](https://ref.ly/Jer8:9)

### Grief to parents

**[PRO 17:25](https://ref.ly/Prov17:25)** *See also* [PRO 10:1](https://ref.ly/Prov10:1), [PRO 15:20](https://ref.ly/Prov15:20), [PRO 17:21](https://ref.ly/Prov17:21)

### Punishment

**[PRO 16:22](https://ref.ly/Prov16:22)** *See also* [PSA 38:5](https://ref.ly/Ps38:5); [PRO 18:6](https://ref.ly/Prov18:6), [PRO 26:3](https://ref.ly/Prov26:3)

### Exclusion from God's way of salvation

**[ISA 35:8](https://ref.ly/Isa35:8)** *See also* [MAT 25:10–25:12](https://ref.ly/Matt25:10-Matt25:12)

Folly is persistent
-------------------

**[PRO 27:22](https://ref.ly/Prov27:22)** *See also* [PRO 15:14](https://ref.ly/Prov15:14), [PRO 26:11](https://ref.ly/Prov26:11)

Remedies for folly
------------------

### The forgiveness of foolish actions

[NUM 12:11](https://ref.ly/Num12:11); ; [JOB 42:8](https://ref.ly/Job42:8); ; [PSA 85:8](https://ref.ly/Ps85:8); ; [TIT 3:3–3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:3-Titus3:5)

### Discipline

[PRO 19:25](https://ref.ly/Prov19:25), [PRO 21:11](https://ref.ly/Prov21:11), [PRO 22:15](https://ref.ly/Prov22:15), [PRO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Prov29:15)

### Wisdom and instruction

[PRO 8:5](https://ref.ly/Prov8:5), [PRO 14:33](https://ref.ly/Prov14:33); ; [ISA 29:24](https://ref.ly/Isa29:24); ; [ROM 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rom2:20)

## 8759: fools (id: 8759)

Those who lack wisdom and understanding, especially in relation to God.

* **Associated Articles:** 8760: fools, characteristics of (ID: `8760@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8761: fools, in teaching of Jesus Christ (ID: `8761@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8760: fools, characteristics of (id: 8760)

Scripture portrays fools as those who have rejected God and his ways and are unable or unwilling to appreciate the wisdom of knowing and obeying him.

The world regards Christians as fools
-------------------------------------

**[1CO 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:10)** *See also* [1CO 1:26–1:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:26-1Cor1:31)

The world regards the gospel as foolish
---------------------------------------

**[1CO 1:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:18)** *See also* [1CO 1:20–1:25](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:20-1Cor1:25)

Fools do not believe in God
---------------------------

**[PSA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Ps53:1)** parallel [PSA 14:1](https://ref.ly/Ps14:1) *See also* [ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21)

### Fools do not trust God

**[HOS 7:11](https://ref.ly/Hos7:11)** *See also* [2CH 16:7–16:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:7-2Chr16:9); [ISA 19:11–19:13](https://ref.ly/Isa19:11-Isa19:13), [ISA 30:1](https://ref.ly/Isa30:1)

### Fools lack knowledge of God

**[JER 4:22](https://ref.ly/Jer4:22)** *See also* [JER 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jer5:4), [JER 5:21](https://ref.ly/Jer5:21); [PSA 92:6](https://ref.ly/Ps92:6); [LUK 24:25](https://ref.ly/Luke24:25); [EPH 5:15–5:17](https://ref.ly/Eph5:15-Eph5:17)

The behaviour of fools
----------------------

### Fools worship idols

**[JER 10:8](https://ref.ly/Jer10:8)** *See also* [JER 10:14](https://ref.ly/Jer10:14) parallel [JER 51:17](https://ref.ly/Jer51:17); [ROM 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Rom1:22-Rom1:23)

### Fools act in a godless manner

**[ISA 32:6](https://ref.ly/Isa32:6)** *See also* [PSA 94:5–94:8](https://ref.ly/Ps94:5-Ps94:8); [PRO 10:23](https://ref.ly/Prov10:23); [ECC 7:25](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:25); [JER 4:22](https://ref.ly/Jer4:22)

### Fools are dishonest

**[JER 17:11](https://ref.ly/Jer17:11)**

### Fools give vent to their anger

**[PRO 29:11](https://ref.ly/Prov29:11)** *See also* [ECC 7:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:9)

### Fools are disobedient

**[1SA 13:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:13)** *See also* [2SA 24:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:10) parallel [1CH 21:8](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:8); [PSA 107:17](https://ref.ly/Ps107:17); [PRO 10:8](https://ref.ly/Prov10:8)

### Fools despise instruction and discipline

**[PRO 1:22](https://ref.ly/Prov1:22)** *See also* [PRO 1:28–1:33](https://ref.ly/Prov1:28-Prov1:33), [PRO 5:23](https://ref.ly/Prov5:23), [PRO 12:15](https://ref.ly/Prov12:15); [ISA 65:2](https://ref.ly/Isa65:2); [JER 18:12](https://ref.ly/Jer18:12); [EZK 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:7)

The speech of fools
-------------------

### Fools speak unwisely

**[PRO 18:6–18:7](https://ref.ly/Prov18:6-Prov18:7)** *See also* [ECC 10:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:12); [EPH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:4)

### Fools are slanderous

**[PRO 10:18](https://ref.ly/Prov10:18)** *See also* [2PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:15)

### Fools are mockers

**[PRO 14:9](https://ref.ly/Prov14:9)** *See also* [PRO 1:22](https://ref.ly/Prov1:22), [PRO 14:6](https://ref.ly/Prov14:6), [PRO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Prov23:9), [PRO 24:9](https://ref.ly/Prov24:9)

### Fools love quarrelling and dissension

**[PRO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Prov20:3)** *See also* [PRO 19:1](https://ref.ly/Prov19:1); [1TI 1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:4), [1TI 6:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:4); [2TI 2:23](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:23); [TIT 3:9](https://ref.ly/Titus3:9)

### The words of fools are empty

**[PRO 15:2](https://ref.ly/Prov15:2)** *See also* [PRO 14:7](https://ref.ly/Prov14:7), [PRO 26:7](https://ref.ly/Prov26:7); [ECC 9:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:17), [ECC 10:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:14)

Fools rely on themselves
------------------------

**[PRO 12:15](https://ref.ly/Prov12:15)** *See also* [PRO 14:12](https://ref.ly/Prov14:12), [PRO 28:26](https://ref.ly/Prov28:26)

Fools rely on words, not deeds
------------------------------

**[JAS 2:20](https://ref.ly/Jas2:20)** *See also* [ECC 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:4); [LUK 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Luke11:39-Luke11:40)

## 8761: fools, in teaching of Jesus Christ (id: 8761)

Jesus Christ speaks about those whose words and actions demonstrate opposition to, or disregard for, the wisdom of God revealed in his words and through the Scriptures, and in parables points to the serious consequences of folly.

The foolishness of the Pharisees
--------------------------------

### Their blindness

**[MAT 23:16–23:17](https://ref.ly/Matt23:16-Matt23:17)** *The Pharisees demonstrate foolishness by attaching undue significance to what is less important whilst neglecting what really matters.* *See also* [MAT 7:3](https://ref.ly/Matt7:3) parallel [LUK 6:41](https://ref.ly/Luke6:41); [MAT 15:14](https://ref.ly/Matt15:14) parallel [LUK 6:39](https://ref.ly/Luke6:39); [MAT 23:23–23:24](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23-Matt23:24)

### Their hypocrisy

**[LUK 11:39–11:40](https://ref.ly/Luke11:39-Luke11:40)** *The Pharisees were concerned with the externals of religion. Jesus Christ is concerned, also, with the inner life.* *See also* [MAT 12:35–12:36](https://ref.ly/Matt12:35-Matt12:36), [MAT 23:25–23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25-Matt23:28)

The foolishness of the disciples
--------------------------------

**[LUK 24:25](https://ref.ly/Luke24:25)** *See also* [MAT 15:16–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:16-Matt15:20) parallel [MRK 7:20–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:20-Mark7:23); [MRK 8:17](https://ref.ly/Mark8:17) parallel [MAT 16:9](https://ref.ly/Matt16:9); [MRK 9:32](https://ref.ly/Mark9:32), [MRK 16:12–16:14](https://ref.ly/Mark16:12-Mark16:14)

Fools in Jesus Christ's parables
--------------------------------

### The rich fool

**[LUK 12:16–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:16-Luke12:21)** *The foolishness of trusting in wealth and not in God.* *See also* [JOB 27:16–27:19](https://ref.ly/Job27:16-Job27:19); [DAN 4:30–4:31](https://ref.ly/Dan4:30-Dan4:31); [MAT 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19)

### The foolish builder

**[MAT 7:24–7:27](https://ref.ly/Matt7:24-Matt7:27)** parallel [LUK 6:49](https://ref.ly/Luke6:49) *See also* [PRO 1:28–1:33](https://ref.ly/Prov1:28-Prov1:33); [ISA 65:2](https://ref.ly/Isa65:2); [JER 5:21–5:25](https://ref.ly/Jer5:21-Jer5:25), [JER 13:10](https://ref.ly/Jer13:10); [EZK 3:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:7)

### The foolish virgins

**[MAT 25:1–25:13](https://ref.ly/Matt25:1-Matt25:13)** *The foolishness of being unprepared for the coming of God's kingdom.* *See also* [MRK 13:35–13:36](https://ref.ly/Mark13:35-Mark13:36); [LUK 12:35–12:36](https://ref.ly/Luke12:35-Luke12:36)

The seriousness of the term \`\`fool''
--------------------------------------

**[MAT 5:22](https://ref.ly/Matt5:22)** *See also* [PRO 1:32](https://ref.ly/Prov1:32); [ECC 7:17](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:17)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 23:16-17; Matthew 7:3; Luke 6:41; Matthew 15:14; Luke 6:39; Matthew 23:23-24; Luke 11:39-40; Matthew 12:35-36; Matthew 23:25-28; Luke 24:25; Matthew 15:16-20; Mark 7:20-23; Mark 8:17; Matthew 16:9; Mark 9:32; Mark 16:12-14; Luke 12:16-21; Job 27:16-19; Daniel 4:30-31; Matthew 6:19; Matthew 7:24-27; Luke 6:49; Proverbs 1:28-33; Isaiah 65:2; Jeremiah 5:21-25; Jeremiah 13:10; Ezekiel 3:7; Matthew 25:1-13; Mark 13:35-36; Luke 12:35-36; Matthew 5:22; Proverbs 1:32; Ecclesiastes 7:17
* **Associated Articles:** 2375: kingdom of God (ID: `2375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5133: blindness (ID: `5133@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5375: law (ID: `5375@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6245: stubbornness (ID: `6245@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7550: Pharisees (ID: `7550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8030: trust (ID: `8030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8721: doubt (ID: `8721@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8767: hypocrisy (ID: `8767@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8834: unbelief (ID: `8834@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8763: forgetting (id: 8763)

The process of accidentally or deliberately failing to remember people, events or ideas. Scripture rebukes those who forget the past work of God in history or in their lives or who neglect their present responsibilities towards God in order to please themselves.

Causes of forgetting
--------------------

### Forgetting can be deliberate

**[2PE 3:5](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:5)**

### Forgetting can be caused by idolatry

**[JER 23:25–23:27](https://ref.ly/Jer23:25-Jer23:27)** *See also* [DEU 32:15–32:18](https://ref.ly/Deut32:15-Deut32:18); [JDG 3:7](https://ref.ly/Judg3:7); [JER 18:15](https://ref.ly/Jer18:15); [HOS 2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:13)

### Forgetting can be caused by pride

**[HOS 13:4–13:6](https://ref.ly/Hos13:4-Hos13:6)** *See also* [2CH 26:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16); [PSA 10:4](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4)

### Forgetting can be caused by drinking

**[PRO 31:4–31:5](https://ref.ly/Prov31:4-Prov31:5)** *See also* [GEN 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/Gen9:20-Gen9:21); [MAT 24:45–24:51](https://ref.ly/Matt24:45-Matt24:51) parallel [LUK 12:42–12:46](https://ref.ly/Luke12:42-Luke12:46)

### Forgetting can be caused by affliction

**[PSA 102:4](https://ref.ly/Ps102:4)** *See also* [GEN 41:28–41:31](https://ref.ly/Gen41:28-Gen41:31); [LAM 3:17–3:20](https://ref.ly/Lam3:17-Lam3:20)

Forgetting people
-----------------

**[GEN 40:23](https://ref.ly/Gen40:23)** *See also* [JOB 19:13–19:15](https://ref.ly/Job19:13-Job19:15); [PSA 31:11–31:12](https://ref.ly/Ps31:11-Ps31:12)

Forgetting God
--------------

**[DEU 32:18](https://ref.ly/Deut32:18)** *See also* [JDG 2:10–2:13](https://ref.ly/Judg2:10-Judg2:13); [1SA 12:9–12:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:9-1Sam12:10)

Forgetting God's acts in the past
---------------------------------

**[PSA 78:11–78:20](https://ref.ly/Ps78:11-Ps78:20)** *See also* [JDG 8:33–8:34](https://ref.ly/Judg8:33-Judg8:34); [NEH 9:16–9:17](https://ref.ly/Neh9:16-Neh9:17); [PSA 106:7–106:13](https://ref.ly/Ps106:7-Ps106:13)

Consequences of forgetting God
------------------------------

### Forgetting God brings suffering on the individual

**[PSA 50:22](https://ref.ly/Ps50:22)** *See also* [1SA 12:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:9); [ISA 17:10–17:11](https://ref.ly/Isa17:10-Isa17:11)

### Forgetting God brings suffering on others

**[2SA 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:9)** *David's forgetting of God in his adultery with Bathsheba led to her husband Uriah suffering death through David's manipulation of events.* *See also* [PSA 106:13–106:15](https://ref.ly/Ps106:13-Ps106:15); [JER 3:21](https://ref.ly/Jer3:21)

### Forgetting God brings destruction on the nation

**[DEU 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Deut8:19-Deut8:20)** *See also* [ISA 65:11–65:12](https://ref.ly/Isa65:11-Isa65:12); [HOS 8:14](https://ref.ly/Hos8:14)

Warnings not to forget God
--------------------------

**[DEU 6:12](https://ref.ly/Deut6:12)** *See also* [DEU 4:9](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9), [DEU 8:10–8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:10-Deut8:14); [2KI 17:35–17:38](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:35-2Kgs17:38)

People may sometimes feel that God has forgotten them
-----------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 13:1](https://ref.ly/Ps13:1)** *See also* [PSA 42:9–42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:9-Ps42:10), [PSA 44:23–44:24](https://ref.ly/Ps44:23-Ps44:24); [ISA 49:14](https://ref.ly/Isa49:14); [MAT 27:46](https://ref.ly/Matt27:46) parallel [MRK 15:34](https://ref.ly/Mark15:34); [PSA 22:1](https://ref.ly/Ps22:1)

God promises never to forget his people
---------------------------------------

**[ISA 44:21](https://ref.ly/Isa44:21)** *See also* [ISA 49:15–49:16](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15-Isa49:16); [HEB 6:10](https://ref.ly/Heb6:10)

Things God urges believers to forget
------------------------------------

**[ISA 43:18](https://ref.ly/Isa43:18)** *See also* [PHP 3:13–3:15](https://ref.ly/Phil3:13-Phil3:15)

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Peter 3:5; Jeremiah 23:25-27; Deuteronomy 32:15-18; Judges 3:7; Jeremiah 18:15; Hosea 2:13; Hosea 13:4-6; 2 Chronicles 26:16; Psalms 10:4; Proverbs 31:4-5; Genesis 9:20-21; Matthew 24:45-51; Luke 12:42-46; Psalms 102:4; Genesis 41:28-31; Lamentations 3:17-20; Genesis 40:23; Job 19:13-15; Psalms 31:11-12; Deuteronomy 32:18; Judges 2:10-13; 1 Samuel 12:9-10; Psalms 78:11-20; Judges 8:33-34; Nehemiah 9:16-17; Psalms 106:7-13; Psalms 50:22; 1 Samuel 12:9; Isaiah 17:10-11; 2 Samuel 12:9; Psalms 106:13-15; Jeremiah 3:21; Deuteronomy 8:19-20; Isaiah 65:11-12; Hosea 8:14; Deuteronomy 6:12; Deuteronomy 4:9; Deuteronomy 8:10-14; 2 Kings 17:35-38; Psalms 13:1; Psalms 42:9-10; Psalms 44:23-24; Isaiah 49:14; Matthew 27:46; Mark 15:34; Psalms 22:1; Isaiah 44:21; Isaiah 49:15-16; Hebrews 6:10; Isaiah 43:18; Philippians 3:13-15
