## 8764: forgetting God (id: 8764)

Scripture warns people how easy it is to forget God in the varying circumstances of life and to fail to give him his due place, and points out the consequences of so doing.

Warnings not to forget God
--------------------------

### Forgetting God's past acts

**[DEU 4:9](https://ref.ly/Deut4:9)** *See also* [DEU 9:7–9:8](https://ref.ly/Deut9:7-Deut9:8); [ISA 51:12–51:13](https://ref.ly/Isa51:12-Isa51:13)

### Forgetting God's covenant

**[DEU 4:23](https://ref.ly/Deut4:23)** *See also* [2KI 17:38](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:38)

### Forgetting God's ways

**[ISA 55:6–55:8](https://ref.ly/Isa55:6-Isa55:8)** *See also* [DEU 18:9–18:13](https://ref.ly/Deut18:9-Deut18:13); [1KI 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs2:1-1Kgs2:4)

Reasons why people forget God
-----------------------------

### Easy circumstances

**[DEU 6:10–6:12](https://ref.ly/Deut6:10-Deut6:12)** *See also* [DEU 8:10–8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:10-Deut8:14)

### Idolatry

**[JDG 8:33–8:34](https://ref.ly/Judg8:33-Judg8:34)** *See also* [EXO 32:1–32:8](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1-Exod32:8); [DEU 12:29–12:31](https://ref.ly/Deut12:29-Deut12:31); [1KI 11:4–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4-1Kgs11:6)

### Pride and stubbornness

**[NEH 9:16](https://ref.ly/Neh9:16)** *See also* [DEU 9:12–9:13](https://ref.ly/Deut9:12-Deut9:13); [2KI 17:14–17:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:14-2Kgs17:17); [JER 7:22–7:26](https://ref.ly/Jer7:22-Jer7:26)

Consequences of people forgetting God
-------------------------------------

### Spiritual adultery

**[HOS 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2)** *See also* [JER 3:6–3:10](https://ref.ly/Jer3:6-Jer3:10); [HOS 4:10–4:13](https://ref.ly/Hos4:10-Hos4:13), [HOS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Hos5:4)

### Social decay

**[HOS 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Hos4:1-Hos4:3)**

### The destruction of the nation

**[DEU 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Deut8:19-Deut8:20)** *See also* [DEU 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:14-Deut6:15)

### The death of the sinner

**[PSA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Ps9:17)** *See also* [JOB 8:11–8:13](https://ref.ly/Job8:11-Job8:13); [PSA 50:22](https://ref.ly/Ps50:22); [ISA 65:11–65:12](https://ref.ly/Isa65:11-Isa65:12)

Examples of people forgetting God
---------------------------------

[JDG 8:33–8:34](https://ref.ly/Judg8:33-Judg8:34); ; [2KI 17:7–17:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:7-2Kgs17:20); ; [PSA 78:40–78:43](https://ref.ly/Ps78:40-Ps78:43), [PSA 106:19–106:22](https://ref.ly/Ps106:19-Ps106:22); ; [ISA 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Isa1:2-Isa1:3); ; [JER 3:19–3:21](https://ref.ly/Jer3:19-Jer3:21)

Exhortations and resolve not to forget God
------------------------------------------

[PSA 103:2](https://ref.ly/Ps103:2), [PSA 119:61](https://ref.ly/Ps119:61), [PSA 119:109](https://ref.ly/Ps119:109)

God's provision to help people not to forget him
------------------------------------------------

### His word

**[PSA 78:5–78:7](https://ref.ly/Ps78:5-Ps78:7)** *See also* [DEU 31:24–31:27](https://ref.ly/Deut31:24-Deut31:27); [JER 20:9](https://ref.ly/Jer20:9)

### Covenantal meals and festivals

[DEU 16:1–16:3](https://ref.ly/Deut16:1-Deut16:3), [DEU 16:9–16:12](https://ref.ly/Deut16:9-Deut16:12); ; [1CO 11:23–11:26](https://ref.ly/1Cor11:23-1Cor11:26)

### External signs

[GEN 9:12–9:17](https://ref.ly/Gen9:12-Gen9:17), [GEN 17:9–17:13](https://ref.ly/Gen17:9-Gen17:13); ; [NUM 15:37–15:39](https://ref.ly/Num15:37-Num15:39); ; [DEU 11:18–11:20](https://ref.ly/Deut11:18-Deut11:20)

God's challenge to those who forget him
---------------------------------------

**[HOS 4:1](https://ref.ly/Hos4:1)** *See also* [ISA 57:11](https://ref.ly/Isa57:11); [JER 2:31–2:32](https://ref.ly/Jer2:31-Jer2:32); [HOS 2:2–2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2-Hos2:13)

God's call to those who forget him
----------------------------------

**[JER 3:19–3:22](https://ref.ly/Jer3:19-Jer3:22)** *See also* [REV 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Rev2:4-Rev2:5), [REV 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rev3:20)

God's grace even when his people do forget him
----------------------------------------------

**[ISA 49:15–49:16](https://ref.ly/Isa49:15-Isa49:16)** *See also* [DEU 4:25–4:31](https://ref.ly/Deut4:25-Deut4:31); [NEH 9:29–9:31](https://ref.ly/Neh9:29-Neh9:31)

## 8765: grudge (id: 8765)

A persistent feeling of ill will or resentment held by one person against another, often as the result of an injury or insult. Christians are not to bear grudges against others; instead they are to forgive those who have wronged them.

Examples of those who held grudges
----------------------------------

### Esau against Jacob

**[GEN 27:41](https://ref.ly/Gen27:41)**

### Joseph's brothers and Joseph

**[GEN 37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:4)** *See also* [GEN 37:5](https://ref.ly/Gen37:5), [GEN 37:8](https://ref.ly/Gen37:8), [GEN 37:18–37:19](https://ref.ly/Gen37:18-Gen37:19), [GEN 37:23–37:24](https://ref.ly/Gen37:23-Gen37:24), [GEN 50:15](https://ref.ly/Gen50:15)

### The Israelites against Moses and Aaron

[NUM 11:1](https://ref.ly/Num11:1), [NUM 14:1–14:4](https://ref.ly/Num14:1-Num14:4), [NUM 14:27](https://ref.ly/Num14:27)

### Saul against David

[1SA 18:7–18:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:7-1Sam18:9)

### Ahab against Elijah

[1KI 18:16–18:17](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:16-1Kgs18:17), [1KI 21:20](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:20), [1KI 22:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:8)

### Herodias against John the Baptist

**[MRK 6:18–6:19](https://ref.ly/Mark6:18-Mark6:19)**

### The disciples against others

*See also* [MAT 26:6–26:9](https://ref.ly/Matt26:6-Matt26:9) parallel [MRK 14:3–14:5](https://ref.ly/Mark14:3-Mark14:5); [JHN 12:3–12:6](https://ref.ly/John12:3-John12:6)

### The Sadducees against the apostles

[ACT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17)

### The Jews against Stephen

[ACT 7:54](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54)

Severe grudges that led to hatred and murder
--------------------------------------------

### That of Abel by Cain

[GEN 4:4–4:8](https://ref.ly/Gen4:4-Gen4:8)

### That of Joseph by his brothers, averted by Reuben

[GEN 37:18–37:20](https://ref.ly/Gen37:18-Gen37:20)

### That of Amnon, on the orders of Absalom

[2SA 13:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:22), [2SA 13:28–13:32](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:28-2Sam13:32)

### That of John the Baptist by Herodias

[MRK 6:22–6:24](https://ref.ly/Mark6:22-Mark6:24), [MRK 6:27–6:28](https://ref.ly/Mark6:27-Mark6:28)

Warnings against the holding of grudges
---------------------------------------

### In the OT

**[LEV 19:18](https://ref.ly/Lev19:18)** *See also* [DEU 15:10](https://ref.ly/Deut15:10); [JDG 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Judg8:1-Judg8:3); [JOB 5:2](https://ref.ly/Job5:2), [JOB 36:13](https://ref.ly/Job36:13); [PRO 3:11](https://ref.ly/Prov3:11), [PRO 20:22](https://ref.ly/Prov20:22), [PRO 23:17](https://ref.ly/Prov23:17)

### In Jesus Christ's teaching

**[MRK 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:23)** parallel [MAT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19-Matt15:20)

### In the apostles' teaching

**[ROM 12:17–12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:17-Rom12:21)**; **[1TH 5:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:15)** *See also* [GAL 5:15](https://ref.ly/Gal5:15), [GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21); [PHP 2:13–2:15](https://ref.ly/Phil2:13-Phil2:15); [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Col3:8); [2TI 2:24](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:24); [JAS 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:16); [1PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:1)

Christians should not hold grudges
----------------------------------

**[MAT 5:43–5:47](https://ref.ly/Matt5:43-Matt5:47)** *See also* [MAT 18:21–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:21-Matt18:35); [COL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Col3:13)

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 27:41; Genesis 37:4; Genesis 37:5; Genesis 37:8; Genesis 37:18-19; Genesis 37:23-24; Genesis 50:15; Numbers 11:1; Numbers 14:1-4; Numbers 14:27; 1 Samuel 18:7-9; 1 Kings 18:16-17; 1 Kings 21:20; 1 Kings 22:8; Mark 6:18-19; Matthew 26:6-9; Mark 14:3-5; John 12:3-6; Acts 5:17; Acts 7:54; Genesis 4:4-8; Genesis 37:18-20; 2 Samuel 13:22; 2 Samuel 13:28-32; Mark 6:22-24; Mark 6:27-28; Leviticus 19:18; Deuteronomy 15:10; Judges 8:1-3; Job 5:2; Job 36:13; Proverbs 3:11; Proverbs 20:22; Proverbs 23:17; Mark 7:21-23; Matthew 15:19-20; Romans 12:17-21; 1 Thessalonians 5:15; Galatians 5:15; Galatians 5:19-21; Philippians 2:13-15; Colossians 3:8; 2 Timothy 2:24; James 3:14-16; 1 Peter 2:1; Matthew 5:43-47; Matthew 18:21-35; Colossians 3:13

## 8766: heresies (id: 8766)

Teachings held by a factional religious party which deny some aspect of established doctrine. NT writers give no toleration to heresies in the church.

Heresy as deviant teaching
--------------------------

**[HEB 13:9](https://ref.ly/Heb13:9)** *See also* [GAL 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/Gal1:6-Gal1:7); [2TI 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:3-2Tim4:4)

Heresies and the factional nature of Judaism
--------------------------------------------

### The party of the Pharisees

**[ACT 26:5](https://ref.ly/Acts26:5)** *See also* [ACT 15:5](https://ref.ly/Acts15:5); [PHP 3:5–3:6](https://ref.ly/Phil3:5-Phil3:6)

### The party of the Sadducees

**[ACT 23:8](https://ref.ly/Acts23:8)** *See also* [MAT 22:23](https://ref.ly/Matt22:23) parallels [MRK 12:18](https://ref.ly/Mark12:18), [LUK 20:27](https://ref.ly/Luke20:27)

### Christianity was perceived as a heresy within Judaism

**[ACT 24:14](https://ref.ly/Acts24:14)** *See also* [ACT 24:5](https://ref.ly/Acts24:5), [ACT 28:22](https://ref.ly/Acts28:22)

Heresies within the church
--------------------------

### The imposition of Jewish regulations on Gentile converts

**[ACT 15:1](https://ref.ly/Acts15:1)** *See also* [GAL 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Gal6:12-Gal6:13); [1TI 1:3–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:7); [TIT 1:10](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10)

### Heresies concerning the person of Jesus Christ

**[2JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/2John1:7)** *See also* [1JN 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/1John2:22-1John2:23), [1JN 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/1John4:1-1John4:3)

### Heresies concerning the return of Jesus Christ

**[2TH 2:1–2:2](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:1-2Thess2:2)** *See also* [MAT 24:4–24:5](https://ref.ly/Matt24:4-Matt24:5) parallels [MRK 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/Mark13:5-Mark13:6), [LUK 21:8](https://ref.ly/Luke21:8); [MAT 24:23–24:24](https://ref.ly/Matt24:23-Matt24:24) parallel [MRK 13:21–13:22](https://ref.ly/Mark13:21-Mark13:22)

The origins of heresy
---------------------

### Erroneous human teaching

**[COL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Col2:8)** *See also* [EPH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Eph4:14); [COL 2:20–2:22](https://ref.ly/Col2:20-Col2:22)

### Deceitful demonic power

**[1TI 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1)** *See also* [1JN 4:2–4:3](https://ref.ly/1John4:2-1John4:3), [1JN 4:6](https://ref.ly/1John4:6)

Christian opposition to heresy
------------------------------

### Heresy warned against

**[2PE 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17)** *See also* [1TI 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:7); [TIT 1:10–1:14](https://ref.ly/Titus1:10-Titus1:14)

### Heresy condemned

**[2PE 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:3)** *See also* [GAL 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Gal1:8-Gal1:9); [2PE 2:17–2:22](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:17-2Pet2:22)

* **Associated Passages:** Hebrews 13:9; Galatians 1:6-7; 2 Timothy 4:3-4; Acts 26:5; Acts 15:5; Philippians 3:5-6; Acts 23:8; Matthew 22:23; Mark 12:18; Luke 20:27; Acts 24:14; Acts 24:5; Acts 28:22; Acts 15:1; Galatians 6:12-13; 1 Timothy 1:3-7; Titus 1:10; 2 John 1:7; 1 John 2:22-23; 1 John 4:1-3; 2 Thessalonians 2:1-2; Matthew 24:4-5; Mark 13:5-6; Luke 21:8; Matthew 24:23-24; Mark 13:21-22; Colossians 2:8; Ephesians 4:14; Colossians 2:20-22; 1 Timothy 4:1; 1 John 4:2-3; 1 John 4:6; 2 Peter 3:17; 1 Timothy 4:7; Titus 1:10-14; 2 Peter 2:3; Galatians 1:8-9; 2 Peter 2:17-22
* **Associated Articles:** 1460: truth (ID: `1460@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5441: philosophy (ID: `5441@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8237: doctrine, false (ID: `8237@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8703: antinomianism (ID: `8703@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8712: denial of Jesus Christ (ID: `8712@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8748: false religion (ID: `8748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8749: false teachers (ID: `8749@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8750: false teachings (ID: `8750@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8774: legalism (ID: `8774@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8775: libertinism (ID: `8775@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8784: nominal religion (ID: `8784@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8831: syncretism (ID: `8831@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8767: hypocrisy (id: 8767)

An outward pretence masking an inner reality. Scripture condemns hypocrisy, especially in matters of faith. Believers should express their commitment to God in their words and their deeds, as well as in their inner motivation.

The origin of hypocrisy
-----------------------

**[JER 17:9](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9)** *See also* [HOS 10:2](https://ref.ly/Hos10:2) *Israel*; [MRK 7:21–7:22](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:22) parallel [MAT 15:19](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19)

The expression of hypocrisy
---------------------------

### Insincere motives

**[MAT 6:2](https://ref.ly/Matt6:2)** *See also* [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5), [MAT 6:16](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16), [MAT 15:7–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:7-Matt15:9), [MAT 22:18](https://ref.ly/Matt22:18), [MAT 23:5–23:7](https://ref.ly/Matt23:5-Matt23:7)

### When deeds do not match words

**[ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)** *See also* [MAT 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/Matt15:7-Matt15:8) parallel [MRK 7:6](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6); [PRO 26:24–26:26](https://ref.ly/Prov26:24-Prov26:26); [JER 9:8](https://ref.ly/Jer9:8), [JER 12:2](https://ref.ly/Jer12:2); [EZK 33:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:31); [MAT 23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:28); [ROM 2:17–2:24](https://ref.ly/Rom2:17-Rom2:24); [JAS 2:14–2:26](https://ref.ly/Jas2:14-Jas2:26)

### A tendency to judge others

**[MAT 7:5](https://ref.ly/Matt7:5)** parallel [LUK 6:41](https://ref.ly/Luke6:41) *See also* [ROM 2:1](https://ref.ly/Rom2:1)

The alternative to hypocrisy
----------------------------

**[HEB 10:22](https://ref.ly/Heb10:22)** *See also* [PSA 24:3–24:4](https://ref.ly/Ps24:3-Ps24:4), [PSA 26:4](https://ref.ly/Ps26:4), [PSA 32:2](https://ref.ly/Ps32:2); [JAS 3:17](https://ref.ly/Jas3:17), [JAS 4:8](https://ref.ly/Jas4:8); [1PE 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:1-1Pet2:3)

Hypocrisy is not to be found in leaders
---------------------------------------

**[1CH 28:8–28:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr28:8-1Chr28:9)** *See also* [MAL 2:6](https://ref.ly/Mal2:6) *the true priest*; [1CO 4:1–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:1-1Cor4:5); [1TI 3:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:8); [TIT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Titus1:8); [JAS 3:1](https://ref.ly/Jas3:1)

Examples of hypocrites
----------------------

### Israel's leaders

**[JER 6:13](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13)** *See also* [JER 8:8](https://ref.ly/Jer8:8)

### The Pharisees

**[MRK 12:38–12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:40)** *See also* [MAT 23:1–23:32](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:32); [LUK 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Luke12:1-Luke12:2), [LUK 12:56](https://ref.ly/Luke12:56)

### Peter and Barnabas

**[GAL 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Gal2:12-Gal2:13)** *By acting in this way, Peter was denying his own conviction that Gentiles could be saved by faith without having to submit to the law.*

### Many false teachers

**[ROM 16:17–16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:17-Rom16:18)** *See also* [PHP 1:17](https://ref.ly/Phil1:17); [1TI 4:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:2); [2TI 3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:5); [JUD 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jude1:16)

Examples of those who did not practise hypocrisy
------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ

**[1PE 2:21–2:22](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21-1Pet2:22)** *See also* [ISA 53:9](https://ref.ly/Isa53:9); [JHN 8:44–8:46](https://ref.ly/John8:44-John8:46)

### Paul and his companions

**[2CO 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:12)** *See also* [2CO 2:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:17); [1TH 2:3–2:10](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:3-1Thess2:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 17:9; Hosea 10:2; Mark 7:21-22; Matthew 15:19; Matthew 6:2; Matthew 6:5; Matthew 6:16; Matthew 15:7-9; Matthew 22:18; Matthew 23:5-7; Isaiah 29:13; Matthew 15:7-8; Mark 7:6; Proverbs 26:24-26; Jeremiah 9:8; Jeremiah 12:2; Ezekiel 33:31; Matthew 23:28; Romans 2:17-24; James 2:14-26; Matthew 7:5; Luke 6:41; Romans 2:1; Hebrews 10:22; Psalms 24:3-4; Psalms 26:4; Psalms 32:2; James 3:17; James 4:8; 1 Peter 2:1-3; 1 Chronicles 28:8-9; Malachi 2:6; 1 Corinthians 4:1-5; 1 Timothy 3:8; Titus 1:8; James 3:1; Jeremiah 6:13; Jeremiah 8:8; Mark 12:38-40; Matthew 23:1-32; Luke 12:1-2; Luke 12:56; Galatians 2:12-13; Romans 16:17-18; Philippians 1:17; 1 Timothy 4:2; 2 Timothy 3:5; Jude 1:16; 1 Peter 2:21-22; Isaiah 53:9; John 8:44-46; 2 Corinthians 1:12; 2 Corinthians 2:17; 1 Thessalonians 2:3-10
* **Associated Articles:** 5381: law, letter and spirit of (ID: `5381@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5812: concealment (ID: `5812@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5820: criticism (ID: `5820@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5908: motives (ID: `5908@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5920: pretence (ID: `5920@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6242: adultery (ID: `6242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7552: Pharisees, attitudes to Jesus Christ (ID: `7552@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7733: leaders (ID: `7733@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8275: honesty (ID: `8275@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8720: double-mindedness (ID: `8720@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8768: idolatry (id: 8768)

The worship or adoration of anyone or anything other than the LORD God. Idolatry includes the worship of other gods, such as those of the nations surrounding Israel, images or idols and the creation itself.

* **Associated Articles:** 8769: idolatry, in OT (ID: `8769@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8770: idolatry, in NT (ID: `8770@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8771: idolatry, objections to (ID: `8771@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8769: idolatry, in OT (id: 8769)

Scripture provides illustrations of idolatry from various periods in the history of the people of God. Idolatry is seen as a constant temptation for believers, especially in times of national or personal stress.

Idolatry among the Gentiles
---------------------------

[JDG 11:24](https://ref.ly/Judg11:24), [JDG 16:23–16:24](https://ref.ly/Judg16:23-Judg16:24); ; [2KI 5:18](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:18); ; [ISA 36:18–36:20](https://ref.ly/Isa36:18-Isa36:20), [ISA 37:38](https://ref.ly/Isa37:38), [ISA 46:1](https://ref.ly/Isa46:1); ; [EZK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:14); ; [ACT 14:11–14:13](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11-Acts14:13); ; [1CO 8:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:5)

Idolatry among God's people
---------------------------

### In patriarchal times

**[JOS 24:2](https://ref.ly/Josh24:2)** *See also* [GEN 31:30](https://ref.ly/Gen31:30), [GEN 31:34](https://ref.ly/Gen31:34) *these household gods may have been either masks or statuettes*; [GEN 35:2](https://ref.ly/Gen35:2)

### In the Mosaic period

**[EXO 32:4](https://ref.ly/Exod32:4)**

### In the period of the judges

**[JDG 17:5](https://ref.ly/Judg17:5)** *See also* [JDG 10:6](https://ref.ly/Judg10:6)

### In the early monarchy

**[1KI 11:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:10)** *See also* [1KI 12:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:28) *The golden calves were perhaps pedestals on which the LORD was thought to sit.*

### In the middle monarchy

**[1KI 16:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:33)** *An Asherah pole was probably a crude carved wooden image of a Canaanite fertility goddess.* *See also* [1KI 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:7-1Kgs11:8), [1KI 16:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:32)

### In the late monarchy

**[2KI 21:2–21:6](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:2-2Kgs21:6)**

### After the fall of Jerusalem

**[EZK 8:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:10)** *See also* [EZK 8:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:3), [EZK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:14), [EZK 8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:16)

Objects of false worship
------------------------

### The sun, moon and stars

**[DEU 4:19](https://ref.ly/Deut4:19)** *See also* [DEU 17:3](https://ref.ly/Deut17:3); [JOB 31:26](https://ref.ly/Job31:26)

### Other objects of worship

**[DEU 4:28](https://ref.ly/Deut4:28)** *See also* [DEU 16:22](https://ref.ly/Deut16:22) *sacred stones*; [1KI 12:31](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:31) *high places*; [ISA 1:29](https://ref.ly/Isa1:29) *oaks*

Practices associated with idolatry
----------------------------------

### The burning of children

**[2KI 23:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:10)**

### The superstitious use of religious symbols

**[2KI 18:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:4)** *See also* [JDG 8:27](https://ref.ly/Judg8:27) *The ephod was a priestly garment sometimes subject to excessive veneration.*

### Sexual deviance

**[DEU 23:17](https://ref.ly/Deut23:17)** *See also* [1KI 14:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:24); [HOS 4:14](https://ref.ly/Hos4:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 11:24; Judges 16:23-24; 2 Kings 5:18; Isaiah 36:18-20; Isaiah 37:38; Isaiah 46:1; Ezekiel 8:14; Acts 14:11-13; 1 Corinthians 8:5; Joshua 24:2; Genesis 31:30; Genesis 31:34; Genesis 35:2; Exodus 32:4; Judges 17:5; Judges 10:6; 1 Kings 11:10; 1 Kings 12:28; 1 Kings 16:33; 1 Kings 11:7-8; 1 Kings 16:32; 2 Kings 21:2-6; Ezekiel 8:10; Ezekiel 8:3; Ezekiel 8:16; Deuteronomy 4:19; Deuteronomy 17:3; Job 31:26; Deuteronomy 4:28; Deuteronomy 16:22; 1 Kings 12:31; Isaiah 1:29; 2 Kings 23:10; 2 Kings 18:4; Judges 8:27; Deuteronomy 23:17; 1 Kings 14:24; Hosea 4:14
* **Associated Articles:** 4155: divination (ID: `4155@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4281: stars (ID: `4281@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5205: alliance (ID: `5205@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5947: shame (ID: `5947@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6243: adultery, spiritual (ID: `6243@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7312: Baal (ID: `7312@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7374: high places (ID: `7374@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7384: household gods (ID: `7384@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7442: shrine (ID: `7442@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7471: temples, heathen (ID: `7471@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8747: false gods (ID: `8747@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8752: false worship (ID: `8752@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8770: idolatry, in NT (id: 8770)

The NT world included many religions which promoted the worship of idols. Idolatry was thus of continuing importance to NT writers, especially Paul.

Idolatry in the Gentile world
-----------------------------

**[ACT 17:16](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16)** *See also* [ACT 14:11–14:13](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11-Acts14:13), [ACT 17:22–17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:22-Acts17:23), [ACT 19:24](https://ref.ly/Acts19:24); [1CO 8:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:5); [GAL 4:8](https://ref.ly/Gal4:8)

Criticism of idolatry
---------------------

### Idolatry leads to other sinful behaviour

**[ROM 1:24](https://ref.ly/Rom1:24)** *Verses 22 and 24–25 show the link between idolatry and immoral conduct.*

### Idolatry is an offence against the doctrine of creation

**[ROM 1:20](https://ref.ly/Rom1:20)** *See also* [ACT 17:24–17:29](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24-Acts17:29)

### Idols are futile and degrading

**[ROM 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Rom1:22-Rom1:23)** *See also* [1CO 8:4](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:4), [1CO 10:19](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:19), [1CO 12:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor12:2)

### Idolatrous worship of human beings

**[ACT 12:22](https://ref.ly/Acts12:22)** *See also* [LUK 20:24–20:25](https://ref.ly/Luke20:24-Luke20:25) *Jesus Christ opposes the hypocrisy which objected to Roman coinage as idolatrous on the grounds that the emperor's head infringed the Ten Commandments*; [ACT 28:6](https://ref.ly/Acts28:6)

### Demonic powers are involved with idolatry

**[1CO 10:20](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:20)** *See also* [REV 9:20](https://ref.ly/Rev9:20), [REV 13:4](https://ref.ly/Rev13:4)

### Food sacrificed to idols

**[ACT 15:20](https://ref.ly/Acts15:20)** *See also* [ROM 14:2–14:3](https://ref.ly/Rom14:2-Rom14:3), [ROM 14:6](https://ref.ly/Rom14:6); [1CO 8:4–8:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:4-1Cor8:13), [1CO 10:14–10:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:14-1Cor10:31)

### Encounters with idolatrous practice

**[ACT 19:28](https://ref.ly/Acts19:28)** *See also* [ACT 14:11–14:18](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11-Acts14:18), [ACT 17:18–17:31](https://ref.ly/Acts17:18-Acts17:31)

Spiritual idolatry
------------------

**[1JN 5:21](https://ref.ly/1John5:21)** *“idols” here may well refer to a wide range of God\-substitutes.*

The temptations of Jesus Christ present three main kinds of spiritual idolatry
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Possessions

**[MAT 4:3](https://ref.ly/Matt4:3)** parallel [LUK 4:3](https://ref.ly/Luke4:3) *See also* [MAT 6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:24) parallel [LUK 16:13](https://ref.ly/Luke16:13); [LUK 18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:23); [PHP 3:19](https://ref.ly/Phil3:19); [COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5); [1TI 6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:10)

### Prestige and self\-esteem

**[MAT 4:6](https://ref.ly/Matt4:6)** parallel [LUK 4:9](https://ref.ly/Luke4:9) *See also* [LUK 3:8](https://ref.ly/Luke3:8), [LUK 10:29](https://ref.ly/Luke10:29), [LUK 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11-Luke18:12), [LUK 18:21](https://ref.ly/Luke18:21); [ROM 2:19](https://ref.ly/Rom2:19)

### Power

**[MAT 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Matt4:8-Matt4:9)** parallel [LUK 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Luke4:6-Luke4:7) *See also* [LUK 9:54–9:55](https://ref.ly/Luke9:54-Luke9:55), [LUK 23:39](https://ref.ly/Luke23:39); [JHN 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/John18:10-John18:11), [JHN 19:10](https://ref.ly/John19:10); [PHP 2:6](https://ref.ly/Phil2:6); [JAS 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jas4:6), [JAS 4:10](https://ref.ly/Jas4:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Acts 17:16; Acts 14:11-13; Acts 17:22-23; Acts 19:24; 1 Corinthians 8:5; Galatians 4:8; Romans 1:24; Romans 1:20; Acts 17:24-29; Romans 1:22-23; 1 Corinthians 8:4; 1 Corinthians 10:19; 1 Corinthians 12:2; Acts 12:22; Luke 20:24-25; Acts 28:6; 1 Corinthians 10:20; Revelation 9:20; Revelation 13:4; Acts 15:20; Romans 14:2-3; Romans 14:6; 1 Corinthians 8:4-13; 1 Corinthians 10:14-31; Acts 19:28; Acts 14:11-18; Acts 17:18-31; 1 John 5:21; Matthew 4:3; Luke 4:3; Matthew 6:24; Luke 16:13; Luke 18:23; Philippians 3:19; Colossians 3:5; 1 Timothy 6:10; Matthew 4:6; Luke 4:9; Luke 3:8; Luke 10:29; Luke 18:11-12; Luke 18:21; Romans 2:19; Matthew 4:8-9; Luke 4:6-7; Luke 9:54-55; Luke 23:39; John 18:10-11; John 19:10; Philippians 2:6; James 4:6; James 4:10
* **Associated Articles:** 2575: Jesus Christ, temptation of (ID: `2575@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4185: sorcery and magic (ID: `4185@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8218: consecration (ID: `8218@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8226: discernment (ID: `8226@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8276: humility (ID: `8276@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8319: perception, spiritual (ID: `8319@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8829: superstition (ID: `8829@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8771: idolatry, objections to (id: 8771)

Scripture presents idolatry as absurd and irrational, offensive to God and a source of spiritual and moral danger.

Idolatry is disparaged
----------------------

### The absurdity of making idols

**[HAB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hab2:18)** *See also* [DEU 4:28](https://ref.ly/Deut4:28); [ISA 40:18–40:20](https://ref.ly/Isa40:18-Isa40:20), [ISA 41:6–41:7](https://ref.ly/Isa41:6-Isa41:7); [JER 10:3–10:9](https://ref.ly/Jer10:3-Jer10:9); [HOS 10:6](https://ref.ly/Hos10:6); [HAB 2:19](https://ref.ly/Hab2:19)

### Idols represent falsehood and fraud

**[PSA 40:4](https://ref.ly/Ps40:4)** *See also* [JER 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jer2:5), [JER 10:14](https://ref.ly/Jer10:14), [JER 16:19](https://ref.ly/Jer16:19), [JER 51:17–51:18](https://ref.ly/Jer51:17-Jer51:18); [HOS 12:1](https://ref.ly/Hos12:1); [AMO 2:4](https://ref.ly/Amos2:4)

### Bowing down to idols is inappropriate

**[PSA 135:17–135:18](https://ref.ly/Ps135:17-Ps135:18)** parallel [PSA 115:5–115:8](https://ref.ly/Ps115:5-Ps115:8) *See also* [ISA 2:8](https://ref.ly/Isa2:8), [ISA 44:15–44:17](https://ref.ly/Isa44:15-Isa44:17), [ISA 46:6](https://ref.ly/Isa46:6); [JER 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jer1:16); [MIC 5:13](https://ref.ly/Mic5:13)

Idols contrasted with the LORD
------------------------------

**[ISA 46:5](https://ref.ly/Isa46:5)** *See also* [ISA 40:25–40:26](https://ref.ly/Isa40:25-Isa40:26); [JER 5:24](https://ref.ly/Jer5:24), [JER 10:10](https://ref.ly/Jer10:10); [HOS 2:8](https://ref.ly/Hos2:8); [ACT 17:24–17:25](https://ref.ly/Acts17:24-Acts17:25)

Idolatry is forbidden by God
----------------------------

**[EXO 20:3–20:4](https://ref.ly/Exod20:3-Exod20:4)** parallel [DEU 5:7–5:8](https://ref.ly/Deut5:7-Deut5:8)

Idolatry incurs severe penalties
--------------------------------

**[ZEP 1:4](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:4)** *See also* [DEU 13:1–13:9](https://ref.ly/Deut13:1-Deut13:9), [DEU 13:12–13:15](https://ref.ly/Deut13:12-Deut13:15); [ISA 66:4](https://ref.ly/Isa66:4); [AMO 5:27](https://ref.ly/Amos5:27); [NAM 1:14](https://ref.ly/Nah1:14)

God abominates idols
--------------------

**[EZK 7:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:20)** *Two terms, that is “idols” and “vile images”, meaning “abomination” are found here in parallel. A third, punning on the word for “dung” is often used by Ezekiel for idols.* *See also* [DEU 7:25](https://ref.ly/Deut7:25); [2KI 23:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:24); [EZK 5:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:9), [EZK 5:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:11), [EZK 6:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:4)

Pressure to worship idols must be resisted and idols abandoned
--------------------------------------------------------------

**[DAN 3:18](https://ref.ly/Dan3:18)** *See also* [ISA 2:20](https://ref.ly/Isa2:20), [ISA 30:22](https://ref.ly/Isa30:22), [ISA 31:7](https://ref.ly/Isa31:7)

The dangers of idolatry
-----------------------

### Blindness

**[ISA 44:18](https://ref.ly/Isa44:18)**

### Becoming like idols

**[PSA 115:8](https://ref.ly/Ps115:8)** *See also* [JER 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jer2:5); [HOS 9:10](https://ref.ly/Hos9:10)

### Spiritual adultery

**[DEU 31:16](https://ref.ly/Deut31:16)** *See also* [JDG 8:33](https://ref.ly/Judg8:33); [ISA 1:21](https://ref.ly/Isa1:21); [JER 13:27](https://ref.ly/Jer13:27); [HOS 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2), [HOS 2:7](https://ref.ly/Hos2:7), [HOS 8:9](https://ref.ly/Hos8:9)

### Injustice

**[AMO 5:7](https://ref.ly/Amos5:7)**

## 8773: jealousy (id: 8773)

A strong feeling of possessiveness, often caused by the possibility that something which belongs, or ought to belong, to one is about to be taken away. The word can be used in a positive sense (e.g., the jealousy of God), meaning a passionate commitment to something which rightly belongs to one. It can also be used in a negative sense (e.g., human jealousy), to mean a self\-destructive human emotion similar to envy.

God's jealousy for his people
-----------------------------

### God's jealousy demands an exclusive response from his people

**[EXO 20:4–20:6](https://ref.ly/Exod20:4-Exod20:6)** *See also* [EXO 34:14](https://ref.ly/Exod34:14); [DEU 32:16](https://ref.ly/Deut32:16), [DEU 32:21](https://ref.ly/Deut32:21)

### God pictured as a jealous husband with an unfaithful wife

[EZK 16:1–16:63](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:1-Ezek16:63)

### God is jealously protective of his people

**[JOL 2:18](https://ref.ly/Joel2:18)** *See also* [ISA 26:11](https://ref.ly/Isa26:11); [ZEC 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Zech1:14-Zech1:15), [ZEC 8:2](https://ref.ly/Zech8:2)

### God possessiveness expresses itself in judgment on his people's unfaithfulness

**[DEU 4:24](https://ref.ly/Deut4:24)** *See also* [DEU 6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:15), [DEU 29:20](https://ref.ly/Deut29:20); [JOS 24:19](https://ref.ly/Josh24:19); [1KI 14:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:22); [EZK 23:25](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:25), [EZK 36:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek36:6); [NAM 1:2](https://ref.ly/Nah1:2); [ZEP 1:18](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:18), [ZEP 3:8](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:8)

Human jealousy is potentially destructive
-----------------------------------------

### Human jealousy arises from sin

**[MRK 7:21–7:23](https://ref.ly/Mark7:21-Mark7:23)** parallel [MAT 15:19–15:20](https://ref.ly/Matt15:19-Matt15:20)

### Human jealousy can be destructive

**[PRO 14:30](https://ref.ly/Prov14:30)** *See also* [JOB 5:2](https://ref.ly/Job5:2); [PRO 27:4](https://ref.ly/Prov27:4); [ECC 9:5–9:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:5-Eccl9:6)

### Human jealousy is criticised by Scripture

**[GAL 5:19–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:21)** *See also* [PSA 37:1–37:2](https://ref.ly/Ps37:1-Ps37:2); [PRO 23:17](https://ref.ly/Prov23:17); [ROM 13:13](https://ref.ly/Rom13:13); [1CO 13:4–13:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:4-1Cor13:7); [TIT 3:3–3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:3-Titus3:5); [JAS 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:16)

Examples of jealousy
--------------------

### In the OT

[GEN 4:3–4:5](https://ref.ly/Gen4:3-Gen4:5) *Cain and Abel*; ; [GEN 27:41](https://ref.ly/Gen27:41) *Esau and Jacob*; ; [GEN 30:1](https://ref.ly/Gen30:1) *Rachel and Leah*; [GEN 37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:4); ; [ACT 7:9](https://ref.ly/Acts7:9); [1SA 18:6–18:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:9) *Saul and David*; ; [ISA 11:13](https://ref.ly/Isa11:13) *Ephraim and Judah*

### In the early church

[ACT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17), [ACT 13:45](https://ref.ly/Acts13:45), [ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Acts17:5); ; [1CO 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:3); ; [2CO 12:20](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:20)

## 8774: legalism (id: 8774)

The belief that salvation demands or depends upon total obedience to the letter of the law. Examples of legalism include an excessive concern for minute details of the law coupled with a neglect of its funadmental concerns, and a preoccupation with human legal traditions.

Legalism represents a fatal misunderstanding of the purpose of OT law
---------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 9:31–9:32](https://ref.ly/Rom9:31-Rom9:32)** *See also* [ROM 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rom3:20); [GAL 3:10–3:11](https://ref.ly/Gal3:10-Gal3:11)

Legalism is contrary to the gospel
----------------------------------

**[GAL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:16)** *See also* [MAT 23:13](https://ref.ly/Matt23:13), [MAT 23:15](https://ref.ly/Matt23:15); [ROM 3:20–3:24](https://ref.ly/Rom3:20-Rom3:24); [PHP 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:8-Phil3:9)

Paul: example of a Jewish legalist
----------------------------------

**[PHP 3:4–3:6](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:6)** *See also* [GAL 1:14](https://ref.ly/Gal1:14)

The ugly effects of legalism
----------------------------

### Lack of love

**[MAT 23:23](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23)** *Legalism actually contradicts the spirit of OT law.* *See also* [HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6); [MIC 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Mic6:7-Mic6:8); [MAT 23:14](https://ref.ly/Matt23:14); [LUK 10:31–10:32](https://ref.ly/Luke10:31-Luke10:32); [JHN 7:49](https://ref.ly/John7:49)

### Spiritual pride

**[LUK 18:11–18:12](https://ref.ly/Luke18:11-Luke18:12)** *See also* [MAT 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Matt6:1-Matt6:2), [MAT 6:5](https://ref.ly/Matt6:5), [MAT 6:16](https://ref.ly/Matt6:16), [MAT 23:5–23:7](https://ref.ly/Matt23:5-Matt23:7); [LUK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:15); [ROM 10:3](https://ref.ly/Rom10:3)

### Formalism

**[ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)** *See also* [MRK 7:6](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6)

### Degeneration into man\-made rules

**[ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)** *See also* [MRK 7:7–7:8](https://ref.ly/Mark7:7-Mark7:8)

### Hypocrisy

**[MAT 23:27–23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:27-Matt23:28)** *See also* [1SA 15:19–15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:19-1Sam15:22); [MAT 15:3–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:3-Matt15:9) parallel [MRK 7:9–7:13](https://ref.ly/Mark7:9-Mark7:13); [MAT 23:25–23:26](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25-Matt23:26); [JHN 7:19](https://ref.ly/John7:19)

### Spiritual blindness

**[JHN 9:16](https://ref.ly/John9:16)**

Warnings against legalism
-------------------------

**[MAT 16:6](https://ref.ly/Matt16:6)** parallel [MRK 8:15](https://ref.ly/Mark8:15) *See also* [MAT 16:12](https://ref.ly/Matt16:12); [GAL 4:10–4:11](https://ref.ly/Gal4:10-Gal4:11), [GAL 5:2–5:4](https://ref.ly/Gal5:2-Gal5:4)

The burden of legalism
----------------------

**[ACT 15:10](https://ref.ly/Acts15:10)** *See also* [PSA 130:3](https://ref.ly/Ps130:3); [JAS 2:10](https://ref.ly/Jas2:10)

The answer to legalism: the grace of Jesus Christ
-------------------------------------------------

**[ACT 15:11](https://ref.ly/Acts15:11)** *See also* [MAT 5:17](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17); [GAL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Gal3:13), [GAL 4:21–4:31](https://ref.ly/Gal4:21-Gal4:31); [EPH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:9)

## 8775: libertinism (id: 8775)

A form of abuse of Christian freedom which refuses to acknowledge any obligation for Christians to obey the law or to restrain sin.

Grace must not lead to libertinism
----------------------------------

**[ROM 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Rom6:1-Rom6:2), [ROM 6:15](https://ref.ly/Rom6:15)** *See also* [ROM 3:5–3:8](https://ref.ly/Rom3:5-Rom3:8); [GAL 2:17–2:21](https://ref.ly/Gal2:17-Gal2:21)

Believers must resist sin
-------------------------

**[ROM 6:12](https://ref.ly/Rom6:12), [ROM 6:14](https://ref.ly/Rom6:14)** *See also* [HEB 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1-Heb12:2); [1JN 5:16–5:18](https://ref.ly/1John5:16-1John5:18)

The law remains valid for believers
-----------------------------------

**[MAT 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Matt5:17-Matt5:18)** *See also* [JOS 23:6](https://ref.ly/Josh23:6); [1CH 22:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr22:12); [PRO 28:7](https://ref.ly/Prov28:7); [ROM 7:12](https://ref.ly/Rom7:12); [1TI 1:8](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:8)

The dangers of libertinism
--------------------------

### Falling back into sin

[ROM 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Rom6:1-Rom6:2); ; [HEB 12:1](https://ref.ly/Heb12:1); ; [1JN 1:8–1:10](https://ref.ly/1John1:8-1John1:10)

### Disobedience towards God

**[1JN 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/1John2:3-1John2:4)** *See also* [1JN 3:4](https://ref.ly/1John3:4); [2CO 10:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:6); [EPH 5:6](https://ref.ly/Eph5:6); [1PE 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:8)

### Selfishness

**[1JN 3:17](https://ref.ly/1John3:17)** *See also* [LUK 6:32–6:34](https://ref.ly/Luke6:32-Luke6:34); [2TI 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:1-2Tim2:4); [1JN 2:9–2:11](https://ref.ly/1John2:9-1John2:11)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 6:1-2; Romans 6:15; Romans 3:5-8; Galatians 2:17-21; Romans 6:12; Romans 6:14; Hebrews 12:1-2; 1 John 5:16-18; Matthew 5:17-18; Joshua 23:6; 1 Chronicles 22:12; Proverbs 28:7; Romans 7:12; 1 Timothy 1:8; Hebrews 12:1; 1 John 1:8-10; 1 John 2:3-4; 1 John 3:4; 2 Corinthians 10:6; Ephesians 5:6; 1 Peter 2:8; 1 John 3:17; Luke 6:32-34; 2 Timothy 2:1-4; 1 John 2:9-11

## 8776: lies (id: 8776)

Statements or answers which are untrue. Lying, false witness, deceit and false dealings are all condemned in Scripture.

Lying is condemned
------------------

**[PSA 34:12–34:13](https://ref.ly/Ps34:12-Ps34:13)**; **[EPH 4:25](https://ref.ly/Eph4:25)**; **[COL 3:9–3:10](https://ref.ly/Col3:9-Col3:10)** *See also* [EXO 20:16](https://ref.ly/Exod20:16) parallel [DEU 5:20](https://ref.ly/Deut5:20); [LEV 19:11–19:12](https://ref.ly/Lev19:11-Lev19:12); [PRO 6:16–6:17](https://ref.ly/Prov6:16-Prov6:17), [PRO 12:22](https://ref.ly/Prov12:22); [JER 17:9–17:10](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9-Jer17:10)

Examples of lying
-----------------

[GEN 3:4–3:5](https://ref.ly/Gen3:4-Gen3:5) *the serpent to Eve*; ; [GEN 4:9](https://ref.ly/Gen4:9) *Cain to the LORD*; ; [GEN 12:11–12:13](https://ref.ly/Gen12:11-Gen12:13) *Abraham urges Sarah to lie to the Egyptians*; ; [GEN 18:15](https://ref.ly/Gen18:15) *Sarah to the LORD*; ; [GEN 20:1–20:2](https://ref.ly/Gen20:1-Gen20:2) *Abraham to Abimelech*; ; [GEN 26:6–26:7](https://ref.ly/Gen26:6-Gen26:7) *Isaac to the men of Gerar*; [GEN 27:18–27:19](https://ref.ly/Gen27:18-Gen27:19), [GEN 27:24](https://ref.ly/Gen27:24); [GEN 39:14–39:18](https://ref.ly/Gen39:14-Gen39:18) *Potiphar's wife to her household servants*; ; [2KI 5:22–5:27](https://ref.ly/2Kgs5:22-2Kgs5:27) *Gehazi to Naaman and then to Elisha*; ; [ACT 5:1–5:10](https://ref.ly/Acts5:1-Acts5:10) *Ananias and Sapphira*; [ACT 6:11](https://ref.ly/Acts6:11), [ACT 6:13](https://ref.ly/Acts6:13); [ACT 25:7](https://ref.ly/Acts25:7); ; [ROM 3:8](https://ref.ly/Rom3:8)

Lying will not go unnoticed or unpunished
-----------------------------------------

**[PRO 19:9](https://ref.ly/Prov19:9)** *See also* [PSA 5:6](https://ref.ly/Ps5:6), [PSA 52:2–52:5](https://ref.ly/Ps52:2-Ps52:5), [PSA 55:23](https://ref.ly/Ps55:23), [PSA 101:7](https://ref.ly/Ps101:7); [JER 6:13–6:15](https://ref.ly/Jer6:13-Jer6:15), [JER 23:32](https://ref.ly/Jer23:32); [NAM 3:1](https://ref.ly/Nah3:1); [1TI 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9-1Tim1:10); [REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8), [REV 21:27](https://ref.ly/Rev21:27), [REV 22:15](https://ref.ly/Rev22:15)

God's people should resist the temptation to lie
------------------------------------------------

[JOB 27:2–27:5](https://ref.ly/Job27:2-Job27:5); ; [REV 2:2](https://ref.ly/Rev2:2)

Lying can be an act of malice
-----------------------------

**[PRO 26:24–26:28](https://ref.ly/Prov26:24-Prov26:28)**

Lying can be the sign of a dead conscience
------------------------------------------

**[1TI 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1-1Tim4:2)**

A lying spirit
--------------

*See also* [1KI 22:21–22:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:21-1Kgs22:23) parallel [2CH 18:20–18:22](https://ref.ly/2Chr18:20-2Chr18:22)

Lying is the hallmark of the devil and the antichrist
-----------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 8:44](https://ref.ly/John8:44)** *See also* [1JN 2:22](https://ref.ly/1John2:22); [REV 13:14](https://ref.ly/Rev13:14), [REV 20:7–20:10](https://ref.ly/Rev20:7-Rev20:10)

## 8777: lust (id: 8777)

An overpowering and compulsive desire or passion, especially of a sexual nature. Scripture condemns lust of all kinds, and urges believers to show self\-control.

Lust has its origins in the heart and mind
------------------------------------------

**[PRO 6:25–6:29](https://ref.ly/Prov6:25-Prov6:29)**; **[MAT 5:28](https://ref.ly/Matt5:28)** *See also* [GEN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6); [JOB 31:1](https://ref.ly/Job31:1); [JAS 1:13–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jas1:13-Jas1:15); [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)

Lust is natural to unbelievers
------------------------------

**[ROM 1:21–1:27](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21-Rom1:27)** *See also* [ROM 7:5](https://ref.ly/Rom7:5); [1CO 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:10); [1PE 4:3](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:3); [2PE 2:14–2:18](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:14-2Pet2:18)

Believers can and must fight against lust by showing self\-control
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:16–5:21](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16-Gal5:21)**; **[COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5)** *See also* [1CO 9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:27); [GAL 5:24](https://ref.ly/Gal5:24); [EPH 4:22](https://ref.ly/Eph4:22); [1TH 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:4-1Thess4:5); [2TI 2:22](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:22); [TIT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Titus2:12); [1PE 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:11)

Examples of lust
----------------

### Lust expressed as sexual desire

[GEN 39:6–39:12](https://ref.ly/Gen39:6-Gen39:12) *Potiphar's wife and Joseph*; ; [2SA 11:2–11:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:5) *David and Bathsheba*

### Lust for money

**[1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10)** *See also* [1TI 3:3](https://ref.ly/1Tim3:3)

### The lustful desire of Israel and Judah for alliances

[EZK 23:1–23:21](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:1-Ezek23:21) *Both kingdoms sought security from such alliances, rather than trusting in God.*

* **Associated Passages:** Proverbs 6:25-29; Matthew 5:28; Genesis 3:6; Job 31:1; James 1:13-15; 1 John 2:16; Romans 1:21-27; Romans 7:5; 1 Corinthians 6:9-10; 1 Peter 4:3; 2 Peter 2:14-18; Galatians 5:16-21; Colossians 3:5; 1 Corinthians 9:27; Galatians 5:24; Ephesians 4:22; 1 Thessalonians 4:4-5; 2 Timothy 2:22; Titus 2:12; 1 Peter 2:11; Genesis 39:6-12; 2 Samuel 11:2-5; 1 Timothy 6:9-10; 1 Timothy 3:3; Ezekiel 23:1-21

## 8778: materialism (id: 8778)

The outlook on life which treats material possessions as being of supreme importance or which denies the spiritual aspects of life. Scripture notes the dangers of material wealth and possessions. The accumulation of wealth can easily become a god in itself and lead people astray from the worship of the true God. Yet it is not money itself, but the love of money, which is seen by Scripture as a fundamental cause of evil.

* **Associated Articles:** 8779: materialism, nature of (ID: `8779@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8780: materialism, as an aspect of sin (ID: `8780@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8779: materialism, nature of (id: 8779)

The attitude to life that places particular emphasis on immediate, physical values rather than on future, spiritual ones or that regards the material world as the only reality.

The basic features of materialism
---------------------------------

### An attitude of godlessness

**[PSA 14:1–14:2](https://ref.ly/Ps14:1-Ps14:2)** parallel [PSA 53:1–53:2](https://ref.ly/Ps53:1-Ps53:2) *See also* [JOB 35:9–35:10](https://ref.ly/Job35:9-Job35:10); [PSA 10:4](https://ref.ly/Ps10:4), [PSA 10:11](https://ref.ly/Ps10:11); [ECC 1:1–1:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl1:1-Eccl1:11); [LUK 12:13–12:19](https://ref.ly/Luke12:13-Luke12:19); [ROM 1:20–1:22](https://ref.ly/Rom1:20-Rom1:22)

### A denial of the resurrection

**[MAT 22:23](https://ref.ly/Matt22:23)** *See also* [ECC 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1-Eccl4:3), [ECC 9:3–9:6](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:3-Eccl9:6); [ACT 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Acts4:1-Acts4:2), [ACT 23:6–23:8](https://ref.ly/Acts23:6-Acts23:8); [1CO 15:12–15:17](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:12-1Cor15:17); [2TI 2:17–2:19](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:17-2Tim2:19)

### Desire for wealth and physical gratification

**[1CO 15:32](https://ref.ly/1Cor15:32)** *See also* [ECC 2:4–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:4-Eccl2:9), [ECC 9:7–9:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:7-Eccl9:10); [MAT 6:19–6:24](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19-Matt6:24)

Materialism condemned
---------------------

**[PSA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Ps52:7)** *See also* [JOB 22:4–22:11](https://ref.ly/Job22:4-Job22:11), [JOB 31:25–31:28](https://ref.ly/Job31:25-Job31:28); [PSA 49:10](https://ref.ly/Ps49:10); [PRO 18:11](https://ref.ly/Prov18:11), [PRO 23:5](https://ref.ly/Prov23:5); [ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10); [MAT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Matt16:26); [LUK 12:23–12:25](https://ref.ly/Luke12:23-Luke12:25), [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34); [JAS 5:1–5:3](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:3)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 14:1-2; Psalms 53:1-2; Job 35:9-10; Psalms 10:4; Psalms 10:11; Ecclesiastes 1:1-11; Luke 12:13-19; Romans 1:20-22; Matthew 22:23; Ecclesiastes 4:1-3; Ecclesiastes 9:3-6; Acts 4:1-2; Acts 23:6-8; 1 Corinthians 15:12-17; 2 Timothy 2:17-19; 1 Corinthians 15:32; Ecclesiastes 2:4-9; Ecclesiastes 9:7-10; Matthew 6:19-24; Psalms 52:7; Job 22:4-11; Job 31:25-28; Psalms 49:10; Proverbs 18:11; Proverbs 23:5; Ecclesiastes 5:10; Matthew 16:26; Luke 12:23-25; Luke 21:34; James 5:1-3
* **Associated Articles:** 5503: rich, the (ID: `5503@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5766: attitudes, to life (ID: `5766@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5785: ambition (ID: `5785@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5832: desire (ID: `5832@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5839: dissatisfaction (ID: `5839@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5864: futility (ID: `5864@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5874: happiness (ID: `5874@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8211: commitment, to the world (ID: `8211@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8260: generosity (ID: `8260@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8755: folly (ID: `8755@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8809: riches, nature of (ID: `8809@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8848: worldliness (ID: `8848@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8780: materialism, as an aspect of sin (id: 8780)

The pursuit of possessions and wealth and a preoccupation with physical things is futile and dissatisfying.

Materialism as the result of human sin
--------------------------------------

### At the fall

**[GEN 3:6](https://ref.ly/Gen3:6)**

### Among pagans

**[ROM 1:22–1:23](https://ref.ly/Rom1:22-Rom1:23)** *See also* [PSA 115:2–115:8](https://ref.ly/Ps115:2-Ps115:8); [ISA 44:9–44:11](https://ref.ly/Isa44:9-Isa44:11); [DAN 3:1](https://ref.ly/Dan3:1), [DAN 3:4–3:7](https://ref.ly/Dan3:4-Dan3:7); [HAB 2:18](https://ref.ly/Hab2:18); [ACT 17:16](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16); [2TI 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:2)

### Within Israel itself

**[EXO 20:4–20:5](https://ref.ly/Exod20:4-Exod20:5)** *See also* [EXO 32:4](https://ref.ly/Exod32:4); [LEV 26:1](https://ref.ly/Lev26:1); [DEU 4:15–4:19](https://ref.ly/Deut4:15-Deut4:19), [DEU 7:25](https://ref.ly/Deut7:25); [1KI 11:1–11:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:1-1Kgs11:6); [PSA 106:19–106:20](https://ref.ly/Ps106:19-Ps106:20); [ISA 42:8](https://ref.ly/Isa42:8); [JER 2:11–2:13](https://ref.ly/Jer2:11-Jer2:13); [1CO 10:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:14)

Materialism and possessions
---------------------------

### Possessions are to be seen as a gift from God

**[1CH 29:12](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:12)** *See also* [GEN 13:2](https://ref.ly/Gen13:2), [GEN 26:12–26:13](https://ref.ly/Gen26:12-Gen26:13); [DEU 8:18](https://ref.ly/Deut8:18); [2CH 32:29](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:29); [PSA 112:1–112:3](https://ref.ly/Ps112:1-Ps112:3); [PRO 10:22](https://ref.ly/Prov10:22); [ECC 5:19](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:19); [HOS 2:8](https://ref.ly/Hos2:8)

### Material goods can turn the heart away from God

**[DEU 8:13–8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:13-Deut8:14)**; **[1TI 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:9-1Tim6:10)** *See also* [JOB 31:24–31:28](https://ref.ly/Job31:24-Job31:28); [EZK 28:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:5); [MAT 19:21–19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:21-Matt19:22) parallel [LUK 18:22–18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:22-Luke18:23); [MAT 26:14–26:16](https://ref.ly/Matt26:14-Matt26:16); [1JN 3:17](https://ref.ly/1John3:17)

### Possessions and riches can make it difficult to enter the kingdom

**[MAT 19:23–19:24](https://ref.ly/Matt19:23-Matt19:24)** parallels [MRK 10:24–10:25](https://ref.ly/Mark10:24-Mark10:25), [LUK 18:24–18:25](https://ref.ly/Luke18:24-Luke18:25) *See also* [MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22) parallels [MRK 4:18–4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:18-Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14); [1TI 6:17–6:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17-1Tim6:19); [JAS 2:2–2:5](https://ref.ly/Jas2:2-Jas2:5), [JAS 5:1–5:6](https://ref.ly/Jas5:1-Jas5:6)

The weaknesses of materialism
-----------------------------

### It is transitory

**[PRO 27:24](https://ref.ly/Prov27:24)** *See also* [JOB 22:20](https://ref.ly/Job22:20); [PSA 49:10](https://ref.ly/Ps49:10), [PSA 49:17](https://ref.ly/Ps49:17); [PRO 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Prov22:1-Prov22:2), [PRO 23:5](https://ref.ly/Prov23:5); [ECC 2:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:18), [ECC 2:21](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:21), [ECC 2:26](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:26); [JER 17:11](https://ref.ly/Jer17:11); [LUK 12:20–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:20-Luke12:21); [1TI 6:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:7)

### It does not satisfy

**[ECC 5:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:10)** *See also* [ECC 4:8](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:8), [ECC 5:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:12); [HAG 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/Hag1:5-Hag1:6); [MAT 6:19](https://ref.ly/Matt6:19)

### It can encourage greed

**[1KI 21:2–21:4](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:2-1Kgs21:4)** *See also* [EXO 20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:17) parallel [DEU 5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:21); [JOS 7:1](https://ref.ly/Josh7:1), [JOS 7:20–7:24](https://ref.ly/Josh7:20-Josh7:24); [1KI 10:23–10:27](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:23-1Kgs10:27)

### It can lead to worldliness

**[MAT 16:26](https://ref.ly/Matt16:26)** parallels [MRK 8:36–8:37](https://ref.ly/Mark8:36-Mark8:37), [LUK 9:24–9:25](https://ref.ly/Luke9:24-Luke9:25) *See also* [JER 17:11](https://ref.ly/Jer17:11); [LUK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:15), [LUK 16:25–16:26](https://ref.ly/Luke16:25-Luke16:26); [JAS 1:9–1:11](https://ref.ly/Jas1:9-Jas1:11); [REV 3:17](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17)

Jesus Christ's non\-materialistic attitude
------------------------------------------

**[MAT 8:20](https://ref.ly/Matt8:20)** parallel [LUK 9:58](https://ref.ly/Luke9:58) *See also* [PHP 2:5–2:6](https://ref.ly/Phil2:5-Phil2:6)

Warnings to believers to guard against materialism
--------------------------------------------------

**[LUK 12:15](https://ref.ly/Luke12:15)** *See also* [JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24); [MAT 6:25–6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25-Matt6:32); [1TI 6:17–6:19](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:17-1Tim6:19); [HEB 13:5](https://ref.ly/Heb13:5); [1JN 2:15–2:17](https://ref.ly/1John2:15-1John2:17)

Godly examples of a non\-materialistic attitude
-----------------------------------------------

[GEN 13:8–13:9](https://ref.ly/Gen13:8-Gen13:9), [GEN 14:23](https://ref.ly/Gen14:23); ; [ACT 3:6](https://ref.ly/Acts3:6), [ACT 4:32–4:37](https://ref.ly/Acts4:32-Acts4:37); ; [PHP 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Phil4:11-Phil4:12)

## 8782: mockery (id: 8782)

A form of ridicule directed especially against individuals, with the purpose of humiliating them. Scripture condemns mockers as arrogant, unrighteous and foolish.

Targets of mockery
------------------

### God

**[ISA 52:5](https://ref.ly/Isa52:5)** *See also* [PSA 74:10](https://ref.ly/Ps74:10), [PSA 74:18](https://ref.ly/Ps74:18), [PSA 74:22](https://ref.ly/Ps74:22)

### Job

**[JOB 30:1](https://ref.ly/Job30:1)** *See also* [JOB 17:2](https://ref.ly/Job17:2), [JOB 21:3](https://ref.ly/Job21:3), [JOB 30:9](https://ref.ly/Job30:9)

### God's servants

**[2CH 36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:16)** *See also* [NEH 2:19](https://ref.ly/Neh2:19); [PSA 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ps22:7), [PSA 35:15–35:16](https://ref.ly/Ps35:15-Ps35:16), [PSA 69:12](https://ref.ly/Ps69:12), [PSA 80:6](https://ref.ly/Ps80:6), [PSA 89:50–89:51](https://ref.ly/Ps89:50-Ps89:51), [PSA 119:51](https://ref.ly/Ps119:51); [JER 20:7](https://ref.ly/Jer20:7); [LAM 3:14](https://ref.ly/Lam3:14), [LAM 3:63](https://ref.ly/Lam3:63)ACT [2:13](https://ref.ly/Lam2:13), [ACT 17:32](https://ref.ly/Acts17:32), [ACT 26:24](https://ref.ly/Acts26:24)

### Jesus Christ

**[MAT 20:17–20:19](https://ref.ly/Matt20:17-Matt20:19)** parallels [MRK 10:32–10:34](https://ref.ly/Mark10:32-Mark10:34), [LUK 18:31–18:33](https://ref.ly/Luke18:31-Luke18:33) *See also* [MAT 26:67–26:68](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67-Matt26:68) parallels [MRK 14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:65), [LUK 22:63–22:65](https://ref.ly/Luke22:63-Luke22:65); [LUK 23:11](https://ref.ly/Luke23:11); [MAT 27:27–27:31](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27-Matt27:31) parallel [MRK 15:16–15:20](https://ref.ly/Mark15:16-Mark15:20); [MAT 27:41–27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:41-Matt27:44) parallels [MRK 15:31–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:31-Mark15:32), [LUK 23:35](https://ref.ly/Luke23:35); [LUK 23:36](https://ref.ly/Luke23:36)

Those who oppose God will become objects of mockery
---------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34)** *See also* [2KI 19:20–19:28](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:20-2Kgs19:28) parallel [ISA 37:22–37:29](https://ref.ly/Isa37:22-Isa37:29); [JOB 22:17–22:20](https://ref.ly/Job22:17-Job22:20); [JER 10:14–10:16](https://ref.ly/Jer10:14-Jer10:16) parallel [JER 51:17–51:19](https://ref.ly/Jer51:17-Jer51:19); [EZK 22:1–22:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:1-Ezek22:5)

False accusations of mockery
----------------------------

**[JOB 11:1–11:3](https://ref.ly/Job11:1-Job11:3)** *See also* [JOB 9:21–9:24](https://ref.ly/Job9:21-Job9:24) *Job accuses God of mocking the plight of innocent sufferers.*

Mockery in metaphorical use
---------------------------

**[PRO 20:1](https://ref.ly/Prov20:1)**

Characteristics of mockers
--------------------------

### They are proud and arrogant

**[PRO 21:24](https://ref.ly/Prov21:24)** *See also* [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34)

### They instigate strife

**[PRO 22:10](https://ref.ly/Prov22:10)** *See also* [PRO 9:7](https://ref.ly/Prov9:7), [PRO 29:8](https://ref.ly/Prov29:8)

### They do not respond to correction

**[PRO 15:12](https://ref.ly/Prov15:12)** *See also* [PRO 1:22–1:26](https://ref.ly/Prov1:22-Prov1:26), [PRO 13:1](https://ref.ly/Prov13:1), [PRO 19:25](https://ref.ly/Prov19:25)

Scripture condemns mockers as unrighteous
-----------------------------------------

**[PSA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1)** *See also* [PRO 14:6](https://ref.ly/Prov14:6) *Resistance to correction accounts for the failure to find wisdom*; [PRO 14:9](https://ref.ly/Prov14:9), [PRO 24:9](https://ref.ly/Prov24:9); [ISA 57:4](https://ref.ly/Isa57:4); [HOS 7:5](https://ref.ly/Hos7:5)

Mockers will be punished
------------------------

**[PRO 1:22–1:26](https://ref.ly/Prov1:22-Prov1:26)** *See also* [PRO 9:12](https://ref.ly/Prov9:12), [PRO 17:5](https://ref.ly/Prov17:5), [PRO 19:28–19:29](https://ref.ly/Prov19:28-Prov19:29), [PRO 21:11](https://ref.ly/Prov21:11), [PRO 30:17](https://ref.ly/Prov30:17); [ISA 28:22](https://ref.ly/Isa28:22), [ISA 29:20](https://ref.ly/Isa29:20); [ZEP 2:10–2:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:10-Zeph2:11); [GAL 6:7](https://ref.ly/Gal6:7)

## 8783: neglect (id: 8783)

The shirking of one's duties or responsibilities, whether to God or to other people. The consequences of such neglect are often serious.

The Israelites sometimes neglected their religious duties
---------------------------------------------------------

### They neglected to support the Levites

**[DEU 14:27](https://ref.ly/Deut14:27)** *See also* [DEU 12:19](https://ref.ly/Deut12:19); [NEH 13:10](https://ref.ly/Neh13:10)

### They neglected the temple

**[NEH 13:11](https://ref.ly/Neh13:11)** *See also* [2CH 24:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:5); [MAL 1:6–1:14](https://ref.ly/Mal1:6-Mal1:14)

### Such neglect earned them the judgment of God

**[2CH 29:6–29:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:6-2Chr29:9)**

The Israelites sometimes neglected their social obligations
-----------------------------------------------------------

### They neglected to ensure justice for the poor

**[ISA 5:7](https://ref.ly/Isa5:7)** *See also* [AMO 2:6–2:8](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:8), [AMO 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Amos4:1-Amos4:3), [AMO 5:7–5:15](https://ref.ly/Amos5:7-Amos5:15)

### Their leaders neglected their responsibilities

**[ISA 56:10–56:12](https://ref.ly/Isa56:10-Isa56:12)** *See also* [EZK 34:1–34:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:1-Ezek34:6); [AMO 6:1–6:7](https://ref.ly/Amos6:1-Amos6:7)

### God punished their neglect

**[ZEP 1:12–1:13](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:12-Zeph1:13)** *See also* [ISA 32:9–32:15](https://ref.ly/Isa32:9-Isa32:15)

Neglect in the NT
-----------------

### Warnings against neglect

**[1TI 4:14](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:14)** *See also* [MAT 23:23](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23) parallel [LUK 11:42](https://ref.ly/Luke11:42); [MAT 25:14–25:30](https://ref.ly/Matt25:14-Matt25:30) parallel [LUK 19:12–19:26](https://ref.ly/Luke19:12-Luke19:26); [LUK 12:35–12:48](https://ref.ly/Luke12:35-Luke12:48)

Examples of neglect
-------------------

### The neglect of salvation

[HEB 2:1–2:4](https://ref.ly/Heb2:1-Heb2:4)

### The neglect of public worship

**[HEB 10:25](https://ref.ly/Heb10:25)**

### Sodom's neglect of the poor

**[EZK 16:49](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:49)**

### The neglect of widows

**[ACT 6:1–6:4](https://ref.ly/Acts6:1-Acts6:4)**

## 8784: nominal religion (id: 8784)

An outward show of piety which may appear to be very devout. However, the heart is far from God and this is confirmed by the person's attitudes and actions.

The nature of nominal religion
------------------------------

### It has an appearance of devotion which masks an evil heart

**[JER 12:2](https://ref.ly/Jer12:2)** *See also* [PRO 26:23](https://ref.ly/Prov26:23); [ISA 58:2](https://ref.ly/Isa58:2); [EZK 33:31–33:32](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:31-Ezek33:32); [MAT 23:27–23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:27-Matt23:28)

### It is based on what human beings may think is right

**[MAT 15:7–15:9](https://ref.ly/Matt15:7-Matt15:9)** parallel [MRK 7:6–7:7](https://ref.ly/Mark7:6-Mark7:7) *See also* [ISA 29:13](https://ref.ly/Isa29:13)

### It becomes apparent by the actions of its adherents

**[TIT 1:16](https://ref.ly/Titus1:16)** *See also* [MAT 7:15–7:23](https://ref.ly/Matt7:15-Matt7:23); [1TI 1:3–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:7); [1JN 2:4](https://ref.ly/1John2:4)

The insufficiency of nominal religion
-------------------------------------

**[1SA 15:22](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:22)** *See also* [ISA 1:11–1:15](https://ref.ly/Isa1:11-Isa1:15); [AMO 5:21–5:24](https://ref.ly/Amos5:21-Amos5:24); [MAT 9:13](https://ref.ly/Matt9:13), [MAT 12:7](https://ref.ly/Matt12:7); [HOS 6:6](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6)

Those who practise nominal religion are condemned
-------------------------------------------------

**[PRO 15:8](https://ref.ly/Prov15:8)** *See also* [ISA 66:3–66:4](https://ref.ly/Isa66:3-Isa66:4); [2TI 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:5)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 12:2; Proverbs 26:23; Isaiah 58:2; Ezekiel 33:31-32; Matthew 23:27-28; Matthew 15:7-9; Mark 7:6-7; Isaiah 29:13; Titus 1:16; Matthew 7:15-23; 1 Timothy 1:3-7; 1 John 2:4; 1 Samuel 15:22; Isaiah 1:11-15; Amos 5:21-24; Matthew 9:13; Matthew 12:7; Hosea 6:6; Proverbs 15:8; Isaiah 66:3-4; 2 Timothy 3:1-5
* **Associated Articles:** 5173: outward appearance (ID: `5173@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5920: pretence (ID: `5920@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7550: Pharisees (ID: `7550@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8142: religion (ID: `8142@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8734: evil (ID: `8734@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8748: false religion (ID: `8748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8767: hypocrisy (ID: `8767@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8774: legalism (ID: `8774@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8822: self-justification (ID: `8822@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8823: self-righteousness (ID: `8823@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8845: unfruitfulness (ID: `8845@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9210: judgment, God's (ID: `9210@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8785: opposition (id: 8785)

The act of resisting individuals and forces. Scripture stresses the reality of sin and evil in the world and calls upon believers to resist them. Scripture also points to the existence of evil forces that oppose God and identifies them so that believers may recognise and resist them. These forces were evident in the ministry of Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 8786: opposition, to sin and evil (ID: `8786@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8787: opposition, to God's person and work (ID: `8787@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8788: opposition, to Jesus Christ (ID: `8788@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8786: opposition, to sin and evil (id: 8786)

Believers are called upon to opppose all that is hostile to God's purposes in the world.

God opposes evil
----------------

**[MAT 21:12–21:13](https://ref.ly/Matt21:12-Matt21:13)** parallels [MRK 11:15–11:16](https://ref.ly/Mark11:15-Mark11:16), [LUK 19:45–19:46](https://ref.ly/Luke19:45-Luke19:46), [JHN 2:14–2:17](https://ref.ly/John2:14-John2:17) *See also* [EXO 4:24](https://ref.ly/Exod4:24); [LEV 20:3](https://ref.ly/Lev20:3), [LEV 26:14–26:17](https://ref.ly/Lev26:14-Lev26:17); [NUM 22:22](https://ref.ly/Num22:22); [MAL 3:2–3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:2-Mal3:5); [JAS 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jas4:6); [1PE 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:5); [REV 2:16](https://ref.ly/Rev2:16)

God's people should oppose evil
-------------------------------

### Within themselves

**[COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5)** *See also* [MAT 5:29–5:30](https://ref.ly/Matt5:29-Matt5:30); [ROM 8:13](https://ref.ly/Rom8:13)

### Among themselves

**[GAL 2:11](https://ref.ly/Gal2:11)** *See also* [1CO 5:12–5:13](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:12-1Cor5:13); [EPH 5:3](https://ref.ly/Eph5:3)

Examples of failure to oppose evil
----------------------------------

### Within the church

[1CO 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:1-1Cor5:2); ; [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20)

### Within the family

[1SA 3:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:13); ; [1KI 1:6](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:6)

### Within the state

**[HAB 1:4](https://ref.ly/Hab1:4)** *See also* [JDG 5:23](https://ref.ly/Judg5:23); [1SA 15:9–15:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:9-1Sam15:11); [JER 5:28](https://ref.ly/Jer5:28)

Examples of unwise opposition to evil
-------------------------------------

[MAT 26:51–26:52](https://ref.ly/Matt26:51-Matt26:52) parallels [MRK 14:47](https://ref.ly/Mark14:47), [LUK 22:50–22:51](https://ref.ly/Luke22:50-Luke22:51); ; [JHN 18:10–18:11](https://ref.ly/John18:10-John18:11); ; [LUK 9:54–9:55](https://ref.ly/Luke9:54-Luke9:55); ; [ACT 19:13–19:16](https://ref.ly/Acts19:13-Acts19:16)

Opposing evil by doing good
---------------------------

### Commanded by God

**[1PE 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:15)** *See also* [MAT 5:44](https://ref.ly/Matt5:44); [ROM 12:20–12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:20-Rom12:21); [1TH 5:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:15)

### Exemplified in Scripture

[NUM 12:13](https://ref.ly/Num12:13); ; [1SA 24:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:17), [1SA 26:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:11); ; [2KI 6:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:22); ; [LUK 22:51](https://ref.ly/Luke22:51), [LUK 23:34](https://ref.ly/Luke23:34)

Motives for opposing evil
-------------------------

### Concern for the honour and glory of God

**[NUM 25:6–25:13](https://ref.ly/Num25:6-Num25:13)** *See also* [1KI 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:10); [EZK 28:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:22); [JHN 2:17](https://ref.ly/John2:17)

### Concern for the good of others

**[GAL 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Gal2:4-Gal2:5)** *See also* [DEU 17:12–17:13](https://ref.ly/Deut17:12-Deut17:13); [1TI 5:20](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:20)

## 8787: opposition, to God's person and work (id: 8787)

The forces of evil are antagonistic towards God, his people and his work, and believers must oppose them.

Targets of opposition from evil forces
--------------------------------------

### The Lord himself

**[PSA 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Ps2:1-Ps2:3)**; **[HEB 12:3](https://ref.ly/Heb12:3)** *See also* [ISA 63:10](https://ref.ly/Isa63:10); [ACT 7:51](https://ref.ly/Acts7:51); [REV 17:14](https://ref.ly/Rev17:14)

### God's people

**[ACT 8:1–8:3](https://ref.ly/Acts8:1-Acts8:3)** *See also* [NUM 16:1–16:3](https://ref.ly/Num16:1-Num16:3); [2SA 16:5–16:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:5-2Sam16:8); [EST 3:6](https://ref.ly/Esth3:6); [JER 18:18](https://ref.ly/Jer18:18); [ACT 26:9–26:11](https://ref.ly/Acts26:9-Acts26:11); [2TI 4:14–4:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:14-2Tim4:15); [REV 13:7](https://ref.ly/Rev13:7)

### God's work

**[1TH 2:2](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:2)** *See also* [EZR 4:4–4:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:4-Ezra4:5); [NEH 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Neh4:7-Neh4:8); [ACT 4:18](https://ref.ly/Acts4:18), [ACT 13:8](https://ref.ly/Acts13:8); [1CO 16:9](https://ref.ly/1Cor16:9)

Sources of opposition
---------------------

### The world

**[JHN 15:18–15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:18-John15:19)** *See also* [JHN 17:14](https://ref.ly/John17:14); [1JN 3:13](https://ref.ly/1John3:13)

### The flesh

**[GAL 5:17](https://ref.ly/Gal5:17)** *See also* [MAT 26:41](https://ref.ly/Matt26:41) parallel [MRK 14:38](https://ref.ly/Mark14:38); [ROM 7:23](https://ref.ly/Rom7:23)

### The devil

**[1PE 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:8)** *See also* [ZEC 3:1](https://ref.ly/Zech3:1); [MAT 13:38–13:39](https://ref.ly/Matt13:38-Matt13:39); [REV 2:10](https://ref.ly/Rev2:10), [REV 12:4](https://ref.ly/Rev12:4), [REV 12:7](https://ref.ly/Rev12:7), [REV 12:9](https://ref.ly/Rev12:9), [REV 12:13](https://ref.ly/Rev12:13), [REV 12:17](https://ref.ly/Rev12:17)

Strategies used in opposition
-----------------------------

### Shows of strength

[JDG 6:5](https://ref.ly/Judg6:5); ; [1SA 17:10–17:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:10-1Sam17:11); ; [2KI 6:14](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:14)

### Discouraging propaganda

**[2KI 18:19–18:22](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:19-2Kgs18:22)** *See also* [REV 13:4–13:6](https://ref.ly/Rev13:4-Rev13:6)

### Surprise

[GEN 39:11–39:12](https://ref.ly/Gen39:11-Gen39:12); ; [PSA 10:8–10:9](https://ref.ly/Ps10:8-Ps10:9), [PSA 64:4](https://ref.ly/Ps64:4)

### Seduction

[MAT 4:9](https://ref.ly/Matt4:9); ; [1JN 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:15-1John2:16); ; [REV 2:14](https://ref.ly/Rev2:14), [REV 2:20](https://ref.ly/Rev2:20)

### Deceitfulness

**[JUD 1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:4)** *See also* [NUM 31:16](https://ref.ly/Num31:16); [1SA 18:17](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:17); [NEH 6:1–6:2](https://ref.ly/Neh6:1-Neh6:2); [ROM 16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:18); [2CO 11:13–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:13-2Cor11:15); [2JN 1:7](https://ref.ly/2John1:7), [2JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/2John1:8); [REV 19:20](https://ref.ly/Rev19:20)

### Sustained pressure

[GEN 39:10](https://ref.ly/Gen39:10); ; [1SA 1:6–1:7](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:6-1Sam1:7); ; [2KI 6:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:25); ; [NEH 6:4](https://ref.ly/Neh6:4)

Motives for opposition
----------------------

### Jealousy

**[1SA 18:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:9)** *See also* [PSA 106:16–106:18](https://ref.ly/Ps106:16-Ps106:18); [MAT 27:18](https://ref.ly/Matt27:18) parallel [MRK 15:10](https://ref.ly/Mark15:10); [ACT 5:17–5:18](https://ref.ly/Acts5:17-Acts5:18), [ACT 13:45](https://ref.ly/Acts13:45), [ACT 17:5](https://ref.ly/Acts17:5)

### Troubled conscience

**[JHN 7:7](https://ref.ly/John7:7)** *See also* [ISA 30:10](https://ref.ly/Isa30:10); [ACT 26:14](https://ref.ly/Acts26:14); [1JN 3:12](https://ref.ly/1John3:12); [REV 11:10](https://ref.ly/Rev11:10)

### Vested interests

[ACT 16:19](https://ref.ly/Acts16:19), [ACT 19:24–19:27](https://ref.ly/Acts19:24-Acts19:27)

### No just cause

[PSA 69:4](https://ref.ly/Ps69:4); ; [JHN 15:25](https://ref.ly/John15:25)

Dealing with opposition
-----------------------

### Being prepared

**[2PE 3:17](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:17)**; **[2CO 10:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:4)** *See also* [MRK 13:9](https://ref.ly/Mark13:9); [JHN 16:1–16:4](https://ref.ly/John16:1-John16:4); [1PE 4:12](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:12)

### Keeping a right perspective

[2KI 6:15–6:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:15-2Kgs6:17); ; [NEH 4:14](https://ref.ly/Neh4:14); ; [ROM 8:31](https://ref.ly/Rom8:31)

### Prayer

**[NEH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Neh6:9)** *See also* [NEH 4:4](https://ref.ly/Neh4:4); [LUK 18:7](https://ref.ly/Luke18:7); [ACT 4:23–4:24](https://ref.ly/Acts4:23-Acts4:24)

### Patience

[2TI 2:25](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:25)

### Prudence

### Perseverance

## 8788: opposition, to Jesus Christ (id: 8788)

See [2545: Jesus Christ, opposition to](2545.md)



## 8790: oppression (id: 8790)

The unfair or cruel treatment of individuals or nations, which prevents them from having the same opportunities, freedom and rights as others.

* **Associated Articles:** 8791: oppression, nature of (ID: `8791@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8792: oppression, God's attitude to (ID: `8792@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8791: oppression, nature of (id: 8791)

Oppression leads to the exploitation of the weak and vulnerable by the strong.

The victims
-----------

### The weak

**[PSA 12:5](https://ref.ly/Ps12:5)** *See also* [PSA 10:2](https://ref.ly/Ps10:2); [ECC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1); [EZK 34:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:4)

### The poor

**[PRO 22:22–22:23](https://ref.ly/Prov22:22-Prov22:23)** *See also* [EXO 23:6](https://ref.ly/Exod23:6); [DEU 15:7](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7); [PRO 22:7](https://ref.ly/Prov22:7), [PRO 22:16](https://ref.ly/Prov22:16), [PRO 28:27](https://ref.ly/Prov28:27); [EZK 22:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:29); [AMO 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6-Amos2:7), [AMO 5:11–5:12](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11-Amos5:12), [AMO 8:4](https://ref.ly/Amos8:4), [AMO 8:6](https://ref.ly/Amos8:6); [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10); [JAS 2:6](https://ref.ly/Jas2:6)

### Widows and orphans

**[EXO 22:22](https://ref.ly/Exod22:22)** *See also* [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17); [JOB 24:3](https://ref.ly/Job24:3), [JOB 24:9](https://ref.ly/Job24:9), [JOB 31:21](https://ref.ly/Job31:21), [JOB 31:22](https://ref.ly/Job31:22); [PSA 94:5–94:6](https://ref.ly/Ps94:5-Ps94:6); [PRO 23:10](https://ref.ly/Prov23:10); [ISA 1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23); [JER 7:6](https://ref.ly/Jer7:6); [ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5); [MRK 12:40](https://ref.ly/Mark12:40)

### The foreigner

**[EXO 22:21](https://ref.ly/Exod22:21)** *See also* [GEN 15:13](https://ref.ly/Gen15:13); [EXO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Exod23:9); [LEV 19:33–19:34](https://ref.ly/Lev19:33-Lev19:34); [DEU 23:7](https://ref.ly/Deut23:7), [DEU 24:17](https://ref.ly/Deut24:17); [PSA 94:6](https://ref.ly/Ps94:6); [JER 22:3](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3); [EZK 22:29](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:29); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5)

### Servants

**[DEU 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Deut24:14-Deut24:15)** *See also* [GEN 16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:6); [EXO 1:11](https://ref.ly/Exod1:11), [EXO 3:9](https://ref.ly/Exod3:9); [LEV 19:13](https://ref.ly/Lev19:13); [ISA 58:3](https://ref.ly/Isa58:3); [MAL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Mal3:5); [ACT 7:24](https://ref.ly/Acts7:24); [JAS 5:4](https://ref.ly/Jas5:4)

Examples of oppression
----------------------

[GEN 16:6](https://ref.ly/Gen16:6) *Sarah oppresses Hagar*; ; [GEN 31:39](https://ref.ly/Gen31:39) *Laban oppresses Jacob*; ; [EXO 1:8–1:22](https://ref.ly/Exod1:8-Exod1:22) *the Egyptians oppress Israel*; ; [ISA 52:4](https://ref.ly/Isa52:4) *the Assyrians oppress Israel*

The oppressors' motives
-----------------------

### Greed

**[JER 5:27–5:28](https://ref.ly/Jer5:27-Jer5:28)** *See also* [PRO 31:5](https://ref.ly/Prov31:5); [ECC 5:8–5:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:8-Eccl5:9); [ISA 1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:23), [ISA 10:2](https://ref.ly/Isa10:2); [JER 22:17](https://ref.ly/Jer22:17); [AMO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Amos4:1)

### Lust for power

**[EXO 1:10](https://ref.ly/Exod1:10)** *See also* [2CH 10:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr10:14), [2CH 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:10); [PRO 28:15](https://ref.ly/Prov28:15)

God's attitude to the oppressor
-------------------------------

**[EXO 22:22–22:24](https://ref.ly/Exod22:22-Exod22:24)** *See also* [DEU 27:19](https://ref.ly/Deut27:19); [PRO 21:13](https://ref.ly/Prov21:13); [ISA 14:5–14:6](https://ref.ly/Isa14:5-Isa14:6); [JER 6:6](https://ref.ly/Jer6:6), [JER 22:3–22:5](https://ref.ly/Jer22:3-Jer22:5); [AMO 1:3](https://ref.ly/Amos1:3), [AMO 1:6](https://ref.ly/Amos1:6), [AMO 1:9](https://ref.ly/Amos1:9), [AMO 1:11](https://ref.ly/Amos1:11), [AMO 2:1](https://ref.ly/Amos2:1); [ZEP 3:1](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:1); [LUK 20:47](https://ref.ly/Luke20:47)

The experience of oppression
----------------------------

### It brings grief and crying to God

**[PSA 42:9](https://ref.ly/Ps42:9)** *See also* [EXO 3:7](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7); [JDG 2:18](https://ref.ly/Judg2:18); [PSA 43:2](https://ref.ly/Ps43:2), [PSA 137:1](https://ref.ly/Ps137:1); [ECC 4:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl4:1)

**[ISA 53:7](https://ref.ly/Isa53:7)** *See also* [MAT 27:27–27:31](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27-Matt27:31) parallel [MRK 15:16–15:20](https://ref.ly/Mark15:16-Mark15:20); [ACT 8:32–8:35](https://ref.ly/Acts8:32-Acts8:35)

**[1PE 2:21](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21)** *See also* [JHN 16:33](https://ref.ly/John16:33); [ACT 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Acts14:22-Acts14:23); [1TH 3:2–3:4](https://ref.ly/1Thess3:2-1Thess3:4); [2TI 2:3](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:3), [2TI 3:12](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:12)

## 8792: oppression, God's attitude to (id: 8792)

God is concerned for the victims of oppression; he hears, sustains and delivers them. Christians are to exhibit the same concern.

God forbids the oppression of the poor and vulnerable
-----------------------------------------------------

**[ZEC 7:10](https://ref.ly/Zech7:10)** *See also* [PRO 22:22–22:23](https://ref.ly/Prov22:22-Prov22:23)

God warns the oppressor
-----------------------

**[JER 7:5–7:7](https://ref.ly/Jer7:5-Jer7:7)** *See also* [ISA 33:14–33:15](https://ref.ly/Isa33:14-Isa33:15)

God condemns the oppressor
--------------------------

**[PSA 72:4](https://ref.ly/Ps72:4)**; **[ISA 10:1–10:3](https://ref.ly/Isa10:1-Isa10:3)** *See also* [PRO 17:5](https://ref.ly/Prov17:5); [ISA 30:12–30:13](https://ref.ly/Isa30:12-Isa30:13); [EZK 22:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:7), [EZK 22:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:13), [EZK 22:31](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:31); [AMO 2:6](https://ref.ly/Amos2:6), [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11); [MIC 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Mic2:1-Mic2:3); [NAM 3:1](https://ref.ly/Nah3:1), [NAM 3:19](https://ref.ly/Nah3:19); [LUK 11:42](https://ref.ly/Luke11:42)

-------------------------------

### Showing mercy to the oppressed

**[PSA 103:6](https://ref.ly/Ps103:6)**; **[DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18)** *See also* [1SA 2:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:8); [PSA 146:7–146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:7-Ps146:9)

### Defending the oppressed

**[PSA 72:4](https://ref.ly/Ps72:4)** *See also* [DEU 10:18](https://ref.ly/Deut10:18); [PSA 9:9](https://ref.ly/Ps9:9), [PSA 10:17–10:18](https://ref.ly/Ps10:17-Ps10:18), [PSA 12:5](https://ref.ly/Ps12:5), [PSA 68:5](https://ref.ly/Ps68:5), [PSA 146:7–146:9](https://ref.ly/Ps146:7-Ps146:9); [JER 50:33–50:34](https://ref.ly/Jer50:33-Jer50:34)

### Delivering the oppressed

**[JOB 5:15](https://ref.ly/Job5:15)** *See also* [EXO 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Exod3:7-Exod3:9); [DEU 6:21](https://ref.ly/Deut6:21); [PSA 35:10](https://ref.ly/Ps35:10), [PSA 107:6](https://ref.ly/Ps107:6) parallels [PSA 107:13](https://ref.ly/Ps107:13), [PSA 107:19](https://ref.ly/Ps107:19); [PRO 20:22](https://ref.ly/Prov20:22); [ISA 14:3–14:4](https://ref.ly/Isa14:3-Isa14:4), [ISA 49:26](https://ref.ly/Isa49:26); [ACT 7:34](https://ref.ly/Acts7:34)

------------------------------

### In prayer

-----------------------------------------
