## 8817: ridicule, objects of (id: 8817)

Ridicule of God and of believers is to be expected, but it will bring divine retribution upon its perpetrators.

God ridiculed
-------------

**[PSA 74:10–74:18](https://ref.ly/Ps74:10-Ps74:18)** *See also* [2KI 19:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:4) parallel [ISA 37:4](https://ref.ly/Isa37:4)

Jesus Christ mocked
-------------------

**[LUK 22:63–22:65](https://ref.ly/Luke22:63-Luke22:65)** parallels [MAT 26:67–26:68](https://ref.ly/Matt26:67-Matt26:68), [MRK 14:65](https://ref.ly/Mark14:65) *See also* [LUK 23:11](https://ref.ly/Luke23:11); [MAT 27:27–27:31](https://ref.ly/Matt27:27-Matt27:31) parallel [MRK 15:16–15:20](https://ref.ly/Mark15:16-Mark15:20); [JHN 19:1–19:3](https://ref.ly/John19:1-John19:3); [MAT 27:39–27:44](https://ref.ly/Matt27:39-Matt27:44) parallels [MRK 15:29–15:32](https://ref.ly/Mark15:29-Mark15:32), [LUK 23:35–23:37](https://ref.ly/Luke23:35-Luke23:37)

God's messengers scorned
------------------------

**[2CH 36:15–36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15-2Chr36:16)** *See also* [2CH 30:6–30:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:6-2Chr30:10); [ACT 2:1–2:13](https://ref.ly/Acts2:1-Acts2:13), [ACT 17:17–17:18](https://ref.ly/Acts17:17-Acts17:18), [ACT 17:32](https://ref.ly/Acts17:32); [HEB 11:36](https://ref.ly/Heb11:36)

God's people may suffer ridicule for their faith
------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 22:6–22:8](https://ref.ly/Ps22:6-Ps22:8)** [JOB 12:4](https://ref.ly/Job12:4), [JOB 16:10](https://ref.ly/Job16:10), [JOB 19:18](https://ref.ly/Job19:18), [JOB 30:1](https://ref.ly/Job30:1), [JOB 30:9](https://ref.ly/Job30:9); [PSA 35:15–35:25](https://ref.ly/Ps35:15-Ps35:25), [PSA 42:10](https://ref.ly/Ps42:10), [PSA 69:10–69:12](https://ref.ly/Ps69:10-Ps69:12), [PSA 79:4](https://ref.ly/Ps79:4), [PSA 89:50–89:51](https://ref.ly/Ps89:50-Ps89:51), [PSA 123:3–123:4](https://ref.ly/Ps123:3-Ps123:4); ; [JER 20:7](https://ref.ly/Jer20:7); ; [LAM 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/Lam2:15-Lam2:16), [LAM 3:14](https://ref.ly/Lam3:14), [LAM 3:63](https://ref.ly/Lam3:63)

Divine judgment because of ridicule
-----------------------------------

**[ZEP 2:8–2:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:8-Zeph2:11)** *See also* [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34); [ISA 28:22](https://ref.ly/Isa28:22)

God ridicules his enemies
-------------------------

**[PSA 2:4–2:5](https://ref.ly/Ps2:4-Ps2:5)** *See also* [PSA 59:8](https://ref.ly/Ps59:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 74:10-18; 2 Kings 19:4; Isaiah 37:4; Luke 22:63-65; Matthew 26:67-68; Mark 14:65; Luke 23:11; Matthew 27:27-31; Mark 15:16-20; John 19:1-3; Matthew 27:39-44; Mark 15:29-32; Luke 23:35-37; 2 Chronicles 36:15-16; 2 Chronicles 30:6-10; Acts 2:1-13; Acts 17:17-18; Acts 17:32; Hebrews 11:36; Psalms 22:6-8; Job 12:4; Job 16:10; Job 19:18; Job 30:1; Job 30:9; Psalms 35:15-25; Psalms 42:10; Psalms 69:10-12; Psalms 79:4; Psalms 89:50-51; Psalms 123:3-4; Jeremiah 20:7; Lamentations 2:15-16; Lamentations 3:14; Lamentations 3:63; Zephaniah 2:8-11; Proverbs 3:34; Isaiah 28:22; Psalms 2:4-5; Psalms 59:8
* **Associated Articles:** 1025: God, anger of (ID: `1025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 2570: Jesus Christ, suffering of (ID: `2570@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5281: crucifixion (ID: `5281@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5568: suffering, causes of (ID: `5568@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7140: people of God (ID: `7140@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8728: enemies, of Israel and Judah (ID: `8728@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8782: mockery (ID: `8782@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8819: scoffing (ID: `8819@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8819: scoffing (id: 8819)

Treating individuals, people, ideas or values with contempt or ridicule. Scripture indicates that God's word and his people can expect to be treated with contempt by the world.

Objects of scoffing
-------------------

### Nations punished by God

**[JER 19:8](https://ref.ly/Jer19:8)** *See also* [1KI 9:6–9:9](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:6-1Kgs9:9); [JER 49:17](https://ref.ly/Jer49:17), [JER 50:13](https://ref.ly/Jer50:13); [LAM 2:15–2:17](https://ref.ly/Lam2:15-Lam2:17); [ZEP 2:13–2:15](https://ref.ly/Zeph2:13-Zeph2:15)

### God's messengers

**[2CH 36:15–36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15-2Chr36:16)**

Reasons for scoffing
--------------------

### Unbelief

**[2PE 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:3-2Pet3:4)** *See also* [ACT 13:41](https://ref.ly/Acts13:41); [HAB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Hab1:5); [JUD 1:17–1:19](https://ref.ly/Jude1:17-Jude1:19)

### Arrogance

**[HAB 1:10](https://ref.ly/Hab1:10)** *See also* [PSA 73:3–73:8](https://ref.ly/Ps73:3-Ps73:8); [ISA 28:14–28:15](https://ref.ly/Isa28:14-Isa28:15)

### Lack of wisdom

**[PRO 29:9](https://ref.ly/Prov29:9)**

God scoffs at the nations
-------------------------

**[PSA 59:5–59:8](https://ref.ly/Ps59:5-Ps59:8)** *See also* [PSA 2:1–2:6](https://ref.ly/Ps2:1-Ps2:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 19:8; 1 Kings 9:6-9; Jeremiah 49:17; Jeremiah 50:13; Lamentations 2:15-17; Zephaniah 2:13-15; 2 Chronicles 36:15-16; 2 Peter 3:3-4; Acts 13:41; Habakkuk 1:5; Jude 1:17-19; Habakkuk 1:10; Psalms 73:3-8; Isaiah 28:14-15; Proverbs 29:9; Psalms 59:5-8; Psalms 2:1-6
* **Associated Articles:** 5818: contempt (ID: `5818@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5893: insults (ID: `5893@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5900: laughter (ID: `5900@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5951: slander (ID: `5951@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8782: mockery (ID: `8782@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8794: persecution (ID: `8794@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8815: ridicule (ID: `8815@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8820: self-confidence (id: 8820)

A positive assessment of one's own abilities and resources. Scripture censures excessive self\-confidence, in that such an attitude lacks humility and fails to recognise the role of God's grace in the Christian life.

Excessive self\-confidence
--------------------------

### In one's own abilities

**[ISA 28:15](https://ref.ly/Isa28:15)**; **[MAT 26:33–26:35](https://ref.ly/Matt26:33-Matt26:35)** parallels [MRK 14:29–14:31](https://ref.ly/Mark14:29-Mark14:31), [LUK 23:33–23:34](https://ref.ly/Luke23:33-Luke23:34), [JHN 13:37–13:38](https://ref.ly/John13:37-John13:38) *See also* [LUK 22:33–22:34](https://ref.ly/Luke22:33-Luke22:34); [JDG 16:20](https://ref.ly/Judg16:20); [1KI 20:11](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:11); [2KI 19:23–19:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:23-2Kgs19:24); [JOB 8:13–8:14](https://ref.ly/Job8:13-Job8:14); [PSA 49:5–49:6](https://ref.ly/Ps49:5-Ps49:6); [JER 17:5](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5); [HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8)

### Excessive self\-confidence leads to pride

**[ISA 9:9–9:10](https://ref.ly/Isa9:9-Isa9:10)**; **[OBA 1:3](https://ref.ly/Obad1:3)** *See also* [GEN 11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:4), [GEN 37:5](https://ref.ly/Gen37:5); [EXO 15:9](https://ref.ly/Exod15:9); [2CH 26:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16); [PRO 16:18](https://ref.ly/Prov16:18); [ISA 16:6](https://ref.ly/Isa16:6); [EZK 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:2); [ACT 8:9](https://ref.ly/Acts8:9); [ROM 11:17–11:18](https://ref.ly/Rom11:17-Rom11:18); [1CO 5:2](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:2); [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)

### Challenges to excessive self\-confidence

**[NEH 6:16](https://ref.ly/Neh6:16)**; **[1CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:7)** *See also* [PSA 49:12–49:13](https://ref.ly/Ps49:12-Ps49:13), [PSA 52:7](https://ref.ly/Ps52:7); [PRO 3:5–3:7](https://ref.ly/Prov3:5-Prov3:7), [PRO 11:28](https://ref.ly/Prov11:28), [PRO 28:26](https://ref.ly/Prov28:26); [ISA 5:21](https://ref.ly/Isa5:21), [ISA 14:13–14:15](https://ref.ly/Isa14:13-Isa14:15); [JER 49:7](https://ref.ly/Jer49:7), [JER 49:4–49:5](https://ref.ly/Jer49:4-Jer49:5); [JAS 4:13–4:16](https://ref.ly/Jas4:13-Jas4:16)

### Excessive self\-confidence is a barrier to justification

[LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14)

Accurate self\-confidence
-------------------------

### Of one's natural advantages

**[PHP 3:4–3:6](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:6)** *See also* [ACT 22:3](https://ref.ly/Acts22:3), [ACT 22:28](https://ref.ly/Acts22:28)

### Of one's experience of God

**[2CO 12:2](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:2)** *See also* [ACT 22:4–22:21](https://ref.ly/Acts22:4-Acts22:21); [2CO 12:1](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:1), [2CO 13:5–13:6](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:5-2Cor13:6); [GAL 1:13–1:17](https://ref.ly/Gal1:13-Gal1:17), [GAL 6:4](https://ref.ly/Gal6:4)

### Of one's calling to serve

**[2CO 10:8](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:8)** *See also* [2CO 10:13–10:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:13-2Cor10:17); [GAL 2:7](https://ref.ly/Gal2:7)

### Of one's service

**[ACT 20:19](https://ref.ly/Acts20:19)**; **[ROM 12:3](https://ref.ly/Rom12:3)** *See also* [ACT 20:18](https://ref.ly/Acts20:18), [ACT 20:20–20:21](https://ref.ly/Acts20:20-Acts20:21); [1CO 9:15–9:18](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:15-1Cor9:18); [2CO 1:12](https://ref.ly/2Cor1:12); [PHP 2:16](https://ref.ly/Phil2:16); [1TH 2:1–2:12](https://ref.ly/1Thess2:1-1Thess2:12)

### Of one's own weakness

**[2CO 11:29–11:30](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:29-2Cor11:30)** *See also* [2CO 11:21–11:28](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:21-2Cor11:28), [2CO 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:9)

### Accurate self\-estimates lead to justification

[LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14)

### Justified self\-confidence may appear as pride

**[2CO 11:17–11:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:17-2Cor11:18)**

Jesus Christ's accurate assessment of himself
---------------------------------------------

**[MAT 16:15–16:17](https://ref.ly/Matt16:15-Matt16:17)** parallels [MRK 8:29](https://ref.ly/Mark8:29), [LUK 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/Luke9:20-Luke9:21) *See also* [MAT 26:62–26:64](https://ref.ly/Matt26:62-Matt26:64); [JHN 13:13](https://ref.ly/John13:13)

The proper source of all confidence
-----------------------------------

**[JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24)** *See also* [PSA 34:2](https://ref.ly/Ps34:2), [PSA 44:6–44:7](https://ref.ly/Ps44:6-Ps44:7); [ISA 10:15](https://ref.ly/Isa10:15); [1CO 1:27–1:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:27-1Cor1:31)

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 28:15; Matthew 26:33-35; Mark 14:29-31; Luke 23:33-34; John 13:37-38; Luke 22:33-34; Judges 16:20; 1 Kings 20:11; 2 Kings 19:23-24; Job 8:13-14; Psalms 49:5-6; Jeremiah 17:5; Hosea 12:8; Isaiah 9:9-10; Obadiah 1:3; Genesis 11:4; Genesis 37:5; Exodus 15:9; 2 Chronicles 26:16; Proverbs 16:18; Isaiah 16:6; Ezekiel 28:2; Acts 8:9; Romans 11:17-18; 1 Corinthians 5:2; 1 John 2:16; Nehemiah 6:16; 1 Corinthians 4:7; Psalms 49:12-13; Psalms 52:7; Proverbs 3:5-7; Proverbs 11:28; Proverbs 28:26; Isaiah 5:21; Isaiah 14:13-15; Jeremiah 49:7; Jeremiah 49:4-5; James 4:13-16; Luke 18:9-14; Philippians 3:4-6; Acts 22:3; Acts 22:28; 2 Corinthians 12:2; Acts 22:4-21; 2 Corinthians 12:1; 2 Corinthians 13:5-6; Galatians 1:13-17; Galatians 6:4; 2 Corinthians 10:8; 2 Corinthians 10:13-17; Galatians 2:7; Acts 20:19; Romans 12:3; Acts 20:18; Acts 20:20-21; 1 Corinthians 9:15-18; 2 Corinthians 1:12; Philippians 2:16; 1 Thessalonians 2:1-12; 2 Corinthians 11:29-30; 2 Corinthians 11:21-28; 2 Corinthians 12:9; 2 Corinthians 11:17-18; Matthew 16:15-17; Mark 8:29; Luke 9:20-21; Matthew 26:62-64; John 13:13; Jeremiah 9:23-24; Psalms 34:2; Psalms 44:6-7; Isaiah 10:15; 1 Corinthians 1:27-31

## 8821: self-indulgence (id: 8821)

Throwing off restraint and discipline and yielding to the desire to gratify selfish appetites and cravings. Scripture warns against such a characteristic and urges, instead, self\-control, generosity and consideration for others.

The desire to satisfy selfish cravings
--------------------------------------

**[EPH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Eph2:3)** *See also* [ROM 7:8](https://ref.ly/Rom7:8), [ROM 16:18](https://ref.ly/Rom16:18); [TIT 3:3](https://ref.ly/Titus3:3); [JAS 1:14](https://ref.ly/Jas1:14); [2PE 2:18–2:19](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:18-2Pet2:19); [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)

Examples of self\-indulgent desires
-----------------------------------

### Selfish ambition

**[JAS 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:16)** *See also* [PRO 18:1](https://ref.ly/Prov18:1); [GAL 5:20](https://ref.ly/Gal5:20); [PHP 1:17](https://ref.ly/Phil1:17)

### The desire for one's own way

**[ISA 56:11–56:12](https://ref.ly/Isa56:11-Isa56:12)** *See also* [DEU 29:19](https://ref.ly/Deut29:19); [EST 1:10–1:12](https://ref.ly/Esth1:10-Esth1:12); [JAS 4:1–4:3](https://ref.ly/Jas4:1-Jas4:3)

### The desire for sexual gratification

**[GAL 5:19](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19)** *See also* [GEN 19:5](https://ref.ly/Gen19:5)NUM [25:1](https://ref.ly/Gen25:1); [1CO 10:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:8)[2SA 11:2–11:4](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:2-2Sam11:4) *David's adultery with Bathsheba*; [2SA 13:11–13:14](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:11-2Sam13:14) *Amnon's rape of Tamar*; [PRO 7:16–7:18](https://ref.ly/Prov7:16-Prov7:18) *the lure of the adulteress*; [2CO 12:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:21) *sexual sin in the church at Corinth*; [2PE 2:14](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:14) *sexual desires of false teachers*

### The desire for wealth

**[JAS 5:3–5:5](https://ref.ly/Jas5:3-Jas5:5)** *See also* [JOS 7:21](https://ref.ly/Josh7:21) *Achan*; [1SA 8:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:3) *Samuel's sons*; [JER 22:17](https://ref.ly/Jer22:17) *Jehoiakim, king of Judah*; [HAG 1:4](https://ref.ly/Hag1:4) *the people of Judah*; [TIT 1:11](https://ref.ly/Titus1:11) *false teachers in Crete*

### Indulgence in food and drink

**[ISA 22:13](https://ref.ly/Isa22:13)** *See also* [PRO 23:20](https://ref.ly/Prov23:20); [ECC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Eccl6:7)

Abandoning restraint
--------------------

**[EPH 4:19](https://ref.ly/Eph4:19)** *See also* [ROM 1:26–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:26-Rom1:31); [2TI 3:1–3:4](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:4); [JUD 1:7](https://ref.ly/Jude1:7)

The consequences of self\-indulgence
------------------------------------

### Self\-indulgence proves futile

**[ECC 2:1–2:11](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:1-Eccl2:11)** *See also* [LUK 12:18–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:18-Luke12:21)

### Self\-indulgence leads to ruin

**[PRO 21:17](https://ref.ly/Prov21:17)** *See also* [PRO 6:26](https://ref.ly/Prov6:26), [PRO 23:21](https://ref.ly/Prov23:21), [PRO 23:29–23:32](https://ref.ly/Prov23:29-Prov23:32); [LUK 15:13–15:16](https://ref.ly/Luke15:13-Luke15:16); [1TI 5:6](https://ref.ly/1Tim5:6)

### Self\-indulgence brings God's judgment

**[ROM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Rom2:8)** *See also* [PRO 5:20–5:21](https://ref.ly/Prov5:20-Prov5:21); [PHP 3:19](https://ref.ly/Phil3:19); [DAN 4:29–4:32](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29-Dan4:32) *God's judgment on Nebuchadnezzar's selfish pride*; [AMO 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Amos4:1-Amos4:2) *God's judgment on Israel's pampered upper\-class women*; [AMO 6:4–6:7](https://ref.ly/Amos6:4-Amos6:7) *God's judgment on Israel's self\-indulgent nobility*; [LUK 12:19–12:20](https://ref.ly/Luke12:19-Luke12:20)

Dealing with self\-indulgence
-----------------------------

### Receiving new life through Jesus Christ

**[MAT 23:25](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25)** *Dealing with self\-indulgence requires an inner transformation.* *See also* [ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2); [COL 2:21–2:23](https://ref.ly/Col2:21-Col2:23)**[ROM 13:14](https://ref.ly/Rom13:14)** *See also* [GAL 5:24](https://ref.ly/Gal5:24); [TIT 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11-Titus2:12)ROM [8:5](https://ref.ly/Titus8:5); [GAL 5:16](https://ref.ly/Gal5:16)

### Seeking God's will

[PSA 119:36](https://ref.ly/Ps119:36); ; [1PE 1:14](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:14), [1PE 4:2](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:2)

### Self\-control

**[1TH 4:3–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:3-1Thess4:5)** *See also* [PRO 23:4](https://ref.ly/Prov23:4); [1CO 9:25–9:27](https://ref.ly/1Cor9:25-1Cor9:27); [1PE 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:7); [2PE 1:6](https://ref.ly/2Pet1:6)

### Ruthless self\-denial

**[COL 3:5](https://ref.ly/Col3:5)** *See also* [DAN 1:8](https://ref.ly/Dan1:8); [MAT 5:28–5:29](https://ref.ly/Matt5:28-Matt5:29); [1PE 2:11](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:11)

### Consideration for others

**[GAL 5:13](https://ref.ly/Gal5:13)** *See also* [1CO 10:23–10:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:23-1Cor10:24); [PHP 2:3–2:4](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3-Phil2:4)

### Generosity

[DEU 15:7–15:8](https://ref.ly/Deut15:7-Deut15:8); ; [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Ephesians 2:3; Romans 7:8; Romans 16:18; Titus 3:3; James 1:14; 2 Peter 2:18-19; 1 John 2:16; James 3:14-16; Proverbs 18:1; Galatians 5:20; Philippians 1:17; Isaiah 56:11-12; Deuteronomy 29:19; Esther 1:10-12; James 4:1-3; Galatians 5:19; Genesis 19:5; Genesis 25:1; 1 Corinthians 10:8; 2 Samuel 11:2-4; 2 Samuel 13:11-14; Proverbs 7:16-18; 2 Corinthians 12:21; 2 Peter 2:14; James 5:3-5; Joshua 7:21; 1 Samuel 8:3; Jeremiah 22:17; Haggai 1:4; Titus 1:11; Isaiah 22:13; Proverbs 23:20; Ecclesiastes 6:7; Ephesians 4:19; Romans 1:26-31; 2 Timothy 3:1-4; Jude 1:7; Ecclesiastes 2:1-11; Luke 12:18-21; Proverbs 21:17; Proverbs 6:26; Proverbs 23:21; Proverbs 23:29-32; Luke 15:13-16; 1 Timothy 5:6; Romans 2:8; Proverbs 5:20-21; Philippians 3:19; Daniel 4:29-32; Amos 4:1-2; Amos 6:4-7; Luke 12:19-20; Matthew 23:25; Romans 12:2; Colossians 2:21-23; Romans 13:14; Galatians 5:24; Titus 2:11-12; Titus 8:5; Galatians 5:16; Psalms 119:36; 1 Peter 1:14; 1 Peter 4:2; 1 Thessalonians 4:3-5; Proverbs 23:4; 1 Corinthians 9:25-27; 1 Peter 4:7; 2 Peter 1:6; Colossians 3:5; Daniel 1:8; Matthew 5:28-29; 1 Peter 2:11; Galatians 5:13; 1 Corinthians 10:23-24; Philippians 2:3-4; Deuteronomy 15:7-8; Luke 19:8

## 8822: self-justification (id: 8822)

A self\-centred attitude by which people defend their actions and beliefs without reference to God. Scripture insists that the confidence and ultimate justification of believers lie with God, and condemns those who look elsewhere for these.

The impossibility of self\-justification
----------------------------------------

### Self\-justification is impossible before a holy God

**[JOB 25:4](https://ref.ly/Job25:4)** *See also* [1SA 6:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:20); [MAL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Mal3:2); [REV 6:17](https://ref.ly/Rev6:17)

### Justification is achieved by God, not by people

**[ROM 5:1–5:2](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1-Rom5:2)** *See also* [ROM 3:27–3:30](https://ref.ly/Rom3:27-Rom3:30); [GAL 2:20–2:21](https://ref.ly/Gal2:20-Gal2:21)

How self\-justification occurs
------------------------------

### Self\-justification occurs through ignoring God and his ways

**[NEH 9:16](https://ref.ly/Neh9:16)** *See also* [LUK 16:14–16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:14-Luke16:15)

### Self\-justification occurs through pride

**[LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14)** *See also* [MAT 23:1–23:7](https://ref.ly/Matt23:1-Matt23:7) parallels [MRK 12:38–12:39](https://ref.ly/Mark12:38-Mark12:39), [LUK 20:45–20:46](https://ref.ly/Luke20:45-Luke20:46); [ROM 2:17–2:27](https://ref.ly/Rom2:17-Rom2:27)

### Self\-justification occurs through legalism

**[LUK 10:25–10:29](https://ref.ly/Luke10:25-Luke10:29)** *See also* [GAL 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/Gal3:1-Gal3:5), [GAL 5:4](https://ref.ly/Gal5:4)

Self\-justification causes separation from God and Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------------------------------

**[GAL 5:4](https://ref.ly/Gal5:4)** *See also* [MAT 18:21–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:21-Matt18:35); [LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14)

Examples of self\-justification
-------------------------------

[GEN 3:8–3:12](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8-Gen3:12) *Adam*; ; [EXO 32:19–32:24](https://ref.ly/Exod32:19-Exod32:24) *Aaron*; ; [1SA 13:7–13:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:7-1Sam13:14) *Saul*; ; [MAT 19:16–19:22](https://ref.ly/Matt19:16-Matt19:22) parallels [MRK 10:17–10:22](https://ref.ly/Mark10:17-Mark10:22), [LUK 18:18–18:23](https://ref.ly/Luke18:18-Luke18:23) *the rich young man*; ; [PHP 3:4–3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:9) *Paul as a Pharisee*

God's attitude to self\-justification
-------------------------------------

### God's condemnation of self\-justification

**[ROM 2:1–2:11](https://ref.ly/Rom2:1-Rom2:11)**

### God's punishment of self\-justification

**[PRO 29:1](https://ref.ly/Prov29:1)** *See also* [GEN 3:8–3:19](https://ref.ly/Gen3:8-Gen3:19); [JER 6:19–6:23](https://ref.ly/Jer6:19-Jer6:23)

The true basis of justification
-------------------------------

### People are justified by God's grace alone

**[EPH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:9)** *See also* [ROM 3:21–3:24](https://ref.ly/Rom3:21-Rom3:24); [TIT 2:11](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11), [TIT 3:4–3:7](https://ref.ly/Titus3:4-Titus3:7)

### God accepts those who trust in his mercy without trying to justify themselves

**[PSA 130:3–130:4](https://ref.ly/Ps130:3-Ps130:4)** *See also* [PSA 143:2](https://ref.ly/Ps143:2); [LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 25:4; 1 Samuel 6:20; Malachi 3:2; Revelation 6:17; Romans 5:1-2; Romans 3:27-30; Galatians 2:20-21; Nehemiah 9:16; Luke 16:14-15; Luke 18:9-14; Matthew 23:1-7; Mark 12:38-39; Luke 20:45-46; Romans 2:17-27; Luke 10:25-29; Galatians 3:1-5; Galatians 5:4; Matthew 18:21-35; Genesis 3:8-12; Exodus 32:19-24; 1 Samuel 13:7-14; Matthew 19:16-22; Mark 10:17-22; Luke 18:18-23; Philippians 3:4-9; Romans 2:1-11; Proverbs 29:1; Genesis 3:8-19; Jeremiah 6:19-23; Ephesians 2:8-9; Romans 3:21-24; Titus 2:11; Titus 3:4-7; Psalms 130:3-4; Psalms 143:2
* **Associated Articles:** 1025: God, anger of (ID: `1025@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4030: world, human behaviour in (ID: `4030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5943: self-deception (ID: `5943@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6178: hardness of heart (ID: `6178@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6666: grace (ID: `6666@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6686: mercy (ID: `6686@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8276: humility (ID: `8276@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8824: self-righteousness, nature of (ID: `8824@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 9250: woe (ID: `9250@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8823: self-righteousness (id: 8823)

A moral self\-confidence and superiority arising from satisfaction in one's own achievements. True righteousness can only be found in a relationship with Jesus Christ.

* **Associated Articles:** 8824: self-righteousness, nature of (ID: `8824@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8825: self-righteousness, and the gospel (ID: `8825@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8824: self-righteousness, nature of (id: 8824)

Self\-righteousness fosters the sinful human illusions of sinlessness, sufficiency and freedom.

The claim to self\-righteousness
--------------------------------

**[ROM 10:3](https://ref.ly/Rom10:3)** *See also* [LUK 10:29](https://ref.ly/Luke10:29), [LUK 18:9](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9); [PHP 3:4–3:6](https://ref.ly/Phil3:4-Phil3:6)

The illusion of self\-righteousness
-----------------------------------

### That people are sinless

**[1JN 1:8](https://ref.ly/1John1:8)** *See also* [PRO 20:9](https://ref.ly/Prov20:9); [HOS 12:8](https://ref.ly/Hos12:8)

### That people are guiltless

**[PRO 16:2](https://ref.ly/Prov16:2)** *See also* [JER 2:34–2:35](https://ref.ly/Jer2:34-Jer2:35)

### That people are deserving

**[DEU 9:4](https://ref.ly/Deut9:4)** *See also* [EZK 28:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:2)

The results of self\-righteousness
----------------------------------

### Self\-deception

**[1JN 1:5–1:6](https://ref.ly/1John1:5-1John1:6)** *See also* [PRO 30:12](https://ref.ly/Prov30:12); [JER 17:9](https://ref.ly/Jer17:9); [MAT 23:23–23:24](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23-Matt23:24), [MAT 23:27–23:28](https://ref.ly/Matt23:27-Matt23:28); [LUK 16:15](https://ref.ly/Luke16:15); [HEB 3:13](https://ref.ly/Heb3:13)

### A sense of superiority

**[LUK 18:9–18:11](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:11)** *See also* [PHP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3); [COL 2:16–2:18](https://ref.ly/Col2:16-Col2:18)

### Separation from God

**[1JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/1John1:10)** *See also* [ZEP 3:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:11); [2CO 10:18](https://ref.ly/2Cor10:18); [GAL 5:4](https://ref.ly/Gal5:4)

Examples of self\-righteousness
-------------------------------

[NUM 16:3](https://ref.ly/Num16:3) *some Israelites opposed to Moses*; ; [1SA 15:13–15:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam15:13-1Sam15:21) *Saul*; ; [PSA 10:5](https://ref.ly/Ps10:5) *the wicked*; [LUK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Luke16:14), [LUK 18:9–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:9-Luke18:14); ; [JHN 9:39–9:41](https://ref.ly/John9:39-John9:41); [REV 3:17–3:18](https://ref.ly/Rev3:17-Rev3:18) *the church at Laodicea*

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 10:3; Luke 10:29; Luke 18:9; Philippians 3:4-6; 1 John 1:8; Proverbs 20:9; Hosea 12:8; Proverbs 16:2; Jeremiah 2:34-35; Deuteronomy 9:4; Ezekiel 28:2; 1 John 1:5-6; Proverbs 30:12; Jeremiah 17:9; Matthew 23:23-24; Matthew 23:27-28; Luke 16:15; Hebrews 3:13; Luke 18:9-11; Philippians 2:3; Colossians 2:16-18; 1 John 1:10; Zephaniah 3:11; 2 Corinthians 10:18; Galatians 5:4; Numbers 16:3; 1 Samuel 15:13-21; Psalms 10:5; Luke 16:14; Luke 18:9-14; John 9:39-41; Revelation 3:17-18
* **Associated Articles:** 5961: superiority (ID: `5961@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6020: sin (ID: `6020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6121: boasting (ID: `6121@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6145: deceit (ID: `6145@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8714: dishonesty (ID: `8714@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8767: hypocrisy (ID: `8767@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8822: self-justification (ID: `8822@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8825: self-righteousness, and the gospel (id: 8825)

Self\-righteousness is an obstacle to the gospel, which insists that true righteousness can only be found in Jesus Christ.

The real issue of righteousness
-------------------------------

**[JOB 9:2](https://ref.ly/Job9:2)** *See also* [1SA 6:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:20); [JOB 4:17](https://ref.ly/Job4:17), [JOB 15:14](https://ref.ly/Job15:14), [JOB 32:2](https://ref.ly/Job32:2) *Although Elihu had misjudged Job, his main point, that only God was righteous, was correct*; [JOB 40:8](https://ref.ly/Job40:8); [PSA 130:3](https://ref.ly/Ps130:3)

True righteousness is not the result of human effort
----------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 3:20](https://ref.ly/Rom3:20)** *Even though God's law tells people what God requires, human beings cannot keep it because of the weakness of their sinful human natures.* *See also* [ROM 9:31](https://ref.ly/Rom9:31); [GAL 2:16](https://ref.ly/Gal2:16), [GAL 3:11](https://ref.ly/Gal3:11); [EPH 2:8–2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8-Eph2:9); [TIT 3:5](https://ref.ly/Titus3:5); [1JN 2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:16)

Self\-righteousness is a contradiction in terms
-----------------------------------------------

**[2CH 6:36](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:36)**; **[ISA 64:6](https://ref.ly/Isa64:6)** *See also* [EZR 9:15](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:15); [PSA 14:3](https://ref.ly/Ps14:3); [ECC 7:20](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:20); [DAN 9:7](https://ref.ly/Dan9:7); [ROM 3:10](https://ref.ly/Rom3:10), [ROM 3:23](https://ref.ly/Rom3:23)

God calls people from their pretended self\-righteousness
---------------------------------------------------------

**[ISA 2:22](https://ref.ly/Isa2:22)** *See also* [PSA 75:2–75:4](https://ref.ly/Ps75:2-Ps75:4); [JER 17:5](https://ref.ly/Jer17:5); [MAT 5:20](https://ref.ly/Matt5:20)

True righteouness
-----------------

### It comes from God

**[ROM 10:3](https://ref.ly/Rom10:3)** *See also* [PSA 98:2](https://ref.ly/Ps98:2); [ISA 46:13](https://ref.ly/Isa46:13), [ISA 51:5](https://ref.ly/Isa51:5); [JER 9:23–9:24](https://ref.ly/Jer9:23-Jer9:24); [ROM 1:17](https://ref.ly/Rom1:17)

### It is grounded in Jesus Christ

**[ACT 13:39](https://ref.ly/Acts13:39)** *See also* [1CO 1:30](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:30); [2CO 5:21](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:21)

### It comes about by faith

**[ROM 5:1](https://ref.ly/Rom5:1)** *See also* [GAL 5:5](https://ref.ly/Gal5:5); [EPH 2:8](https://ref.ly/Eph2:8)

### It is received in humility

**[LUK 18:13–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:13-Luke18:14)** *See also* [ROM 3:27](https://ref.ly/Rom3:27), [ROM 4:2](https://ref.ly/Rom4:2); [1CO 1:31](https://ref.ly/1Cor1:31), [1CO 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Cor4:7); [2CO 11:30](https://ref.ly/2Cor11:30), [2CO 12:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:9); [GAL 6:13–6:14](https://ref.ly/Gal6:13-Gal6:14); [EPH 2:9](https://ref.ly/Eph2:9); [PHP 3:7–3:9](https://ref.ly/Phil3:7-Phil3:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Job 9:2; 1 Samuel 6:20; Job 4:17; Job 15:14; Job 32:2; Job 40:8; Psalms 130:3; Romans 3:20; Romans 9:31; Galatians 2:16; Galatians 3:11; Ephesians 2:8-9; Titus 3:5; 1 John 2:16; 2 Chronicles 6:36; Isaiah 64:6; Ezra 9:15; Psalms 14:3; Ecclesiastes 7:20; Daniel 9:7; Romans 3:10; Romans 3:23; Isaiah 2:22; Psalms 75:2-4; Jeremiah 17:5; Matthew 5:20; Romans 10:3; Psalms 98:2; Isaiah 46:13; Isaiah 51:5; Jeremiah 9:23-24; Romans 1:17; Acts 13:39; 1 Corinthians 1:30; 2 Corinthians 5:21; Romans 5:1; Galatians 5:5; Ephesians 2:8; Luke 18:13-14; Romans 3:27; Romans 4:2; 1 Corinthians 1:31; 1 Corinthians 4:7; 2 Corinthians 11:30; 2 Corinthians 12:9; Galatians 6:13-14; Ephesians 2:9; Philippians 3:7-9
* **Associated Articles:** 6676: justification (ID: `6676@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8020: faith (ID: `8020@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8154: righteousness (ID: `8154@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8774: legalism (ID: `8774@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8827: selfishness (id: 8827)

A self\-centred concern for oneself, without due regard to the needs of others. Scripture treats selfishness as an aspect of sin and urges believers to care for others as well as themselves.

Selfishness is a characteristic of the sinner
---------------------------------------------

**[ROM 8:5](https://ref.ly/Rom8:5)**; **[2CO 5:15](https://ref.ly/2Cor5:15)** *See also* [PRO 18:1](https://ref.ly/Prov18:1); [ROM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Rom2:8); [EPH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Eph2:3)

Selfishness is a characteristic of sinful society
-------------------------------------------------

**[2TI 3:1–3:2](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:2)** *See also* [JDG 17:6](https://ref.ly/Judg17:6), [JDG 21:25](https://ref.ly/Judg21:25)

Expressions of selfishness
--------------------------

### Greed

**[MAT 23:25](https://ref.ly/Matt23:25)** *See also* [MRK 12:7](https://ref.ly/Mark12:7); [LUK 12:13–12:21](https://ref.ly/Luke12:13-Luke12:21); [ACT 5:3](https://ref.ly/Acts5:3)

### Ambition

**[PHP 2:3](https://ref.ly/Phil2:3)** *See also* [JER 45:5](https://ref.ly/Jer45:5); [GAL 5:19–5:20](https://ref.ly/Gal5:19-Gal5:20); [PHP 1:17](https://ref.ly/Phil1:17); [JAS 3:14–3:16](https://ref.ly/Jas3:14-Jas3:16)

### Boastfulness

**[DAN 4:30](https://ref.ly/Dan4:30)**

Examples of selfishness
-----------------------

*See also* [GEN 11:4](https://ref.ly/Gen11:4) *the builders of the tower of Babel*; [GEN 13:10–13:11](https://ref.ly/Gen13:10-Gen13:11) *Lot in his choice of territory*; [EZK 28:2–28:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek28:2-Ezek28:5) *the king of Tyre*; [EZK 34:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:8) *Israel's leaders*; [MRK 10:35](https://ref.ly/Mark10:35) *James and John*

Self\-absorption as the result of pain or depression
----------------------------------------------------

**[JOB 14:22](https://ref.ly/Job14:22)**; **[1KI 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:10)**

Antidotes to selfishness
------------------------

### God's word

**[PSA 119:36](https://ref.ly/Ps119:36)**

### The example of Jesus Christ

**[ROM 15:3](https://ref.ly/Rom15:3)** *See also* [PHP 2:4–2:8](https://ref.ly/Phil2:4-Phil2:8)

### Dying to the old nature

**[ROM 6:6](https://ref.ly/Rom6:6)** *See also* [EPH 4:22](https://ref.ly/Eph4:22); [COL 3:9](https://ref.ly/Col3:9)

### Serving one another

**[1PE 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:10)** *See also* [1CO 10:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:24)

### Loving one another

**[JHN 13:34](https://ref.ly/John13:34)** *See also* [MAT 22:39](https://ref.ly/Matt22:39); [ROM 13:8–13:10](https://ref.ly/Rom13:8-Rom13:10)

* **Associated Passages:** Romans 8:5; 2 Corinthians 5:15; Proverbs 18:1; Romans 2:8; Ephesians 2:3; 2 Timothy 3:1-2; Judges 17:6; Judges 21:25; Matthew 23:25; Mark 12:7; Luke 12:13-21; Acts 5:3; Philippians 2:3; Jeremiah 45:5; Galatians 5:19-20; Philippians 1:17; James 3:14-16; Daniel 4:30; Genesis 11:4; Genesis 13:10-11; Ezekiel 28:2-5; Ezekiel 34:8; Mark 10:35; Job 14:22; 1 Kings 19:10; Psalms 119:36; Romans 15:3; Philippians 2:4-8; Romans 6:6; Ephesians 4:22; Colossians 3:9; 1 Peter 4:10; 1 Corinthians 10:24; John 13:34; Matthew 22:39; Romans 13:8-10
* **Associated Articles:** 4030: world, human behaviour in (ID: `4030@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5503: rich, the (ID: `5503@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5558: storing (ID: `5558@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5786: ambition, negative aspects of (ID: `5786@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5869: greed (ID: `5869@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6121: boasting (ID: `6121@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6133: coveting (ID: `6133@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6166: flesh, sinful nature (ID: `6166@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8356: unselfishness (ID: `8356@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8802: pride (ID: `8802@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8821: self-indulgence (ID: `8821@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8821: self-indulgence (ID: `8821@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8828: spite (id: 8828)

The malicious desire to retaliate in words or action. It may be in response to good or evil. Believers are to rid themselves of any such vindictive spirit.

Examples of people acting out of spite
--------------------------------------

### In response to good

[1KI 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:1-1Kgs19:2) *Jezebel*; ; [2CH 16:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:10) *Asa, king of Judah*; ; [EST 3:6](https://ref.ly/Esth3:6) *Haman*; ; [MAT 14:3–14:12](https://ref.ly/Matt14:3-Matt14:12) parallel [MRK 6:17–6:29](https://ref.ly/Mark6:17-Mark6:29) *Herodias*; ; [JHN 12:10](https://ref.ly/John12:10) *the chief priests*; [ACT 7:54](https://ref.ly/Acts7:54), [ACT 7:57–7:58](https://ref.ly/Acts7:57-Acts7:58)

### In response to evil

**[PSA 140:3](https://ref.ly/Ps140:3)** *See also* [GEN 27:41](https://ref.ly/Gen27:41) *Esau*[GEN 37:4](https://ref.ly/Gen37:4), [GEN 37:8](https://ref.ly/Gen37:8), [GEN 37:11](https://ref.ly/Gen37:11), [GEN 37:18–37:27](https://ref.ly/Gen37:18-Gen37:27)[EZK 25:15](https://ref.ly/Ezek25:15) *the Philistines*; [ACT 23:12–23:15](https://ref.ly/Acts23:12-Acts23:15) *some Jews*

Spite has no place in the lives of believers
--------------------------------------------

### Believers are to rid themselves of all spitefulness

**[EPH 4:31](https://ref.ly/Eph4:31)** *See also* [1CO 5:8](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:8); [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Col3:8); [1PE 2:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:1)

### As a result believers are enabled not to retaliate against others

**[LEV 19:18](https://ref.ly/Lev19:18)** *See also* [PRO 20:22](https://ref.ly/Prov20:22), [PRO 24:29](https://ref.ly/Prov24:29); [MAT 5:38–5:40](https://ref.ly/Matt5:38-Matt5:40); [ROM 12:17](https://ref.ly/Rom12:17), [ROM 12:19–12:21](https://ref.ly/Rom12:19-Rom12:21); [1TH 5:15](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:15); [1PE 2:21–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:21-1Pet2:23), [1PE 3:9](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:9)

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 19:1-2; 2 Chronicles 16:10; Esther 3:6; Matthew 14:3-12; Mark 6:17-29; John 12:10; Acts 7:54; Acts 7:57-58; Psalms 140:3; Genesis 27:41; Genesis 37:4; Genesis 37:8; Genesis 37:11; Genesis 37:18-27; Ezekiel 25:15; Acts 23:12-15; Ephesians 4:31; 1 Corinthians 5:8; Colossians 3:8; 1 Peter 2:1; Leviticus 19:18; Proverbs 20:22; Proverbs 24:29; Matthew 5:38-40; Romans 12:17; Romans 12:19-21; 1 Thessalonians 5:15; 1 Peter 2:21-23; 1 Peter 3:9
* **Associated Articles:** 5495: revenge, and retaliation (ID: `5495@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5510: rumours (ID: `5510@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5546: speech (ID: `5546@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5817: conspiracies (ID: `5817@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5818: contempt (ID: `5818@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5875: hatred (ID: `5875@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5917: plans (ID: `5917@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5951: slander (ID: `5951@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8829: superstition (id: 8829)

Misplaced credulity concerning the supernatural, which leads to irrational fear, misdirected reverence, false religion and magic, and which brings God's judgment. Scripture warns against and condemns such things.

Superstition causing irrational fear
------------------------------------

**[JER 10:2](https://ref.ly/Jer10:2)** *See also* [JOB 15:20–15:24](https://ref.ly/Job15:20-Job15:24), [JOB 18:11](https://ref.ly/Job18:11)

### Fear of Jesus Christ or his disciples

**[MAT 14:26–14:27](https://ref.ly/Matt14:26-Matt14:27)** parallel [MRK 6:49–6:50](https://ref.ly/Mark6:49-Mark6:50) *See also* [MAT 14:2](https://ref.ly/Matt14:2) parallel [MRK 6:14](https://ref.ly/Mark6:14); [ACT 5:13](https://ref.ly/Acts5:13)

### Fear of other gods

**[1KI 20:23](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:23)** *See also* [1KI 18:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:28), [1KI 20:28](https://ref.ly/1Kgs20:28); [ACT 17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:23)

Misunderstanding God's work
---------------------------

**[ACT 8:18–8:19](https://ref.ly/Acts8:18-Acts8:19)** *See also* [LUK 24:36–24:37](https://ref.ly/Luke24:36-Luke24:37); [ACT 12:15](https://ref.ly/Acts12:15), [ACT 19:13](https://ref.ly/Acts19:13) *invoking the name of Jesus Christ for exorcism as though it were a magic formula*; [ACT 28:3–28:4](https://ref.ly/Acts28:3-Acts28:4)

Superstition leading to misdirected reverence
---------------------------------------------

**[ACT 14:11–14:13](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11-Acts14:13)** *See also* [ACT 28:5–28:6](https://ref.ly/Acts28:5-Acts28:6); [REV 22:8–22:9](https://ref.ly/Rev22:8-Rev22:9)

False religion
--------------

**[JDG 10:6–10:7](https://ref.ly/Judg10:6-Judg10:7)** *See also* [1SA 7:3–7:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:3-1Sam7:4); [1KI 13:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs13:33), [1KI 14:15–14:16](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:15-1Kgs14:16), [1KI 14:22–14:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:22-1Kgs14:24); [2CH 28:2–28:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:2-2Chr28:4) parallel [2KI 16:3–16:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:3-2Kgs16:4); [JER 9:14](https://ref.ly/Jer9:14), [JER 44:17–44:18](https://ref.ly/Jer44:17-Jer44:18); [HOS 11:2](https://ref.ly/Hos11:2)

Unhelpful speculations
----------------------

**[1TI 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:3-1Tim1:4)** *See also* [ISA 65:2–65:3](https://ref.ly/Isa65:2-Isa65:3), [ISA 66:17–66:18](https://ref.ly/Isa66:17-Isa66:18); [EZK 13:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:2), [EZK 13:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:17); [1TI 6:3–6:4](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:3-1Tim6:4)

The true demonic source of superstition
---------------------------------------

**[1TI 4:1](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:1)** *See also* [REV 9:20–9:21](https://ref.ly/Rev9:20-Rev9:21)

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 10:2; Job 15:20-24; Job 18:11; Matthew 14:26-27; Mark 6:49-50; Matthew 14:2; Mark 6:14; Acts 5:13; 1 Kings 20:23; 1 Kings 18:28; 1 Kings 20:28; Acts 17:23; Acts 8:18-19; Luke 24:36-37; Acts 12:15; Acts 19:13; Acts 28:3-4; Acts 14:11-13; Acts 28:5-6; Revelation 22:8-9; Judges 10:6-7; 1 Samuel 7:3-4; 1 Kings 13:33; 1 Kings 14:15-16; 1 Kings 14:22-24; 2 Chronicles 28:2-4; 2 Kings 16:3-4; Jeremiah 9:14; Jeremiah 44:17-18; Hosea 11:2; 1 Timothy 1:3-4; Isaiah 65:2-3; Isaiah 66:17-18; Ezekiel 13:2; Ezekiel 13:17; 1 Timothy 6:3-4; 1 Timothy 4:1; Revelation 9:20-21
* **Associated Articles:** 1409: dream (ID: `1409@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4130: demons (ID: `4130@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4155: divination (ID: `4155@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4185: sorcery and magic (ID: `4185@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5423: myths (ID: `5423@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5864: futility (ID: `5864@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8333: reverence (ID: `8333@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8709: astrology (ID: `8709@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8747: false gods (ID: `8747@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8748: false religion (ID: `8748@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8750: false teachings (ID: `8750@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8830: suspicion (id: 8830)

The belief that people or things are not as they appear. It may be based on evidence, but it can also be ill\-founded, occasioned by jealousy or malicious gossip.

Suspicion of sexual impurity
----------------------------

**[DEU 22:13–22:19](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13-Deut22:19)** *See also* [NUM 5:11–5:31](https://ref.ly/Num5:11-Num5:31)

Suspicion of drunkenness
------------------------

**[1SA 1:9–1:18](https://ref.ly/1Sam1:9-1Sam1:18)** *See also* [ACT 2:13–2:15](https://ref.ly/Acts2:13-Acts2:15)

Suspicion of espionage
----------------------

**[2SA 10:1–10:5](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:1-2Sam10:5)**

Suspicion of treachery
----------------------

**[1SA 18:6–18:9](https://ref.ly/1Sam18:6-1Sam18:9)** *See also* [EXO 1:9–1:10](https://ref.ly/Exod1:9-Exod1:10); [1SA 20:24–20:31](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:24-1Sam20:31), [1SA 21:10–21:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam21:10-1Sam21:12), [1SA 29:1–29:11](https://ref.ly/1Sam29:1-1Sam29:11)

Lack of suspicion
-----------------

**[2SA 15:7–15:12](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:7-2Sam15:12)** *See also* [1SA 27:1–27:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:1-1Sam27:12); [2SA 13:1–13:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:1-2Sam13:11), [2SA 13:23–13:29](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:23-2Sam13:29)

Evil suspicions
---------------

**[1TI 6:3–6:5](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:3-1Tim6:5)**

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 22:13-19; Numbers 5:11-31; 1 Samuel 1:9-18; Acts 2:13-15; 2 Samuel 10:1-5; 1 Samuel 18:6-9; Exodus 1:9-10; 1 Samuel 20:24-31; 1 Samuel 21:10-12; 1 Samuel 29:1-11; 2 Samuel 15:7-12; 1 Samuel 27:1-12; 2 Samuel 13:1-11; 2 Samuel 13:23-29; 1 Timothy 6:3-5
* **Associated Articles:** 5969: treachery (ID: `5969@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6242: adultery (ID: `6242@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8277: innocence (ID: `8277@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8719: distrust (ID: `8719@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8721: doubt (ID: `8721@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8773: jealousy (ID: `8773@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8831: syncretism (id: 8831)

The incorporation into religious faith and practice of elements from other religions, resulting in a loss of integrity and assimilation to the surrounding culture.

Syncretism in Israel
--------------------

### God's claim to exclusive worship, the basis of the rejection of syncretism

**[EXO 20:3](https://ref.ly/Exod20:3)** parallel [DEU 5:7](https://ref.ly/Deut5:7) *See also* [DEU 6:13–6:15](https://ref.ly/Deut6:13-Deut6:15); [JOS 24:14–24:15](https://ref.ly/Josh24:14-Josh24:15); [1KI 18:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:21)

### Syncretism forbidden to Israel

**[EXO 34:15–34:16](https://ref.ly/Exod34:15-Exod34:16)** *See also* [DEU 7:1–7:6](https://ref.ly/Deut7:1-Deut7:6); [JOS 23:16](https://ref.ly/Josh23:16); [1SA 7:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam7:3)

### Israelite practice of syncretism

**[JER 19:4–19:5](https://ref.ly/Jer19:4-Jer19:5)** *See also* [2KI 17:34–17:41](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:34-2Kgs17:41), [2KI 21:1–21:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:1-2Kgs21:7) parallel [2CH 33:1–33:7](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:1-2Chr33:7); [EZK 8:9–8:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:9-Ezek8:16)

### The consequence of syncretism

**[JER 2:5](https://ref.ly/Jer2:5)** *See also* [DEU 18:9–18:13](https://ref.ly/Deut18:9-Deut18:13); [2KI 17:15–17:17](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:15-2Kgs17:17); [ISA 2:6](https://ref.ly/Isa2:6)

### Punishment for syncretism

**[ZEP 1:4–1:5](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:4-Zeph1:5)** *See also* [DEU 17:2–17:7](https://ref.ly/Deut17:2-Deut17:7); [JER 19:10–19:13](https://ref.ly/Jer19:10-Jer19:13); [HOS 2:2–2:13](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2-Hos2:13)

Syncretism in NT times
----------------------

### Mediterranean society was syncretistic

[ACT 14:11–14:13](https://ref.ly/Acts14:11-Acts14:13), [ACT 17:16–17:23](https://ref.ly/Acts17:16-Acts17:23), [ACT 19:13–19:16](https://ref.ly/Acts19:13-Acts19:16)

### NT opposition to syncretism

**[HEB 13:9](https://ref.ly/Heb13:9)** *See also* [1CO 10:21](https://ref.ly/1Cor10:21); [GAL 4:9–4:10](https://ref.ly/Gal4:9-Gal4:10); [COL 2:18](https://ref.ly/Col2:18)

Monotheism is incompatible with syncretism
------------------------------------------

**[ISA 44:6](https://ref.ly/Isa44:6)** *See also* [DEU 6:4–6:5](https://ref.ly/Deut6:4-Deut6:5); [ISA 43:10–43:12](https://ref.ly/Isa43:10-Isa43:12); [1CO 8:4–8:6](https://ref.ly/1Cor8:4-1Cor8:6)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 20:3; Deuteronomy 5:7; Deuteronomy 6:13-15; Joshua 24:14-15; 1 Kings 18:21; Exodus 34:15-16; Deuteronomy 7:1-6; Joshua 23:16; 1 Samuel 7:3; Jeremiah 19:4-5; 2 Kings 17:34-41; 2 Kings 21:1-7; 2 Chronicles 33:1-7; Ezekiel 8:9-16; Jeremiah 2:5; Deuteronomy 18:9-13; 2 Kings 17:15-17; Isaiah 2:6; Zephaniah 1:4-5; Deuteronomy 17:2-7; Jeremiah 19:10-13; Hosea 2:2-13; Acts 14:11-13; Acts 17:16-23; Acts 19:13-16; Hebrews 13:9; 1 Corinthians 10:21; Galatians 4:9-10; Colossians 2:18; Isaiah 44:6; Deuteronomy 6:4-5; Isaiah 43:10-12; 1 Corinthians 8:4-6
* **Associated Articles:** 1345: covenant (ID: `1345@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5441: philosophy (ID: `5441@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7312: Baal (ID: `7312@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7374: high places (ID: `7374@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 7442: shrine (ID: `7442@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8138: monotheism (ID: `8138@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8747: false gods (ID: `8747@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8768: idolatry (ID: `8768@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8832: testing (id: 8832)

God allows his people to pass through certain experiences, so that he may see, and prove to them, that their faith in him is genuine. Believers sometimes doubt God and test him, to see if he is true to his word, but this is forbidden in Scripture.

God tests his people
--------------------

**[JAS 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Jas1:2-Jas1:3)**; **[1PE 1:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:7)** *See also* [JOB 7:17–7:18](https://ref.ly/Job7:17-Job7:18), [JOB 23:10](https://ref.ly/Job23:10); [PSA 11:5](https://ref.ly/Ps11:5); [ISA 1:25](https://ref.ly/Isa1:25); [ZEC 13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:9); [MAL 3:3](https://ref.ly/Mal3:3); [JAS 1:12](https://ref.ly/Jas1:12)

Examples of God testing his people collectively
-----------------------------------------------

**[EXO 16:4](https://ref.ly/Exod16:4)**; **[DEU 8:2–8:3](https://ref.ly/Deut8:2-Deut8:3)** *See also* [EXO 20:20](https://ref.ly/Exod20:20) *at the giving of the law*; [DEU 8:16](https://ref.ly/Deut8:16) *over the gathering of manna*; [DEU 13:3–13:4](https://ref.ly/Deut13:3-Deut13:4) *by their attitude to a false prophet*; [JDG 2:20–3:4](https://ref.ly/Judg2:20-Judg3:4) *by leaving nations in the promised land*

Examples of God testing his people individually
-----------------------------------------------

**[GEN 22:1–22:2](https://ref.ly/Gen22:1-Gen22:2)** *See also* [JDG 7:4–7:7](https://ref.ly/Judg7:4-Judg7:7) *Gideon*; [2CH 32:31](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:31) *Hezekiah*; [2CO 12:7–12:9](https://ref.ly/2Cor12:7-2Cor12:9) *Paul*

Jesus Christ tested the faith of people when he was on earth
------------------------------------------------------------

**[JHN 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/John6:5-John6:6)** *See also* [MAT 15:22–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:22-Matt15:28) parallel [MRK 7:25–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:25-Mark7:30) *a Canaanite woman*; [MRK 8:17–8:21](https://ref.ly/Mark8:17-Mark8:21) *his disciples*

Believers have a responsibility to test the quality of their lives
------------------------------------------------------------------

**[ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2)** *See also* [PSA 17:3](https://ref.ly/Ps17:3)PSA [26:2](https://ref.ly/Ps26:2), [PSA 139:23](https://ref.ly/Ps139:23)[LAM 3:40](https://ref.ly/Lam3:40); [2CO 13:5](https://ref.ly/2Cor13:5); [GAL 6:4](https://ref.ly/Gal6:4); [1TH 5:21](https://ref.ly/1Thess5:21)

God will make a final test of believers' lives and service
----------------------------------------------------------

**[1CO 3:12–3:15](https://ref.ly/1Cor3:12-1Cor3:15)**

People may test God as an expression of faith and obedience
-----------------------------------------------------------

**[MAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:10)** *See also* [JDG 6:39](https://ref.ly/Judg6:39)

People must not test God as an expression of doubt and disobedience
-------------------------------------------------------------------

**[EXO 17:2](https://ref.ly/Exod17:2)** *See also* [EXO 17:7](https://ref.ly/Exod17:7); [NUM 14:22–14:23](https://ref.ly/Num14:22-Num14:23); [DEU 6:16](https://ref.ly/Deut6:16), [DEU 33:8](https://ref.ly/Deut33:8); [PSA 78:18](https://ref.ly/Ps78:18), [PSA 78:41](https://ref.ly/Ps78:41), [PSA 78:56](https://ref.ly/Ps78:56), [PSA 95:8](https://ref.ly/Ps95:8); [MAT 4:7](https://ref.ly/Matt4:7) parallel [LUK 4:12](https://ref.ly/Luke4:12); [DEU 6:16](https://ref.ly/Deut6:16); [MAT 19:3](https://ref.ly/Matt19:3) parallel [MRK 10:2](https://ref.ly/Mark10:2); [ACT 5:9](https://ref.ly/Acts5:9), [ACT 15:10](https://ref.ly/Acts15:10); [HEB 3:8–3:9](https://ref.ly/Heb3:8-Heb3:9)

The command to test the spirits
-------------------------------

**[1JN 4:1](https://ref.ly/1John4:1)**

* **Associated Passages:** James 1:2-3; 1 Peter 1:7; Job 7:17-18; Job 23:10; Psalms 11:5; Isaiah 1:25; Zechariah 13:9; Malachi 3:3; James 1:12; Exodus 16:4; Deuteronomy 8:2-3; Exodus 20:20; Deuteronomy 8:16; Deuteronomy 13:3-4; Judges 2:20-3:4; Genesis 22:1-2; Judges 7:4-7; 2 Chronicles 32:31; 2 Corinthians 12:7-9; John 6:5-6; Matthew 15:22-28; Mark 7:25-30; Mark 8:17-21; Romans 12:2; Psalms 17:3; Psalms 26:2; Psalms 139:23; Lamentations 3:40; 2 Corinthians 13:5; Galatians 6:4; 1 Thessalonians 5:21; 1 Corinthians 3:12-15; Malachi 3:10; Judges 6:39; Exodus 17:2; Exodus 17:7; Numbers 14:22-23; Deuteronomy 6:16; Deuteronomy 33:8; Psalms 78:18; Psalms 78:41; Psalms 78:56; Psalms 95:8; Matthew 4:7; Luke 4:12; Matthew 19:3; Mark 10:2; Acts 5:9; Acts 15:10; Hebrews 3:8-9; 1 John 4:1
* **Associated Articles:** 4351: refining (ID: `4351@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 4645: fleece (ID: `4645@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5473: proof, through testing (ID: `5473@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5593: trial (ID: `5593@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8027: faith, testing of (ID: `8027@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8104: assurance (ID: `8104@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8124: guidance (ID: `8124@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8833: threats (id: 8833)

Intimidating announcements of action to be taken unless conditions are met. God uses threats against tyrannical and disobedient earthly rulers as a means of restoring them to obedience and humility in his sight. God's people are not to threaten others and though the wicked will sometimes seek to intimidate them, they are to rely on God's protection.

God threatened Pharaoh
----------------------

*See also* [EXO 8:1–8:4](https://ref.ly/Exod8:1-Exod8:4), [EXO 8:21](https://ref.ly/Exod8:21), [EXO 9:2–9:3](https://ref.ly/Exod9:2-Exod9:3), [EXO 9:13–9:14](https://ref.ly/Exod9:13-Exod9:14), [EXO 10:3–10:6](https://ref.ly/Exod10:3-Exod10:6)

God threatened his own people
-----------------------------

### In general

**[JOS 23:15–23:16](https://ref.ly/Josh23:15-Josh23:16)** *See also* [LEV 26:14–26:20](https://ref.ly/Lev26:14-Lev26:20); [DEU 11:26–11:28](https://ref.ly/Deut11:26-Deut11:28), [DEU 28:15–28:20](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:20); [1SA 12:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:15), [1SA 12:25](https://ref.ly/1Sam12:25); [1KI 9:6–9:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs9:6-1Kgs9:7)

### Regarding the Babylonian captivity

**[EZK 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek6:9-Ezek6:10)** *See also* [JER 6:8](https://ref.ly/Jer6:8), [JER 22:5–22:7](https://ref.ly/Jer22:5-Jer22:7), [JER 44:28–44:29](https://ref.ly/Jer44:28-Jer44:29)

Threats made by God may be averted
----------------------------------

### Through a faithful intercessor

**[EXO 32:11–32:14](https://ref.ly/Exod32:11-Exod32:14)** *See also* [NEH 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Neh1:8-Neh1:9)

### Through turning from evil

**[JON 3:10](https://ref.ly/Jonah3:10)**

The result of threats inflicted by God may be alleviated
--------------------------------------------------------

### Through returning to the LORD

**[DEU 30:1–30:3](https://ref.ly/Deut30:1-Deut30:3)**

### Through submission to God's will

*See also* [JER 27:6–27:13](https://ref.ly/Jer27:6-Jer27:13)

God's people may be threatened by others
----------------------------------------

**[ACT 9:1](https://ref.ly/Acts9:1)** *See also* [2KI 18:17–18:35](https://ref.ly/2Kgs18:17-2Kgs18:35) parallel [ISA 36:1–36:20](https://ref.ly/Isa36:1-Isa36:20); [NEH 4:7–4:8](https://ref.ly/Neh4:7-Neh4:8); [PSA 55:9–55:11](https://ref.ly/Ps55:9-Ps55:11), [PSA 73:8](https://ref.ly/Ps73:8)

God's people, when threatened, are to rely on him
-------------------------------------------------

**[PSA 64:1–64:10](https://ref.ly/Ps64:1-Ps64:10)** *See also* [2KI 19:6–19:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:6-2Kgs19:7) parallel [ISA 37:6–37:7](https://ref.ly/Isa37:6-Isa37:7); [NEH 6:11–6:14](https://ref.ly/Neh6:11-Neh6:14); [ISA 30:17–30:18](https://ref.ly/Isa30:17-Isa30:18); [ACT 4:17–4:21](https://ref.ly/Acts4:17-Acts4:21), [ACT 4:29–4:30](https://ref.ly/Acts4:29-Acts4:30)

God's people are not to threaten others
---------------------------------------

**[EPH 6:9](https://ref.ly/Eph6:9)** *See also* [1PE 2:20–2:23](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:20-1Pet2:23)

The rich are more likely to be threatened than the poor
-------------------------------------------------------

[PRO 13:8](https://ref.ly/Prov13:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 8:1-4; Exodus 8:21; Exodus 9:2-3; Exodus 9:13-14; Exodus 10:3-6; Joshua 23:15-16; Leviticus 26:14-20; Deuteronomy 11:26-28; Deuteronomy 28:15-20; 1 Samuel 12:15; 1 Samuel 12:25; 1 Kings 9:6-7; Ezekiel 6:9-10; Jeremiah 6:8; Jeremiah 22:5-7; Jeremiah 44:28-29; Exodus 32:11-14; Nehemiah 1:8-9; Jonah 3:10; Deuteronomy 30:1-3; Jeremiah 27:6-13; Acts 9:1; 2 Kings 18:17-35; Isaiah 36:1-20; Nehemiah 4:7-8; Psalms 55:9-11; Psalms 73:8; Psalms 64:1-10; 2 Kings 19:6-7; Isaiah 37:6-7; Nehemiah 6:11-14; Isaiah 30:17-18; Acts 4:17-21; Acts 4:29-30; Ephesians 6:9; 1 Peter 2:20-23; Proverbs 13:8

## 8834: unbelief (id: 8834)

The lack of faith and trust in God that challenges his truthfulness and finds expression in disobedience and rebellion.

* **Associated Articles:** 8835: unbelief, nature and effects of (ID: `8835@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8836: unbelief, as response to God (ID: `8836@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8837: unbelief, and life of faith (ID: `8837@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8835: unbelief, nature and effects of (id: 8835)

Unbelief, or the failure to trust God, holds back his blessing and leads to condemnation and spiritual death.

The nature of unbelief
----------------------

### Not trusting God

**[PSA 78:22](https://ref.ly/Ps78:22)**

### Rebelling against God

**[HEB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Heb3:12)** *See also* [PSA 78:40](https://ref.ly/Ps78:40); [HEB 3:16](https://ref.ly/Heb3:16)

### Disobeying God

**[HEB 11:31](https://ref.ly/Heb11:31)** *NIV footnote.* *See also* [EPH 2:2](https://ref.ly/Eph2:2); [HEB 3:18–3:19](https://ref.ly/Heb3:18-Heb3:19), [HEB 4:11](https://ref.ly/Heb4:11)

### Questioning God's truthfulness

**[1JN 5:10](https://ref.ly/1John5:10)** *See also* [PSA 106:24](https://ref.ly/Ps106:24); [1JN 1:10](https://ref.ly/1John1:10)

Unbelief as the result of the devil's work
------------------------------------------

### Taking away God's word

**[LUK 8:12](https://ref.ly/Luke8:12)** parallels [MAT 13:19](https://ref.ly/Matt13:19), [MRK 4:15](https://ref.ly/Mark4:15)

### Causing spiritual blindness

**[2CO 4:4](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:4)** *See also* [ROM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Rom1:21); [2CO 4:3](https://ref.ly/2Cor4:3); [EPH 4:18](https://ref.ly/Eph4:18)

Unbelief as the result of not belonging to God
----------------------------------------------

**[JHN 10:26](https://ref.ly/John10:26)** *See also* [JHN 5:38](https://ref.ly/John5:38), [JHN 8:44–8:45](https://ref.ly/John8:44-John8:45), [JHN 12:37–12:40](https://ref.ly/John12:37-John12:40); [1CO 2:14](https://ref.ly/1Cor2:14)

Unbelief hinders Jesus Christ's miracles
----------------------------------------

**[MAT 13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:58)** parallel [MRK 6:5–6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:5-Mark6:6)

The consequences of persistent unbelief
---------------------------------------

### Failure to receive God's reward

**[HEB 3:19](https://ref.ly/Heb3:19)** *The “rest” that God's people enjoyed on entering the promised land is here taken to symbolise the final reward prepared by God for his people.* *See also* [HEB 4:1–4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:1-Heb4:2), [HEB 4:6](https://ref.ly/Heb4:6); [JUD 1:5](https://ref.ly/Jude1:5)

### Exclusion from the people of God

**[ROM 11:20](https://ref.ly/Rom11:20)** *See also* [HEB 6:4–6:6](https://ref.ly/Heb6:4-Heb6:6)

### Death in sin

**[JHN 8:24](https://ref.ly/John8:24)** *See also* [PSA 106:43](https://ref.ly/Ps106:43); [MRK 4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:12); [ACT 10:43](https://ref.ly/Acts10:43)

### God's wrath

**[JHN 3:36](https://ref.ly/John3:36)** *See also* [ROM 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rom1:18), [ROM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Rom2:5), [ROM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Rom2:8)

### Condemnation by God

**[JHN 3:18](https://ref.ly/John3:18)** *See also* [MRK 16:16](https://ref.ly/Mark16:16); [LUK 12:46](https://ref.ly/Luke12:46); [JHN 12:48](https://ref.ly/John12:48); [2TH 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:12); [REV 21:8](https://ref.ly/Rev21:8)

* **Associated Passages:** Psalms 78:22; Hebrews 3:12; Psalms 78:40; Hebrews 3:16; Hebrews 11:31; Ephesians 2:2; Hebrews 3:18-19; Hebrews 4:11; 1 John 5:10; Psalms 106:24; 1 John 1:10; Luke 8:12; Matthew 13:19; Mark 4:15; 2 Corinthians 4:4; Romans 1:21; 2 Corinthians 4:3; Ephesians 4:18; John 10:26; John 5:38; John 8:44-45; John 12:37-40; 1 Corinthians 2:14; Matthew 13:58; Mark 6:5-6; Hebrews 3:19; Hebrews 4:1-2; Hebrews 4:6; Jude 1:5; Romans 11:20; Hebrews 6:4-6; John 8:24; Psalms 106:43; Mark 4:12; Acts 10:43; John 3:36; Romans 1:18; Romans 2:5; Romans 2:8; John 3:18; Mark 16:16; Luke 12:46; John 12:48; 2 Thessalonians 2:12; Revelation 21:8

## 8836: unbelief, as response to God (id: 8836)

The stubborn and sinful refusal to accept God's word and his messengers. Since the coming of Jesus Christ it is frequently expressed in the rejection of his person and teaching.

Unbelief expressed towards God
------------------------------

**[PSA 78:22](https://ref.ly/Ps78:22)**

### Rebellion against God

**[2KI 17:14–17:15](https://ref.ly/2Kgs17:14-2Kgs17:15)** *See also* [NEH 9:16](https://ref.ly/Neh9:16); [PSA 78:17–78:19](https://ref.ly/Ps78:17-Ps78:19), [PSA 95:8–95:11](https://ref.ly/Ps95:8-Ps95:11); [ISA 65:2](https://ref.ly/Isa65:2); [JER 7:26](https://ref.ly/Jer7:26); [ACT 7:51](https://ref.ly/Acts7:51); [HEB 3:12–3:19](https://ref.ly/Heb3:12-Heb3:19)

### Rejection of God's promises

**[PSA 106:24](https://ref.ly/Ps106:24)** *See also* [ROM 4:20](https://ref.ly/Rom4:20), [ROM 10:16](https://ref.ly/Rom10:16); [HEB 4:2](https://ref.ly/Heb4:2)

Unbelief expressed towards Jesus Christ
---------------------------------------

### Rejection by those close to him

**[JHN 1:11](https://ref.ly/John1:11)** *See also* [MAT 13:54–13:58](https://ref.ly/Matt13:54-Matt13:58) parallel [MRK 6:1–6:6](https://ref.ly/Mark6:1-Mark6:6); [LUK 4:14–4:30](https://ref.ly/Luke4:14-Luke4:30); [JHN 7:5](https://ref.ly/John7:5)

### Rejection of his miracles

**[MAT 12:22–12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:22-Matt12:24)** parallels [MRK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22), [LUK 11:14–11:16](https://ref.ly/Luke11:14-Luke11:16)

### Rejection of his claims

*See also* [LUK 22:67](https://ref.ly/Luke22:67); [JHN 5:37–5:38](https://ref.ly/John5:37-John5:38), [JHN 6:36](https://ref.ly/John6:36), [JHN 6:64](https://ref.ly/John6:64), [JHN 10:24–10:26](https://ref.ly/John10:24-John10:26); [1PE 2:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet2:7)

### Rejection of his words

**[JHN 3:11–3:12](https://ref.ly/John3:11-John3:12)** *See also* [JHN 5:46–5:47](https://ref.ly/John5:46-John5:47), [JHN 8:45](https://ref.ly/John8:45)

### Demands for miraculous signs

**[JHN 4:48](https://ref.ly/John4:48)** *See also* [MAT 12:38–12:39](https://ref.ly/Matt12:38-Matt12:39) parallel [LUK 11:29](https://ref.ly/Luke11:29); [MAT 16:4](https://ref.ly/Matt16:4) parallel [MRK 8:12](https://ref.ly/Mark8:12); [LUK 11:16](https://ref.ly/Luke11:16)

Unbelief expressed towards God's messengers
-------------------------------------------

[GEN 19:16](https://ref.ly/Gen19:16) *Lot*; ; [EXO 4:1](https://ref.ly/Exod4:1) *Moses*; ; [2KI 7:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs7:2) *Elisha*; ; [2CH 32:15](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:15) *Sennacherib of Assyria urges the inhabitants of Jerusalem to disbelieve Hezekiah's words*; ; [ISA 53:1](https://ref.ly/Isa53:1) *Isaiah*; ; [MAT 21:32](https://ref.ly/Matt21:32) *John the Baptist*; [ACT 13:50](https://ref.ly/Acts13:50), [ACT 14:2](https://ref.ly/Acts14:2); [ACT 17:13](https://ref.ly/Acts17:13) *Paul and Silas*; [ACT 19:9](https://ref.ly/Acts19:9), [ACT 28:24](https://ref.ly/Acts28:24)

Unbelief expressed in unrighteousness
-------------------------------------

**[TIT 1:15](https://ref.ly/Titus1:15)** *See also* [ROM 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rom1:18); [2TH 2:12](https://ref.ly/2Thess2:12), [2TH 3:2](https://ref.ly/2Thess3:2); [1TI 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:13)

Unbelief despite miraculous signs
---------------------------------

**[NEH 9:16–9:17](https://ref.ly/Neh9:16-Neh9:17)** *See also* [EXO 4:8–4:9](https://ref.ly/Exod4:8-Exod4:9); [PSA 78:32](https://ref.ly/Ps78:32); [JHN 9:18](https://ref.ly/John9:18), [JHN 10:31–10:33](https://ref.ly/John10:31-John10:33), [JHN 12:37](https://ref.ly/John12:37)

Means of overcoming unbelief
----------------------------

[JHN 10:37–10:38](https://ref.ly/John10:37-John10:38) *the evidence of Jesus Christ's miracles*; ; [ROM 10:14–10:15](https://ref.ly/Rom10:14-Rom10:15) *preaching the gospel*; ; [1CO 14:22–14:24](https://ref.ly/1Cor14:22-1Cor14:24) *prophecy in worship*; ; [1PE 3:1](https://ref.ly/1Pet3:1) *the behaviour of a believing spouse*; ; [1TI 1:13](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:13) *God's mercy*

## 8837: unbelief, and life of faith (id: 8837)

Believers may go through periods of doubt and indecision, especially on account of difficulties in trusting God or accepting certain aspects of his will for them.

General examples of unbelief in believers
-----------------------------------------

### Abraham

**[GEN 17:17](https://ref.ly/Gen17:17)** *See also* [GEN 15:8](https://ref.ly/Gen15:8)

### Sarah

[GEN 18:13–18:14](https://ref.ly/Gen18:13-Gen18:14)

### Moses

[NUM 11:21–11:22](https://ref.ly/Num11:21-Num11:22)

### Gideon

[JDG 6:12–6:13](https://ref.ly/Judg6:12-Judg6:13), [JDG 6:17](https://ref.ly/Judg6:17), [JDG 6:36–6:40](https://ref.ly/Judg6:36-Judg6:40)

### Elisha's servant

[2KI 4:42–4:44](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:42-2Kgs4:44)

### Zechariah

[LUK 1:20](https://ref.ly/Luke1:20)

### John the Baptist

[MAT 11:3](https://ref.ly/Matt11:3)

The unbelief of Jesus Christ's disciples
----------------------------------------

### Unbelief concerning Jesus Christ's power over the natural world

**[MAT 8:24–8:26](https://ref.ly/Matt8:24-Matt8:26)** parallels [MRK 4:38–4:40](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38-Mark4:40), [LUK 8:23–8:25](https://ref.ly/Luke8:23-Luke8:25)**[MAT 14:26](https://ref.ly/Matt14:26)** parallel [MRK 6:49](https://ref.ly/Mark6:49)**[MAT 14:29–14:31](https://ref.ly/Matt14:29-Matt14:31), [MAT 16:8–16:10](https://ref.ly/Matt16:8-Matt16:10)** parallel [MRK 8:17–8:21](https://ref.ly/Mark8:17-Mark8:21) *See also* [MRK 6:37](https://ref.ly/Mark6:37) parallel [JHN 6:7](https://ref.ly/John6:7); [MRK 6:52](https://ref.ly/Mark6:52); [MAT 15:33](https://ref.ly/Matt15:33) parallel [MRK 8:4](https://ref.ly/Mark8:4); [JHN 11:39](https://ref.ly/John11:39); [ACT 12:15–12:17](https://ref.ly/Acts12:15-Acts12:17)

### Unbelief concerning Jesus Christ's resurrection

**[MRK 16:14](https://ref.ly/Mark16:14)**; **[LUK 24:25](https://ref.ly/Luke24:25)** *See also* [MRK 16:9–16:13](https://ref.ly/Mark16:9-Mark16:13); [LUK 24:11](https://ref.ly/Luke24:11), [LUK 24:37–24:41](https://ref.ly/Luke24:37-Luke24:41); [JHN 20:25](https://ref.ly/John20:25)

### Unbelief concerning Jesus Christ's power to heal

**[MAT 17:17](https://ref.ly/Matt17:17)** parallels [MRK 9:19](https://ref.ly/Mark9:19), [LUK 9:41](https://ref.ly/Luke9:41) *See also* [MAT 17:19–17:20](https://ref.ly/Matt17:19-Matt17:20)

Encouragements in overcoming unbelief
-------------------------------------

**[MRK 9:24](https://ref.ly/Mark9:24)**; **[JHN 14:11](https://ref.ly/John14:11)** *See also* [MAT 11:4–11:6](https://ref.ly/Matt11:4-Matt11:6); [JHN 10:37–10:38](https://ref.ly/John10:37-John10:38), [JHN 20:30–20:31](https://ref.ly/John20:30-John20:31); [ROM 4:20](https://ref.ly/Rom4:20)

## 8839: unfaithfulness (id: 8839)

The failure to fulfil the obligations of a relationship, resulting in disloyalty to God and to others.

* **Associated Articles:** 8840: unfaithfulness, to God (ID: `8840@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8841: unfaithfulness, to people (ID: `8841@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8840: unfaithfulness, to God (id: 8840)

In the OT the terms of the covenant with God were constantly broken by Israel by acts of idolatry and disobedience. The consequence was God's judgment, which eventually included exile.

Warnings within the covenant
----------------------------

[NEH 1:8](https://ref.ly/Neh1:8); ; [LEV 26:1](https://ref.ly/Lev26:1), [LEV 26:14–26:17](https://ref.ly/Lev26:14-Lev26:17), [LEV 26:31–26:33](https://ref.ly/Lev26:31-Lev26:33); ; [DEU 4:25–4:27](https://ref.ly/Deut4:25-Deut4:27), [DEU 8:19–8:20](https://ref.ly/Deut8:19-Deut8:20); ; [PRO 2:22](https://ref.ly/Prov2:22); ; [EZK 14:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek14:13), [EZK 15:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek15:8)

Unfaithfulness seen in idolatry
-------------------------------

### Idolatry as marital unfaithfulness

**[JER 3:20](https://ref.ly/Jer3:20)** *The covenant between God and Israel at Sinai viewed in terms of a marriage contract*; **[JER 3:6–3:9](https://ref.ly/Jer3:6-Jer3:9)** *See also* [JER 3:12–3:14](https://ref.ly/Jer3:12-Jer3:14), [JER 9:2](https://ref.ly/Jer9:2), [JER 31:32](https://ref.ly/Jer31:32); [HOS 1:2](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2)

### Idolatry as prostitution

[1CH 5:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr5:25); ; [HOS 4:10–4:12](https://ref.ly/Hos4:10-Hos4:12), [HOS 9:1](https://ref.ly/Hos9:1)

### Idolatry as abandoning God

**[JER 1:16](https://ref.ly/Jer1:16)** *See also* [JDG 10:13](https://ref.ly/Judg10:13); [JER 5:19](https://ref.ly/Jer5:19), [JER 15:6](https://ref.ly/Jer15:6)

Unfaithfulness seen in other forms of disobedience
--------------------------------------------------

### Trust in money and possessions

**[JOB 31:24–31:28](https://ref.ly/Job31:24-Job31:28)** *See also* [JER 49:4](https://ref.ly/Jer49:4)

### Doing wrong to others

**[NUM 5:5–5:6](https://ref.ly/Num5:5-Num5:6)** *See also* [LEV 6:2](https://ref.ly/Lev6:2); [PRO 11:3](https://ref.ly/Prov11:3), [PRO 13:2](https://ref.ly/Prov13:2); [ISA 1:21–1:23](https://ref.ly/Isa1:21-Isa1:23); [HOS 6:6–6:7](https://ref.ly/Hos6:6-Hos6:7)

### Marrying foreign wives

**[EZR 10:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:2)** *See also* [EZR 9:1–9:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:1-Ezra9:4); [NEH 13:27](https://ref.ly/Neh13:27)

### Withholding tithes and offerings

[JOS 7:1](https://ref.ly/Josh7:1), [JOS 22:20](https://ref.ly/Josh22:20); ; [MAL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Mal3:8)

### Breaches in the ritual law

[1SA 14:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:33); ; [MAL 1:7–1:8](https://ref.ly/Mal1:7-Mal1:8)

Unfaithfulness as a characteristic of God's people throughout the OT
--------------------------------------------------------------------

**[JER 5:11](https://ref.ly/Jer5:11)** *See also* [ACT 7:37–7:41](https://ref.ly/Acts7:37-Acts7:41); [JDG 2:16–2:17](https://ref.ly/Judg2:16-Judg2:17); [NEH 9:26](https://ref.ly/Neh9:26); [HAG 1:7–1:9](https://ref.ly/Hag1:7-Hag1:9)

Unfaithfulness among Israel's leadership
----------------------------------------

### The unfaithfulness of the priesthood

**[EZR 9:1–9:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:1-Ezra9:2)** *See also* [2CH 36:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:14); [EZK 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:26); [MAL 2:7–2:8](https://ref.ly/Mal2:7-Mal2:8)

### Unfaithful kings

Solomon's unfaithfulness leads to unfaithfulness among his subjects: [1KI 11:4–11:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:4-1Kgs11:5), [1KI 11:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:33); [2CH 12:1–12:2](https://ref.ly/2Chr12:1-2Chr12:2) *Rehoboam*; ; [2CH 26:16–26:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:16-2Chr26:18) *Uzziah's bad example*; [2CH 28:19–28:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:19-2Chr28:23), [2CH 29:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr29:19); [2KI 21:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:9) parallel [2CH 33:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:9); ; [2KI 21:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs21:16); ; [2CH 33:19](https://ref.ly/2Chr33:19)

Unfaithfulness leads to judgment
--------------------------------

[DEU 32:20](https://ref.ly/Deut32:20); ; [PSA 73:27](https://ref.ly/Ps73:27), [PSA 78:56–78:59](https://ref.ly/Ps78:56-Ps78:59); ; [PRO 21:18](https://ref.ly/Prov21:18)

### Individuals judged for unfaithfulness

[JOS 7:1](https://ref.ly/Josh7:1), [JOS 7:25](https://ref.ly/Josh7:25); ; [1CH 10:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr10:13)

### Wandering in the desert

**[NUM 14:33](https://ref.ly/Num14:33)** *See also* [NUM 14:34–14:35](https://ref.ly/Num14:34-Num14:35)

### The people taken into exile

**[EZK 39:23](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:23)** *See also* [1CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:1); [DAN 9:7](https://ref.ly/Dan9:7)

Restoration following repentance
--------------------------------

**[EZK 39:25–39:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:25-Ezek39:26)** *God's purpose through the exile is to bring about the eventual repentance and restoration of his people.* *See also* [LEV 26:40–26:42](https://ref.ly/Lev26:40-Lev26:42); [DEU 30:1–30:3](https://ref.ly/Deut30:1-Deut30:3); [NEH 1:8–1:9](https://ref.ly/Neh1:8-Neh1:9)

* **Associated Passages:** Nehemiah 1:8; Leviticus 26:1; Leviticus 26:14-17; Leviticus 26:31-33; Deuteronomy 4:25-27; Deuteronomy 8:19-20; Proverbs 2:22; Ezekiel 14:13; Ezekiel 15:8; Jeremiah 3:20; Jeremiah 3:6-9; Jeremiah 3:12-14; Jeremiah 9:2; Jeremiah 31:32; Hosea 1:2; 1 Chronicles 5:25; Hosea 4:10-12; Hosea 9:1; Jeremiah 1:16; Judges 10:13; Jeremiah 5:19; Jeremiah 15:6; Job 31:24-28; Jeremiah 49:4; Numbers 5:5-6; Leviticus 6:2; Proverbs 11:3; Proverbs 13:2; Isaiah 1:21-23; Hosea 6:6-7; Ezra 10:2; Ezra 9:1-4; Nehemiah 13:27; Joshua 7:1; Joshua 22:20; Malachi 3:8; 1 Samuel 14:33; Malachi 1:7-8; Jeremiah 5:11; Acts 7:37-41; Judges 2:16-17; Nehemiah 9:26; Haggai 1:7-9; Ezra 9:1-2; 2 Chronicles 36:14; Ezekiel 22:26; Malachi 2:7-8; 1 Kings 11:4-5; 1 Kings 11:33; 2 Chronicles 12:1-2; 2 Chronicles 26:16-18; 2 Chronicles 28:19-23; 2 Chronicles 29:19; 2 Kings 21:9; 2 Chronicles 33:9; 2 Kings 21:16; 2 Chronicles 33:19; Deuteronomy 32:20; Psalms 73:27; Psalms 78:56-59; Proverbs 21:18; Joshua 7:25; 1 Chronicles 10:13; Numbers 14:33; Numbers 14:34-35; Ezekiel 39:23; 1 Chronicles 9:1; Daniel 9:7; Ezekiel 39:25-26; Leviticus 26:40-42; Deuteronomy 30:1-3; Nehemiah 1:8-9

## 8841: unfaithfulness, to people (id: 8841)

A breach of the trust that is expected within specific relationships, and also a failure of mutual responsibility within a community life; the breaking of the last six of the Ten Commandments.

Unfaithfulness to one another breaks God's commandments
-------------------------------------------------------

**[LEV 19:18](https://ref.ly/Lev19:18)** *See also* [EXO 20:12–20:17](https://ref.ly/Exod20:12-Exod20:17); [DEU 5:16–5:21](https://ref.ly/Deut5:16-Deut5:21); [MAT 22:39](https://ref.ly/Matt22:39) parallel [MRK 12:31](https://ref.ly/Mark12:31); [ROM 1:29–1:31](https://ref.ly/Rom1:29-Rom1:31)

Unfaithfulness to promises made
-------------------------------

**[NUM 30:2](https://ref.ly/Num30:2)** *See also* [NUM 30:9](https://ref.ly/Num30:9); [JOS 9:20](https://ref.ly/Josh9:20); [1SA 20:42](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:42)

Unfaithfulness in business
--------------------------

[MAT 25:24–25:26](https://ref.ly/Matt25:24-Matt25:26); ; [LUK 16:3–16:12](https://ref.ly/Luke16:3-Luke16:12), [LUK 19:8](https://ref.ly/Luke19:8)

Unfaithfulness to the marriage covenant
---------------------------------------

**[MAL 2:14–2:15](https://ref.ly/Mal2:14-Mal2:15)**

### The seriousness of marital unfaithfulness

Adultery in the OT punishable by death: [LEV 20:10](https://ref.ly/Lev20:10); ; [DEU 22:22](https://ref.ly/Deut22:22); ; [JHN 8:3–8:5](https://ref.ly/John8:3-John8:5); [MAT 5:32](https://ref.ly/Matt5:32), [MAT 19:9](https://ref.ly/Matt19:9)

### Examples of marital unfaithfulness

[JDG 19:1–19:2](https://ref.ly/Judg19:1-Judg19:2); [HOS 1:2–1:3](https://ref.ly/Hos1:2-Hos1:3), [HOS 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hos2:2), [HOS 2:5](https://ref.ly/Hos2:5)

Unfaithfulness among family and friends
---------------------------------------

**[MIC 7:5–7:6](https://ref.ly/Mic7:5-Mic7:6)**

### God's people may face opposition from their families

**[JER 12:6](https://ref.ly/Jer12:6)** *See also* [2SA 15:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam15:10); [MAT 10:21](https://ref.ly/Matt10:21) parallels [MRK 13:12](https://ref.ly/Mark13:12), [LUK 21:16](https://ref.ly/Luke21:16)

### Unfaithful friends cause great sorrow

**[PSA 55:12–55:14](https://ref.ly/Ps55:12-Ps55:14)** *See also* [PSA 41:9](https://ref.ly/Ps41:9), [PSA 55:20–55:21](https://ref.ly/Ps55:20-Ps55:21)JOB [6:14–6:17](https://ref.ly/Ps6:14-Ps6:17); [PSA 38:11](https://ref.ly/Ps38:11); [PRO 25:19](https://ref.ly/Prov25:19)

Unfaithfulness to Jesus Christ during his earthly ministry
----------------------------------------------------------

### Jesus Christ deserted by his followers

**[JHN 6:66](https://ref.ly/John6:66), [JHN 16:32](https://ref.ly/John16:32)** *See also* [MAT 26:31](https://ref.ly/Matt26:31) parallel [MRK 14:27](https://ref.ly/Mark14:27); [ZEC 13:7](https://ref.ly/Zech13:7); [MAT 26:56](https://ref.ly/Matt26:56) parallel [MRK 14:50](https://ref.ly/Mark14:50)

### Jesus Christ betrayed by Judas

[MAT 26:47–26:50](https://ref.ly/Matt26:47-Matt26:50) parallels [MRK 14:43–14:46](https://ref.ly/Mark14:43-Mark14:46), [LUK 22:47–22:48](https://ref.ly/Luke22:47-Luke22:48); ; [JHN 18:2–18:5](https://ref.ly/John18:2-John18:5)

### Jesus Christ denied by Peter

[MAT 26:74](https://ref.ly/Matt26:74) parallels [MRK 14:71–14:72](https://ref.ly/Mark14:71-Mark14:72), [LUK 22:59–22:61](https://ref.ly/Luke22:59-Luke22:61); ; [JHN 18:26–18:27](https://ref.ly/John18:26-John18:27)

Unfaithfulness to God's appointed leaders
-----------------------------------------

### The Israelites challenge their leaders

**[EXO 16:3–16:8](https://ref.ly/Exod16:3-Exod16:8)** *See also* [1SA 8:7–8:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:7-1Sam8:8)

### Paul's friends desert him

**[2TI 1:15](https://ref.ly/2Tim1:15)** *See also* [2TI 4:9–4:11](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:9-2Tim4:11), [2TI 4:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:16)

Examples of treachery and betrayal
----------------------------------

[JDG 16:18–16:19](https://ref.ly/Judg16:18-Judg16:19) *Samson betrayed by Delilah*; ; [2SA 3:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:27) *Joab murders Abner*; ; [2SA 4:5–4:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:5-2Sam4:6) *Ish\-Bosheth assassinated by Recab and Baanah*; ; [2SA 11:14–11:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam11:14-2Sam11:15) *David has Uriah killed in battle*; ; [2SA 20:9–20:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:9-2Sam20:10) *Amasa killed by Joab*; ; [1KI 21:8–21:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:8-1Kgs21:10) *Jezebel's treachery towards Naboth*

## 8843: unforgivable sin (id: 8843)

The wilful, outwardly expressed and impenitent slander against the Holy Spirit, when Jesus Christ's mighty works, clearly performed by the power of the Holy Spirit, are attributed to Satan, thus subjecting Christ to public disgrace.

The unforgivable sin is slandering the Holy Spirit
--------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 12:31–12:32](https://ref.ly/Matt12:31-Matt12:32)** parallel [MRK 3:28–3:29](https://ref.ly/Mark3:28-Mark3:29) *See also* [LUK 12:10](https://ref.ly/Luke12:10); [HEB 10:26–10:29](https://ref.ly/Heb10:26-Heb10:29)

The unforgivable sin is attributing Jesus Christ's miracles to Satan
--------------------------------------------------------------------

**[MRK 3:30](https://ref.ly/Mark3:30)** *See also* [MAT 12:24](https://ref.ly/Matt12:24) parallels [MRK 3:22](https://ref.ly/Mark3:22), [LUK 11:15](https://ref.ly/Luke11:15)

* **Associated Passages:** Matthew 12:31-32; Mark 3:28-29; Luke 12:10; Hebrews 10:26-29; Mark 3:30; Matthew 12:24; Mark 3:22; Luke 11:15
* **Associated Articles:** 2369: Jesus Christ, responses to (ID: `2369@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 3245: Holy Spirit, blasphemy against (ID: `3245@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5800: blasphemy (ID: `5800@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 5951: slander (ID: `5951@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 6193: impenitence (ID: `6193@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8704: apostasy (ID: `8704@DictionaryBibleThemes`); 8844: unforgiveness (ID: `8844@DictionaryBibleThemes`)


## 8844: unforgiveness (id: 8844)

God remains unforgiving to those described as unrepentant, who blaspheme against the Holy Spirit and who are themselves unforgiving. Being unforgiving and demonstrating true love are mutually exclusive.

The unforgiveness of God
------------------------

### Towards the unrepentant

**[DEU 29:18–29:21](https://ref.ly/Deut29:18-Deut29:21)** *See also* [EXO 23:21](https://ref.ly/Exod23:21), [EXO 34:7](https://ref.ly/Exod34:7); [NUM 14:18](https://ref.ly/Num14:18); [JOS 24:19](https://ref.ly/Josh24:19); [2KI 24:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:4); [ISA 30:12–30:14](https://ref.ly/Isa30:12-Isa30:14); [JER 5:7](https://ref.ly/Jer5:7); [LAM 3:42](https://ref.ly/Lam3:42); [HOS 1:6](https://ref.ly/Hos1:6); [LUK 18:10–18:14](https://ref.ly/Luke18:10-Luke18:14); [HEB 10:28–10:31](https://ref.ly/Heb10:28-Heb10:31)

### Towards those whose hearts are hardened

[JOS 11:20](https://ref.ly/Josh11:20); ; [MAT 13:13–13:15](https://ref.ly/Matt13:13-Matt13:15) parallels [MRK 4:11–4:12](https://ref.ly/Mark4:11-Mark4:12), [LUK 8:9–8:10](https://ref.ly/Luke8:9-Luke8:10); ; [ISA 6:9–6:10](https://ref.ly/Isa6:9-Isa6:10); ; [JHN 9:39–9:41](https://ref.ly/John9:39-John9:41), [JHN 20:23](https://ref.ly/John20:23)

### Towards those who blaspheme against the Holy Spirit

**[MAT 12:31–12:32](https://ref.ly/Matt12:31-Matt12:32)** parallels [MRK 3:28–3:29](https://ref.ly/Mark3:28-Mark3:29), [LUK 12:10](https://ref.ly/Luke12:10) *Blasphemy against the Spirit probably involves attributing the authenticating miracles of Jesus Christ to the work of Satan rather than to the power of the Holy Spirit.* *See also* [1JN 5:16](https://ref.ly/1John5:16)

### Towards those who are not forgiving

**[MAT 6:14–6:15](https://ref.ly/Matt6:14-Matt6:15)**; **[JAS 2:12–2:13](https://ref.ly/Jas2:12-Jas2:13)** *See also* [MAT 5:23–5:24](https://ref.ly/Matt5:23-Matt5:24), [MAT 7:1–7:2](https://ref.ly/Matt7:1-Matt7:2) parallel [LUK 6:37–6:38](https://ref.ly/Luke6:37-Luke6:38); [MAT 18:32–18:35](https://ref.ly/Matt18:32-Matt18:35), [MAT 23:23](https://ref.ly/Matt23:23); [MRK 11:25–11:26](https://ref.ly/Mark11:25-Mark11:26) *NIV footnote at verse 26*; [ROM 14:9–14:13](https://ref.ly/Rom14:9-Rom14:13)

God's people call upon him not to forgive the wicked
----------------------------------------------------

**[JER 18:23](https://ref.ly/Jer18:23)** *See also* [NEH 4:5](https://ref.ly/Neh4:5); [ISA 2:9](https://ref.ly/Isa2:9)

True love is forgiving
----------------------

**[ROM 5:8](https://ref.ly/Rom5:8)**; **[1CO 13:4–13:5](https://ref.ly/1Cor13:4-1Cor13:5)** *See also* [PSA 86:5](https://ref.ly/Ps86:5), [PSA 130:3–130:4](https://ref.ly/Ps130:3-Ps130:4); [DAN 9:9](https://ref.ly/Dan9:9); [MIC 7:18](https://ref.ly/Mic7:18); [ACT 13:38](https://ref.ly/Acts13:38); [ROM 15:7](https://ref.ly/Rom15:7); [2CO 2:5–2:11](https://ref.ly/2Cor2:5-2Cor2:11); [EPH 4:32](https://ref.ly/Eph4:32); [COL 3:13](https://ref.ly/Col3:13)
