## 8845: unfruitfulness (id: 8845)

A lack of fruitfulness, whether material or spiritual, which is often seen as a sign of God's disfavour or anger.

Unfruitfulness as a moral or spiritual condition
------------------------------------------------

**[MAT 21:19](https://ref.ly/Matt21:19)** parallel [MRK 11:14](https://ref.ly/Mark11:14) *See also* [JOB 15:32–15:33](https://ref.ly/Job15:32-Job15:33); [EZK 17:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:9), [EZK 19:10–19:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek19:10-Ezek19:12); [MAT 21:43](https://ref.ly/Matt21:43); [HEB 6:7–6:8](https://ref.ly/Heb6:7-Heb6:8)

Reasons for unfruitfulness
--------------------------

### Disobedience

**[LEV 26:18–26:20](https://ref.ly/Lev26:18-Lev26:20)** *See also* [DEU 11:17](https://ref.ly/Deut11:17), [DEU 28:15–28:16](https://ref.ly/Deut28:15-Deut28:16); [PSA 78:46](https://ref.ly/Ps78:46), [PSA 107:33–107:34](https://ref.ly/Ps107:33-Ps107:34); [ISA 5:10](https://ref.ly/Isa5:10), [ISA 32:10](https://ref.ly/Isa32:10); [HOS 8:7](https://ref.ly/Hos8:7); [HAG 2:19](https://ref.ly/Hag2:19)

### A continuing preoccupation with the world

**[MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22)** parallels [MRK 4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14)

### A broken relationship with Jesus Christ

**[JHN 15:4](https://ref.ly/John15:4)** *See also* [JHN 15:6](https://ref.ly/John15:6)

Some results of unfruitfulness
------------------------------

### Unfruitfulness condemned

[ISA 5:1–5:7](https://ref.ly/Isa5:1-Isa5:7)

### Unfruitfulness leads to rejection

[MAT 3:8–3:10](https://ref.ly/Matt3:8-Matt3:10) parallel [LUK 3:9](https://ref.ly/Luke3:9); ; [MAT 7:19](https://ref.ly/Matt7:19)

Unfruitfulness retrieved
------------------------

**[LUK 13:6–13:9](https://ref.ly/Luke13:6-Luke13:9)** *The parable holds out the possibility of restoration.*

### Discipline with a view to renewed fruitfulness

**[JHN 15:2](https://ref.ly/John15:2)**

## 8846: ungodliness (id: 8846)

The state of believing in God, while adopting a lifestyle which seems to deny this. Believers are warned to guard against ungodliness.

Expressions of ungodliness
--------------------------

### A defiant spirit towards God

**[MAT 7:6](https://ref.ly/Matt7:6)** *Jesus Christ is teaching his disciples not to share the riches of spiritual truth with persons who are persistently unappreciative, vicious and defiant.* *See also* [JOB 36:8–36:13](https://ref.ly/Job36:8-Job36:13); [PSA 73:8–73:11](https://ref.ly/Ps73:8-Ps73:11)

### Mocking God and/or his people

**[2PE 3:3–3:4](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:3-2Pet3:4)** *See also* [PSA 35:15–35:16](https://ref.ly/Ps35:15-Ps35:16); [ACT 13:40–13:41](https://ref.ly/Acts13:40-Acts13:41); [HAB 1:5](https://ref.ly/Hab1:5)

### Evil behaviour

**[ISA 32:6–32:7](https://ref.ly/Isa32:6-Isa32:7)** *See also* [PRO 11:9](https://ref.ly/Prov11:9); [JON 1:1–1:2](https://ref.ly/Jonah1:1-Jonah1:2); [1TI 1:9–1:11](https://ref.ly/1Tim1:9-1Tim1:11)

Examples of ungodliness
-----------------------

[2CH 36:15–36:16](https://ref.ly/2Chr36:15-2Chr36:16) *the southern kingdom of Judah*; ; [PSA 52:1–52:3](https://ref.ly/Ps52:1-Ps52:3) *Doeg the Edomite*; ; [ISA 9:13–9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:13-Isa9:17) *the northern kingdom of Israel*; [JER 23:11–23:12](https://ref.ly/Jer23:11-Jer23:12), [JER 23:15–23:17](https://ref.ly/Jer23:15-Jer23:17); [1CO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:11); ; [EPH 2:1–2:3](https://ref.ly/Eph2:1-Eph2:3); [2TI 3:1–3:5](https://ref.ly/2Tim3:1-2Tim3:5) *people in general during the last days (the time between the first and second comings of Jesus Christ)*; ; [HEB 12:16](https://ref.ly/Heb12:16) *Esau*

God will judge the ungodly
--------------------------

### In this life

**[PRO 11:31](https://ref.ly/Prov11:31)** *See also* [PRO 1:28–1:33](https://ref.ly/Prov1:28-Prov1:33), [PRO 3:33–3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:33-Prov3:34); [ISA 10:5–10:7](https://ref.ly/Isa10:5-Isa10:7), [ISA 33:14](https://ref.ly/Isa33:14); [ROM 1:18](https://ref.ly/Rom1:18)

### At the final judgment

**[2PE 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Pet3:7)** *See also* [JOB 27:8](https://ref.ly/Job27:8); [PRO 3:34](https://ref.ly/Prov3:34); [1PE 4:18](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:18); [2PE 2:4–2:10](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:4-2Pet2:10); [JUD 1:14–1:15](https://ref.ly/Jude1:14-Jude1:15)

Jesus Christ died for the ungodly
---------------------------------

[ROM 5:6–5:8](https://ref.ly/Rom5:6-Rom5:8), [ROM 11:26](https://ref.ly/Rom11:26); ; [ISA 59:20](https://ref.ly/Isa59:20)

God's people must guard against ungodliness
-------------------------------------------

### By relying upon God's grace

**[TIT 2:11–2:12](https://ref.ly/Titus2:11-Titus2:12)**

### By putting no confidence in ungodly people

**[1CO 6:1](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:1)** *See also* [PSA 1:1](https://ref.ly/Ps1:1); [JUD 1:3–1:4](https://ref.ly/Jude1:3-Jude1:4), [JUD 1:17–1:19](https://ref.ly/Jude1:17-Jude1:19)

### By avoiding ungodly teaching and behaviour

**[2TI 2:16](https://ref.ly/2Tim2:16)** *See also* [1TI 4:7](https://ref.ly/1Tim4:7), [1TI 6:20–6:21](https://ref.ly/1Tim6:20-1Tim6:21)

## 8847: vulgarity (id: 8847)

Believers are to avoid vulgar intentions
----------------------------------------

### The men of Sodom had vulgar intentions

[GEN 19:5](https://ref.ly/Gen19:5)

### They were judged by God

[GEN 19:24–19:25](https://ref.ly/Gen19:24-Gen19:25); ; [2PE 2:6–2:7](https://ref.ly/2Pet2:6-2Pet2:7); ; [JUD 1:7](https://ref.ly/Jude1:7)

### The men of Gibeah had vulgar intentions

[JDG 19:22](https://ref.ly/Judg19:22), [JDG 19:25](https://ref.ly/Judg19:25)

### They were punished by the Israelites

[JDG 20:4–20:7](https://ref.ly/Judg20:4-Judg20:7), [JDG 20:33–20:36](https://ref.ly/Judg20:33-Judg20:36), [JDG 20:48](https://ref.ly/Judg20:48)

### Such vulgarity is condemned by God

[LEV 18:22](https://ref.ly/Lev18:22); ; [ROM 1:24–1:28](https://ref.ly/Rom1:24-Rom1:28)

Paul warns against vulgar speech
--------------------------------

[EPH 4:29](https://ref.ly/Eph4:29), [EPH 5:4](https://ref.ly/Eph5:4); ; [COL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Col3:8)

Believers are to avoid vulgar actions
-------------------------------------

### Noah got drunk and exposed himself

[GEN 9:21–9:22](https://ref.ly/Gen9:21-Gen9:22)

### Lot's daughters became pregnant by their father

[GEN 19:31–19:36](https://ref.ly/Gen19:31-Gen19:36)

Some converts led vulgar and depraved lives before conversion
-------------------------------------------------------------

[1CO 6:9–6:11](https://ref.ly/1Cor6:9-1Cor6:11)

The Cretans had a reputation for vulgarity
------------------------------------------

[TIT 1:12](https://ref.ly/Titus1:12), [TIT 1:15–1:16](https://ref.ly/Titus1:15-Titus1:16)

## 8848: worldliness (id: 8848)

The sinful tendency to conform to the world, to be contrasted with the biblical command to maintain contact with the world while avoiding becoming like the world. Believers are called to live in the world, while not being of the world. This attitude is supremely exemplified in the ministry of Jesus Christ.

The paradox of God's love for a sinful world
--------------------------------------------

[JHN 3:16](https://ref.ly/John3:16); ; [1JN 2:15–2:16](https://ref.ly/1John2:15-1John2:16)

God's people are called to be separate from the world, yet a source of blessing to it
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The example of Abraham

**[GEN 12:1–12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:1-Gen12:3)** *The life of Abraham illustrates the important biblical balance, called to separation from his native environment to be a blessing to the nations.* *See also* [GEN 15:7](https://ref.ly/Gen15:7), [GEN 18:18](https://ref.ly/Gen18:18); [GAL 3:8](https://ref.ly/Gal3:8)

### The example of others

[GEN 41:56–41:57](https://ref.ly/Gen41:56-Gen41:57) *Joseph*; ; [DAN 2:48–2:49](https://ref.ly/Dan2:48-Dan2:49) *Daniel and his friends*

Conformity to the pagan standards of the OT world disparaged
------------------------------------------------------------

[LEV 18:28](https://ref.ly/Lev18:28) *with respect to sexual perversion*; [1SA 8:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:5); ; [1KI 21:7](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:7); [1KI 11:7–11:8](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:7-1Kgs11:8) *with respect to idolatry*; ; [ISA 13:19](https://ref.ly/Isa13:19) *with respect to pride*; [ISA 31:1](https://ref.ly/Isa31:1); ; [ZEC 4:6](https://ref.ly/Zech4:6)

God's concern for the nations of the world
------------------------------------------

**[ISA 49:6](https://ref.ly/Isa49:6)** *See also* [JON 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jonah4:11); [REV 22:2](https://ref.ly/Rev22:2)

Jesus Christ is a model of the required balance of being in the world but not of it
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### He was in the world

**[JHN 3:16–3:17](https://ref.ly/John3:16-John3:17)** *See also* [JHN 9:5](https://ref.ly/John9:5), [JHN 16:28](https://ref.ly/John16:28)

### He was not of the world

**[JHN 8:23](https://ref.ly/John8:23)** *See also* [LUK 4:5–4:8](https://ref.ly/Luke4:5-Luke4:8) parallel [MAT 4:8–4:10](https://ref.ly/Matt4:8-Matt4:10); [JHN 17:14](https://ref.ly/John17:14), [JHN 18:36](https://ref.ly/John18:36); [1JN 3:1](https://ref.ly/1John3:1)

### He was a friend of the disreputable

**[MAT 11:19](https://ref.ly/Matt11:19)** *See also* [MRK 2:16](https://ref.ly/Mark2:16); [LUK 7:44–7:48](https://ref.ly/Luke7:44-Luke7:48)

### He was concerned for outsiders

**[JHN 10:16](https://ref.ly/John10:16)** *See also* [MAT 28:19](https://ref.ly/Matt28:19); [MRK 7:24–7:30](https://ref.ly/Mark7:24-Mark7:30) parallel [MAT 15:21–15:28](https://ref.ly/Matt15:21-Matt15:28); [ACT 1:8](https://ref.ly/Acts1:8)

Believers are called to live in the world, while not being of it
----------------------------------------------------------------

### They have been chosen out of the world

**[JHN 15:19](https://ref.ly/John15:19)** *See also* [JHN 17:14](https://ref.ly/John17:14), [JHN 17:16](https://ref.ly/John17:16); [2CO 6:17](https://ref.ly/2Cor6:17)

### They must avoid conformity with the world

**[ROM 12:2](https://ref.ly/Rom12:2)** *See also* [1TH 4:3–4:5](https://ref.ly/1Thess4:3-1Thess4:5); [2TI 4:10](https://ref.ly/2Tim4:10); [TIT 2:12](https://ref.ly/Titus2:12); [JAS 1:27](https://ref.ly/Jas1:27); [1PE 1:14–1:16](https://ref.ly/1Pet1:14-1Pet1:16), [1PE 4:3–4:4](https://ref.ly/1Pet4:3-1Pet4:4); [1JN 2:15](https://ref.ly/1John2:15), [1JN 5:4](https://ref.ly/1John5:4)

### They must maintain contact with the world

**[1CO 5:9–5:10](https://ref.ly/1Cor5:9-1Cor5:10)** *See also* [JHN 17:11](https://ref.ly/John17:11)

### They must do good in the world

**[GAL 6:10](https://ref.ly/Gal6:10)** *See also* [TIT 2:14](https://ref.ly/Titus2:14)

* **Associated Passages:** John 3:16; 1 John 2:15-16; Genesis 12:1-3; Genesis 15:7; Genesis 18:18; Galatians 3:8; Genesis 41:56-57; Daniel 2:48-49; Leviticus 18:28; 1 Samuel 8:5; 1 Kings 21:7; 1 Kings 11:7-8; Isaiah 13:19; Isaiah 31:1; Zechariah 4:6; Isaiah 49:6; Jonah 4:11; Revelation 22:2; John 3:16-17; John 9:5; John 16:28; John 8:23; Luke 4:5-8; Matthew 4:8-10; John 17:14; John 18:36; 1 John 3:1; Matthew 11:19; Mark 2:16; Luke 7:44-48; John 10:16; Matthew 28:19; Mark 7:24-30; Matthew 15:21-28; Acts 1:8; John 15:19; John 17:16; 2 Corinthians 6:17; Romans 12:2; 1 Thessalonians 4:3-5; 2 Timothy 4:10; Titus 2:12; James 1:27; 1 Peter 1:14-16; 1 Peter 4:3-4; 1 John 2:15; 1 John 5:4; 1 Corinthians 5:9-10; John 17:11; Galatians 6:10; Titus 2:14

## 8849: worry (id: 8849)

A sense of uneasiness and anxiety about the future. Scripture indicates that such anxiety is ultimately grounded in a lack of trust in God and his purposes.

The causes of worry
-------------------

### Being world\-centred

**[MAT 6:25](https://ref.ly/Matt6:25)** parallel [LUK 12:22](https://ref.ly/Luke12:22) *See also* [MAT 6:34](https://ref.ly/Matt6:34), [MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22) parallels [MRK 4:19](https://ref.ly/Mark4:19), [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14); [LUK 12:29](https://ref.ly/Luke12:29), [LUK 21:34](https://ref.ly/Luke21:34); [1CO 7:33](https://ref.ly/1Cor7:33)

### Lacking confidence in God

**[JHN 14:1](https://ref.ly/John14:1)** *See also* [MAT 6:26–6:32](https://ref.ly/Matt6:26-Matt6:32) parallel [LUK 12:27–12:30](https://ref.ly/Luke12:27-Luke12:30); [MAT 8:25](https://ref.ly/Matt8:25) parallels [MRK 4:38](https://ref.ly/Mark4:38), [LUK 8:24](https://ref.ly/Luke8:24); [MAT 14:31](https://ref.ly/Matt14:31), [MAT 16:8](https://ref.ly/Matt16:8)

### Abandoning God

**[DEU 28:64–28:65](https://ref.ly/Deut28:64-Deut28:65)** *See also* [LAM 1:3](https://ref.ly/Lam1:3); [EZK 4:16–4:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek4:16-Ezek4:17), [EZK 12:19](https://ref.ly/Ezek12:19)

Worry leads people away from God
--------------------------------

**[PSA 37:8](https://ref.ly/Ps37:8)** *See also* [PRO 12:25](https://ref.ly/Prov12:25); [MAT 13:22](https://ref.ly/Matt13:22) parallel [LUK 8:14](https://ref.ly/Luke8:14); [LUK 10:40–10:42](https://ref.ly/Luke10:40-Luke10:42)

Worry is futile
---------------

**[MAT 6:27](https://ref.ly/Matt6:27)** parallel [LUK 12:25](https://ref.ly/Luke12:25) *See also* [ECC 2:22–2:23](https://ref.ly/Eccl2:22-Eccl2:23)

Remedies for worry
------------------

### Being God\-centred

**[ISA 26:3](https://ref.ly/Isa26:3)**; **[MAT 6:33](https://ref.ly/Matt6:33)** parallel [LUK 12:31](https://ref.ly/Luke12:31) *See also* [JER 17:7–17:8](https://ref.ly/Jer17:7-Jer17:8); [ROM 8:6](https://ref.ly/Rom8:6)

### Prayer

**[PSA 55:22](https://ref.ly/Ps55:22)**; **[PHP 4:6–4:7](https://ref.ly/Phil4:6-Phil4:7)** *See also* [PSA 94:18–94:19](https://ref.ly/Ps94:18-Ps94:19), [PSA 139:23](https://ref.ly/Ps139:23); [1PE 5:7](https://ref.ly/1Pet5:7)
